title: Testimony of Muhammed Kaya
section: Presiding Judge вҖӢ
text:
Presiding Judge вҖӢ

Presiding Judge : Muhammed Kaya. Muhammed, youвҖҷre wearing red again.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I had no other coat. I had no other coat, sir.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Very well. You are detained, I believe. In another investigation. First of all, you have the right to refrain from testifying. On one side is your uncle, on another your uncleвҖҷs son. On another your aunt-in-law. You have the right to refrain from testifying. Do not let it be misunderstood by us that refraining from testifying means you answered questions and ran away. It is your legal right. Do you wish to testify?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Okay.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Then we will hear you without oath. There is an investigation concerning you. Because of that investigation I will intervene in some questions. You also have the right not to answer questions that impute a crime to you, that allege you are an offender. Let me also explain this to the press so it is not misunderstood. Friends, here is the situation. The witness is a suspect in another investigation. Therefore, they have the right not to answer questions here that would affect their status in that investigation. This is a right granted by the CMK. Do you understand? The presiding judgeвҖҷs intervention is an intervention in this direction. Such a question would constitute an accusation of crime against him. For example: Did you help Salim kill the body? That question is one that imputes an offense to him. That is why I intervene. This is the reason for my intervention. Do you understand? Do not let it be misunderstood. Everything you put out is being taken to other meanings. Let me explain this to you as well.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes. Muhammed. Let us start from the day Narin went missing. Where were you that day? What were you doing? Do you remember?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I woke up at eight. We were distributing invitations. My cousin UДҹurcan had a wedding. Around two, two-thirty, the four of us headed toward Mardin Derik. We were going to distribute invitations there. Around two, two-thirty we took the village roads toward Derik. At seven-thirty we took a meal break at the house of my uncle BarДұЕҹ GГјranвҖҷs mother-in-law. At eight-thirty-five my cousin Devran GГјran got word that Narin had gone missing. At eight-thirty we set off from there toward the village. We arrived from Derik to the village in about forty-five minutes to an hour.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : And you began search activities?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I searched the village. Then at night there was someone called Uncle Emir. He said he had jinn and so on.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Soldiers, move to that side please. Do not stand around our witness. You as well, come. Come here. We have no issue. Let our witness be comfortable. You are comfortable, right? You are giving your statement of your own free will. Okay. Our duty as the court is to ensure you can give your statement comfortably. Yes. We continue.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I started searching the village. I looked into wells. I opened the closed well and looked. I looked into empty wells. I checked the canal side. I, UДҹurcan GГјran.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Now can you come to the day you found the slipper?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : The day I found the slipper was the second day. The 22nd of the month. We were on the canal bridge. My aunt-in-law called me and said the children were out of milk. I have two nieces/nephews. вҖңCan you buy milk? Do you have a car?вҖқ I said yes. I was going to buy milk. I saw the slipper. On the village road. I passed over the slipper from about two and a half kilometers away. There were GypsiesвҖҷ tents on the side. I thought it belonged to them. I bought the milk and so on. On the way back I saw the slipper again. I thought, maybe itвҖҷs theirs, maybe it isnвҖҷt, so I should take it. I showed it to my aunt-in-law.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you drove over the slipper. At first.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : It was in the middle of the road.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Well Muhammed, here is the key point. Here is the matter of debate. Perhaps thousands of people, maybe hundreds, passed along that road. They did not see that slipper but you did. The allegation is this. You have the right not to answer this question. Did you, at the prompting or direction of one of the defendants, place that slipper there and make such a move in order to derail the investigation? Did anyone tell you to place that slipper there, find it, and draw the gendarmerie in that direction?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did Salim, or the othersвҖ”Nevzat, YГјksel, EnesвҖ”give such an instruction?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Absolutely not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Then what is the reason that you found that slipper there while, as I said, the other defendants and other family members could not?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Well, it could simply be a coincidence. For example, a car could have passed and a child could have thrown the slipper out of the car. Or it could belong to the Gypsies.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : YouвҖҷre saying, how would I know? I passed and I saw it.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I passed and I saw the slipper. At first I said it must belong to the Gypsies. I passed it by. Then on the way back it was still there. It was in the same place on the way back. I drove over it again. Then I thought, just in case, let me show it to my aunt-in-law. If the slipper is hers, we already know the place. If it is not hers, it is trash anyway. I went to the village. There was a commotion at my uncleвҖҷs house. It was very crowded. New cars and so on. The gendarmerie were shouting. Thinking something had happened there, I jumped out of the car and ran straight to the house. вҖңHas something happened?вҖқ They said no. They were going to hold a meeting. They were clearing everyone out of the house. A gendarme was shouting. There was a commander shouting. My mother and so on were there. I am against that sort of thing. I said, вҖңCommander, donвҖҷt shout, I will clear everyone out.вҖқ He said okay. I cleared everyone out of the house. My motherвҖҷs maternal aunt is diabetic and cannot walk on her own.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : What is your motherвҖҷs name?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Д°lhan.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Д°lhan.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : My aunt Azize helped my great-aunt. They brought her to the stairs. I took her by the legs and carried her down the stairs. Then I said, let me take you to Uncle SalimвҖҷs. She said no, take me to HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs house, his brotherвҖҷs. I said okay. вҖңIs there a car?вҖқ вҖңYes, right across.вҖқ I put her in the car. I gave the bag to my aunt. My phone was on charge, I asked for it. Then I went to HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs house. I took her out of the car; at that moment Osman GГјran arrived. вҖңIвҖҷm out of cigarettes. LetвҖҷs go to the petrol station.вҖқ I said okay. We went to the petrol station and bought cigarettes. Then my cousin UДҹurcan called. вҖңWeвҖҷre up on the hill. Come sit for five or ten minutes.вҖқ Okay. We went up. Then Osman said, IвҖҷm going to the village. Osman said, IвҖҷm going to the village. YouвҖҷll come. We had two cars by then. I said okay. He got into that car. At that moment, his legs are longer than mine. He adjusted the seat. He noticed the slipper.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : He noticed the slipper. He said, whose slipper is this? Only then did it come to my mind. I said, I found it on the road. вҖңBring it right away to your aunt-in-lawвҖ”letвҖҷs send it to Baran. Let her show it.вҖқ If it is the slipper, we already know the place. If not, itвҖҷs trash.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So the reason you forgot the slipper was because they were going to hold that meeting, due to the commotion.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : After that I was never alone. And I had been without sleep for 36 hours.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I had been without sleep for 36 hours. But Muhammed, what do you do for a living?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I am a civil servant. At Trakya University.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : This is a very serious piece of evidence.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I came on Saturday. I came for the wedding. My cousinвҖҷs happinessвҖ”we were going to celebrate it. Now such a thing happened to us.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : What I mean is this: finding the slipper was a big deal that day. If a slipper was found, the search would be directed that way. In this regard, you are a civil servant and also a bright young man. From your answers I understand as much. You can express yourself quite well. The fact that you could forget this came across as a bit odd to us. Perhaps it is because this struck us as odd that you are detained. I am not concerned with why you are detained, but as a court this is the aspect that seems odd to us. When you found the slipper, shouldnвҖҷt you have shown it to everyone first?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Because of the commotion there, I forgot the slipper.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : That is the matter.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Afterwards, since there were always people with me, I couldnвҖҷt remember the slipper.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Afterward you found the slipper. Whom did you inform to ask whose slipper it was?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I called Baran; her brother didnвҖҷt answer. I called my own brother, Berat Kaya. I said I found a slipper on the road. IвҖҷll send you a photo; show it to my aunt-in-law. If itвҖҷs hers, itвҖҷs hers; if not, it isnвҖҷt. I hung up; Baran called. NarinвҖҷs brother. NarinвҖҷs brother called. Yes. Baran called; I told him the same thing I had told my brother. Then I sent the photo. Two or three minutes later Baran called. There were screams in the background. He said the slipper is hers. вҖңWhere are you? Come to the place by the woods.вҖқ Okay. We went there. The commander came. We hadnвҖҷt given any statement yet. вҖңWho found it?вҖқ вҖңI did.вҖқ вҖңGive your number and donвҖҷt go anywhere.вҖқ Okay. My uncle Arif came over the slipper. He said, вҖңI will take the slipper.вҖқ Meaning it is her slipper. We then went to the station, in order to give statements anyway.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So how was it understood that the slipper did not belong to BaranвҖҷs sister? Were you at the station at that time?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I was at the station. I went to the station around 10:00вҖ“10:30. They released me at 6 p.m. the next day.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : During that time, that the slipper was not the girlвҖҷs slipperвҖ”that it was not NarinвҖҷs slipper. You donвҖҷt know who said that and so on?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : There was a Petty Officer Bayram.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Not Baran. That it was not NarinвҖҷs slipper.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : There was Petty Officer Bayram. He came into the room and said, вҖңDidnвҖҷt you notice at all? Would an eight-year-old girl wear size 25 slippers?вҖқ I told that commander, вҖңI donвҖҷt have a daughter. I have never bought slippers for a little girl. How would I know whether the size is 25 or 31?вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So it was understood in that way that the slipper in question did not belong to the girl, by the size, is that right?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : That is what he said. That is what he told me.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : The commander said so. The commander said it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes. Let us avoid repetition. Do not go into it. I believe I asked quite detailed questions, but if you did not like my questions, of course you may ask yours. Counsel for Mr.В ArifвҖ”no questions. Any questions from our DiyarbakДұr Bar Association attorneys? No.В Any from the Ministry of Family? No.В From the defendantsвҖ”Ms.В YГјksel, what will you say?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : (Repeats YГјkselвҖҷs question) I will summarize. Very well. When the slipper came to me and they said it was a red slipper, I said, yes, it could be my daughterвҖҷs slipper. I screamed about this. However, at first nothing about the size of the slipper occurred to us. Then we checked, she said, and thatвҖҷs the situation. Please, Mr.В Prosecutor. Our Prosecutor has a question.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Muhammed Kaya
section: Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ
text:
Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ

Trial Prosecutor : I will ask it in a different way without repetition. Let me start from a different angle, then. Around what time did you return from Derik?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : My cousin got a call at 08:30вҖ“08:45. I mean, we came from Derik at 180вҖ“220.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : The distance is about an hour anyway.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : We reached the village in about forty-five minutes to an hour. That isвҖҰ Half pastвҖҰ It was around nine-thirty or ten when we were in the village.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Around ten you are recorded as being in the area of Dicle University.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Correct. I was talking. Another question came up there. It interrupted. It interrupted. Someone called Uncle EmirвҖ”our worker. A watchman. Ali RДұza GГјran. A worker of my motherвҖҷs uncle. A watchman. He has a wire. He says he has jinn. Believing him, he whispers to the wire. He moves the wire. We asked if he could find her. He said he could. He said he was getting a signal. He directed the wire toward the Dicle University faculty side. We went in three cars that way.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Is there a tea garden operated by someone from the GГјran family there?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : AtвҖҰ Beylerbeyi Garden. Upstream of the new bridge. Uncle Emir the watchman is already there.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : I understand. You do not have to answer this question, but did Salim GГјran come there that evening?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : No.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : He did not come.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : No.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : In your statement you have such a sentence about Salim GГјran: that he sent Devran and someone to a village with a white Egea, sent his son.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : That was with the commanders, at the bridge.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You say on August 22 he sent Devran and an unknown person to an unknown village.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : On August 22, together with the commanders, together with the commander, someone was sent. My uncle also sent his son. That person next to him was either someone from intelligence or a commander. I do not know who it was. He was sent in that manner. He also gave me the key. Of my uncle ArifвҖҷs car. That way I became the driver as well.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Madam President, to you: if that slipper had been there as first visible, everyone would have seen it. On the contrary, I think that slipper was never placed there. That slipper was always with you. Since you found this outвҖ”you do not have to answer thisвҖ”why didnвҖҷt you go to the gendarmerie? There is gendarmerie at every step there anyway. Showing this to YГјksel, getting an opinion on whether the slipper belonged to her, and throwing it awayвҖ”was that within your or your brotherвҖҷs remit?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : No, not at all. I found the slipper. The gendarmerie are on the road anyway. There was no gendarmerie there, you would throw it away directly. Right thereвҖ”when I went there, there was no gendarmerie on the road. Everyone was at the house. As I said, there was a very big commotion there. They had all cars moved from there as well. By showing it to my aunt-in-lawвҖ”anyway the gendarmes were there. It is forensic.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Very well. Thank you, Mr.В Muhammed. We move to questions from defense counsel.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Muhammed Kaya
section: Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir : You said, вҖңAfter the slipper was found, they took me away,вҖқ but you did not fully describe that part. We are curious about that as well. What did you experience? What happened?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I found the slipper. They took me to the station at around half past ten. Me, Osman GГјran, UДҹurcan GГјran, HГјseyin GГјran, BarДұЕҹ GГјranвҖҷs son. They put each of us in a separate room. A man from intelligence came in and called me a murderer.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I am intervening at this point. The addressee of these questions is a criminal complaint to the DiyarbakДұr Chief Public ProsecutorвҖҷs Office. As it will add nothing to the file, I am skipping this question. Explain it, but I have skipped this question.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : The indictment starts with this, you know.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : We have obtained what we needed. We have heard our witness.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Shall I explain what I asked anyway?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Briefly, of course.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : The indictment starts with the slipper. After the slipper, was the family targeted, was there suspicion, and here he himself alleges he was called a murderer. There are also allegations that, for example, although the girl was missing, the family thought she was dead. These allegations are connected with this. That is why I asked.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : File a criminal complaint regarding this alleged torture. If you have such an allegation.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : They beat me for nine hours. Twenty intelligence officers constantly. Every hour they came and I was beaten.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You may file a criminal complaint on this matter, friends. Do you understand? It is your legal right. You have the right to file a criminal complaint with the DiyarbakДұr Chief Public ProsecutorвҖҷs Office. Very well.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : What was said was left incomplete. If he could continue explaining.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : They dragged me and called me murderer. вҖңHow did you kill? Did you strangle her? Did you crush her head with a stone?вҖқ They used such words. Then a commander came. вҖңYou are not the murderer. IвҖҷll cut off my member and make myself a woman.вҖқ Things like that were said. I said I was in Derik. вҖңNo, you were not in Derik. You are the murderer.вҖқ They used sentences like that.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Until what time did the night searches continue? On the first night, that is on the 21st, until what time did the searches continue? Until what time were you awake?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I did not stop until the time I found the slipper. Let me put it that way.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : You had not slept at all?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I had slept four hours the previous day. In total I had about 36 hours awake.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : And the scuffle part was, I think, left incomplete, not fully understood. You said there was a commotion. What exactly happened in that commotion? You found the slipper, came back, forgot it. What happened that you forgot?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He explained that well. Go on, explain it once more. I understood it quite well.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I found the slipper. I came to the village. There was a commotion there. As I said, it was quite crowded. New cars had arrived. IntelligenceвҖ”I did not know at the time, of course. At that moment I ran straight from the car to the house. The commander was shouting at my mother and so on. вҖңCommander, what happened?вҖқ вҖңThere is a meeting.вҖқ I said, вҖңDo not shout at anyone. I will get whoever is in the house out.вҖқ He said okay. Together with my mother we got everyone out. My motherвҖҷs sister has diabetes. She cannot walk on her own. My aunt Azize and my mother helped her. She reached the stairs. I held her legs and carried her down. Then I said, let me take you to HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs house. The guests were going there as well. вҖңNo, take me to HГјseyinвҖҷs house,вҖқ she said. Okay, I said. I put her in the car. We went to HГјseyinвҖҷs house. I took her out of the car. Osman GГјran came. вҖңI have no cigarettes. LetвҖҷs go to ГҮarДұklДұ.вҖқ Okay, I said. We went to ГҮarДұklДұ. He bought his cigarettes and so on. My cousin UДҹurcan called. вҖңIвҖҷm on the hill at the livestock market. Come get some air.вҖқ Osman and HГјseyin were with me. вҖңNo problem, let them go,вҖқ he said. Okay. We went there. We sat for 15вҖ“20 minutes. Then Osman said, I will go to the village. There were already two cars. Okay, I said. He got into the car and noticed the slipper. He noticed the slipper. He said, whose slipper is this? I said, I found it on the road. I forgot it. I brought it to the village to show it. I forgot. Since you showed it, it came to mind. I immediately sent a photo to Baran. Then to the bridge area. IntelligenceвҖ”I donвҖҷt know them.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, alright. You told the rest. In fact you omitted one thing. You omitted one thing. You told the commander, вҖңI donвҖҷt like such things, IвҖҷll handle it.вҖқ You forgot to mention that. I meant it in a good way. I said something favorable to you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay, okay, I understand.вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : He immediately attacked those clustered houses there. He shouted at the Gypsies, вҖңWhere is the girl?вҖқ That.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay, okay, thatвҖҷs not what we mean.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Which vehicle was with you?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : The Egea. It was my uncle ArifвҖҷs car.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : And you were taken into custody regarding this vehicle. Was the first examination made then?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : I understand. Thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you. Any other questions? Go ahead, Mr.В Onur, counsel for Salim.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Muhammed Kaya
section: Defense counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did your uncle Salim GГјran direct the search activities for you? Specifically, regarding the matter of the Gypsies, was it Salim GГјran who directed you to the place where the Gypsies were?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : No, absolutely not. I only found the slipper. I sent the slipper to Baran and the others; after your aunt-in-law confirmed it was hers, they came that way.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I have already asked this as well.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : That night, could Salim GГјran have gone alone to EДҹertutmaz creekвҖ”on the night of the incidentвҖ”could Salim GГјran have gone alone to EДҹertutmaz creek in his own vehicle?вҖӢ


Muhammed Kaya : I did not see it, so I say no.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Osman GГјran
text:
Reading Time
~ 13 min


Word Count
2584

title: Testimony of Osman GГјran
section: Presiding Judge вҖӢ
text:
Presiding Judge вҖӢ

Presiding Judge : You have the right to refrain from testifying. First of all, this is not a wrong thing. вҖңI wish to refrain from testifying.вҖқ You have the right to testify or not to testify. In our view, this does not make you guilty. Alright? Because on one side is your uncle, on the other side your first cousin. You have this right. Do you wish to testify?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I do.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Then we will hear you without oath.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Should I tell about the day of the incident?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : We will start from the day of the incident and then continue. Mr.В Osman, let me remind you again of this point. You have the right not to answer questions that are self-incriminating. I will also intervene on this matter. Because some questions may lead to allegations of praising a criminal, concealing criminal evidence, or harboring a criminal. I also expect sensitivity from our colleagues on this matter. Yes, Mr.В Osman, I am listening to you. What are these conversations? Did you meet or speak with Enes? Tell us about the day of the incident.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I only met Enes that day. I met him after she went missing. I realized around seven, seven-thirty in the evening that she was missing. My aunt called out from the path, saying she was missing. At that moment, everyone was there along with Enes. Together with Enes and the villagers, we went out to search. I had not seen Enes earlier that day. Only that day around six, six-thirty in the evening I went in front of my grandfatherвҖҷs house. I live in the city center. I do not have a house in the village; when I go to the village with my grandfather, I go to my grandfather. I only met Enes that day when we realized she was missing.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, you only met Enes that day, when she went missing.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I mean, by вҖңmet,вҖқ we did not have any conversation. We were only in the same environment.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You were in the same environment. Osman, then you started searching the next day.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : A slipper was found, a red slipper.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Yes, correct.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : This matterвҖ” could you tell us about it as well? I mean, could you describe this incident?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Yes. I was in front of my grandfatherвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, Osman. What is this slipper matter?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Yes. I was in front of my grandfatherвҖҷs house. There were guests. It was evening. My cousin Muhammed Kaya drove down in my uncle Arif GГјranвҖҷs car. Our elderly great-aunt, Fatma Гңnsal, got out of the car. He had brought her down from above, from my uncle ArifвҖҷs house. Law enforcement had said only the family should remain at Arif GГјranвҖҷs house. At that time, Fatma Гңnsal and the guests were also at my uncle ArifвҖҷs house. Seeing that the elderly were standing, in that commotion he brought the elderly down. When he brought them down, I said there was a guestвҖҷs car behind my own car. Since you are here, I said, letвҖҷs go as far as the Kadoil petrol station. At the entrance to ГҮarДұklДұ. We went together to the Kadoil petrol station. We bought some things there. Then Muhammed called our cousin UДҹurcan. He asked, вҖңWhere are you?вҖқ He said, вҖңIвҖҷm sitting in an open area near the livestock market.вҖқ вҖңAlright,вҖқ he said. вҖңWeвҖҷll come there too. WeвҖҷll sit with you for a bit.вҖқ When we went there, of course HГјseyin GГјran was also in the car. HГјseyin GГјran, my uncle BarДұЕҹвҖҷs son.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Then we went there, Your Honor. When I got out of the car, I said to Muhammed: UДҹurcan already has a car. There are guests at home. There is no young man to serve them. Let HГјseyin and me return to the village with my uncle ArifвҖҷs car. After you two sit for a while, you can come back again. Then he said okay. We got into my uncle ArifвҖҷs car. I sat in the driverвҖҷs seat. HГјseyin sat next to me. While adjusting the seat to myself, I saw a red slipper in the floor mat tray. I picked it up. I asked, вҖңWhose is this?вҖқ Muhammed said, вҖңI went to the market for my nephews and nieces to shop. I picked it up when I came across it along the way. But in that commotion I forgot to mention it when I brought the elderly down.вҖқ I then said, вҖңLetвҖҷs send it to older brother Baran. It might be NarinвҖҷs.вҖқ Then to older brother BaranвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I did not understand. Where did Muhammed say he took the slipper from?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : On the way. He picked it up while going along the way.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Well, that day everyone was already looking for a slipper or something. How did he forget this?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Law enforcement had said, while the elderly were up there, to leave my uncle ArifвҖҷs house. Only the family should remain. Perhaps they were going to have a talk with the family. He came there after leaving the market, after buying items. When he saw the elderly outside standing, he said, вҖңCome, IвҖҷll take you down.вҖқ He put the elderly into the car. He said he forgot it at that moment.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But isnвҖҷt it very interesting that he forgot? Did it make sense to you?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : That is what he said.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Then did you report this slipper to law enforcement?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Yes. We sent it to older brother Baran via WhatsApp. Baran called us back a minute later. He said it could be NarinвҖҷs. Then we said we would go to where Muhammed saw the slipper. We said you can come there as well. We handed the slipper over to law enforcement.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You handed the slipper over to law enforcement.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Muhammed remembered where he found it, right? He took you there. Where he found it. Was it a pair or two pairs?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : It was a single slipper.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He found it there and forgot to mention it. You got into the car and went there. These are the questions we will ask. The Lady Judge has a point that is on her mind. While you were searching, you say вҖңwe went to sit,вҖқ вҖңUДҹurcan,вҖқ etc. What is meant by вҖңto sitвҖқ or вҖңI was sitting thereвҖқ? While the whole village is in a mobilized state searching for the girl, what does вҖңto sitвҖқ mean?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Not during the sitting. Before the slipper was found, UДҹurcan was already sitting in an open area by the livestock market, without us knowing.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Which day are you talking about? 21.08 or 22.08?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : It was 3вҖ“4 days after the incident, Your Honor. 3вҖ“4 days later.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Actually the next day is 22.08. The day you found the slipper is the next day, right?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : It was the third day of the incident exactly. I do not remember.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : That is normal. There is nothing odd about not remembering.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Osman GГјran
section: Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ
text:
Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ

Trial Prosecutor : We have come to the slipper issue again. In this statement of yours, I recall: thinking that it belonged to Syrians, he first took it. But he thought it might belong to Narin. You have such a sentence in your statement. You said you used that road 6 times.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I did not see this slipper.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You have that sentence, right? I remember you gave timesвҖ” morning, noon, eveningвҖ” for a road you passed 6 times. Exactly. That neither you in those 6 passes, nor the gendarmerie, nor AFAD saw it. Muhammed says he took it without even getting out of the vehicleвҖ” bending from the driverвҖҷs door without getting out of the vehicle. Do you remember if he gave you his statement like this? How did he take the slipper?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : He told me this: вҖңI went to the market. It caught my eye when I went. I did not take it. I bought the items. Then on the way back I even drove over the slipper. In fact, I stopped again. When I backed up, I took the slipper. I opened my door and put it on the floor mat.вҖқвҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So he says it was in a visible place, and you said you passed there 6 times and the whole village passed there like that.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : If I had seen it, I would have picked it up.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Your Honor, I have another question. Nesrin GГјran is your mother, isnвҖҷt she? There is a womenвҖҷs quarrel in which your mother was present. You know that, right? Do you have anything your mother said on this matter? Did you hear anything? Do you have any information about it?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Was there an insult directed at YГјksel?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : No, my mother was in front of SГјveyla Г–zkanвҖҷs house that day. She was not even up there during the quarrel. That is what is said. I did not see it with my own eyes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : No, I was only asking if you had any knowledge.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : IвҖ” my motherвҖ” such a thingвҖ” Okay.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Thank you.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : When you spoke with Muhammed, at what time did Muhammed find that slipper?вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : How many hours had passed?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : When he told me, he said half an hour to forty minutes earlier. вҖңI went to the market.вҖқвҖӢ

title: Testimony of Osman GГјran
section: DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ
text:
DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ

Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir вҖӢ

Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : He told you he found it half an hour earlier. Then did anyone say to you, вҖңThis could be NarinвҖҷs slipperвҖқ?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : On the first day of the incident, law enforcement came to my aunt YГјksel GГјran. They asked what clothes the girlвҖ” NarinвҖ” was wearing. She said a black dress, and she had red slippers on her feet. Everyone there heard that at that time, and that is how it stuck in our minds.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : No.В What I want to ask is thisвҖ” let me make it clearer. Did anyone from the GГјran family say to you, вҖңThis could be NarinвҖҷs slipper; give your statement in that wayвҖқ?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : BarДұЕҹ, to whom did you send the photo message?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : We sent it to older brother Baran.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs what he is asking.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : No.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Osman GГјran
section: Defense Counsel for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Now, the prosecution and the questionsвҖ” I assume influenced by the indictmentвҖ” state that in the quarrel among the women there was an insult directed toward YГјksel GГјran. This matter is constantly being asked to witnesses.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : However, please read the minutes. There is no insult directed at YГјksel GГјran. It is toward her house. In other words, there is manipulation here.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Possibly. I would call it not manipulation but interpretation. Yes, as the prosecution it is very fine that you look for faults, but it is not manipulationвҖ” it is interpretation. Maybe it was said to YГјksel. Maybe it was said to another woman behind YГјksel. Maybe it was said to Arif. Maybe it was said to Nevzat behind ArifвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : When a house is described, generally the manвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Possibly. Possibly. That is yoursвҖ” your interpretation. The interpretation recorded in the minutes by the prosecution and the DiyarbakДұr Bar AssociationвҖ” none of it binds us. The court will take all interpretations and state its own interpretation at the end.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Osman GГјran
section: Defense Counsels for Enes GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsels for Enes GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Mahir Akbilek вҖӢ

Law. Mahir Akbilek : Mr.В Osman, how many days were you in custody with law enforcement?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I was with law enforcement for about one day. I was in the lockup for about six or seven days.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Did you encounter ill-treatment, rough beating, behavior that would offend you, discourtesy, severe insult, or any other similar treatment?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Yes, I did. On the day NarinвҖҷs body was found, they took all of my family and brought us to the station. At law enforcement, they attempted to put pincers to my teeth and pull out my toenails. Until nighttime, they held us against a bare wall and beat us. We experienced things like that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You experienced torture.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Despite this, why did you not file a complaint?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : As a family, we wanted the culprit to be found. We kept quiet, wondering whether the culprit was truly within our family.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : This would not have contributed to the file anywayвҖ” but itвҖҷs finished now. Alright. Had it not been, it would not have contributed to the file.вҖӢ



Law. Mustafa Demir вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir : The slipper issue was asked, but let me ask again. Who later determined that the slipper did not belong to Narin?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Law enforcement asked for the size of the slipper. I think they asked the family what size she wore. That is how I remember it. I cannot be precise, but they said it was not her slipper.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : That it was not her slipper because the size did not match.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Was it understood in a short time? Or did it take long?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I think it was understood the next day.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Your fatherвҖҷs statement about who determined it was different. Now I will ask this. There is a vehicle with plate number 56.вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : 56 XX 763. It appears on the schoolвҖҷs camera records. It arrives at 18:32. Who was driving this vehicle?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I was.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : It was you. Where did you come from at that time?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I plant all of my grandfatherвҖҷs fields. At that time, I was in the cotton field to check on the workers. I came from the cotton field to my grandfatherвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : So you arrived exactly at this time. Then after this timeвҖ” it says 18:56, but I think it says 59вҖ” it was 4 minutes fast. At 18:55, Salim GГјranвҖҷs car departs from here. Were you there at that time?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Correct. I was in front of the house. My uncle Salim came to our house. He sat. He greeted my aunt Fatma. He greeted my grandfather. He greeted me. We sat. We even talked about cotton. We talked about pesticides and pests. We had our normal conversation. He said he was calling his worker. And he went to the corn field.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : So before 18:55, in front of your houseвҖ” that is, your grandfatherвҖҷs houseвҖ” you sat and spoke with Salim. Afterwards, because Ms.В YГјksel did not see Narin at home, I think she calls out in your direction. First, rather, toward your grandfatherвҖҷs house, to Ms.В MaЕҹallah. Did you see any of that?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : On the path above, my aunt YГјksel only called out, asking whether Narin was there. We said, вҖңShe is not here, we have not seen her.вҖқ Then we understood that she was missing.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Then Ms.В YГјksel and Enes go down into the village to search. At those moments, were you still there? Did you see them?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : We only encountered my aunt YГјksel once in the village.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : With Enes?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : We encountered Enes up above.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Did Enes have any bruising around his eye?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : I did not see bruising.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : So you saw him and did not see any bruising, correct?вҖӢ


Osman GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Baran GГјran
text:
Reading Time
~ 33 min


Word Count
6322

title: Testimony of Baran GГјran
section: Presiding Judge вҖӢ
text:
Presiding Judge вҖӢ

Presiding Judge : We are starting the hearing. First, YГјksel is your mother. Enes is your brother, and Salim is your uncle. Because of this, you have the right to refrain from testifying. That is, you have the right not to testify.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I can.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Do you wish to testify?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You wish to testify. We will hear you without oath under the CMK. Yes, Baran. First, in general, what would you like to say about these events? What did you witness? LetвҖҷs hear you.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I donвҖҷt know either. How did things happen like this? How did it get here? How did it unfold like this? I have no idea about that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, where are you? What do you do?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I am a heavy machinery operator.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Where do you work? You work in Erzurum. In Erzurum.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : BaЕҹkale, yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Where were you on the day of the incident?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I was in DiyarbakДұr.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You were in DiyarbakДұr. One moment. When did you learn that our girl Narin was missing?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : It was around 7вҖ“8 p.m., I think. My brother Muhammed called me.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Muhammed called. Did you notify the gendarmerie as soon as you found out?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I went to the village first.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You went to the village first.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I first went by the mosque. There was a large crowd. From there, without stopping, I went up. To the house area. I asked where Narin was, and so on. ThenвҖ”I donвҖҷt remember exactlyвҖ”was it 112, what was it, I called. I called to report a missing person, even was it 105?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I called, then we learned about the canal. I mean the canal came to mind because it had cooled down.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs it. Now, after these events, the family allegedly held secret meetings among themselves. That is, meetings among family members. Your unclesвҖ”cameras were installed in houses. These are the allegations. What do you say about this?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Your Honor, if there had been a secret meeting, would it have been held in this village? In a way everyone could see? Otherwise, in a hidden placeвҖ”DiyarbakДұr is a very big place. And during these events sometimes ministers were coming, visiting the village. When a minister came, they would say empty the house. We would send out all the guests coming from Batman, from DiyarbakДұr. They would say a minister is coming, they will speak, empty the house. Besides that, we didnвҖҷt tell anyone there was a private meeting, nor was there one.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : There was no such thing.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I personally did not witness it. If there had been a very private meeting, our houseвҖ”indeed all the family membersвҖҷ housesвҖ”are not blind spots. If such a highly secret meeting had been held in a way the villagers could see, it wouldnвҖҷt have been held in the village.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : It wouldnвҖҷt have been held in the village. Now, there was also a fight among the women.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I wasnвҖҷt there at that moment because they first took me for identification that day. I wasnвҖҷt there when the fight happened.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You werenвҖҷt there. Alright. Do you have any information about why these women fought, that your uncles tried to silence them?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Your Honor, because there was estrangement and such, everyone was very angry. Everyone was burning inside. At the slightest word, everyone would explode. I myself argued with my family members many times as well. Not about this matter, but because the subject was Narin, at the slightest thing a person would immediately explode. Because of that anger.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So these statements like вҖңYou know everything. DonвҖҷt make us speak. What did we do anyway, as if the men did itвҖқвҖ”are these because of an outburst rather than because they knew something?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I think because of the outburst. I mean I donвҖҷt thinkвҖ”pardon me for interrupting, Your HonorвҖ”if they had known, I believe they would have told. Because there is an innocent girl at the center.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Now, for approximately 15вҖ“20 days you too were searching. If they had known, would you have gained an impression of that? I tried to ask that yesterday. You know how rumors are heard in a village. Your father received thousands of calls from countless people for hoursвҖ”вҖңYour daughter is with meвҖқ and so on. Did you hear anything like this in the village as wellвҖ”this girl was killed, buried there; this girl was abducted; so-and-so abducted her; Bahtiyar killed her; Nevzat killed herвҖ”before she was found? Did you hear such a thing?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : No, Your Honor. There was no such thing, but this did happen: on the day of the incident, some people in the village said a SyrianвҖ”what we call вҖңmДұrtДұpвҖқвҖ”a car came to the village that day. When, after she came from the QurвҖҷan course and went up, we said, was she abducted perhaps? Because no thought at all occurred to us that she had died. Since we had nothing of that sort, we said maybe she was abducted by someone. And this wasnвҖҷt on the very first day. After three or four days had passedвҖ”and, for example, they say there are вҖңsheikhsвҖқ and such. There are people with such beliefs. Out of desperation they even consulted them. And 80% of them were saying this: if she had passed away, I would sense it, and so on. They kept giving hopeвҖ”she is alive; she is being hidden by someone and is alive.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So this red car talk, the Syrians talk, the Gypsies talkвҖ”especially what Salim allegesвҖ”these are things heard in the village; that is, you hear such things in the village and tell the gendarmerie.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Is that so?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes, yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Otherwise you did not personally see anything.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : We did not personally witness it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Since you conveyed every piece of information to the gendarmerie, these also came out.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Indeed after I returned, I also saidвҖ”why donвҖҷt we go and surround their area? Why donвҖҷt we search their cars? I said it repeatedly. Because, I mean, when one says вҖңSyrianвҖқ today, people thinkвҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Not peopleвҖҷs thoughts now. When your sister is missing. On the first day, you had not yet concluded she was dead.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : It is quite natural and your human duty to look under every stone. But later these matters were considered suspicious, so we are asking you about thisвҖ”how did you receive these reports?вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Baran GГјran
section: DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ
text:
DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ

Law. Nahit Eren вҖӢ

Law. Nahit Eren : Good morning. I would like to wish everyone in the courtroom a good morning as well. Your Honor, I mentioned something yesterday before the hearing. I will make a brief introduction.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : We couldnвҖҷt take the floor before the hearing. Because I want to begin by saying these to Baran. Just like to his mother. Now, while we were questioning yesterday, you were outside. You were outside. You were waiting as a witness at the door. While Salim GГјranвҖҷs questioning was being conducted, I asked some things. They may be wrong, they may be incomplete. Of course we also respect the right to defense. Our colleagues are there. He said to me, вҖңWhat are you after?вҖқ He asked such a question. More precisely, he reacted to my question like that. First I would like to explain what we are after. Because Baran and I were together in front of the Forensic Medicine Institute. I will come to that as well. Because I feltвҖ”and thoughtвҖ”that Baran too was after what I was after. That was my feeling. What am I after? Mr.В Arif, please, please, I am also a father. But if I do not say this, if I do not explain this in this courtroom, it seems the dried-up consciences here will not open, will not speak. What am I after? We are people who, with three prosecutors, fourteen staff, and all the prosecutors conducting this investigation, witnessed with three masks a smell we will never forget in our livesвҖ”the state to which a child, who has the most beautiful scent in life, was brought. On the fourth day, while I was at this courthouse, do you know what one of the law enforcement officers there said to me? вҖңThat smell is still on you,вҖқ he said, Your Honor. I will never forget this in my life. That dayвҖ”do you know what we are after? Today we are fighting against the state to which a child who has the most beautiful scent in life was brought; we are fighting for justice. We will fight no matter what it costs. Even if it costs us our lives, we will fight. I say this here today in this courtroom because yesterday there were those who shouted in this courtroom as well. I state once again: I am the father of three children. I am the father of three girls. I know so well what girls are. May God preserve themвҖ”you have five or six boys, you wish for a son. When my wife had her third childbirth, I did not go with her. I didnвҖҷt go, thinking it might be because of me. When she went to the doctorвҖҷs appointmentвҖ”now that this one was also a girlвҖ”I am so happy. You know, we have that feelingвҖ”maybe itвҖҷs because of me; we thought the third would be a boy. We have such a sentiment. Look, we say let us also have a son. Why am I telling this, Baran? We were in front of the Forensic Medicine Institute, our prosecutorsвҖ”three prosecutorsвҖ”are conducting this investigation; they prepared an indictment, in one way or another, with shortcomings, good or bad. They were going to have you make an identification. I did not allow that identification. I said an elder brother should not see this child in this condition. He should not inhale this smell. Let him remain with the scent of Narin as he knew when she was small, the scent he kissed and smelled every day. You were very insistent on being shown. I came to you outside. I said, Baran, do not seeвҖ”itвҖҷs better. But I am sure you too smelled the scent coming out to the outside, Baran. You smelled it, didnвҖҷt you, Baran? You did. That is whyвҖ”what am I after? I am after those who caused this. The lawyers of the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association are after this. Sixteen lawyers, alright.вҖӢ


Complainant Arif GГјran : He is saying things that are not understood.вҖӢ


Due to raised voices from defense attorneys, representatives of the complainant, relatives of the accused, and audience members, the dialogue could not be transcribed.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Once again, I apologize to the family, believing that the emotional reaction we had regarding Narin stemmed from a human response. If that was the case, I apologize. Now, moving to my question. When we were in front of the Forensic Medicine Institute, we spoke with you about the burial of the bodyвҖ”about NarinвҖҷs burial and the need to decide where she would be buried. There was a law enforcement officer present. While sitting there, I asked you: Baran, in general, there is a widespread objection to bringing Narin back to the village for burial. Could she be buried somewhere else? What would you say to such a request? At that point, you were the only family member who was not in custody. I asked what your answer was, Baran. IвҖҷd like to hear that answer once again.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : From the beginning, I was going to take Narin to be buried in her own village. That was a fact. Of course, there was public reaction there. I didnвҖҷt care about that. Whatever happened, we were going to take her to her own village. But there, the prosecutor said to me, вҖңWhere?вҖқ He asked me that once or twice. вҖңWhere, where,вҖқ he said. And at that moment, I said, itвҖҷs already decided, but if you like, speak with my father. And they sent me to my father.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm asking you to recall the exact answer you gave me to my question.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Where exactly? DidnвҖҷt we discuss this topic with you? You donвҖҷt remember?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : No.В We had already said we would take Narin to the village.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷd like to remind you of something you said to me.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Maybe youвҖҷll remember. I said, you have uncles outside, elder family members. If you want, consult them. The respected elders of the village are here. I use the term вҖңvillageвҖқ to mean family. After that, you gave me an answer.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : What did I say?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Shall I remind you if youвҖҷd like?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes, remind me, but I think my answer was about my father and me on this matterвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You said, вҖңNo family member other than my father and me will have the right to speak.вҖқвҖӢ


Baran GГјran : ThatвҖҷs true. ItвҖҷs the way it is, brother.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷd like to know what you meant by that.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : IвҖҷll tell you, brother. YouвҖҷre taking this completely out of context.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : No, no. Not at all.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Brother, let me finish. YouвҖҷre misunderstanding it. There, I was left completely alone. Even though my family was right outside the door, none of them were allowed inside to support me. You left a brother waiting in front of his sister for maybe more than seven hours and let no one in. I waited alone in front of the Forensic Medicine Institute. I asked for ten family members to be allowed in. You didnвҖҷt allow them even though they were at the door. At that point, as a wounded brotherвҖ”and my mother, as far as I knew, had already been taken in that morning. I knew she wouldnвҖҷt be coming there. So I said, my father and I will decide on this matter. It doesnвҖҷt concern the other family members.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So in that sense, you meant that as the heads of the family, only you two could decide?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : We didnвҖҷt say we are the family elders. We already have elders in the family. What I meant was only this: if you had brought even one of my uncles to stand with me after you left me there for six or seven hours aloneвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You said that earlier. When I said go outside to consult, you said that.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : You didnвҖҷt let me, brother.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : I said we could arrange that. I even said IвҖҷd speak to the prosecutorsвҖ”if you wanted to meet with family outside, I said okay. Now IвҖҷll ask one more thing, Baran, for the sake of revealing the material truth. You came to meet with us at the Bar Association twice. In the first meeting, you said you wanted the whole truth to come out. Whoever did this should be punished. You said even if itвҖҷs Nevzat or someone from our family in the village, they should be punished. And, as it wasnвҖҷt just the two of usвҖ”the board members were there tooвҖ”I asked you this question, remembering those moments. The power of attorney you give us means that, at the hearing, weвҖҷll sit side by side as complainants, but if this turns into an indictment, since your mother and brother are currently detained, theyвҖҷll be across from us. WeвҖҷll be questioning them. I asked, do you accept that, Baran? Can you repeat your answer here in the courtroom? I apologize for asking again.вҖӢ


It was observed that the defense counsel objected to the question without speaking into the microphone.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I accept your objection to this question. IвҖҷm skipping it, on the grounds that it pertains to a matter between two power-of-attorney relationships.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : There are video recordings included in the case file. We watched them. There are also recordings that were made public. There are conversations between you and your brother in prison. We listened to all of them. In those conversations, you encouraged your brother, told him to stand firm, to stay strong. I respect that. But one thing caught my attention, and I want to ask about it. Your brother keeps asking you about the camera. You say, вҖңYes, it was found, itвҖҷs out.вҖқ He says, вҖңThere are two people,вҖқ and you say, вҖңYes.вҖқ But I didnвҖҷt see any such camera footage in the file. Did you actually see this footage somewhere? Because we were working together to uncover the truth. If such footage exists, where did you see it? And how did you come into contact with this evidence that you could bring it before the court?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : First of all, there were tons of false reports circulating on social media. Later, an explanation was made. I saw it on social media. They said there were two dark figures seen on the Kartal Eye camera at the military base area. We thought maybe there were two people. Second, my brother is eighteen years old. HeвҖҷs never been inside a police station in his life. Same for my mother. TheyвҖҷre not in a position to defend themselves. If you had listened to all of my brotherвҖҷs conversations, you would understand what I meant. Most likely not all of them were included.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : I listened to all of them.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Oh. Then youвҖҷd knowвҖ”I repeatedly said, Enes meant that once the footage came out, everything would be revealed. вҖңWeвҖҷre happy,вҖқ he said. вҖңHopefully,вҖқвҖ”may I finish, brother?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm not asking about your comment. IвҖҷm asking whether you had access to such evidence. Did you?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : No, they were false reports on social media.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You saw it on social media.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes, and there were also those other videos of me and my father that spread widely. We donвҖҷt even know who shared them. After all, we were talking on the phone. Besides, heвҖҷs an eighteen-year-old boy and in prison.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : ThatвҖҷs not what IвҖҷm asking.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : We were just trying to comfort him.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : YouвҖҷre right about that. IвҖҷm not criticizing that. I said that already.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : He was told to stand tall, and the reason was this: вҖңStand tall. God is great.вҖқ Because a slander had been thrown upon him. Sooner or later the truth will come out. Just because heвҖҷs imprisoned, should we abandon our brother?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : ThatвҖҷs not what I asked.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, alright. He gave more than enough of an answer. ThatвҖҷs fine.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Your Honor, that was all I wanted to ask. Thank you. Once again, if I have hurt anyone emotionally or spiritually, I apologize again. Truly, I apologize.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Everyone calm down now. From this point on, we go straight to questions. Otherwise, IвҖҷll do what I mentioned before. ItвҖҷs something I hate to do, but donвҖҷt force me to do it. LetвҖҷs continue properly. And let me remind you again, Baran вҖ” for self-incriminating questions, I will intervene. You may also say, вҖңI do not wish to answer.вҖқ Saying that does not mean you are lying or anything else. Since the entire family has suspect status, I will interveneвҖ”but there might be moments I miss something. You have the right not to answer any question, especially self-incriminating ones. That is a clear legal right.вҖӢ



Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir вҖӢ

Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Mr.В Baran, as far as you remember, on the day of the incident when did you first realize your sister was missing? When did you start searching? Do you remember?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I learned what time she went missing a day or two later. BecauseвҖ”according to the school camera in the village, she was last seen at 3:15. After that, no one saw her. So I only learned this later by relying on the camera footage. It was around the third day of the incident.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Around what time did you start searching? As far as you remember?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : The first day?вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : The first day, yes.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : As soon as I arrived in the village.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : About what time? Can you give an hour, or not?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Toward the evening, I donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : You donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : If I said now, it would be a lie.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Well, at 20:43 on the day of the incident you made a report. You called and said, вҖңWeвҖҷve been searching for five hours and canвҖҷt find her.вҖқ By the time you made the report, had about five hours passedвҖ”as far as you recall?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : By the time I learned of it, that much time hadnвҖҷt passed. The reason I said that is that in such cases they usually arrive late. I said it on purposeвҖ”said sheвҖҷd been missing for a long time, for many hoursвҖ”so the necessary personnel would come sooner. Otherwise, if IвҖҷm not mistaken, my uncle KurtuluЕҹ was having dinner when my brother called me. From the time I made that call, either an hour had passed or it hadnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : About an hour had passed.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Either it had or it hadnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : But your statement clearly says, вҖңWeвҖҷve been searching for five hours and canвҖҷt find her.вҖқвҖӢ


Baran GГјran : The only reason I said that was just to get them there as soon as possibleвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, alright.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : AgainвҖ”have him explain once more.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He explained; I heard it.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Have him explain again.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : No, no, he already explained it a moment ago.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : If I had said sheвҖҷd been missing for an hour, they might have said, вҖңMaybe sheвҖҷs around here somewhere.вҖқ With that in mind, I said sheвҖҷd been missing a long time. That was the purpose.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, you already said thatвҖ”twice. Counsel may not have heard.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Baran GГјran
section: Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ
text:
Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ

Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz вҖӢ

Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Baran, what do you think happened to your sister? I was going to connect it to something, but let me ask directly first. What do you think happened? I mean, life is going on as usualвҖ”sheвҖҷs going to QurвҖҷan course, coming back. What do you think could have happened?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : My own view is only this: I think my sister was abducted by this despicable person before she went up the hillвҖ”taken away.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Why do you think that, for example? There were many searches.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : He says he was at home at that time, right?вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Okay, but your uncle also says he was at home at that time. And your mother says she was at home at that time.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You have the right not to answer this question. It is incriminating.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : I wonвҖҷt prolong it, as I said, but during the search effortsвҖ”your sister was searched for 19 daysвҖ”strange things happened. There were fires, the power went out, women fought each other, there were inconsistencies. There are gendarmerie records stating they were misdirected during the search. What do you think about this? For example, the gendarmerie says the villagers, led by your uncle, misdirected us. This was recorded and is in the file. Your Honor also asked Salim GГјran about it. What do you think? Did you witness anything like that? After 19 days she was found, and the place she was foundвҖ”a streamвҖ”had been searched many times before. There are inconsistencies in the file. For example, Melike changes her statement; Birsen changes her statement; MaЕҹallah changes his statement; Hediye GГјranвҖ”Hediye changes her statement.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Counsel wants to ask this: were these inconsistent statements made as part of a plan, or were they spontaneous statements?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Your Honor, as I said, my family are not people who have spent their lives giving statements before the police, prosecutors, or judges. ItвҖҷs quite normal for them to mix things up. They werenвҖҷt raised in an urban environment. They all grew up in the village. Why does no one take that into account?вҖӢ



Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun вҖӢ

Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : There is a prison visit with Enes. He tells you, вҖңNarin used my toothbrush.вҖқ He asks if anything would come out. What was he afraid would be found?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : From this we see that the boy doesnвҖҷt really understand. DNA might truly be on EnesвҖҷs toothbrush. Why? When you say DNAвҖ”since IвҖҷm not very knowledgeable about thisвҖ”I would also think of that. Did she last use my toothbrush in her mouth, orвҖ”for example, I even asked, I last kissed Narin. Is that what they mean as DNA? ThatвҖҷs the idea.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs the idea.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : He keeps asking whether the report has arrived.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Because he was curious. He wanted it to come as soon as possible. You probably didnвҖҷt fully read that conversation. You didnвҖҷt listen.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : Did Enes have a tendency toward violence or substance use? Have you ever witnessed anger control problems?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : In no way. I can say this plainly. You can tell the entire village: Enes is the one who never fights with anyone in the village.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : He was biting his own arm. He punched himself in the face.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : He lost his sister. IsnвҖҷt that normal?вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : But you didnвҖҷt do that.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I banged my head against the wall. Did you see me? I smashed my phone against the wallвҖ”did you see that? They generally donвҖҷt take reports for those kinds of things.вҖӢ



Law. Emine AkГ§alДұ вҖӢ

Law. Emine AkГ§alДұ : We heard your mother yesterday. She didnвҖҷt give very enlightening information. My question is this: do you think your mother is protecting your uncle, or is she protecting Enes?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You also have the right not to answer this question. It implies harboring a criminal and tampering with evidence.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : My mother doesnвҖҷt protect anyone.вҖӢ


Law. Emine AkГ§alДұ : Did you decide to do this on your own?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : On my own judgment.вҖӢ


Law. Emine AkГ§alДұ : Yes, youвҖҷre 20 anyway.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : 21.вҖӢ


Law. Emine AkГ§alДұ : Had you ever asked beforeвҖ”why they didnвҖҷt report it, or whether you knew they hadnвҖҷt reported it?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I donвҖҷt know about the incident itselfвҖ”whether they searched or notвҖ”but in the panic of such a situation, itвҖҷs normal for a person not to think of that. You get flustered and just start searching.вҖӢ


Law. Emine AkГ§alДұ : Yes. We asked family members yesterday about Nevzat. What do you think? He didnвҖҷt leave a very good impression on us, in the eyes of the family. Because Nevzat and his wife said they were friends with your uncle. But your family members and your uncle said there was absolutely no such thing. I wanted to ask about Nevzat.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : A long time.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Watch your words. DonвҖҷt use insulting or offensive language, alright?вҖӢ


Law. Emine AkГ§alДұ : Were they only friends? Eating and drinking togetherвҖҰвҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I knew they hadnвҖҷt spoken for a long time. Before that, it was normalвҖ”they were neighbors after all, itвҖҷs a village. You see each other at weddings and such. Just normal village relations. As for NevzatвҖҷs family membersвҖ”you said so. As for why Nevzat mostly says, вҖңI donвҖҷt know, I donвҖҷt remember,вҖқ why do people react so stronglyвҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : WeвҖҷll evaluate that, Baran. ThatвҖҷs our job. ItвҖҷs my job. Alright. ThatвҖҷs for the court panel to assess. Alright.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Baran, is Narin the same age as NevzatвҖҷs grandchild? Do you know anything about that?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : His granddaughterвҖ”FadileвҖҷs daughterвҖ”theyвҖҷre about the same age, I think.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Baran GГјran
section: Defense Counsels for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsels for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Right before the incident, did you hear that Nevzat gave Narin some allowance money?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I heard it a few times in the village. Even villagers were swearing about it. I donвҖҷt remember exactly who, but they said despite having four or five kids, he only gave money to Narin.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Only to Narin.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Only to Narin.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You heard this from villagers. Yes. Like when you said you heard about the GypsiesвҖ”from villagers, right?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : But I think it was my uncle BarДұЕҹвҖҷs daughter who said it, as far as I remember.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Your uncle BarДұЕҹвҖҷs daughter said that?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : SheвҖҷs young too, one of NarinвҖҷs close friends. Alright, I donвҖҷt remember exactly though.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : IвҖҷm just asking out of curiosity, by name.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : So, among that group of kids, he gave money only to Narin, right? Yes. Thank you. Now, was there any tension or argument between your father and Nevzat over a vehicle transaction? Did you witness anything?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes, I did. I was there.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : What happened?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : There was some damage. My father explained it calmly to Nevzat at first, saying thereвҖҷs this issue. He supposedly said he was afraid of usвҖ”people say that. Then in my uncleвҖҷs house, he raised his voice and said, вҖңI wonвҖҷt pay, I donвҖҷt accept it.вҖқ Then my father said, вҖңYouвҖҷre saying that?вҖқ He called two older men from the villageвҖ”said, вҖңCome, youвҖҷre elders, you decide.вҖқ Nevzat began stammering there, saying, вҖңI donвҖҷt know.вҖқ Then my father got angry. He said, вҖңI wonвҖҷt hit you, but you will pay this money.вҖқ They argued there. I was going to step in, but my father stopped me.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Did you say anything to him?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : No, I didnвҖҷt. But I was about to react, because he was speaking disrespectfully to my father. When my father reacted, I stayed silent and left.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Do you think Nevzat could have done this to Narin because of that argument?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : He could have. Because, let me put it this way: he is so cold-blooded, so calmвҖ”as if heвҖҷs committed fifty murdersвҖ”acting as though nothing happened.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Alright, thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you. Alright, next please.вҖӢ



Law. Tuncay ErkuЕҹ вҖӢ

Law. Tuncay ErkuЕҹ : Mr.В Baran, the day after little Narin disappeared, your auntвҖҷs son Muhammed Kaya found a slipper. A photo of this slipper was sent to you. He asked you to show it to your family. Can you tell us what you know about this slipper incident?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : We were at home, sitting on the balcony. Around that time, it was either Muhammed or OsmanвҖ”IвҖҷm pretty sure it was MuhammedвҖ”he sent me a picture. He said, вҖңLook at this slipper, we found it on the road. Show it to your mother to see if itвҖҷs hers or not. Could it belong to Narin or not?вҖқ I showed it to my mother. My mother said, вҖңThatвҖҷs NarinвҖҷs slipper.вҖқ We immediately jumped up. We went straight to where the Gypsies were. The military personnel had surrounded the whole area. We started searching there.вҖӢ


Law. Tuncay ErkuЕҹ : Yes. Also, we want to ask this: you said you hurt yourself when you learned about the incident, and your brother Enes hurt himself by biting his arm and hitting his face. Did any of your other siblings hurt themselves too? I mean, did any of them throw things, or break down emotionallyвҖ”so upset they didnвҖҷt know what they were doing? Anyone?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Osman broke his phone. I broke glasses several times. Honestly, I even hit my head against the wall more than once.вҖӢ


Law. Tuncay ErkuЕҹ : Of course, because of the shock of the event.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Because of the shock. I broke my own phone too. Just so my mother wouldnвҖҷt be upset. Even before this man was handed over, I would get up at night, go to the roof, and cry aloneвҖ”so no one would hear, so my mother and father wouldnвҖҷt be upset. TheyвҖҷre very attached to us. ThatвҖҷs why I hurt myself during those times.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Baran, you said Nevzat gave money. You said Nevzat is very cold-blooded, could commit a murder, and go on with life as if nothing happened. Why didnвҖҷt you suspect him when he gave that money then?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Because, Your Honor, itвҖҷs a village environment. For example, even now, if someone in the village gave some money to my younger sibling, I wouldnвҖҷt be surprised.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Right. You wouldnвҖҷt be surprised. Quite normal.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Everyone is like an uncle in the village. Our village has a large population.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : And I wasnвҖҷt surprised either. ThatвҖҷs why I didnвҖҷt focus on it muchвҖ”until you said heвҖҷs very cold-blooded, could commit murder, and act like nothing happened. ThatвҖҷs why IвҖҷm asking you now. Normally, I wouldnвҖҷt find the money thing strange. Sometimes we say, вҖңYou survived trouble today.вҖқ In our religious belief, giving charity averts misfortune. Maybe he thought, вҖңLet me make a few children happy today so misfortune will pass.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs from our belief. But since you said heвҖҷs that cold-blooded, thatвҖҷs why IвҖҷm asking you this now. Notice, I didnвҖҷt ask such a question at first.вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Yes, thatвҖҷs right, Your Honor. He himself said in his statement that it was the first time he committed a murder. I mean, how can a person not panic? How can a person not be afraid? How could he come every night, sit with my father, drink tea with us?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But Baran, he doesnвҖҷt say, вҖңI committed a murder.вҖқ He says, вҖңI destroyed the body.вҖқвҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I think heвҖҷs lying, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright. Of course, letвҖҷs proceed. You may introduce yourself and ask your question.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Baran GГјran
section: Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Recep KДұzДұlok вҖӢ

Law. Recep KДұzДұlok : First, my condolences. When did you see Enes on the day of the incident?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : I came in the evening that day. I donвҖҷt remember exactly what time because everything was hecticвҖ”he came to the hospital. That day, there was a report of a child resembling Narin at the public hospitalвҖ”an unattended child. I clearly remember that part, but not the rest.вҖӢ


Law. Recep KДұzДұlok : Did Enes seem anxious or panicked? Did you sense anything unusual?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : We were crying together.вҖӢ


Law. Recep KДұzДұlok : You were crying together. Did you notice any bruises on his face or bite marks on his arm?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Recep KДұzДұlok : You didnвҖҷt notice anything, right? Okay, thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Your Honor, since we are last to ask questions, for the sake of fairness, could the defense begin first next time?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright. Agreed. YouвҖҷll go first next.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : IвҖҷll ask a question related to the previous one. We learned that Enes went to the hospital that day. The court requested the CCTV footage from Memorial Hospital. The hospital sent only one camera recording. And we had some bad luck againвҖ”like everything else. As we explained yesterday, there are several cameras viewing that area. But no time synchronization was done between those cameras.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, direct your question.вҖӢ



Law. Mustafa Demir вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir : Yes, the question. In this footage, is the person shown his father?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Go ahead. You may approach the witness. Yes, come closerвҖ”you can ask near the witness. We placed the microphone there; donвҖҷt misunderstand. Counsel, please move here. Yes, here.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : He already saidвҖ”it was his father.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I didnвҖҷt hear. Ah, let me show you too. May I have the photo? Yes. He has the CCTV footage from Memorial Hospital. The family members havenвҖҷt seen it. Alright. The defense counsel asked whether the person in the ambulance footage from Memorial, wearing a white t-shirt, was his father. Baran said it was his father. Yes. In the courtвҖҷs viewing, the person in question was observed to resemble Arif GГјran. Proceed.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Did Enes have any bruises around his eyes that day?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : No, I didnвҖҷt see any.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Baran GГјran
section: Defense Counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : ThereвҖҷs a rumor that Ali RДұza GГјran made a settlement offer to the Bahtiyar family on your behalf. Did you or your family, or your father, ever ask Ali RДұza GГјran to do such a thing? Did you talk to him about it? Or is he someone who could do such a thing?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : My sister would never do something like that.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Alright. After the incident, what was Salim GГјranвҖҷs mental state like? Was he sad, emotional? Did you ever suspect him? Did he take an active part in the search efforts?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Salim GГјran is my uncle. During that period, he was with the gendarmerie 24/7. HeвҖҷd often come back at four in the morning. He was out at dawn, and weвҖҷd search together until evening. The gendarmerie never left his side.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Had Salim GГјran ever hit Enes before? I mean before the incident. Being his uncle, maybe if Enes misbehavedвҖ”had he ever hit him?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : If he misbehaved, an uncleвҖҷs scolding is normal. HeвҖҷs an uncle after all. But I personally never saw it.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : So does Salim GГјran have a habit of hitting his nephews? Does he hit them?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : God is my witness, no.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Now, letвҖҷs imagine this: there are three men in a room, and itвҖҷs said that your uncle was with your mother. Because of that relationship, your sister Narin is killed. You know EnesвҖ”how would he react to his uncle in that situation?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : He would have shot him in the head. If heвҖҷd had a gun, heвҖҷd have shot him in the head.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Besides that, would Enes ever protect his uncleвҖ”hide himвҖ”even though his sister was killed?вҖӢ


Baran GГјran : Never. If Enes had seen Salim GГјran and Nevzat there, even without a gun, he would have stabbed them.вҖӢ

title: Salim GГјran Mahkeme Д°fadesinin KapsamlДұ Г–zeti
text: Salim GГјran, NarinвҖҷin kaybolmasДұyla ilgili yГјrГјtГјlen soruЕҹturmada hakkДұnda Г§Дұkan iddialar ve suГ§lamalar Гјzerine mahkeme huzurunda ifade vermiЕҹtir. Д°fadesi boyunca, yГ¶neltilen suГ§lamalarДұ ve Г§eliЕҹkileri reddederek kendi masumiyetini savunmuЕҹtur.

title: Salim GГјran Mahkeme Д°fadesinin KapsamlДұ Г–zeti
section: Salim GГјranвҖҷДұn Genel Д°fadesi ve Olay GГјnГј Rutini вҖӢ
text:
Salim GГјranвҖҷДұn Genel Д°fadesi ve Olay GГјnГј Rutini вҖӢ
Salim GГјran, olay gГјnГј olan 21 AДҹustosвҖҷtaki rutinini anlatarak ifadeye baЕҹlamДұЕҹtДұr. Sabah erken kalktДұДҹДұnДұ, tarlaya sulamacДұlarДұyla birlikte gittiДҹini ve Г§alДұЕҹtДұДҹДұnДұ belirtmiЕҹtir. Kendini bildi bileli Г§iftГ§i olduДҹunu ve oturduДҹunu deДҹil, Г§alДұЕҹtДұДҹДұnДұ vurgulamДұЕҹtДұr. Saat 10-11 gibi eve geldiДҹini, Г§ocuklarДұyla birlikte DiyarbakДұr BaДҹlar SaДҹlДұk OcaДҹДұвҖҷna dГјДҹГјn iГ§in elbise almaya gittiklerini sГ¶ylemiЕҹtir. DГ¶nГјЕҹte OpetвҖҷten TRY 200 yakДұt aldДұДҹДұnДұ ve kГ¶ye saat 02:45-02:50 civarДұnda vardДұklarДұnДұ belirtmiЕҹtir. Evde yemek yedikten ve yaklaЕҹДұk 1 saat 20 dakika dinlendikten sonra tekrar sondaja gittiДҹini, burada iki elektrikГ§iyle karЕҹДұlaЕҹtДұДҹДұnДұ ve amcasДұnДұn oДҹlunun trafosundaki arДұza nedeniyle onlara yardДұm ettiДҹini ifade etmiЕҹtir. Bu sДұrada pamuk tarlasДұnda dolaЕҹarak pamuklarДұn kurtlandДұДҹДұnДұ fark ettiДҹini ve amcasДұnДұn oДҹlunu arayarak ilaГ§lamasДұ gerektiДҹini sГ¶ylediДҹini anlatmДұЕҹtДұr. ElektrikГ§ilerin arДұzayДұ 3500 TLвҖҷye yapmayДұ kabul ettiДҹini, Miran oДҹlunu da alДұp eve saat 18:00 civarДұnda dГ¶ndГјДҹГјnГј belirtmiЕҹtir.



TableВ 1: Salim GГјranвҖҷДұn mahkeme ifadesine dayalДұ olay gГјnГј rutini.
