title: The Collapse of Justice, the Dispersion of Truth, and the Narin Case
text:
AUTHOR


Melek Borak


LAST UPDATED


April 16, 2026


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 7 min


Word Count


1202







Almost everything that could be said about Narin under this sky has been said. But the matter is knotted precisely here: the sheer abundance of what has been said did not serve to bring the truth to light. On the contrary, it turned into noise that covered, fragmented, and neutralised the truth. For this reason, everyone who feels a sense of responsibility must take the matter up again. Because truth gains meaning not merely by being voiced, but by being defended with the right method and on solid ground.
The Narin case, from the very beginning, proceeded as a process carrying the risk of breaking away from the normative framework of law. Over time, a picture emerged in which legal evaluations were, at places, overshadowed by public pressure and emotional reactions. This shows that the matter is something more than an ordinary judicial case. What is at stake here is not merely a discussion of crime or perpetrator, but the question of how truth is constructed, directed, and perceived.
This distortion advanced through two fundamental dynamics. The first was the imperceptible narrowing of the boundaries of truth. As debates intensified, the public kept turning in circles within a limited frame presented to them, even as they believed they were searching for the truth. The second emerged more indirectly: as the quality of discourses defending the truth weakened, the thing being defended was itself discredited. Emotional, inconsistent, or methodologically deficient arguments, over time, produced an effect that reduced rather than increased the visibility of the truth.
At the intersection of these two dynamics, loud yet low-content debates emerged. No common rational ground could be produced; the discussions repeated themselves instead of progressing.
At this point, one must not overlook the societal and political dimension of the matter. The Narin case is not merely an individual tragedy; it has also become an example showing how societal reflexes operate. On a ground where violence against women and children is already visible, such cases are quickly discussed by being loaded with symbolic meanings. Rather than the event itself, the narratives built around it come to the fore.
This leads the event to turn into a kind of representation. But what is at issue here is not a simple relation of representation. The process has a transformation mechanism operating through specific stages. This mechanism can be thought of as a chain proceeding in the form of reality, representation, reproduction, and displacement.
In the first stage, reality is the raw, scattered body of facts that has not yet been interpreted. But reality does not enter social circulation directly in this form.
In the second stage, representation comes into play. Through the media, social networks, and various actors, the event is placed within certain narrative patterns; some elements are foregrounded while others are left in the background. Thus reality becomes visible not as it is, but within a specific frame.
In the third stage, reproduction, this representation is continuously repeated through different channels. Each repetition simplifies, sharpens, and makes the narrative more suitable for circulation. At this point, people begin to recognise not the event itself, but its circulating versions.
The final stage is displacement. What is now discussed is no longer the event itself, but its reproduced representations. Reality withdraws into a hard-to-reach background, while representation takes the place of truth. At this point the simulation is complete. What is at hand is no longer a simple reflection of reality, but a narrative that has taken its place.
Understanding the motivation behind this simulation requires a separate layer. For such events do not grow of their own accord; they acquire meaning through the existing political and social fault lines. On a ground where the murder of children and women already carries political content, such cases quickly become part of political narratives. From the oppositionвҖҷs perspective, this event is read as a societal example that can make the weaknesses of the government visible. The entrenched perception that the government will protect its own circle, that the judiciary will work in a biased manner, and that вҖңloyalists will be favouredвҖқ enters into play before the facts have even become clear.

People cling more rapidly to stories that confirm frameworks they already believe in. The collective memory of justice having been harmed is reactivated in such events, and individuals turn, rather than to investigating the truth, to narratives that reinforce their existing beliefs. Thus truth withdraws; its place is taken by a fiction that is easier to believe.

What is striking here is the manner in which society turns toward this narrative. This is not merely a political choice but also a psychological inclination.
For this reason, the expression вҖңsimulationвҖқ does not denote the complete disappearance of reality, but rather the fact that access to reality has been made increasingly difficult by the layers that represent it. In modern societies, knowledge is constructed not through direct experience but through mediators, which makes such transformations inevitable.
When one looks at the investigation process, the fundamental problem is the instability of method. For judicial processes to proceed in a healthy manner, technical data, expert knowledge, and analytical evaluation must be determinative. Yet in such cases, public pressure and the intense emotional atmosphere can influence the direction of the process. This both increases the risk of faulty evaluation and damages trust in justice.
At this point the issue cannot be explained merely by individual errors. Within a broader frame, questions of institutional capacity, merit, and method must also be discussed. For the mechanism of justice produces trust only through consistent and professional functioning.
It is known that similar situations have occurred throughout history. In periods when social tension rises, there are examples in which rumours have taken precedence over evidence and crowds have replaced the judiciary. In such situations, a common mechanism operates: perception overtakes reality; emotion suppresses reason; hasty judgements replace sound evaluations.
This risk is clearly visible in the debates conducted around the Narin case. For certain sections of society, the matter has become less about understanding the truth than about taking a position. Yet a sound evaluation requires an approach purified of prejudices, based on evidence, and composed. Law speaks not through opinions but through concrete data.
In conclusion, the Narin case is not merely a criminal file; it is also an indicator of societyвҖҷs relationship with truth. When representations take the place of truth, justice is not only delayed вҖ” it begins to lose its meaning.
In place of a last word: a human being is a human being through human rights. A dignified life and a humane end are possible only through the survival of justice. When justice weakens, not only law but also social trust is shaken. For this reason, defending the truth is not merely a matter of one case вҖ” it is the preservation of a shared future.
The fundamental principle to be remembered here is clear: law concerns itself with facts. When one moves away from facts, surrender to perceptions becomes inevitable. And the only way to overcome perceptions is, without tiring and without losing heart, to put forth the facts. For beyond the facts themselves, the real matter is the truth; and the road to the truth once again passes through the facts themselves.



 Back to top

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
text:
AUTHOR


Hilal Seven


LAST UPDATED


May 4, 2026


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 19 min


Word Count


3024
Hilal Seven / London
The file concerning eight-year-old Narin GГјran вҖ” who disappeared on 21 August 2024 in the village of TavЕҹantepe, DiyarbakДұr, and whose lifeless body was found 19 days later in the bed of the EДҹertutmaz stream near her home вҖ” is far from being a closed investigation. Due to evidentiary disputes and contradictions in technical analyses, the file has become one of the rare judicial files whose reopening is now under serious discussion.
In recent months, the file has come up not only inside courtrooms but also within relevant state institutions, under the heading of renewed technical evaluation. While certain inquiries underway within the Ministry of Justice are debating whether the existing file contains sufficient technical clarity to support its rulings, the possibility of a вҖңkeЕҹifвҖқ вҖ” a re-examination on site вҖ” has also been brought to the table.
Although the convictions handed down by the DiyarbakДұr 8th Heavy Penal Court were upheld by the Court of Cassation in December 2025, exhausting the ordinary judicial route, the file now stands at the critical threshold of the Constitutional Court (AYM) application process.
This news report, in which all of these matters will be addressed, is not a text based merely on official statements.
It was prepared on the basis of meetings held in Ankara with Ministry of Justice officials; face-to-face and written interviews with experts working in digital forensics, forensic medicine, and law in Turkey and across Europe (the United Kingdom, Germany, and Belgium); and official correspondence sent to international regulatory bodies.
The work involved more than 100 interviews, drawing on the assessments of figures from a wide range of disciplines вҖ” from digital forensic experts to forensic medicine professionals, from lawyers to human rights advocates, from academics to journalists. A multi-layered analytical process was carried out to understand the technical, legal, and social dimensions of the file.
The final phase of the fieldwork was conducted in the village of TavЕҹantepe, where on-site observations and inspections allowed the technical data to be assessed alongside the reality on the ground.
This roughly five-month investigation focuses less on the existing claims in the file and more on the processes by which evidence was produced, the limits of the technical methods used, and the differences in interpretation that have surfaced during the trial.
In this first part of the file, we discuss the scientific limits of HTS data and biological findings; yet there is a вҖңzero pointвҖқ at which all of these technical debates begin: 21 August 2024, 15:11 вҖ” the moment Narin was last seen on camera. The 19 days that elapsed between the moment ordinary life was suspended in TavЕҹantepe and the morning of 8 September, when her lifeless body was discovered, were not merely a search operation. They were also the period in which contradictory testimonies, false tip-offs, and efforts that increasingly hampered the search obscured the central question that remains unresolved to this day: how did Narin disappear after 15:11?

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: A social wave growing in the shadow of the investigation
text:
A social wave growing in the shadow of the investigation
The public reaction that followed Narin GГјranвҖҷs disappearance quickly turned into an intense flow of information on social media. Over time, however, this flow drifted away from the production of verified information and evolved into a structure dominated by premature judgments.
The journalists I spoke with describe the period as marked by serious pressure for speed both on social media and in mainstream media, which weakened information-verification processes. The commentary that emerged while the investigation was still under judicial confidentiality, together with content blaming family members, shows that the file was dragged early on into a вҖңsocial trial.вҖқ

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: The scientific limits of cellular data: the expert report and technical contradictions
text:
The scientific limits of cellular data: the expert report and technical contradictions
The вҖңNarrow-Area BaseвҖқ report, prepared on the instruction of the DiyarbakДұr Public ProsecutorвҖҷs Office and signed by a three-member panel of experts, plays a decisive role in the conviction ruling. Composed of technical personnel from public security units, the panel analyzed HTS (Communication Traffic System) data on a microscopic scale, claiming to identify the suspectsвҖҷ locations at the moment of the incident with metre-level precision and offering location-determination data for specific rooms inside the house, such as the вҖңkitchenвҖқ or an вҖңempty room.вҖқ
This report, however, brought to the surface a fundamental technical divergence within the digital forensic literature. International digital forensic literature and technical regulatory bodies emphasise the inherent physical limits of cellular data. According to those standards, base-station records (CDR) are not data points that pinpoint a deviceвҖҷs coordinates; they are probability data showing only which coverage area a device was being served by. Therefore, location determination is defined not as the result of a technical вҖңmeasurementвҖқ but as an вҖңinferenceвҖқ made by an expert that must always be presented together with margins of error. This scientific threshold is a reminder that HTS analyses should be evaluated not as absolute proof but as expert opinions with methodological limitations. Field measurements taken on the same device with different subscriptions (SIM cards) showing variability prove that signal data cannot be considered independently of environmental factors, base-station load, and geographical obstacles. As a result, whether HTS records constitute absolute proof of location or only a limited probabilistic inference forms the most fundamental scientific and legal axis of debate in the case.

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: Can base-station data be definitive evidence? HTS, narrowed base, and forensic science standards
text:
Can base-station data be definitive evidence? HTS, narrowed base, and forensic science standards
Digital forensic expert Tuncay BeЕҹikГ§i, in our face-to-face interview, stated that HTS data, by its nature, does not provide a location determination but only limited information about which base-station coverage area a device may have been within. According to BeЕҹikГ§i, this data consists of broad-area coverage information and does not technically allow precise room-level location determination.



Tuncay BeЕҹikГ§i


BeЕҹikГ§i noted that elements such as signal strength, base-station coverage, and environmental variables directly affect how the data is interpreted, and emphasised that the absence of signal strength (dBm) information from operator records вҖ” common in such datasets вҖ” makes the precision of retrospective analyses even more disputable.
He also said that the approach known as вҖңnarrowed baseвҖқ (daraltДұlmДұЕҹ baz) is not a technical standard, and that it can instead be regarded as an analytic method based on the after-the-fact interpretation of past HTS data.
This technical debate is not limited to differences in methodology; it also extends to whether the approach used aligns with international forensic science standards. In this context, official correspondence with the UK-based Forensic Science Regulator (FSR) clarified the regulatory framework concerning cell-site analysis.



Tuncay BeЕҹikГ§i and Hilal Seven


The October 2023 Code of Practice issued by the FSR defines the limits of cell-site analysis explicitly. According to the text:

вҖңPinpointing the phone to a specific location is almost always impossible.вҖқ

In the same regulation, the determination of location is defined as вҖңinferenceвҖқ вҖ” that is, an expert interpretation вҖ” and it is stated that this interpretation must always be presented together with the limits of the method, margins of error, and uncertainties. The error rates and limitations of the method used must also be explicitly disclosed to the opposing party.
Article 99.11.5 of the code calls particular attention to technical limits:

вҖңLarge rural macro cells may provide service over 10вҖ“20 km, thus offering much lower precision.вҖқ

This framework strengthens the argument that the вҖңnarrowed baseвҖқ method used in the Turkish file does not have a one-to-one standardised counterpart in the international literature.
At this point the ISO 17025 accreditation standard becomes relevant. This standard requires that measurement and analysis methods used in forensic laboratories be verifiable, that measurement uncertainty be reported clearly, and that results be reproducible.
In assessments made within this framework, it is stated that if вҖңnarrowed baseвҖқ analyses are presented without a clearly defined measurement uncertainty, the result obtained is not an absolute location determination but rather an interpreted dataset.
Therefore, both the technical interviews with BeЕҹikГ§i and the official correspondence with the FSR converge on the same point: HTS data does not directly produce a location, but only a limited probability area.
The critical question for Turkey thus becomes: to what extent is a dataset that is scientifically uncertain sufficient to produce вҖңdefinitive convictionвҖқ in criminal law?

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: Theory and reality: dynamic deviations in field measurements
text:
Theory and reality: dynamic deviations in field measurements
Beyond theoretical debate, the field tests we conducted in TavЕҹantepe allowed us to see the technical contradiction with the naked eye. Simultaneous signal measurements taken at the homes of Salim GГјran, Arif GГјran, and Nevzat Bahtiyar вҖ” and at critical surrounding points вҖ” using two separate Turkcell lines on the same device revealed dramatic variability in the data. Even though we remained at a fixed point, the signals constantly jumped between different cells, and each measurement produced different coverage-area data. This concretely demonstrated that location determinations claimed to be made вҖңat the room levelвҖқ cannot carry scientific certainty due to dynamic signal behaviour and geographical deviations on the ground.



Signal data shifting in real time on the same phone





Table 1

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: Forensic Institute Founder Prof.В Allan Jamieson: вҖңA process built on weak evidenceвҖқ
text:
Forensic Institute Founder Prof.В Allan Jamieson: вҖңA process built on weak evidenceвҖқ
Prof.В Allan Jamieson вҖ” the world-renowned forensic medicine expert known for his role in the Diana case in the United Kingdom and the founder of The Forensic Institute вҖ” stated, in his comments on the biological findings and technical evaluations raised in the Narin GГјran case, that there are serious question marks regarding the scientific reliability of the evidence in the file.
In particular, in his assessment of the PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen) finding, Jamieson said that such a marker cannot, on its own, be interpreted as strong forensic evidence:

вҖңPSA is also produced by women, but in very small amounts, and the locations where it is found are generally not the places where it was reported on NarinвҖҷs body. Therefore, this is meaningless. I would probably expect it in blood, but it would not be on the body, and it would be at undetectable amounts.вҖқ




Allan Jamieson


When reminded that the file reports PSA findings on different areas such as вҖңthe vagina, the outside of the underwear, the skirt and the headscarf,вҖқ Jamieson raised methodological questions about the laboratory methods and test choices used:

вҖңHow was this defined? Which test did they use for PSA? Why did they use PSA rather than another stain marker?вҖқ

Referring also to past observations on TurkeyвҖҷs forensic infrastructure, Jamieson said he had been in Turkey in 2013 and expressed his criticisms of certain laboratory processes:

вҖңIn 2013 I was in Ankara, I went to five different places in Turkey. They were building things in the middle of the desert with the windows open; you cannot do that in a laboratory. So basically, these people are not experts. To begin with, I do not believe in this. Because they had only just started.вҖқ

While describing the evidentiary structure in the case as вҖңweak,вҖқ Jamieson defined the methodological approach that should be followed for defence or forensic evaluation as follows:

вҖңThere are probably a lot of people who are going to go to prison, but there is very bad (weak) evidence. So I would start with the вҖҳexpertвҖҷ: what is their expertise, where were they trained? The next step is always to ask: how do you know that?вҖқ

In his evaluation of a few rooted hair samples among the biological evidence, Jamieson drew attention to the limits of mitochondrial DNA analysis. Noting that hair samples can be examined in two different ways, he said:

вҖңThere are two different ways a hair can be tested: one is standard DNA, the other is mitochondrial DNA. With mitochondrial DNA, the motherвҖҷs DNA will be present in that hair.вҖқ

For this reason, he emphasised that hair analysis alone cannot produce a definitive result in identifying a perpetrator.
Explaining his approach to case analysis through вҖңreconstructionвҖқ of events, Jamieson summarised the basic logic of forensic examination as follows:

вҖңFrom a birdвҖҷs-eye view, you should have two strategic aims: first, to test the existing evidence; second, to evaluate possible alternative scenarios. What I do is to reconstruct the events from beginning to end in my mind and see what may have happened.вҖқ

Stating that forensic examination, by its very nature, requires a questioning method, Jamieson concluded:

вҖңOur work, so to speak, requires a bit of stubbornness; if you tell me something, I look at the evidence.вҖқ

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: The meeting in Ankara: the possibility of an on-site вҖңkeЕҹifвҖқ on the table
text:
The meeting in Ankara: the possibility of an on-site вҖңkeЕҹifвҖқ on the table
Within the framework of Minister of Justice AkДұn GГјrlekвҖҷs вҖңCentury of JusticeвҖқ vision, a more active examination process has been initiated in files that have deeply affected the public conscience. The technical examination processes revived in the GГјlistan Doku and Rabia Naz investigations have brought the Narin GГјran file to a similar threshold of вҖңre-evaluation.вҖқ
In order to follow this process closely on site, I held a meeting in Ankara with officials of the newly established Department for the Investigation of Unsolved Crimes within the General Directorate of Criminal Affairs of the Ministry of Justice. During the meeting, the possibility of re-evaluating the file not only at a desk but, where necessary, in the field вҖ” that is, the option of вҖңconducting a keЕҹifвҖқ вҖ” was directly placed on the agenda.

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: A precedent for 75 provinces and 638 files
text:
A precedent for 75 provinces and 638 files
The case carries a structural significance for the Turkish judicial system that goes beyond Narin GГјran alone. A ruling delivered without addressing the contradictions in the suspectsвҖҷ statements and the scientific gaps in the technical data may result in serious rights-violation rulings during the AYM or ECHR processes.
Re-examining this file in light of scientific data will also serve as an illuminating precedent for the work being done on 638 files in 75 provinces and on 693 victims still awaiting clarification.

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: Confronting the technical contradictions
text:
Confronting the technical contradictions
This meeting became a platform on which the technical method used in the file (narrowed base) was confronted with international standards. The UK-based Forensic Science Regulator (FSR) report I presented to officials clarified the following critical point that could change the course of the case:

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: The Ministry: вҖңIt cannot be calculated in the city anywayвҖқ
text:
The Ministry: вҖңIt cannot be calculated in the city anywayвҖқ
While I was laying out the technical limits and scientific uncertainties of narrowed-base analyses with data, a Ministry official gave the short, clear answer that effectively closed the discussion: вҖңNarrow base cannot be calculated in the city anyway.вҖқ
This brief sentence became the most concrete proof of the impasse вҖ” even from the technical perspective in Ankara вҖ” into which the reports presented in the file with claims of вҖңcentimetre-by-centimetre location determinationвҖқ had run. The officialвҖҷs stance also explained why the possibility of a site visit (keЕҹif) is being considered so seriously.

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: Conclusion: science or interpretation?
text:
Conclusion: science or interpretation?
My purpose in going to Ankara was to ask, directly to those concerned, about the scientific limits of digital data. The general framework that emerged from the meeting was not the outright rejection of evidence sets, but rather the approach of testing the limits of existing technical methods on site (through keЕҹif).
The Narin GГјran file is no longer merely a murder investigation; it is a major methodological debate about how telecommunication data should be interpreted within the law. As long as the contradiction between HTS analyses and field reality remains unresolved, claims of вҖңtechnical certaintyвҖқ will continue to hang in the air.
Yet the most fundamental question still stands: to what extent can data whose technical certainty is in doubt form the legal basis of a definitive ruling?

title: Ministry of Justice approves: вҖҳOn-site examinationвҖҷ on the agenda in the Narin GГјran file
section: Procedural and substantive debates in the legal process
text:
Procedural and substantive debates in the legal process
Former Constitutional Court rapporteur Ali RДұza ГҮoban, who evaluates the Narin GГјran murder case within the framework of an вҖңimpunity project,вҖқ voices significant criticisms regarding both procedure and the assessment of evidence. ГҮoban argues that, in the file, the framework concerning the perpetratorвҖҷs identity and the motivation for the act has not been fully clarified by concrete and definitive data:

вҖңOne of the most fundamental problems in the case is that three people have been convicted as вҖҳco-perpetratorsвҖҷ and given the heaviest possible sentence. Yet there is a vital deficiency: it has not been determined exactly by whom and with what motive Narin was killed. This is where the greatest weakness of the case lies. It is striking that, in such a critical case concluded in two months by a local court at a speed unfamiliar in Turkey, the concrete perpetrator has not been clarified.вҖқ

According to ГҮoban, while the court reached the вҖңco-perpetratorвҖқ conclusion (the legal definition: those who, in the commission of the offence, act in unity of intent and action and jointly take part in the decision) on the basis of HTS records and camera footage, it failed to reach a вҖңsГјbutвҖқ (proven establishment of the offence with definitive evidence) that would set out the manner in which the crime was committed beyond doubt. This directly opens to debate one of the fundamental principles of criminal procedure: вҖңin case of doubt, the defendant benefits.вҖқ
The report also draws attention to the speed of the trial. It criticises the preparation of the indictment within a month of the discovery of NarinвҖҷs body, arguing that the evidence entered the file before being technically deepened. ГҮoban states that this speed, when considered together with public pressure, creates risks for the right to defence and for evidentiary debate:

вҖңIn Turkey, this indictment was written very quickly. The body was found on 8 September; on 21 October the indictment was leaked in writing to the press; and on 23 October the court accepted it. In a case as complex and as full of uncertainty as this, that speed is a serious problem. We can think of this as having been done solely to ease public pressure. With so much uncertainty, it is unusual for such heavy sentences to be upheld this quickly.вҖқ

ГҮoban also assesses that the existing uncertainties in the file may lead to a finding of вҖңrights violationвҖқ during the Constitutional Court process, or may open the way for a substantive re-examination of the file.
This case is not independent of the justice problems in the trials in Turkey. The available data may place these individuals in the position of вҖңsuspects,вҖқ but it may not be sufficient to prove directly that they are вҖңperpetrators.вҖқ During the AYM stage, the framing of which fundamental rights have been violated must be very carefully constructed. It is not easy to predict what will happen in current AYM practice; however, if the file passes the вҖңadmissibilityвҖқ stage and is taken into substantive review, the likelihood of a violation finding or a remand of the file due to the deficiencies it contains is high.
Tomorrow, within the framework of the question вҖңhow and why did Narin GГјran disappear?вҖқ, the first 19 days that elapsed until Narin was found will be re-examined step by step.

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
text:
AUTHOR


Hilal Seven


LAST UPDATED


March 8, 2026


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 16 min


Word Count


2932
What followed the murder of Narin GГјran was no different from a modern stoning ritual. The target was once again a woman: YГјksel GГјran, who was lynched by an entire society while mourning her own child, her dignity and past buried in a cell. This piece, poured from the conscience of a journalist, is a cry of truth addressed to institutions that stayed silent, a society complicit in the lynch, and the execution of justice.
Hilal Seven вҖ“ London
Remember that horrifying scene etched into our memories by the film вҖңThe Stoning of Soraya M.вҖқ: A woman is buried up to her waist in the village square and the first stone is thrown. Then the stones rain down; small, large, sharpвҖҰ Most of those throwing stones are familiar faces: neighbours, relatives, villagers. While the woman is still breathing, the crowdвҖҷs verdict of execution has long been delivered.
Since 21 August, when Narin GГјran disappeared, this scene has been playing in my mind as I watched events unfold. Because what we witnessed was no different from a modern stoning ritual. Headlines, television commentary and social media lynching took the place of stones; but the target was the same: A woman.
That womanвҖҷs name was YГјksel GГјran. A mother mourning her own child was declared a вҖңkillerвҖқ from the very first days. While there was no evidence whatsoever, before anyone had even come close to the truth, stones began raining down on her. TГјrkiyeвҖҷs media and public opinion buried a grieving mother in the ground and placed her on a target. Every headline was a stone, every comment a blow. Today YГјksel GГјran may be breathing in a cell; yet society has long since buried her life, her honour and her past by tearing them to shreds. Moreover, most of those who carried out this collective execution feel not the slightest pang in their conscience.

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
section: A Modern Execution: Rhythm 0 and Marina AbramoviДҮ
text:
A Modern Execution: Rhythm 0 and Marina AbramoviДҮ
To understand this societal hysteria, one must look back to 1974, to Marina AbramoviДҮвҖҷs Rhythm 0 performance. AbramoviДҮ placed 72 objects on a table вҖ” a rose, honey, a razor blade and a loaded pistol among them вҖ” and left this note: вҖңI am an object. During this time you can do anything to me.вҖқ
At first, gentle hands reached for the rose, spread the honey; as the hours progressed, they gave way to savagery. Her clothes were cut with the razor, her skin was slashed, a gun was held to her head. Years later AbramoviДҮ would say of those moments: вҖңI was ready to die.вҖқ The crowdвҖҷs reflex in the Narin GГјran case was exactly this. On stage was not an artist but a real family; yet the appetite for lynching was the same.



A scene from Marina AbramoviДҮвҖҷs Rhythm 0 (1974) performance.


From the very first days the mother was convicted, the entire family was besieged, the neighbourhood was combed through. The words that came from peopleвҖҷs mouths were sharper than those razor blades. Why does the worst-case scenario come to mind first? Why do we choose to accuse rather than understand? That dark impulse that brought AbramoviДҮ to the point of death in six hours tore apart the GГјran familyвҖҷs privacy and reputation.
The real problem is this: this evil is not the work of just a few people. Thanks to this lynch, some were rewarded, some were promoted, and some continue their lives as though nothing happened. As a society, we are not outside this evil but at its very centre. Yet there is a forgotten difference: what AbramoviДҮ experienced was an art performance testing boundaries. What happened after Narin was not a performance; it was organised, real and deliberate evil that descended upon a family, a neighbourhood and a life.

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
section: A Call to Truth: Technical Evidence and Expert Opinions
text:
A Call to Truth: Technical Evidence and Expert Opinions
I ask you to spare just 11 minutes of your time. This short animated film lays bare the realities of the Narin GГјran murder, withholding no detail:

  

 
   
     
     YouTube'da izlemek iГ§in tДұklayДұn
   
 

    
  


If this has piqued your curiosity, I ask you to spare another 35 minutes to watch the documentary produced by Mind Vorteks:

  

 
   
     
     YouTube'da izlemek iГ§in tДұklayДұn
   
 

    
  


After this striking introduction, be sure to make time for the comprehensive broadcast that examines the caseвҖҷs real truths through the lens of law, politics and media[1]. These 2 hours and 47 minutes are a debt we owe to the memory of a child and to justice.
In this broadcast, forensic informatics expert Tuncay BeЕҹikГ§i, who presents scientific reports[2] that could change the course of the case, makes a striking observation: вҖңThe great manipulation mechanisms of the past are now operating through the death of an 8-year-old child. Perception is being managed through media and bureaucracy.вҖқ
BeЕҹikГ§iвҖҷs technical analyses fundamentally demolish the lie of narrowed-base station records[3], the caseвҖҷs biggest pillar. Image records and internet traffic clearly show that:

Salim GГјran was in his own home at the time of the incident.
Enes and their mother YГјksel GГјran were likewise in their own home.
Six-year-old ErenвҖҷs testimony also corroborated this technical data.




Enes, Narin, YГјksel and family


Despite this, the court signed off on life imprisonment sentences while ignoring this concrete data. It was not only the base station records; forensic medicine findings were also left in the dark. Meanwhile, the time of the murder was fixed by three camera images, the duration of the murder was established, and it was certain from those cameras that the perpetrator was Nevzat Bahtiyar вҖ” yet this evidence was disregarded.
Child psychiatry specialist Prof.В Dr.В Veysi ГҮeri, speaking in the same broadcast, drew attention to the PSA (Prostate Specific Antigen) test found on the body. Emphasising that detecting PSA on a body that had been in water for 19 days was no ordinary finding, ГҮeri noted that this could point to systematic abuse. Yet the media and public opinion chose to push these technical facts aside and direct all arrows at the family. I urge you to read the comprehensive post shared from the account of X user Dr.В Islamzade[4], written with NarinвҖҷs justice profile photo, covering the details from A to Z.
Another benefit of listening to this broadcast from start to finish is seeing the accurate journalism analyses of important journalists such as Atakan SГ¶nmez and Ali Duran Topuz regarding the case; and becoming aware of the assessments made by former presidents of the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association Mehmet Emin Aktar, family lawyer Onur AkdaДҹ and other legal professionals, delivered as though lecturing from a university podium. I have asked you to devote roughly five to six hours to these broadcasts; your valuable time and your unprejudiced attentionвҖҰ After all, what is half a day of your time to understand the savage tearing of a tiny 8-year-old human being from this world? Would it be too much to listen once more to Arif GГјran and his speech that made all our hair stand on end?

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
section: The Execution of Justice: Nevzat Bahtiyar and the GГјran Family
text:
The Execution of Justice: Nevzat Bahtiyar and the GГјran Family
Narin was an 8-year-old child whose happiness could be read in her eyes in every photograph, and whose upbringing in a loving environment was evident in everything about her. Sadly, she was torn from life within the merciless reality of the geography where she was born. When we look at the course of the case, a picture that is difficult to explain with rational thought emerges: Nevzat Bahtiyar, the name at the centre of material evidence and contradictory statements, was for some reason turned into the figure whose accounts were вҖңtaken as the basisвҖқ during the trial.





On the other hand, the mechanism of justice operated with an entirely different severity for the fifteen members of the GГјran family. While allegations of severe torture in interrogation rooms filled the air, the most fundamental principle of law вҖ” вҖңthe benefit of the doubt belongs to the defendantвҖқ вҖ” was virtually suspended for this family. We must now stop and ask: How did these people вҖ” whose statements contained not a single contradiction, who raised their children with great care, and who had no rational motivation to murder their child вҖ” suddenly become the perpetrators of the gravest crime? Let us recall the moment they met when they visited the graves of the murdered family members days later.
The real question that wounds our conscience at this point is this: How can a person who kidnapped and killed Narin and then continued with life as though nothing had happened, notable for their cold-bloodedness, be perceived as though they were the вҖңleast culpableвҖқ party in the case? As things stand today, YГјksel GГјran, Enes and Salim GГјran have lost not only their freedom but all their reputation and family integrity. They are now behind four walls, mourning their one and only child while trying to pay the price of a murder to all of society.

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
section: The Scales of Conscience: A Call to Institutions and Society
text:
The Scales of Conscience: A Call to Institutions and Society
For those who once used their pen and social media power to demonise this family, perhaps today is the time to look in the mirror. It is not too late for anything. Examine the data presented for the sake of your conscience; if you were party to even the slightest injustice, show the virtue of apologising. Stand against the systematic injustice that targeted this family simply because they are Kurdish.
Today the world is debating the dark networks that abuse girls, the Epstein case. In this vile world where kings and presidents are implicated, we see how Virginia Giuffres who seek justice are driven to death. Today the blind system that tore Narin from life is imposing a similar helplessness on her mother. A woman, under the lynch of eighty million, is unjustly wasting away before our eyes. Speak up before it is too late.
From the opposition media that brazenly turned the family into news material to corner the government вҖ” what did this family do to you? A family whose child attended a QurвҖҷan course, whose women covered their heads, who were deemed worthy of lynching simply because they are Kurdish, and who were turned into вҖңthe otherвҖҷs otherвҖқ because their members held different political views. Everyone united and lived out their rebellion against what they hated through the GГјrans. The government wing that dared not question the state apparatus, the opposition that could not speak up against the handicaps in the justice system, the secular segment that seethed inside at the sight of a covered person yet did not dare reveal its Islamophobia, and the Kurdish politics that wrote off the family from the start saying вҖңthey donвҖҷt vote for us anyway, theyвҖҷre not one of usвҖқвҖҰ All of them failed in their stance.
Just as those who targeted Alevis in the past, those who threw forks at Ahmet Kaya, or those who prepared the process that led Tahir ElГ§i to his death are remembered today, so too will history remember those who entrusted the scales of conscience to populist figures who fancied themselves artists. This countryвҖҷs innocent people, whose hearts have been burned in every era, will never forget the injustices they have suffered, and will never shelve those who betrayed humanity in this case. That mob which accused an uncle who couldnвҖҷt even afford to plaster his house of being вҖңrich enough to buy the mediaвҖқ is under an enormous burden of guilt.





Think for a moment about why the GГјran familyвҖҷs children work on construction sites. A family that cannot even pay its lawyersвҖҷ fees, that struggles to bring two morsels home вҖ” how can you brand these people as powerful, land-owning and wealthy enough to be exempt from punishment? History, whether written or oral, always illuminates such evils. Tomorrows always shed light on todays; the voice of truth may be faint today, but tomorrow it will make consciences roar.

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
section: Journalism and Conscience: Bearing Witness to Truth
text:
Journalism and Conscience: Bearing Witness to Truth
As a woman journalist, I had only one compass throughout this process: the principle our culture taught us of standing by the oppressed. Unlike those who succumbed to the hunger for popularity, we must see that this case is a matter of honour. My words are to my own quarter; to the honourable bearers of the socialist and humanist struggle:

Dear Saturday Mothers: YГјksel GГјran is a mother who could not even attend her childвҖҷs funeral. Should you not extend your hand to her?
Dear Human Rights Association: Why did you stay silent while family members were subjected to severe torture, while YГјksel GГјran was forced to give a statement in a language she does not know?
Dear WomenвҖҷs Movements: Why did you ignore YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs identity as a вҖңwomanвҖқ and the lynch she was subjected to?

Let me remind you; father Arif GГјran, speaking to DEM Party MP Г–mer Faruk GergerlioДҹlu after the court ruling, said[5]: вҖңThey buried my family alive in the courtroom. Everything was evaluated one-sidedly.вҖқ





Moreover, secular and religious segments virtually united; they described the people of TavЕҹantepe as вҖңsuperhumanвҖқ beings who killed children through rituals. Some regional correspondents tried to overcome their professional failures by profiteering from this case; they turned an entire extended family into a tool for their own interests.
Even the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association, which fights against rights violations in Kurdistan, turned a blind eye to the severe torture endured by fifteen family members. While one had a ruptured eardrum and another bore marks of torture on their body, the attitude of the Bar President shattered our hope for humanity. The most painful part was this: a woman was forced to express the profound devastation she experienced in her mother tongue in the language of the вҖңmale stateвҖқ that had planted a fig tree in her hearth. This is plainly torture through language. WomenвҖҷs rights advocates sat in silence in the face of this oppression.

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
section: A Candle in the Dark
text:
A Candle in the Dark
This country watched 35 people being burned alive in MadДұmak, the killers of Hrant Dink, Ceylan and Cemile going unpunished. Now the same judicial system has decided that NarinвҖҷs killers are her family. An entire truth was sacrificed so that a media showman could get a promotion, channels could gorge on ratings, and some could gain fame. Yet life is not entirely dark. Since 21 August, there is a conscientious group numbering in the hundreds who left their work behind and have been meticulously following documents and case notes, trying to make the GГјran familyвҖҷs innocence heard.
On the other hand, good journalists still exist. If you have not yet read Faruk BildiriciвҖҷs observations[6] in his piece dated 14 August 2025, please read it now. Then, please carefully read the article series вҖңThe Silence of the LambsвҖқ by Ali Duran Topuz, published on the Д°lke TV news site[7], in which he covers every important piece of information that went unreported in this case.
In addition, read from beginning to end at least once the article series by DEM Party DiyarbakДұr MP Sevilay ГҮelenk[8], penned in the manner of an investigative journalism masterclass, which begins with the words вҖңWe owe Narin a debt of truth.вҖқ Look, and you will see: the truth is actually plain as day. Never mind the efforts of the media lynchers who deceived millions вҖ” look at the research of the many conscientious people who devoted their efforts to this case. And finally, spare three and a half minutes to listen to the words of NarinвҖҷs grandmother:

  

 
   
     
     YouTube'da izlemek iГ§in tДұklayДұn
   
 

    
  


In this video by The National News, hear the pain of Remziye, the grandmother whose grandchild lies beneath the ground and whose daughter has been imprisoned in a cell.

title: Forgive Us, YГјksel GГјran; They Struck You Through Your Womanhood, and We Could Not Prevent It
section: An Assignment of Justice for the Future
text:
An Assignment of Justice for the Future
Dear people of my eighty-million-strong country, whose presumption of conscience is mired in mud: If you wish, do not watch or read any of this, continue to wrongly accuse people as you have from the very first day; but know this: Berze, Ferhat, Leyla, Remziye, Rana, Melek, the late Nuri KarakaЕҹ and dozens of beautiful-hearted people whose names I cannot list are shouldering your shame. They are fighting the battle for justice for Narin without rest, day and night. I owe these beautiful people two words: Thank you for existing. The world turns thanks to the grace of people like you. The voice of conscience in these lands is heard thanks to you.
To be accountable, to turn back from error, to be able to apologiseвҖҰ To go beyond the hunger for popularity and remain вҖңhumanвҖқвҖҰ That is our real test. Let us apologise to the people of TavЕҹantepe. Let us say вҖңstopвҖқ to this disgrace against humanity inflicted upon EnesвҖҷs stolen youth and Salim GГјran. For a moment, put yourself in YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs place; feel what it would be like to try to describe an unimaginable pain in an unknown language.





I appeal to all your consciences: Who really killed Narin?
In this case, everyone who contributed to the lynch has stained NarinвҖҷs memory. Societal rot has been made flesh and bone. It was very easy to convict a family simply because they are Kurdish and poor. This case is another shade of the Kurdish question. In these lands where news is reported under bombs, where rights-based journalism is practised despite every kind of pressure and violence, in the words of a woman from Urfa fighting for justice for Narin: вҖңA city as ancient as DiyarbakДұr should not have done this.вҖқ Indeed, some journalists working in the region acted even more ruthlessly than that dark character in the film Nightcrawler. From its law enforcement to its judiciary, from its media to its self-proclaimed human rights advocates, a male-dominated order built its own fiction upon the death of a girl child.
To all the women in the GГјran family, foremost among them YГјksel GГјran; to all our women friends in prison who fight for a free and dignified worldвҖҰ And to all the mothers whose efforts helped you stay human against all oddsвҖҰ
Happy International Working WomenвҖҷs Day.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
text:
AUTHOR


Faruk Bildirici


LAST UPDATED


August 14, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 52 min


Word Count


9299
This is not an allegation. Even in the DiyarbakДұr 8th High Criminal CourtвҖҷs reasoned judgment, a coherent narrative explaining how the murder was committed cannot be established; it is explicitly acknowledged that the motive for the murder could not be determined. Moreover, the court could not answer the question of why Salim GГјran, instead of taking the body himself and hiding it in the stream, handed it over to Nevzat BahtiyarвҖ”thereby sharing a very important secret that still remains unknown.
Indeed, the President of the 1st Criminal Chamber of the Regional Court of Appeal, who dissented from the ChamberвҖҷs decision to uphold the verdict, particularly emphasized that the motive and manner of the murder could not be determined. The President listed the legal deficiencies in the investigation and prosecution in 14 points.
The point that struck me most in the PresidentвҖҷs detailed dissenting opinion was his emphasis that вҖңaccording to the new situation that emerged after news and discussion content in social media and TV bulletins, there were changes in the defendantsвҖҷ statements.вҖқ The President had clearly observed the mediaвҖҷs influence on the trial through the statements.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: There was no such gas station employee; the account was fake
text:
The truth: There was no such gas station employee; the account was fake
After Nevzat Bahtiyar was caught, in his first statement to the prosecutor on 9 September, he said that Salim GГјran had shown him something wrapped in a blanket inside the car and told him, вҖңYou will dispose of this,вҖқ and that he had seen NarinвҖҷs body in SalimвҖҷs vehicle and taken it from there. He even repeated this during the camera-recorded вҖңscene reconstruction.вҖқ These statements were consistent with the вҖңgas station employeeвҖҷsвҖқ social media post! Even though in his second statement on 10 September he recounted different details, he still claimed he took the body from the car.
But in his 21 September statement to the prosecutor, Nevzat Bahtiyar completely changed his account of taking NarinвҖҷs body from SalimвҖҷs vehicle:
вҖңSalim GГјran called me. We went together to ArifвҖҷs house. I saw Narin GГјran lying motionless on the ground. After placing her in a blanket, Salim GГјran handed the body to me. I took the body into my arms, left the house, and carried it to my residence to put it in my vehicle.вҖқ
His claim that he took the body from the familyвҖҷs home overturned his earlier narrative and erased most of his first two statements.
It is unknown whether this change in statement was related, but shortly afterward it emerged that the Facebook account of the so-called вҖңgas station employeeвҖқ was fake, that no such employee existed, and that the information shared was false. On 2 October, reports[6] confirming this were published.
The court accepted as true Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs final statementвҖ”repeated during the hearingsвҖ”that he took the body from the house, first carried it to his barn, put it in a sack, and transported it by his own vehicle to EДҹertutmaz Stream. Thus the court concluded that Narin was not killed in uncle SalimвҖҷs vehicle and her body was not transported with that vehicle.
Nevertheless, relying on Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs assertion that вҖңthere was a frothy liquid in NarinвҖҷs mouth,вҖқ the court reasoned that this liquid might have gotten on Salim GГјranвҖҷs hand and that he might have wiped his hand on the seat. Salim denied this вҖңabsolutelyвҖқ and said, вҖңIt would be normal for NarinвҖҷs DNA to be found in my vehicle; I would be surprised if it wasnвҖҷt. It is the family car.вҖқ
Yet the courtвҖҷs reasoned judgment stated, вҖңIt is clear that after Narin was killed, either Narin herself or an object that came into contact with her body was transported with SalimвҖҷs vehicle.вҖқ The court thus based its decision not on evidence but on assumption. The President of the Chamber, who dissented from the appellate decision, also emphasized that the investigation on this point was incomplete.
Although the blanket was never found and the вҖңgas station employeeвҖқ turned out to be fake, the reports published before NarinвҖҷs body was foundвҖ”вҖңHorrifying allegation from gas station employee! Was she lying unconscious in her uncleвҖҷs vehicle?вҖқвҖ”contributed significantly to the perception that uncle Salim GГјran and the family were guilty.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: There was no sexual relationship between the uncle and the mother
text:
The truth: There was no sexual relationship between the uncle and the mother
Nevzat Bahtiyar repeated in court his allegation that Narin was killed because she witnessed a relationship between Salim and YГјksel. But the allegation must not have been very convincing, because the presiding judge reminded him that the time when Narin was last seen on the path leading home was close to the time Nevzat arrived at the home and asked, вҖңWhen did Salim have time to have relations with YГјksel for Narin to have seen it?вҖқ Nevzat answered, вҖңI donвҖҷt know. I did not see it.вҖқ Salim and YГјksel GГјran both vehemently rejected the allegation as вҖңslander.вҖқ
Indeed, the courtвҖҷs reasoned judgment stated that вҖңit has been concluded that defendant YГјksel did not have a relationship with defendant Salim at the time of the incident.вҖқ However, the court still accepted NevzatвҖҷs account as true and rejected Salim GГјranвҖҷs defense as false. This acceptance was based on the courtвҖҷs conclusion that вҖңdefendant Salim made this statement to defendant Nevzat to conceal the real motive for killing Narin togetherвҖ”a motive that our court has also been unable to determine.вҖқ
Thus the court concluded that Salim GГјran committed the murder to conceal a motive вҖңmore importantвҖқ than a вҖңsexual relationshipвҖқ with his sister-in-law YГјksel but, somehow, entrusted the task of hiding the murder to Nevzat Bahtiyar. Yet the court could not identify this very important вҖңreal motive.вҖқ
The President of the 1st Criminal Chamber of the DiyarbakДұr Regional Court of Appeal, who dissented from the appellate decision, found the courtвҖҷs decision вҖңcontrary to reason, logic, the ordinary flow of life, and the patriarchal structure of the region.вҖқ He stated: вҖңMore important than whether the relationship between YГјksel and Salim was seen or known is the question of what the real motive for killing Narin was. No conclusion has been expressed within the scope of the evidence, and the conclusion and reasoning reached in the form of mind-reading based on abstract assumptions is unlawful.вҖқ

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: TГјlin GГјran, who was born disabled, died of pneumonia while receiving treatment at the hospital
text:
The truth: TГјlin GГјran, who was born disabled, died of pneumonia while receiving treatment at the hospital
Reports that the DiyarbakДұr Chief Public ProsecutorвҖҷs Office included TГјlin GГјranвҖҷs death in the investigation and asked the hospital for the cause of death were accurate.
However, documents[11] from the DiyarbakДұr ChildrenвҖҷs Hospital proved that TГјlin GГјran, who had a physical disability, died in 2009 at the age of five while receiving treatment for pneumonia. Contrary to what news reports claimed, the hospitalвҖҷs death report stated that there was вҖңno suspicious condition or sign of traumaвҖқ in her death and that she died during treatment, which is why no autopsy was performed.
Thus, the scenarios claiming that TГјlin GГјran was killed by being pushed down the stairs collapsed. Yet these reports also played a significant role in intensifying suspicion toward the family.
Reports about TГјlinвҖҷs death also surfaced during the hearings. When questioned by the judge, YГјksel GГјran said her daughter was born disabled and complained about the accusations:

вҖңPolice officers blamed me for TГјlinвҖҷs death; why did TГјlin die? If TГјlin had not passed away at DaДҹkapДұ Hospital, I swear they would have exhumed her grave. вҖҳYou killed your daughter TГјlin.вҖҷ Everyone knows it; there is a report, thank God.
вҖҳYou threw TГјlin down the stairs; what kind of mother are you?вҖҷ In that station they interrogated me about TГјlin. In this case they portrayed me as guilty. I was in the holding cell; someone came and shook his finger at me: вҖҳLook, IвҖҷm going to DaДҹkapДұ. If your daughter did not die there, your life is over.вҖҷ They went to DaДҹkapДұ Hospital and retrieved my daughterвҖҷs death report. How can there be such cruelty? I lost my daughter 17 years ago.вҖқ

Naturally, the courtвҖҷs reasoned judgment did not mention TГјlin GГјranвҖҷs death at all, because it clearly had no criminal aspect. Some outlets that published the false reports did not even bother to correct them; the uncorrected errors remain in digital archives.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: It was proven that Enes GГјran did not use drugs
text:
The truth: It was proven that Enes GГјran did not use drugs
Those who interpreted the bleeped вҖңs..вҖқ as referring to drugs were wrong. The programвҖҷs host, Didem Arslan YДұlmaz, attempted to correct the mistake on her 28 August program by replaying the motherвҖҷs remarks.
YДұlmaz said: вҖңRTГңK does not want the word cigarette to be used. Tobacco is interpreted as illegal substance or drug in the region. No, sir, he is smoking a cigarette. It is not an illegal substance; it is tobacco.вҖқ Yet despite YДұlmazвҖҷs efforts, reports and commentary claiming Enes used illegal substances continued.
Older brother Baran GГјran was also asked about this on Kanal DвҖҷs вҖңNeler Oluyor HayattaвҖқ program on 29 October. Baran GГјran said that Enes вҖңhad never smoked a cigarette in his life and that this was shown in tests conductedвҖқ [14].
Baran GГјran later repeated this in court. During the hearings, the lawyer representing the Ministry of Family and Social Policies, Elif AslДұ Еһahin, asked, вҖңDid Enes have a tendency toward violence or substance use? Have you ever witnessed an anger management problem?вҖқ Baran GГјran replied, вҖңAbsolutely never. I can state this clearly. You may ask the whole village. Enes is someone who never fought with anyone.вҖқ
During his own testimony, when describing how his mother called him while he was smoking that day, Enes was also questioned on this matter. The Presiding Judge asked, вҖңWas it marijuana or a cigarette? In DiyarbakДұr, marijuana is also called вҖҳplain cigarette.вҖҷвҖқ Enes replied, вҖңNo, we were smoking cigarettes.вҖқ
Later, at the third day of the hearing, the Presiding Judge said, вҖңI just received a message. A report on Enes has come out. It is negative,вҖқ announcing the report[15]. Thus, the allegations that Enes GГјran used illegal substances were definitively resolved. The reasoned judgment did not mention the allegation at all.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: The Kurdish translation was incorrect; he did not say вҖңSheвҖҷs not dead yetвҖқ
text:
The truth: The Kurdish translation was incorrect; he did not say вҖңSheвҖҷs not dead yetвҖқ
In reality, such a conversation did not match any of the information revealed up to that dayвҖ”or even Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs account. Suspecting that the Kurdish phrase вҖңne mДұreвҖқ had been mistranslated, the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association requested a new expert analysis[16].
On 20 September, Anadolu Agency reported the new translation conducted by the expert. According to this, the phrase вҖңSheвҖҷs not dead yetвҖқ did not exist in the Turkish translation of the Kurdish conversation. Ramazan was actually saying, вҖңOne of them is on the ground, haaa,вҖқ and Salim responded, вҖңOkay, I will go and get it now.вҖқ
Thus the correct translation emerged, but reports about the phrase вҖңSheвҖҷs not dead yetвҖқ in the phone call continued for about a week. Moreover, most media outlets did not even correct their false reports afterwardвҖҰ
This phone call was also addressed in court. After playing the Kurdish recording again, DiyarbakДұr Bar Association President and intervening lawyer Nahit Eren asked: вҖңWhat did you mean by saying вҖҳPick up the thing of yours that fell down thereвҖҷ?вҖқ Salim GГјran replied, вҖңWe usually hide that illegal (electric) device under the bushes near the rocks there. That may be it; nothing else. What else would I tell Ramazan?вҖқ
Upon this, Nahit Eren said, вҖңSo this conversation is not about Narin. Now that we have clarified thisвҖҰвҖқ and moved on to another topic.
Thus, it was definitively established that the phone conversation was not related to Narin, and that the phrase вҖңSheвҖҷs not dead yetвҖқ had never been used. The judges did not even dwell on this conversation. The reasoned judgment did not mention it at all.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: It could not be proven that the carpets were washed
text:
The truth: It could not be proven that the carpets were washed
Mother YГјksel GГјran was asked during her interrogation whether the carpets in the house had been washed. She answered: вҖңI did not wash the carpets. But because people were coming and going to the house, as far as I remember, on the fifth or sixth day my sister Yasemin came and asked whether I had clean carpets because they were dirty. I said I had. Yasemin took the clean carpet and laid it down. My old carpet is still in the house unwashed.вҖқ
Indeed, halktv.com.trвҖ”which on 7 October claimed to have found images proving the carpets were washedвҖ”corrected its report[17] three days later with a new story titled вҖңThe mystery of the carpets on the balcony in the Narin murder unraveled!вҖқ stating: вҖңIt has been determined that the carpets seized by the gendarmerie were not washed, and that this finding matches mother YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs statement. It was stated that the carpets were taken from the house shortly after the incident and that no washing traces were found on them.вҖқ
A forensic report from the Van Gendarmerie Criminal Laboratory stated that only one carpet sample had the DNA profile of a male and that no findings related to Narin were detected.
Intervening lawyer Nahit Eren argued that the presence of the DNA of only one person was proof that the carpets had been washed. Eren said: вҖңThirty-seven carpets, kilims, rugs were taken from the house, some were taken while still laid out. Of the samples taken from those 37 carpets, DNA belonging to only two individuals was found! WouldnвҖҷt they contain hair?вҖқ Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs lawyer Adnan AtaЕҹ expressed similar views.
Enes GГјranвҖҷs lawyer Mustafa Demir also argued that the gendarmerie did not conduct a thorough examination, saying: вҖңThey only checked with blue light, because they were looking for blood or bodily fluids. They did not find any other DNA. They clung to this idea: The carpets were washed, so nothing remains. Who told this to the press? Again the gendarmerie, I claim.вҖқ
YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs lawyer DoДҹuЕҹcan Kurucu argued that the forensic report showed вҖңthe carpets were not washed and no external intervention was detected.вҖқ
Witnesses also did not confirm that the carpets had been washed, but most importantly, there was no concrete evidence proving that the carpets were washed. The claim remained only as an assumption in the media archive.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: The auntвҖҷs statement contained no mention of wrestling in the barn
text:
The truth: The auntвҖҷs statement contained no mention of wrestling in the barn
But in Hediye GГјranвҖҷs statements to the gendarmerie and the prosecutor, there was no mention of such an incident. On the contrary, she gave entirely different information.
Hediye GГјran said she went to YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs home twice that day, first to bring the laundry that had been left with her to wash, and that Enes answered the door and said his mother was sleeping. She said she returned around 15:00, found YГјksel awake, and they sat together for 1.5вҖ“2 hours. She said that Enes woke up after she left and that she did not see him when she was leaving, but that she saw him returning home as she was departing.
Moreover, Hediye GГјran did not mention any observation that YГјksel was angry or that there was a problem between her and Enes.
In fact, contrary to the reports in AkЕҹam and other media outlets, Hediye GГјranвҖҷs statement did not accuse YГјksel or Enes; it supported their statements. Indeed, she was tried in the other case with 16 defendants for this reason. The court ruled that вҖңher statements regarding the hours when the incident occurred were made to protect Enes GГјran by saying he was sleepingвҖқ and that вҖңher statements were made to conceal the truth about NarinвҖҷs murder,вҖқ sentencing her to 3 years and 6 months in prison.
Enes GГјranвҖҷs lawyer also objected in the appellate application to the courtвҖҷs rejection without justification of Hediye GГјranвҖҷs statement, which contradicted Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs account.
Additionally, it was never confirmed that Enes took three showers that day.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: Footage of older brother Baran GГјran at a DEM rally surfaced
text:
The truth: Footage of older brother Baran GГјran at a DEM rally surfaced
It was incorrect to associate the broadcast ban with Hezbollah and weapons. Because the broadcast ban imposed on 29 August was lifted on 9 SeptemberвҖ”the very day these Hezbollah allegations began to spread widely.
It was also false that вҖңnumerous weapons belonging to Hezbollah were foundвҖқ during searches in the village. No such information appeared in the case file, nor did any further news emerge in the media confirming this claim.
What is true is that, in the early days when the gendarmerie was searching for Narin, during their search of Arif GГјranвҖҷs barn, they found 380 AK-47 Kalashnikov long-barrel rifle cartridges wrapped in towels and hidden inside hazelnut cream containers, transparent plastic tomato paste buckets, and white flour sacks [25]. A criminal investigation was launched under Law No.В 6136 on Firearms regarding the illegally held ammunition. In court, Salim GГјran also repeated that 380 bullets had been found.
The вҖң32.GГјnвҖқ documentary on Hezbollah was not filmed in NarinвҖҷs village. Teyit.org determined that the documentary was filmed in YolaГ§ (Susa) Village in DiyarbakДұrвҖҷs Silvan district [26]. The name of YolaГ§ Village appeared in the documentary; in one frame, the mosqueвҖҷs signboard was visible. There is an 87-kilometer distance between TavЕҹantepe and YolaГ§.
There is no information or document showing that TavЕҹantepe Village is fully under Hezbollah/HГңDAPAR influence or that the majority of villagers belong to the organization. HГңDAPAR officials also stated that the village was not theirs.
While DEM initially adopted an approach linking the murder to Hezbollah, this stance shifted in the following days. DEM DiyarbakДұr MP Sevilay ГҮelenk said, вҖңIf you look at the familyвҖҷs photos, you can clearly see from everything that they have no connection with Hezbollah.вҖқ ГҮelenk evaluated that вҖңrumors such as Hezbollah, counter-guerrilla, village guards, etc. were criminalizing TavЕҹantepe and its residentsвҖқ [27].
In the elections in TavЕҹantepe, HDP was the leading party in 2015, Д°YД° Party in 2023, and AKP in the 2024 local elections [28]. In the last election, AKP received 100 votes, DEM 32 votes, and HГңDAPAR 16 votes [27]. This shows that the village is not politically homogeneous to the extent that had been suggested.
Salim GГјran also said in court that he had no problems with political parties, and that during the days when Narin was being searched for, they вҖңexpelled DEMвҖҷs meal truck from the village because journalists were following them.вҖқ
Moreover, it emerged that NarinвҖҷs older brother Baran GГјran had shared footage from DEM Party rallies on social media. Following this, pro-government media outlets such as Takvim[29] and Yeni Еһafak[30] published stories titled вҖңNarinвҖҷs brother Baran at DEM demonstrations! The spiral of silence in TavЕҹantepe and the DEM Party factorвҖқ and вҖңFootage emerges showing that NarinвҖҷs brother Baran GГјran is a PKK sympathizer.вҖқ
In conclusion, no concrete information or documents were produced showing that either Hezbollah/HГңDAPAR or DEM had dominance in the village. The courtвҖҷs verdict and the appellate decision also contained no references to political organizations in the village. However, these reports and comments influenced journalists and the public to align themselves in the Narin GГјran murder case based on their own political positions.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: There was no secret witness
text:
The truth: There was no secret witness
In reality, there was no вҖңsecret witness.вҖқ On the afternoon of 8 SeptemberвҖ”the day NarinвҖҷs body was found early that morningвҖ”Nevzat Bahtiyar was captured and immediately admitted that he had buried the body in the stream.
Most of the media quickly realized that Nevzat Bahtiyar was not a вҖңsecret witnessвҖқ and stopped referring to one. Moreover, Nevzat Bahtiyar did not go to the gendarmerie by himself or request anonymity; he was arrested.
When the gendarmerie identified that a red vehicle had gone to EДҹertutmaz Stream at the time Narin disappeared, they first suspected not Nevzat Bahtiyar but his son. When they went to the sonвҖҷs home in ГҮarДұklДұ, they found Nevzat there. Nevzat Bahtiyar surrendered, saying, вҖңThe person you are looking for is not my son, it is me. I guessed that you would eventually come to me.вҖқ
After being caught, Nevzat began talking. But even though his later statements contradicted his first statement, the media mostly continued to label his statements as вҖңconfessions.вҖқ
Of course, at that time there were also a few journalistsвҖ”including BarДұЕҹ TerkoДҹlu and Д°smail SaymazвҖ”who did not agree with the widespread вҖңconfessionвҖқ narrative and said that NevzatвҖҷs statements вҖңdid not make sense.вҖқ After the first statement emerged, Д°smail Saymaz said on Halk TV: вҖңThere are inconsistencies in Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs statement. There is a self-exonerating aspect to it. There must be a substantial reason for a person to take the body of someone killed by another person and bury it in the stream. The village headman has been detained for ten days. Why did he stay silent? Why did he not speak? What are you afraid of? I think this person actively participated in the crime.вҖқ However, the trial did not proceed in this direction.
Additionally, the claim on A Haber during the hearings that вҖңthere is a new secret witnessвҖқ and that his statement would change the course of the trial also proved to be false. Immediately after this claim circulated, the presiding judge announced during the hearing: вҖңThere is no secret witness statement submitted to our courtвҖқ [35].
However, Abdurrahman ЕһimЕҹekвҖ”who presented the вҖңnew secret witnessвҖқ claimвҖ”did not issue a correction regarding his report[36]. Even the report published on SabahвҖҷs website remains there in its incorrect form.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: The mother did not send her; on the contrary, Narin insisted on going
text:
The truth: The mother did not send her; on the contrary, Narin insisted on going
Yet, during the investigation stages and at trial, Salim GГјran completely rejected the murder allegation. At no stage of the investigations did any situation arise in which he вҖңconfessed to the murder.вҖқ
It is also not true that mother YГјksel GГјran admitted that she had deliberately sent Narin, who did not want to go to the Quran course, to the course. On the contrary, YГјksel GГјran stated that she did not want to send Narin to the Quran course that day, and that she could not refuse Narin because Narin insisted on going.
What is more, interestingly, in the two interviews[38] that YГјksel GГјran gave to Ferit Demir two days after Narin disappearedвҖ”the first on the balcony of the house and the second in the living roomвҖ”she also said that Narin had insisted:

I saw that Narin was dusting the television. Suddenly, her eyes went to the clock. She said, вҖңOh,вҖқ she said, вҖңI forgot the Quran course.вҖқ It was her first time going tooвҖҰ I said, вҖңMy daughter, donвҖҷt go, it is hot, the course is already over.вҖқ She did her finger like this, вҖңThe imam gives four hours, we still have time. Mother, I beg you. When I leave the course, I will go to my uncleвҖҷs,вҖқ she said.

What the mother said on camera was also summarized in Halktv.com.trвҖҷs report titled вҖңIn her first interview, the mother had signalled this to Halk TV!вҖқ [38]. Furthermore, in her statements to the prosecutor and in court, mother YГјksel GГјran also recounted that Narin insisted on going to the Quran course, just as she had said in front of the Halk TV camera on 23 August. In court, she described the conversation she had with her daughter at that moment as follows: вҖңI said, вҖҳMy daughter, donвҖҷt go. It is hot, donвҖҷt, donвҖҷt.вҖҷ I could not persuade her. She persuaded me. She got up and went, I told her: вҖҳAlright, my daughter,вҖҷ she screamed. She was very happy.вҖқ
No other information or testimony emerged proving that the motherвҖҷs statement that вҖңit was Narin herself who wanted to go to the courseвҖқ was untrue. The interview Ferit Demir conducted with YГјksel GГјran on 28 August focused on the issue that вҖңNarin would not get into a strangerвҖҷs carвҖқ [39]; her going to the Quran course was not discussed[38]; therefore, YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs account regarding going to the course was always the same.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: The stones weighed 30, 25 and 20 kilos
text:
The truth: The stones weighed 30, 25 and 20 kilos
In fact, the stones placed on Narin were not that large or extremely heavy. In the footage of his statement to the prosecutor on 10 September, when asked about the size of the stone, Nevzat Bahtiyar opens his hands to both sides to indicate the size, and when the prosecutor asks, вҖңWas it 15вҖ“20 kilos?вҖқ he confirms with a nod.
There was also no mystery in the вҖңthree stonesвҖқ issue. The problem stemmed only from how the statement was taken at the prosecutorвҖҷs office and how the words were recorded in the transcript. Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs words were recorded in the statement transcript as follows: вҖңI put a stone on it so that the sack would not be found by someone. The stone weighed around 15вҖ“20 kilos. There were also stones on each side. I did not put brushwood on it. It was already covered, and there was brushwood next to it.вҖқ
When the footage of the statement is watched, it becomes clear that Nevzat Bahtiyar carried one stone and placed it on Narin, but used the two stones at the sides to conceal it further. Since the brushwood covered it immediately, he did not feel the need to put additional brushwood on top.
However, at the trial, Bahtiyar also statedвҖ”as in his third statement to the prosecutor on 21 SeptemberвҖ”that he вҖңleft only one stoneвҖқ and did not remember the brushwood on top. Yet the GendarmerieвҖҷs Crime Scene Investigation reports do not confirm BahtiyarвҖҷs statement. In the reports reflected in the investigation file, it was stated that there were three large stones and brushwood on the sack containing NarinвҖҷs body found by the stream, and that these stones weighed 30, 25 and 20 kilos.
At the hearing, the prosecutor did not dwell on this discrepancy in Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs statement. After Bahtiyar said he had вҖңleft only one stone,вҖқ the prosecutor continued his questions as follows: вҖңAlright, according to you, Salim GГјran goes to the place where the lifeless body of our daughter Narin was found at 22:44 in the evening by giving some information and from camera footage. You mention one stone or 2вҖ“3 stones, donвҖҷt you? There is extra brushwood left on the lifeless body, could Salim GГјran have done this?вҖқ He replied, вҖңI have no idea, I did not see it.вҖқ
The prosecutor then reminded Salim GГјran of Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs words and asked him, вҖңAre you the one who added the remaining two stones and the brushwood?вҖқ He also denied this by saying, вҖңAbsolutely not.вҖқ
Thus, the issue of the three stones was not fully clarified at trial, but the panel of judges must not have seen it as suspicious, as the number of stones was not emphasized in the reasoned judgment.
However, whether it was one stone or three, it was clear that, as NazlДұ ГҮelik said, вҖңthese stones were not too big for one person to carry.вҖқ It was understood that Nevzat Bahtiyar could carry stones of this weight by himself. After all, he is a plasterer, works on construction sites, and had been able to carry Narin, who weighed approximately 25 kilos, on his own from the car to the stream bank without difficulty.

title: The 12 media mistakes that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murder case
section: The truth: They interrogated the shepherd asking вҖңDid you see Narin?вҖқ and beat him
text:
The truth: They interrogated the shepherd asking вҖңDid you see Narin?вҖқ and beat him
