TГңBД°TAK was unable to recover the deleted camera footage, but the recordings were sent to the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association via a tip, and they submitted them to the court. The footage, which became part of the case file, was also watched in court.
Shepherd Ahmet AkgГјn, who was seen being pushed and shoved in the footage, was heard at the hearing as a witness. Ahmet AkgГјn, a cousin of Nevzat AkgГјn, recounted that Erhan GГјran had asked him, вҖңDid you see anything?вҖқ, and that he replied that he had not seen anything, that he had been with the animals all day anyway. Ahmet AkgГјn said, вҖңI said I had not seen anything. They got angry and beat me, although I was not guilty. Then they apologized.вҖқ
Uncle Erhan GГјran also said, вҖңWe gathered so that if anyone knew anything, they would speak. If it were secret, we would not have gathered under the camera.вҖқ Speaking in line with what Ahmet AkgГјn had said, Erhan GГјran stated that they asked him, вҖңDid you see Narin?вҖқ, that when he said вҖңI did not see anyone,вҖқ they reacted by saying вҖңDo you go home with your eyes closed? You are lying,вҖқ and that the young men took him to the back. Regarding the beating, Erhan GГјran said, вҖңThere was no excessive beating. That is, he may have been slapped once or twice.вҖқ
The course of the trial showed that the gathering in front of Erhan GГјranвҖҷs house was not вҖңsecretвҖқ and that they had gathered not to cover up the murder, but to find information about NarinвҖҷs disappearance. It was confirmed by both partiesвҖҷ statements that shepherd Ahmet AkgГјn had been interrogated by being asked вҖңDid you see Narin?вҖқ and beaten.

title: What if the GГјran family really is innocent?
text:
AUTHOR


Faruk Bildirici


LAST UPDATED


June 4, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 10 min


Word Count


1661
The village of TavЕҹantepe in DiyarbakДұr had turned into a TV studio; instead of reporting concrete findings, some journalists entered a ratings race by conveying rumors and their own judgments. The predominant view in the media, which poisoned and influenced the judicial process, was that mother YГјksel, older brother Enes, and uncle Salim GГјran had committed this murder.
Indeed, the court also ruled in line with the expectations of the public opinion created by the media; family members YГјksel, Enes, and Salim GГјran were sentenced to aggravated life imprisonment for the crime of murder, while Nevzat Bahtiyar was sentenced to 4 years and 6 months in prison for the crime of вҖңdestroying, concealing or altering evidence of a crime.вҖқ
Unfortunately, not only was this decision of the DiyarbakДұr 8th High Criminal Court insufficiently questioned in the media, but journalists also stepped aside with the comfort of having completed their duties. They showed little interest in last weekвҖҷs appellate decision affirming the verdict and passed over it with small news items.
Yet the 1st Criminal Chamber of the Regional Court of Appeal had reached its decision by majority vote, and the president of the panel had attached a detailed dissenting opinion. In most media outlets, including AA, AHaber, AkЕҹam, Cumhuriyet, Halktv, Ekoltv, HГјrriyet, CNNTГјrk, Д°HA, Sabah, SГ¶zcГј, Nefes, TГјrkiye, and Yeni Еһafak, this information appeared only between the lines.
Among the newspapers, only Milliyet printed the presiding judgeвҖҷs dissenting opinion in a separate box. As far as I have seen, only Tele1[2]вҖҷs news site published the full text of the dissent, which was reported in detail by DHA[1] and Serbestiyet.
It is apparent that the President wrote the dissenting opinion, which is longer than the text of the judgment, after carefully examining all the evidence, statements, and reports in the file. The first point that caught my attention in the dissent was his emphasis that, вҖңAccording to the new situation that emerged after the news and debates in social media and TV bulletins,вҖқ there were changes in the defendantsвҖҷ statements. The President had clearly observed in the statements the influence of the media on the trial.
The President lists one by one the legal shortcomings in the investigation and trial, the contradictions between the reports, the ancillary evidentiary nature of the narrowed base station reports, the failure to determine the motive and manner of the murder, and the deficiencies and errors in the conviction.
After examining the dissenting opinion[2] published on Tele1 under the byline of Ersin EroДҹlu, I recalled the concern I had felt during the process that began with the disappearance of Narin GГјran. What if all or some members of the GГјran family are innocent? What if we journalists also negatively influenced the judiciary and led to innocent people being convicted? These questions pained my conscienceвҖҰ
In fact, I had already fallen into the same concern about the courtвҖҷs decision after reading DEM MP and communication scholar Sevilay ГҮelenkвҖҷs article published on Bianet, вҖңWe owe Narin the truth: How did events turn against the GГјran family?вҖқ article and then her interview[3] with GГ¶kГ§er TahincioДҹlu on T24, as well as Esra ArsanвҖҷs piece вҖңFrom earthquake reporting to the murder of Narin GГјran, the exposed state of a journalism: The Ferit Demir legend (!)вҖқ[4] and the interview[5] in which Ferit Demir responds to the criticisms. These texts also contained striking observations that members of the GГјran family might be innocent and about the mediaвҖҷs mistakes.
Now, to the questions raised in those articles have been added the legal objections emphasized in the dissenting opinion in the appellate process. The necessity has arisen to reopen this file, to examine it with journalistic cool-headedness, to pursue the evidence. If even one of those convicted, with our contribution as well, is innocent, this is a huge responsibility, a heavy burden on the conscienceвҖҰ

title: What if the GГјran family really is innocent?
section: Mind-reading based on abstract assumptions
text:
Mind-reading based on abstract assumptions
The President of the 1st Criminal Chamber of the DiyarbakДұr Regional Court of Appeal begins his dissenting opinion by emphasizing the principles of вҖңpresumption of innocenceвҖқ and вҖңin dubio pro reoвҖқ (вҖңthe benefit of the doubt goes to the defendantвҖқ). Most importantly, he concludes regarding the courtвҖҷs conviction decision that вҖңwithout expressing a conclusion based on the evidence in the file, the conclusion and reasoning reached in the form of mind-reading based on abstract assumptions is unlawful.вҖқ Without looking at whether it would be in favor of or against the defendants, he lists, one by one and in careful language, all the legal issues he has identified in the courtвҖҷs decision; moreover, the appellate decision approved by the other two judges does not contain answers to the PresidentвҖҷs objections.
The PresidentвҖҷs 16-page dissenting opinion, which includes the view that the courtвҖҷs decision should be overturned вҖңfor the reasons identified as being unlawful in terms of incomplete examination, legal qualification of the offense, proof, occurrence and acceptance,вҖқ will significantly ease the work of the panel that will examine the file at the Court of Cassation stage. The sections summarized from the dissenting opinion below show that the judge is trying to conduct a cool-headed legal assessment by stepping outside the influence of the media:

It was not taken into consideration that there are contradictions between the narrowed base station report and the farm camera footage, which constitutes conclusive material evidence, and NevzatвҖҷs statements regarding the occurrence.
As it is seen that in both reports (Prof.В Labudde and Ulusal Kriminal) sufficient enhancement could not be achieved and that there are contradictions between the two reports regarding the dark figure resembling Narin, I am of the opinion that it is not possible to render a decision based on these two reports.
It is unlawful to render a conviction against Nevzat for the offense of concealing evidence of a crime, on the basis of the narrowed base station report, which contradicts the occurrence and concrete evidence and needs to be supported by other evidence, and his contradictory statements, while accepting that the act of killing is not proven with respect to him.
Considering the existing state of evidence regarding YГјksel and Enes, it is necessary to assess in what manner their participation in the alleged offense occurred and what the legal classification of the offense should be.
It is not established that there was a prior criminal decision taken concerning the killing of Narin before the incident.
With respect to what the real purpose was for killing Narin, which is more important than the relationship between YГјksel and Salim becoming known, no conclusion has been expressed within the scope of the evidence; the conclusion and reasoning reached in the form of mind-reading based on abstract assumptions is unlawful.
Considering NevzatвҖҷs statements that he received NarinвҖҷs lifeless body from Salim, it is unlawful that the question of whether the act of killing directed at Narin was carried out personally by Salim was not discussed in the reasoned judgment.
It is unlawful that, as a result of incomplete examination and by attributing the quality of conclusive material evidence to the narrowed base station report, which can only be accepted as ancillary evidence and does not allow for judicial review, the acts of YГјksel and Enes were deemed proven and judgment was rendered accordingly.
Apart from NevzatвҖҷs allegation that вҖңSalim GГјran told me вҖҳI killed this girl because she saw that we were together with YГјkselвҖҷвҖқ, no concrete evidence has been presented regarding these claims.
It is unlawful that a decision was rendered without considering, within the scope of concrete material evidence, whether the crime scene was the driverвҖҷs seat of the vehicle used by Salim and whether the act of killing was carried out personally by Salim alone.
It is understood that, during the hearings, attempts were made to obtain the witnessвҖҷs knowledge regarding matters not acquired through the five senses, and that the court panel, counsel, and attorneys acted contrary to the Code of Criminal Procedure (CMK) by asking questions containing commentary that would not contribute to revealing the material truth, without complying with the rules of cross-examination.

title: What if the GГјran family really is innocent?
section: The abuse case in BeylikdГјzГј
text:
The abuse case in BeylikdГјzГј
I do not know whether you remember the news report вҖңAllegation of abuse of a 2-year-old child in BeylikdГјzГјвҖқ; the judicial process regarding the murder of Narin GГјran and, most recently, the appellate decision reminded me of that incident.
That report, published on Bianet about three years ago, was based on a doctorвҖҷs account, a hospital report, and the fact that the BГјyГјkГ§ekmece Chief Public ProsecutorвҖҷs Office had launched an investigation. But later, the Forensic Medicine report did not confirm the allegation of sexual abuse; the investigation was closed.
When examining the report amid the ensuing debates, I had stated that the hospital report could not be disregarded and that, with the data at hand, the вҖңnewsworthiness of the allegationвҖқ arose, but I had also considered it a shortcoming that the family had not been interviewed. I had written that it was not the report but the allegation that had turned out to be wrong.
Later on, I thought a great deal about this assessment. Over time, I came to the conclusion that I had not sufficiently taken into account the pain of the family subjected to such a serious accusation. Until the allegation was clarified, the family members were subjected to an accusation of sexual abuse; they could not even experience the grief of their deceased child. Now, when I think of their situation, my heart aches.
The lesson I have drawn is this: following up on an allegation of sexual abuse вҖ“ especially if it is directed at a child вҖ“ is important, but it is also important to obtain the views of all parties, to use careful language, and to respect the familyвҖҷs grief.
In the murder of Narin GГјran, if even one of the family members is innocent and we, as journalists, have caused that innocent person to be convicted, this would be an unforgivable journalistic offense. We are faced with a duty to find answers to the questions left in the dark, to ensure that the legal problems in the case are resolved, and that the murder is fully clarified in all its aspects.



External References (5)


DHA: The murder of Narin GГјran; the sentences given to 4 defendants upheld вҶ©пёҺ


TELE1: Ersin EroДҹlu: Sentences upheld in the Narin murder: Dissent from the presiding judge вҶ©пёҺ


T24: GГ¶kГ§er TahincioДҹlu: An opposing view on the murder of Narin GГјran from DEM MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: There is no such thing as a вҖҳfamily murder whose motive has not been foundвҖҷ, I expect acquittal at appeal! вҶ©пёҺ


ESRA ARSAN BLOG: From earthquake reporting to the murder of Narin GГјran, the pitiful state of a journalism: The Ferit Demir legend (21 February 2025) вҶ©пёҺ


JOURNO: Semra Pelek - We spoke with Ferit Demir, whose reports on Narin GГјran were criticized: вҖңIt was not good that I wrote the name of the arrested child in fullвҖқ (17 March 2025) вҶ©пёҺ

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
text:
AUTHOR


Cansu ГҮamlДұbel


LAST UPDATED


May 4, 2026


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 37 min


Word Count


6898
Narin GГјran would have been 10 years old today, had she not been killed in August 2024. The file of Narin GГјran вҖ” one of the murders of girl-children that has shaken all of Turkey вҖ” appears in some sense to have been legally concluded with developments last week. Nevzat Bahtiyar, who initially directed suspicion toward the family by claiming that uncle Salim GГјran committed the murder and that he gave him the body to bury, was sentenced in retrial to 17 years in prison for вҖңqualified aiding in the deliberate killing of a child.вҖқ Before this verdict was announced, the documentary вҖңЕһeytantepeвҖқ by 140journos brought the contradictions in the case file back to public attention and reopened the debate. Last week, father Arif GГјran also joined the discussion in an interview with my colleague Д°smail Saymaz, complaining that suspicions of Narin having been sexually abused вҖ” either before or after being killed by Nevzat вҖ” were not investigated.
I wanted to talk about the debate over the court rulings and the errors in the investigation with Sevilay ГҮelenk, DEM Party DiyarbakДұr MP, who has been following the case very closely from day one. After all, Sevilay вҖ” a communications expert and longtime academic вҖ” has insisted from the very start, at the cost of standing against her own party and even the civil-society circles she came from, on the вҖңthe family is innocentвҖқ argument that has only just become popular. In fact, exactly because of this, at one point in DiyarbakДұr people stopped greeting her. Those who reacted against ГҮelenk are people who, like many of us, were quick to take as вҖңtrueвҖқ the rumours about the family. Talking to her, I came to feel вҖ” with an aching heart вҖ” that none of us is free of this collective delusion, and that without realizing it, we have all played some role in the spiralling of these errors.
Having pored over forensic and expert reports, listening to all the defendants in court for hours, following the lawyersвҖҷ work вҖ” Sevilay ГҮelenk is assertive. According to her, every second the three GГјran family members вҖ” sentenced to aggravated life imprisonment on the word of a paedophile like Nevzat Bahtiyar вҖ” spend in prison is a crime against humanity.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThere is no conspiracy in this case; the inexperience and inadequacy of the gendarmerie lie at the root of the investigationвҖҷs errorsвҖқ
text:
вҖңThere is no conspiracy in this case; the inexperience and inadequacy of the gendarmerie lie at the root of the investigationвҖҷs errorsвҖқ
вҖ” As someone who has watched almost every hearing from the very beginning, you arrived very quickly at the point вҖ” only now being discussed by many вҖ” that вҖңmembers of the GГјran family cannot have committed this murder.вҖқ The date you wrote your first article on this was January 2025. The opening of this debate today seems to have been influenced by the recently released вҖңЕһeytantepeвҖқ documentary by 140journos. According to that documentary, Nevzat Bahtiyar was somehow exonerated with the help of the gendarmerie. If this theory is realistic, who is Nevzat Bahtiyar that the gendarmerie would protect and shelter him? Or, through an acquaintance within the gendarmerie, can a villager be favoured for so long in a case under such intense national scrutiny?
Since we are going to talk about the documentary and the gendarmerie, let me recall the claim there. Expert witness Tuncay BeЕҹikГ§i says, вҖңThis is a gendarmerie conspiracy.вҖқ I do not agree with this at all. I also think that calling it вҖңconspiracyвҖқ damages the essence of this matter. Because when you say вҖңconspiracy,вҖқ sensation comes to mind, and a tinge is created that overshadows the gravity of the event. In my view there is no conspiracy at all. The fundamental problem in this case is the inexperience and inadequacy of the gendarmerie. The DiyarbakДұr Gendarmerie has no crime-scene-investigation knowledge or experience. Once journalists, politicians, rights organizationsвҖҰ everyone fixed their attention there, in my view the gendarmerie even forgot what it knew. It failed to do many things it was expected to do вҖ” or could have done in a calm environment. And of course, from the second day of the incident, the phrase вҖңThese things happen out thereвҖқ began circulating everywhere. By this they were implying, вҖңOut there, children can be killed by family for revenge.вҖқ

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңI think they understood the truth the day they caught Nevzat, but they could not turn back from their mistakeвҖқ
text:
вҖңI think they understood the truth the day they caught Nevzat, but they could not turn back from their mistakeвҖқ
вҖ” With this phrase, an implicit reference was constantly being made to intra-family sexual relations or incest. The thesis that Narin had seen a relationship between her mother and her uncle вҖ” that is, вҖңsaw something she should not have seenвҖқ вҖ” and was therefore killed by the perpetrators of the relationship, suddenly became very popular.
Yes, at first the mother and uncle were named. When that didnвҖҷt stick, theories about the older brother were produced. Yet Enes was nowhere in the story at the start. This was put forward through the so-called вҖңnarrowed baseвҖқ study. This time the thesis that Enes might have done вҖңsomethingвҖқ to Narin was processed for months. And these people were tortured and ill-treated in custody. The gendarmerie made so many mistakesвҖҰ They got these people to sign untruthful, incorrect statement transcripts. Because under the pressure they were under, they didnвҖҷt know what to do. And now it appears that, in fact, Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs circle continually directed the investigation toward the family. There were fake Facebook messages and so on. As a result, even the familyвҖҷs most distant relatives were taken into custody and questioned, while the closest place to the point where the child disappeared вҖ” NevzatвҖҷs house вҖ” wasnвҖҷt visited even once. By the 19th day, when Nevzat was caught, the investigation had advanced from such a wrong starting point that they could no longer say вҖңNot the family, but Nevzat.вҖқ Because all the authoritative voices of the state вҖ” from Ali Yerlikaya to YДұlmaz TunГ§ and Mahinur GГ¶ktaЕҹ вҖ” had made statements in this vein. They could not later come out and say вҖңIt was Nevzat after all.вҖқ Yet I believe they understood it the day they caught Nevzat.
вҖ” If those running the investigation on the state side actually understood the day Nevzat was caught that he was the one who killed Narin, why did the trial not proceed in that direction?
Expectations had been raised so far in another direction that there was no turning back.





Nevzat Bahtiyar

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңEveryone made the same mistake вҖ” every segment of societyвҖқ
text:
вҖңEveryone made the same mistake вҖ” every segment of societyвҖқ
вҖ” Yet this is a country that has seen countless cases of women and small girls killed after being sexually abused. So why was this possibility pushed to the back, and the thesis of dark intra-family relationships given so much credit?
Here it is not just one segment that made the mistake, everyone did. For instance, before coming to meet you, I re-read your interview at the time with the experienced lawyer Eren Keskin. While only 25 days had passed since the incident, with the family not yet having stood trial, Eren Keskin too openly and gravely accuses the family, saying, вҖңThey will pin it on the older brother or on Nevzat Bahtiyar, theyвҖҷll save the family.вҖқ This was the general conviction in society, and because it was such, the gendarmerie, while interrogating Nevzat, said, вҖңTake it easy. All of Turkey is behind you. Speak comfortably.вҖқ This interrogation footage is in the documentary. Such an interrogation cannot be!
But while saying this, I do not think вҖ” as some do вҖ” that вҖңNevzat was protected for some other reason.вҖқ From what I see, the gendarmerieвҖҷs stance was not because Nevzat had a value for someone. They could not turn back from the mistakes they had made from the very beginning. They were so unable to turn back that they all became entangled together in this lie.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңGalip EnsarioДҹluвҖҷs statement was very wrong; he doesnвҖҷt really know the family, he heard the rumours like everyone else and believed them вҖ” the cost of what he did was very heavyвҖқ
text:
вҖңGalip EnsarioДҹluвҖҷs statement was very wrong; he doesnвҖҷt really know the family, he heard the rumours like everyone else and believed them вҖ” the cost of what he did was very heavyвҖқ
вҖ” DonвҖҷt the practices weвҖҷve been observing for decades lie at the root of the assumption or prejudice that the state protects perpetrators? The recent revelations in the GГјlistan Doku case are clear examples. Indeed, wasnвҖҷt the impression that the GГјran family was being protected formed by an AKP politician, Galip EnsarioДҹlu, coming out in the early days of the incident and saying, вҖңWe know almost all members of the family. We have a close friendship. There are things we sometimes donвҖҷt know, sometimes know and shouldnвҖҷt sayвҖқ?
I never spoke with him personally. In fact I tried to talk to everyone. From the AKP I think I met with about 40 people, all very long meetings. But I did not speak with Galip EnsarioДҹlu because what he did was very wrong. I think when he said that sentence, in this climate of social polarization, he could not foresee that the AKPвҖҷs bad track record in cases of femicide and child murder would be charged to the family, creating a horrifying public outrage. After all, at that time I, too, was hearing about the relationship between the uncle and the mother. But these are not things we as MPs can definitively establish. Also, how well EnsarioДҹlu knows that village is debatable. The reason he said вҖңWe know the family wellвҖқ is voting tendencies. The AKP can produce two or three MPs from DiyarbakДұr. The villages voting for the AKP have been clear since the days of Milli Selamet. The reason he said вҖңWeвҖҷve known them for forty yearsвҖқ is just that, no more. So thereвҖҷs no great friendship. He had clearly been convinced by the rumours like everyone elseвҖҰ Yet had he really known the family well, he should not have been so easily convinced that this child was killed within the family. But he heard the rumours, believed them and made a statement on top of them. The cost of this was very heavy. At one point during the process I thought he might come out and explain this. He did not. Because clearly, he had received a serious lynching.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңToday, in the GГјlistan Doku case, a decision is being made about who is expendableвҖқ
text:
вҖңToday, in the GГјlistan Doku case, a decision is being made about who is expendableвҖқ
вҖ” Setting aside what EnsarioДҹlu believed or said, I want to come back to societyвҖҷs prior assumption that the state will once again protect somebody and the crime will go unpunished. Do you think it is precisely because of this awareness вҖ” and to repair this вҖ” that todayвҖҷs Justice Minister AkДұn GГјrlek took up the GГјlistan Doku case? When and why does the state stop protecting those it once protected?
A decision is being made about who is expendable. I never built long sentences on the GГјlistan Doku file because I do not speak about things I do not know. I just take a principled stance against femicide. Beyond that I donвҖҷt get involved. But the visible thing is this: for five years not even her body has been found. And we are not at all surprised when we hear, when we see this вҖ” because, yes, they have always been protected. Because we know this too, we projected all our existing consciousness onto the Narin GГјran file.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThe government saw a 200-vote village in TavЕҹantepe that they could expend, and stepped backвҖқ
text:
вҖңThe government saw a 200-vote village in TavЕҹantepe that they could expend, and stepped backвҖқ
вҖ” In one of your articles you summarized this collective state of mind very effectively: вҖңWe tried to take revenge for the years wasted by the AKP, but this was an impossible revenge. The phrase вҖҳThere are things we know and cannot sayвҖҷ locked our suspicions onto this. And we tried to settle scores with the AKP through this. But in the end, the AKP government was again the one who slipped out of this affair.вҖқ
ThatвҖҷs how it happened, unfortunately. And that situation left us with the feeling that real reckoning was happening вҖ” as if we had won. Yet that was not the truth. They saw a 200-vote village they could easily expend there, and stepped back.





Father Arif GГјran

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңFather Arif GГјran asked me, вҖҳYour party is in power here, will you not have a policy against this oppression?вҖҷ вҖ” I cannot forget thatвҖқ
text:
вҖңFather Arif GГјran asked me, вҖҳYour party is in power here, will you not have a policy against this oppression?вҖҷ вҖ” I cannot forget thatвҖқ
вҖ” I never quite understood that part either. Was a portion of the GГјran family voting for HГңDA-PAR, for example? Or how can anyone be sure they all collectively voted for the AKP?
According to the vote rates announced by the Supreme Election Board, in TavЕҹantepe the majority of votes go to the AKP. But 20вҖ“30 votes from the village always go to the DEM Party. For instance, the mother YГјksel GГјran is known as a DEM Party supporter вҖ” the daughter of a family that migrated from a forcibly evacuated village in Derik. On the other hand, father Arif GГјran, against allegations of close ties with Hizbullah, says, вҖңIn our village only one person prays вҖ” that is my mother-in-law. And she is a DEM Party supporter.вҖқ
Looking at Arif GГјranвҖҷs statements, he is in fact expressing his expectations from those in power rather than from a particular political party. To explain what he means by вҖңthose in power,вҖқ IвҖҷd like to share a dialogue we had. Months ago when I went to the village, they had me conduct an inspection. He was there too, as were the lawyers. He turned to me and said, вҖңHonourable MP, you are putting in tremendous effort. If it werenвҖҷt for you, we wouldnвҖҷt be sitting here keeping this justice vigil. They wouldnвҖҷt allow it. Your party is in power here.вҖқ These words really moved me, I cannot forget them. вҖңHere it is the DEM Party that produces 8 MPs. Here all the municipalities belong to the DEM Party. Here those in power are the DEM Party. Will you not have a policy against this oppression?вҖқ he asked. Whether or not he is a DEM Party supporter, he had hoped that the DEM PartyвҖҷs understanding of justice and its stance in the womenвҖҷs struggle would help him.
вҖ” And Arif GГјran had great expectations of the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association.
Yes, absolutely. We are talking about a village and a family that has experienced such a horrifying targeting. And this man comes out and says, вҖңOur Bar knows us. They will not look at us through the eyes of the others. We thought that while everything else was demonising us, they would not. But there too, they did this to us.вҖқ When you put all of Father Arif GГјranвҖҷs observations side by side, you see вҖ” there is a very wise side to the man. What he has lived through over a year and a half has, I think, made him a different person.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңDEM Party fell into the same clichГ©s as everyone else, but everyone was there; charging the errors in the file to our party is ideological вҖ” opposition in safe watersвҖқ
text:
вҖңDEM Party fell into the same clichГ©s as everyone else, but everyone was there; charging the errors in the file to our party is ideological вҖ” opposition in safe watersвҖқ
вҖ” Behind Arif GГјranвҖҷs disappointment with the DiyarbakДұr Bar lies the fact that the media, society вҖ” a bit of all of us вҖ” were too quick to form opinions about the family, and that they too fell into this. DidnвҖҷt your party, the DEM Party, in the end, fall into the same clichГ©s?
It did.
вҖ” So the DEM Party, which produces 8 MPs from DiyarbakДұr and claims to know the people and the region better than anyone else, also could not really set out a different line from other political parties or the average view. Why?
I figured out the reason for this only quite recently. In answering this question, I want to return again to your interview with Eren Keskin. You had asked a very good question: вҖңHas the womenвҖҷs movement been able to overcome political polarizations, the polarizations in society?вҖқ Unfortunately the womenвҖҷs movement has not overcome polarizations. But on the other hand I should also say: at the point we have now reached, I find it extremely ideological to charge the errors in the Narin file to the DEM Party. That is what Д°smail Saymaz does in the documentary too. Charging something to the DEM Party, putting it in the crosshairs, is an easy way out. As a party we did fall into clichГ©s, but everyone fell into the same clichГ©s together. These criticisms directed at us, without taking on those in power, are a form of opposition in safe waters. The DEM Party is seen as expendable. The documentary too commits this injustice. The only politician seen in the documentary is our co-chair TГјlay HatimoДҹullarДұ. This is a great injustice.
In bringing this criticism, I do not want to harm the documentaryвҖҷs function in the rights struggle. I want 5 million people to watch it. If the wind in public opinion is going to change, we will set aside the injustice done to us. But it is unjust to act as if the DEM Party was the first to come out and speak on this. Together with my advisor we did a chronological archive search. The first politician to bring it onto the political agenda was Гңmit Г–zdaДҹ. вҖңNarin died as a result of your decadent, pig-tie-wedding-producing feudal culture,вҖқ he said. He was making a Hizbullah allusion through the вҖңpig tieвҖқ reference. Г–zgГјr Г–zel in his statement on 9 September emphasized вҖңfeudal relationship.вҖқ On 10 September, then Interior Minister Ali Yerlikaya, when reminded by journalists that вҖңYou did not offer condolences to the family,вҖқ said, вҖңIt is quite open, there is not much more to say. It is a situation that everyone, when they read it, understands and feels. Is there a need to explain it again? I hope we never face such a tableau in which we are ashamed of our humanity.вҖқ Yet injustice is being done to TГјlay HatimoДҹullarДұ, who made the first statement days after all these statements, on 16 September.
вҖ” But the impression that the DEM Party circles were among those who fanned the Hizbullah-connection allegation is not entirely groundless either, surelyвҖҰ
Yes, in our party in the early days too вҖ” unfortunately вҖ” there are grave statements from one or two hasty MPs. Because this is being poured everywhere. Not everyone has our experience. Younger people can be more superficial. So yes, there are a few statements that were made.
вҖ” And itвҖҷs impossible not to notice the sentence Ms.В TГјlay made by going to TavЕҹantepe at the time: вҖңWeapon depots etcвҖҰ The residents of certain villages in the region that have been turned into the bases of certain political parties should know that these weapons turn around and shoot the villager themselves, these weapons turn around and make the people kill each other.вҖқ
Our people had perhaps something like the following reflex: when the area suddenly began to be othered with arguments such as incest and child-killing, they perceived this as something directed at the entire Kurdish society and reacted. IвҖҷm not saying this because I justify them вҖ” IвҖҷm trying to analyze why those reactions were given in the first place.





DEM Party DiyarbakДұr MP Sevilay ГҮelenk

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңBecause, contrary to everyone, I said вҖҳThe family cannot be guilty,вҖҷ I experienced a kind of social exclusion in DiyarbakДұr, I was left alone, greetings were cut offвҖқ
text:
вҖңBecause, contrary to everyone, I said вҖҳThe family cannot be guilty,вҖҷ I experienced a kind of social exclusion in DiyarbakДұr, I was left alone, greetings were cut offвҖқ
вҖ” Ultimately, a party that wages a struggle for the rights of the Kurdish people also made statements that served the othering of a Kurdish family. So Arif GГјran told you too, that this was not the stance expected of the DEM Party.
Of course it isnвҖҷt expected. This is very grave and very wrong. But the unseen thing is this: everyone was there. From the CHP to the Yeni Yol Group, everyone spoke, everyone said something. At the first hearing there were MPs from every party in the courtroom, and they all sat next to one another. But there is a DEM Party accusation in the documentary as if they were not in the story at all. The CHP isnвҖҷt in the documentary at all, for example. I may have my own grievances. I really had a hard time during this process. I experienced a kind of exclusion in DiyarbakДұr.
вҖ” Why?
Because Sevilay ГҮelenk came out on 30 January 2025 and suddenly said something that ran contrary to everyone. She opposed what the entire country was saying. Now some are racing to say вҖңWe said it too,вҖқ but at that time no one said that the GГјran family could not have committed this murder in this way. After that a few articles came out properly examining the contradictions: YДұldДұray OДҹurвҖҷs article, Ali Duran TopuzвҖҷs article. Then the courtвҖҷs reasoned judgment came out and the discussion was cut as if with a knife. None of those who today say вҖңWe said itвҖқ have the courage to say вҖңthis verdict is debatableвҖқ on those television programmes. They say two sentences and move on. I was left alone. Not just DEM Party circles but also womenвҖҷs circles, legal circlesвҖҰ you understand it from the cessation of greetings when you go somewhere вҖ” you face a great reaction. It is very hard to operate in such an environment.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThose with different views in the party always listened to me, but I am sad that we couldnвҖҷt bring it to the point of forming a legal commissionвҖқ
text:
вҖңThose with different views in the party always listened to me, but I am sad that we couldnвҖҷt bring it to the point of forming a legal commissionвҖқ
вҖ” You have long been in the media, in academia, in civil society but you are relatively new to politics. How much did you struggle inside the DEM Party?
There were times I struggled, but I can also say with an open heart that they always said, вҖңHocam, letвҖҷs talk about this,вҖқ and we talked. We held three separate long conversations and each ended as one in which I had left very serious question marks in their minds. After the verdict, on 28 December, I called the party from the courtroom. I called the two relevant commissions. I called our WomenвҖҷs Council and ChildrenвҖҷs Council co-spokespersons, вҖңDonвҖҷt make any big statements. I have been watching here for three days. Something very strange happened here. LetвҖҷs wait for the reasoned judgment. LetвҖҷs not say anything that will put the Party in a difficult position around it. Let us issue a cautious statement,вҖқ I said. In fact, in the courtroom I had a tense exchange with someone from my own party. Because they had been horrified at my words on the family possibly not being guilty. I left the courtroom and went straight to my motherвҖҷs house just opposite the courthouse. Although I was authorized and assigned to follow the trial, I did not say a single word to the media. My party also did what I had recommended вҖ” issued no statement to the effect that the family was guilty. Since that day, attention has continued to be paid to this in the party. But is anyone looking at what the justice ministers, the interior ministers said?
In fact the Deputy Gendarmerie Commander came to the Parliament and gave information, saying things like вҖңThe Narin case has been solved in the most perfect way. From now on it is just gossip.вҖқ Ultimately, when everyone in the DEM Party was in the mode of вҖңClose this file,вҖқ the party would not have allowed an MP who continued to scrutinize the file вҖ” even without being convinced by what I was saying вҖ” to keep up this argument. There could have been a parting of ways. I am someone who, with the group leader in front of me and MPs behind me, shouted at Vice President Cevdet YДұlmaz in Parliament, вҖңYou are making a historic mistake,вҖқ and put my question to him. Then I made an appointment and met with him. So I waged this struggle everywhere, including in Parliament. Inside the party as wellвҖҰ Then GergerlioДҹlu joined too. In the end many people inside told me, вҖңWhat a great job you are doing.вҖқ One day someone said, вҖңThe GГјran family may not have done it, but we are not as familiar with the file as you are.вҖқ Well then, you should have set up a legal commission, and your MPs in that commission would all have looked at the file together. Our party has so many lawyer-MPs. I could not bring it to that point. I am very sorry about that.





Mother YГјksel GГјran

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңIn the intensity of the peace process, no one realized this was the judicial scandal of the centuryвҖқ
text:
вҖңIn the intensity of the peace process, no one realized this was the judicial scandal of the centuryвҖқ
вҖ” At the point we have come to, despite your effort, does the DEM Party seem to have managed to stand somewhere against the widespread conviction concerning the family?
It did not. Unfortunately, we are not in a position to say вҖңnoвҖқ to that. But to say вҖңthe DEM Party shaped thisвҖқ is something else entirely. After all there is a party dealing with many matters. And there is also a peace process that started right after the period of the murder. So a period that coincides with that. In that intensity, they could not realize that this case is вҖ” as I have called it вҖ” вҖңthe judicial scandal of the century.вҖқ

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThey spat into the motherвҖҷs mouth during interrogationвҖқ
text:
вҖңThey spat into the motherвҖҷs mouth during interrogationвҖқ
вҖ” Why do you say вҖңthe judicial scandal of the centuryвҖқ? IsnвҖҷt our recent history full of many other contentious, problematic cases?
An eight-year-old child is murdered. The whole country, with its law enforcement, its judiciary, its media, locks onto that place. And from day one, everyone treats the family вҖ” who lost their only daughter вҖ” as вҖңkillers.вҖқ On the day the childвҖҷs body is found, the mother is tortured. On the day they find their childвҖҷs lifeless body, in interrogation, they open her mouth and spit into it. She herself says this. My heart aches as I repeat this sentence.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңOn a paedophileвҖҷs word, three members of a family received aggravated life sentencesвҖқ
text:
вҖңOn a paedophileвҖҷs word, three members of a family received aggravated life sentencesвҖқ
вҖ” Are they doing this during the gendarmerie interrogation?
I no longer know that level of detail but probably yes. Interrogation is something that happens in police custody. Her son is tortured and then the footage is shown to the mother. They are trying to get 18-year-old Enes to say вҖңI did it.вҖқ The child loses his mind there. An 18-year-old. These are people who lost a member of their family. Even just watching the birthday video they prepared for Narin makes you understand: that little child was being raised like a princess in that home. The only girl. This is the judicial scandal of the century, because the highly likely perpetrator вҖ” a sociopath, a paedophile вҖ” is slipping out of this as easily as drawing a hair from butter. With the sentences he has uttered, three members of a family are receiving aggravated life sentences.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThe family members spoke for hours in court вҖ” no one listened to themвҖқ
text:
вҖңThe family members spoke for hours in court вҖ” no one listened to themвҖқ
вҖ” And he is doing this without speaking very much, actually.
Yes, in court he uttered only four sentences. People who lie speak little like this. As little as Nevzat Bahtiyar speaks, the mother and uncle speak that much. And without ever stoppingвҖҰ The person I looked at most suspiciously was the uncle, but you look at his body language, you look at what he says вҖ” thereвҖҷs no air of someone trying to hide something. ThereвҖҷs no anxiety like вҖңWhat if I let something slip and it turns more against me?вҖқ My dear Enes, under that horrific trauma, speaks for two hours. The mother, in her broken Turkish, says вҖңWe were a happy family.вҖқ They tell and tell, but no one listens to them. It struck me as utterly horrifying.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThe family was unable to convey its case вҖ” there is a language barrier, an Eastern reticenceвҖқ
text:
вҖңThe family was unable to convey its case вҖ” there is a language barrier, an Eastern reticenceвҖқ
вҖ” While searching for an answer to the question вҖңWhy couldnвҖҷt this family explain themselves, make their voices heard?вҖқ, you mentioned the language barrier in your articles. Mother YГјksel GГјran expresses herself in Kurdish, her Turkish is very weak. But isnвҖҷt there also a cultural barrier or jargon factor? Even when they speak Turkish, words that mean one thing for us can mean something else for them, right? Their vocabulary seemed to fall short of conveying their case.
You expressed it very well. They fell very short in conveying their grievances. Actually some of the men interestingly express themselves well. Father Arif GГјran, for example, expresses himself well, but uncle Salim cannot. There is the Eastern reticence you point to. Yet the listeners want from them an emotional outburst right away. The mother, for example, could never get her statements approved by Turkey. The woman has never seen a camera in her life and suddenly she sees herself in dozens of live broadcasts. I watched her very carefully in court. She does not have the psychology of someone guilty. In court she once said, вҖңToday they told me вҖҳMy condolences,вҖҷ and just imagine, I was happy even about that.вҖқ How heavy a state of mind this is, people donвҖҷt understand.
The sentences she uses are different too. For example she says, вҖңMaybe the child came homeвҖҰвҖқ She wants to say, вҖңThe child may have come home but I donвҖҷt know,вҖқ but instead she says, вҖңMaybe the child came homeвҖҰвҖқ When that is taken and turned into a headline, itвҖҷs a horrible thing of course.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңI, too, believe that Narin was abusedвҖқ
text:
вҖңI, too, believe that Narin was abusedвҖқ
вҖ” Last week, in his interview with Д°smail Saymaz, father Arif GГјran for the first time openly stated his belief that Nevzat Bahtiyar sexually abused his daughter. To what extent was this issue scrutinized in court?
The GГјran familyвҖҷs lawyers said this in court. They said вҖңPSA was not examined.вҖқ It wasnвҖҷt a revelation to me; I reached this conclusion because I followed the trial, read all the reports and joined the on-site inspections. The lawyers were really very good. I, too, think Nevzat sexually abused the child. But everything happened in 18 minutes. I think the child quickly woke up to the fact that something was about to happen to her. At first, because she knew Nevzat Bahtiyar, when he called her she went to him, but then she immediately woke up to the fact that something was off. When Nevzat panicked, I think he killed her right away. So I do not think he threw her into the barn and killed her after abusing her there. I could be wrong, of course. ItвҖҷs very upsetting to talk about these things anyway.
вҖ” Even if he killed first and abused after, that would say something else about the killerвҖҷs personality, presumablyвҖҰ
I have said a few times that this could have been the case. After the incident, his car stands there for 39 minutes for no reason. There can be two reasons. The first is what you said; the second is that maybe he tried to dismember the body. Indeed one of the childвҖҷs legs is severed. He probably wasnвҖҷt sitting there meditating for 39 minutes. In the forensic report there is no penetration of the child but there is PSA fluid in the underwear region. This cannot be explained.
вҖ” Is that why Arif GГјran says, вҖңThey turned my daughter into a woman!вҖқ вҖ” or for another reason?
He says, вҖңThey are saying вҖҳPSA cannot be in a child, only in adult women or men.вҖҷ That is, they made these statements as though my daughter were an adult woman.вҖқ He says it to react to the comments. He doesnвҖҷt use it in the sense of вҖңthey raped her.вҖқ But ultimately the family circle believes in the abuse. I heard this in a very wide context in the area.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThe process is at a historical crossroads, the partyвҖҷs energy is locked thereвҖқ
text:
вҖңThe process is at a historical crossroads, the partyвҖҷs energy is locked thereвҖқ
вҖ” Just now, while answering criticisms directed at the DEM Party through the Narin file, you made a statement that stuck with me. You said, вҖңOur party has to deal with very different issues. And then thereвҖҷs the heavy workload of the peace process in this period.вҖқ WouldnвҖҷt this be perceived as feeding the perception that вҖңTheir only agenda is the Kurdish questionвҖқ?
First of all, for our party, the resolution of the Kurdish question really is a very fundamental matter. Very fundamental and very urgent. The economic, sociological dimensions of the Kurdish question, intertwined with the womenвҖҷs question, are well known. But the fact that this is so does not mean that the DEM Party does not work in other areas. Look at the recent minersвҖҷ protests, the workersвҖҷ protestsвҖҰ one of our MPs spent the night with them. As for animal rights, we are there too; with earthquake survivors we set up separate solidarity. We are in every kind of struggle and everywhere. But this does not mean that we can do everything as fully as possible. We are 57 people, after all. The process is also at a very historical crossroads, so a great deal of energy is locked there. But on the other side too, we really do more than what we can.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThe decision to lay down arms is not something that can be reversedвҖқ
text:
вҖңThe decision to lay down arms is not something that can be reversedвҖқ
вҖ” You said, вҖңThe process is at a historical crossroads,вҖқ but according to Murat KarayДұlan, вҖңthe process has been frozen.вҖқ
Actually he is making a situational assessment. After all, the ruling bloc takes one step forward and two steps back, doesnвҖҷt do anything significant about the expected legal framework, while the other side is expected to say вҖңWe have laid down armsвҖқ вҖ” or to do what is required. While doing this, the state side is watching the developments in Syria and Iran. Given this, for the other side to say вҖңwe have laid down armsвҖқ may no longer look strategically correct. In my view the process is actually not in the freezer, but I read KarayДұlanвҖҷs words as вҖңIf this continues, the conditions for laying down arms will also disappear.вҖқ That is, he is hinting at this. Of course, what is meant should be explained by the organization itself. Because my personal feeling is that the decision to lay down arms is not reversible. That was a very big, very historical step that cannot be reversed. Everyone sees this.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңThe organization itself can explain what it means, but in my view we should not arrive at the point of вҖҳWe didnвҖҷt actually lay down armsвҖҷвҖқ
text:
вҖңThe organization itself can explain what it means, but in my view we should not arrive at the point of вҖҳWe didnвҖҷt actually lay down armsвҖҷвҖқ
вҖ” But didnвҖҷt Murat KarayДұlan, with this latest statement, in some way challenge the expectation that they would lay down arms?
In some sense it can be called a challenge. Essentially itвҖҷs a situational assessment. The Kurdish society which has had such a bad experience is in the middle of a brand-new conjuncture beginning with the Iran war. If in the current environment none of the expected steps are being taken, I think he is trying to say that they are aware that the state too is reconsidering the situation. Most likely they themselves will explain more. I think it should be so. If you ask my opinion, we should not arrive at the point of вҖңWe didnвҖҷt actually lay down arms.вҖқ That is my view.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңГ–calan has no opportunity to produce thought on a file like Narin other than from what he hears within four walls from the screensвҖқ
text: вҖңГ–calan has no opportunity to produce thought on a file like Narin other than from what he hears within four walls from the screensвҖқ

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңWhen I heard what he said, I thought, вҖҳI wish accurate information had been provided to him,вҖҷ I spoke with SДұrrДұ SГјreyya Г–nderвҖқ
text:
вҖңWhen I heard what he said, I thought, вҖҳI wish accurate information had been provided to him,вҖҷ I spoke with SДұrrДұ SГјreyya Г–nderвҖқ
вҖ” One of those who made statements emphasizing the вҖңfeudal orderвҖқ on Narin was Abdullah Г–calan. In a letter he sent to the PKK congress last May, he claimed the GГјran family descended from a mullah family that participated in the conquest of Istanbul. He said, вҖңThat regrettable incident in TavЕҹantepe is connected to the tribe; through the agency of that tribe, that terrible element of rape inside has been expressed as a massacre against a tiny girl-child unparalleled. It is a symbolic incident but its meaning is very striking. This is the expression of a culture.вҖқ So he too in some way joined the tribe / feudal order critique.
There is the following situation: someone who has been imprisoned for 27 years follows what is happening only through statements on the television channels he can watch. Journalists say one thing, rights organizations say something similar вҖ” even the DiyarbakДұr Bar says the same thing. Everyone in unison says, вҖңThe family is guilty.вҖқ Г–calan has no opportunity to produce thought other than from what he hears within four walls. When I heard him use the expressions you reminded me of, I thought, вҖңI wish accurate information had been provided to him.вҖқ Yesterday, on the first anniversary of his death, I spoke with SДұrrДұ SГјreyya Г–nder about this matter. вҖңThe information that the family may not have killed Narin needs to reach there,вҖқ I said. SДұrrДұ already knew that I was closely interested in the topic, he had read my articles. He told me, вҖңHocam, I am absolutely behind you to the end. I will explain this matter too if necessary.вҖқ With the speed of a person who feels real interest in stories, he understood what I told him. Not everyone had understood as fast as SДұrrДұ. But Г–calan thought what came from a limited information source вҖ” but was conveyed by different circles in the same way вҖ” was correct. Of course no one is fully free of prejudices. He is not either. ThatвҖҷs how it is.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңГ–calan is someone who has experienced none of the phases of the transformation in IT and social mediaвҖқ
text: вҖңГ–calan is someone who has experienced none of the phases of the transformation in IT and social mediaвҖқ

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңAmong the things he says, those giving the feeling вҖҳThis is not quite soвҖҷ will naturally occurвҖқ
text:
вҖңAmong the things he says, those giving the feeling вҖҳThis is not quite soвҖҷ will naturally occurвҖқ
вҖ” What you describe is, in a sense, an anecdote specific to the Narin file, but couldnвҖҷt it also be interpreted as an example fitting the question marks about how much and how Г–calan can evaluate what is happening outside?
We are talking about so many years that have passed under heavy isolation. He is someone who can meet with a limited circle of people. It is unclear whether he can read whatever he wants. Yet still he reads a lot, and on certain matters he produces a more realistic Middle East analysis than many of the actors on todayвҖҷs political stage. For instance, on the likely course of the Iran war he makes a very good analysis. But in terms of grasping the sociology and psychology of society, seeing its tendencies and analyzing this multilayered thing, 27 years of isolation is something very serious. He has not experienced any phase of the transformation in information technologies. He has not experienced social media or the new media. So in the end the practice of getting information in prison is what he has. So naturally, of course, among the things he says to us, those giving the feeling вҖңThis isnвҖҷt quite soвҖқ will occur. Yet still we see someone who handles macro issues with a very serious organizational genius.

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңWhy does the family receive life imprisonment while Nevzat gets 17 years?вҖқ
text: вҖңWhy does the family receive life imprisonment while Nevzat gets 17 years?вҖқ

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңEvery second YГјksel, Enes, and Salim GГјran spend in prison is a crime against humanity in which most of us are complicitвҖқ
text:
вҖңEvery second YГјksel, Enes, and Salim GГјran spend in prison is a crime against humanity in which most of us are complicitвҖқ
вҖ” LetвҖҷs wrap up by recalling the latest situation in the Narin GГјran case. At the hearing on 16 April at the DiyarbakДұr 8th High Criminal Court, Nevzat Bahtiyar was sentenced to 17 years in prison without discretionary reduction for вҖңqualified aiding in the deliberate killing of a child.вҖқ In the announced reasoned decision it says, вҖңIt has been concluded that the defendant participated in the act of killing the victim by being beside defendant Salim, by reinforcing the decision to commit the offense before and during the act, and by aiding after the commission of the act, in the capacity of one who aids the killing; thus, in line with the Court of CassationвҖҷs ruling, a conviction was rendered against the defendant for the offense of qualified aiding in the deliberate killing.вҖқ Why, in your view, is this decision flawed?
If Nevzat is standing next to Salim like this, if he is reinforcing the killing motivation, what becomes of the role of the mother and the brother? Why do they receive life imprisonment while Nevzat Bahtiyar gets 17 years? Everything is becoming so absurd! The Court of Cassation, with this retrial, is making a completely different interpretation from the court of first instance вҖ” and the Court of Cassation has no right to make such an interpretation outside of its review of legal compliance. There is no new situation that would form the basis for this either.
Every second that YГјksel GГјran, Enes GГјran, and Salim GГјran spend in prison is a crime against humanity in which most of us are complicit. None of them should remain in prison even for a second. As guilty parties to a murder they had no part in, and exposed to terrible slanders, they are in their cells gazing at the ceiling. I really cannot think of anything more grave.
As one last thing, IвҖҷd also like to say this: another misfortune of this family is that, because some of the familyвҖҷs members were called HГңDA-PAR or JД°TEM, the people I had hoped would at some point take up the file вҖ” names from rights organizations, the womenвҖҷs movement, the legal community вҖ” kept their distance. I know how those who put this information into circulation reached people, because they reached me too. I donвҖҷt want to talk about that part. Everyone investigating this matter was reached and people were tried to be stopped with phrases like вҖңYou donвҖҷt know these things.вҖқ

title: DEM Party MP Sevilay ГҮelenk: On a paedophileвҖҷs word, three GГјran family members got heavy prison sentences
section: вҖңAnother misfortune of the family was that the information that they are вҖҳJД°TEM membersвҖҷ was put into circulation by someoneвҖқ
text:
вҖңAnother misfortune of the family was that the information that they are вҖҳJД°TEM membersвҖҷ was put into circulation by someoneвҖқ
вҖ” Could you explain exactly what you mean?
Because they reached me too, with a journalistic instinct I did some research. The GГјran family has another branch вҖ” children of the maternal aunt, so the surnames are different. The information that one of these people, whose name was associated with YeЕҹil, was a former PKK confessant entered circulation. In a region where for 40 years a kind of state of war has continued, whichever family you scratch, on one side you may find a PKK confessant, a PKK member, or a JД°TEM operative.
