title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
text:
AUTHOR


Ali Duran Topuz


LAST UPDATED


October 27, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 18 min


Word Count


3397
In todayвҖҷs section there is no theory, no wordiness, there are вҖңincidents.вҖқ
Perhaps the most critical day in the Narin case was 31 August. On that day, uncle Salim GГјran, who was also the headman of the village, was taken into custody; the news reported that вҖңsuspicious DNA,вҖқ or DNA belonging to Narin, had been found in his vehicle. It was an extremely critical day, especially because of a post that a Facebook user named Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca made that same day. That post would set the direction of both the media and social media and, it seems, of the case file itself. A month later it turned out to be fake, and afterward almost no one cared about it anymore, but it had two important features: First, it used some information that at that time was not known to the public but was known to those conducting the investigation (and to the lawyers, journalists, and politicians in contact with them); second, it laid the foundation for the вҖңthe family is terribly guiltyвҖқ theory that would survive all the way to the judgment stage.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
section: The FatherвҖҷs Complaint: DonвҖҷt Hurt Us on Social Media
text:
The FatherвҖҷs Complaint: DonвҖҷt Hurt Us on Social Media
Before getting to that critical day and that critical post, we need a few more notes in order to understand the atmosphere between 27 and 31 August 2024; some of these may sound repetitive, but their importance will become clearer as we proceed. That day, Arif GГјran (the father) would say he would file a complaint[1] about humiliating news and posts:

вҖңThey call in the headman (that is, Salim GГјran) for a statement, they call me in, they call her mother in. Why this slander? If my son is guilty, if I am guilty, thatвҖҷs something for the state to know. WeвҖҷre waiting. They said the same about my son. They said the teeth marks were NarinвҖҷs, thatвҖҷs a lie.вҖқ
вҖңHe is the village headman; canвҖҷt he go and give a statement, or go in for someone else, or go for his own statement? We are waiting just like you. We canвҖҷt sleep anyway, we canвҖҷt drink. Our minds arenвҖҷt even with us. DonвҖҷt hurt us on social media.вҖқ

Why was Arif GГјran complaining? A theory was slowly taking shape on social media (under the effect of statements given, leaked information, and the highly flawed reporting of those statements and leaks), and posts accompanied by harsh accusations against the family were starting to circulate. As early as 27 August, even if not yet in newspapers, social media had already begun to fill up with heavy accusations against Enes GГјran and thus against the family. The number of users who were sure that Enes GГјran and two of his friends вҖңsmoked weedвҖқ and even more sure that they had harmed a dog (at first it was mistreatment, then it became torture, and finally it was said to be rape) was increasing at a steady pace. The door had been opened to direct insults against the family.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
section: She Saw Something She ShouldnвҖҷt Have Seen
text:
She Saw Something She ShouldnвҖҷt Have Seen
The argument вҖңNarin saw something she shouldnвҖҷt have seenвҖқ was also first voiced on social media: the oldest trace of that phrase on social media goes back to 28 August; an account with not many followers and apparently choosing to remain вҖңanonymousвҖқ wrote the following:

вҖңA journalist on NTV identified the person. Someone very close. I think Narin saw something she shouldnвҖҷt have seen. I hope IвҖҷm wrong and Narin is found.вҖқ

The leaked procedures and information about Enes and the theories of these zealous champions of conscience were simmering day by day, moving toward becoming the вҖңgeneral public perceptionвҖқ on 31 August and afterward. The false claim that Enes and his friends вҖңhurt/raped a dogвҖқ was showing signs of being merged with the campaign against government proposals about stray animals, and people were being called to the rally to be held in YenikapДұ on 1 September 2024. The formula вҖңNarin saw something she shouldnвҖҷt have seenвҖқ spread rapidly especially after Salim GГјranвҖҷs arrest on 2 September, and would become virtually the essence and summary of the whole affair. Now, in order to approach 31 August day by day, it is useful to look closely at some critical posts:

title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
section: Social Media Posts
text:
Social Media Posts

1. The Geography Will Sell Out Anything to Save Its Own Skin
27 August, from an account with respectable followers:

вҖңNarin must have seen them raping the dog. These types are extremely dangerous, mark my words. The geography will sell anything, abandon anything, just to save its own skin.вҖқ

The post was dropping the first hint of the arguments that would later, in September, be deployed by orientalist and colonialist masterminds, with the word geography.


2. Who Did the Mother Fear So Much ThatвҖҰ
27 August, from a respectable account with 11,000 followers, which would receive 2.3 million views:

And the motherвҖҰ She spoke on air about a detail that may change the course of the investigation: вҖңIвҖҷm saying this for the first time, I didnвҖҷt say it because I thought theyвҖҷd suspect us. At the hour when #Narin disappeared, her older brother and two of his friends were behind the house smoking tobacco and they harmed a #dog. I said son, thatвҖҷs a sin, have mercyвҖҰвҖқ After these words, in the interest of the integrity of the investigation вҖ” which is how it should be вҖ” the motherвҖҷs voice was cut from the broadcast and it was announced that the gendarmerie had called her in again to give another statement.
Is the вҖҳtobaccoвҖҷ she mentioned marijuana?
From her lament вҖңSon, have mercy,вҖқ what are we supposed to understand? That they ra**d the dog?
Who/what was the mother afraid of that she didnвҖҷt include this detail in her statement?! Time is running out, and when the pieces come together, the picture that emerges tears my heart apart! A village of 20 households! 7 days, just 7 days!! But donвҖҷt look at me, childвҖҰ Still we keep hope alive. IвҖҷm saying, just come back! #FindNarin #WhereIsNarin #Apocalypse #WhereAreYouNarinGГјran



3. A Person Who Kills an Animal Kills a Human
28 August 2024, a post:

#1EylГјldeYenikapДұdayДұz #narin This fiend (referring to the person who assaulted and killed the child named EylГјl YaДҹlДұkara) raped an animal; if he had been jailed for that, he wouldnвҖҷt have preyed on EylГјl. A person who kills an animal will kill a human. A person who rapes an animal will rape a human. Know this, shape the law accordingly, and protect our childrenвҖҰ DonвҖҷt release perverts and killersвҖҰ

This was a low-follower account with little engagement, but the post it quoted from 27 August had received 6,000 likes, 1,000 reposts, and more than 400,000 views.


4. What They Did to the Dog Cannot Be Ignored

Recall that the killer of little EylГјl (referring to a victim in another case) had previously ra**d a dog! DonвҖҷt forget! What the brother and his two friends did to the dog, which the mother said she kept silent about because вҖңtheyвҖҷd suspect us,вҖқ also cannot be ignored. #Narin #WhereIsNarinGГјran



5. Those Who Encourage Killing DogsвҖҰ Are Instigators
Post dated 28 August, one of the attempts to link animal rights activism to the case:

If the killer of Narin turns out to be the BROTHER who TORTURED and RAPED a dog, Those who CAUSED this torture and rape, Those who say the streets will be safe only if there are no stray dogs, Those who ENCOURAGE KILLING dogs and thus pave the way for killing people, THEY ARE THE INSTIGATORS OF THIS MURDER!!!
#SokakHayvanlariSahipsizDegildir #1EylГјldeYenikapДұdayДұz



6. A 9-Million-Follower Account: вҖңTHE FAMILY AND THE UNCLE, ITвҖҷS ALL A BIT MESSYвҖқ
That same day, from a very famous and philanthropic musicianвҖҷs account with more than 9 million followers вҖ” which, compared to others, was вҖңrelativelyвҖқ mild:

Ahbaplar investigated.
#narin
Bite marks were found on her brotherвҖҷs arm.
He said вҖңI bit myself.вҖқ
The matter has been referred to forensics.
WeвҖҷre waiting for the result.
The family and the uncle, itвҖҷs all a bit messy.
The authorities are also looking into this special situation.
There are inconsistencies in family membersвҖҷ statements.

As one would expect, it got 8 million views, 52,000 likes, 5,000 reposts, and over 2,000 replies, mentions, etc.
One feature of the post was that it showed not only journalists and lawyers but also volunteers involved in social aid efforts had access to certain information; вҖңthe familyвҖҷs relations are a bit messy,вҖқ coupled with вҖңthe authorities are also looking into this special situation,вҖқ tells us this. Let us note immediately that in the Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca post there are insinuations of problematic relationships between Uncle-Brother and Uncle-Mother; this shows that the fake account holder and certain people around the case were feeding from the same pool of information.
In the comments below, the dog theory was voiced, the Minister of the Interior was mentioned, and people were saying things like вҖңpoor girl born into the wrong family.вҖқ


7. Poor Little Girl Born into the Wrong Family
Another deep-conscience post from 28 August:

I wish you were alive, beautiful child, I wish they could just take you from that family and protect you, I wish! вҖ“ Yesterday, her father, a HГјda-Par sympathizer, said вҖңI turned my phone offвҖқ! That struck me as oddвҖҰ And both the mother and the father have always been chillingly cold-blooded! вҖ“ Today itвҖҷs been announced that the brother was detained to collect DNA, and that there were bite marks on his arm. So, despite tДұrnak marks on his arms from torturing and raping the dog, the brother was detained and then released! And afterwards he says in his statement вҖңI did those marks myselfвҖқ! The part about the brother hurting the dog, thatвҖҷs sexual abuse, and of course maybe they killed it afterward! Unfortunately Narin was killed, the most innocent one in the family is goneвҖҰ What theyвҖҷre looking for isnвҖҷt Narin, itвҖҷs her body! In the first hours, if a missing child isnвҖҷt found, the chance of finding them alive is next to zero. The moment she disappeared they should have taken the entire family in! Her father, her mother, her brother, all of them! Poor missing little girl born into the wrong family! As for everyone left behind вҖ” including the crying wailing mother вҖ” may you all rot in hell! #WeAreLookingForNarinGueran #WhereAreYouNarinGГјran

One million views, seven thousand likes, and more than 650 reposts.


8. Educate Your Sons First, Not Your Daughters

So it all starts not with sending them to Quran courses, but with teaching morals to your child within the family. Educate your sons first, not your daughters. That bastard of a brother вҖ” even if he didnвҖҷt do anything to Narin вҖ” should still be prosecuted for that disgusting thing he did to the dog.



9. The Whole Family Knows the Truth and Is Hiding It
Post dated 29 August:

NarinвҖҷs brother Enes raped a dog that day, Smoked weed, There are NarinвҖҷs teeth marks on his arm.
Most likely Narin is dead and her killer is her brother. The whole family knows the truth and is hiding it..
From the very first moment they gave only partial information. Their facial expressions were more shock and panic than sadness.

The idea of trying to build a theory by reading the emotional state of the family members вҖ” вҖңtheir facial expressions were more shock and panic than sadnessвҖқ вҖ” was perhaps the most important issue caused by the leaks. Later, both in the publicly available documents of the main case and in the вҖңaiding in concealing the perpetratorвҖқ case, we would see that this method was adopted by the investigation/prosecution units themselves.


10. A Post
On 31 August, a representative of an organization doing work in the public interest issued a warning to comply with the broadcast ban:

Regarding the ongoing speculative posts about Narin, apart from UCД°M Chair Saadet Г–zkan, Deputy Chair YГјcel Ceylan, and the associationвҖҷs National Legal Coordinators and Legal Counsel, we kindly request that no unverified information be published in the name of UCД°M by any of our other units; we also state that we bear no legal responsibility in the event of a violation of the broadcast ban.

But the same person did not see any need to correct or remove their 27 August post вҖңin line with the request not to publish unverified informationвҖқ:

The mother has started talking!! The brother whoвҖҷs about to go to the army and his two friends were behind the house at the time Narin disappeared, smoking cigarettes and harming a dog. The mother said вҖңIвҖҷm saying this for the first time, I didnвҖҷt say it because I thought theyвҖҷd suspect us.вҖқ The mother also knows that people who harm animals canвҖҷt be trusted. The truth will come out, even if late; just a little longer.
#WhereIsNarinGГјran

In response to that post, someone with 23,000 followers and respected followers wrote:

To them, raping a dog is such a normal thing that the woman tells it calmly, and she didnвҖҷt even intervene. WeвҖҷve been saying this all along, but we couldnвҖҷt get it across: I hope youвҖҷve now understood that the thing you stayed silent about today, chalking it up to вҖңjust a dog,вҖқ will not remain limited to the dog.

Note the emphasis on вҖңthem,вҖқ which, like the word вҖңgeography,вҖқ carries the markers of veiled hate speech.
In short, at that point the вҖңharm to the dogвҖқ lie still seemed вҖңrelativelyвҖқ plausible, but the broadcaster of the program had already issued a correction on 28 August вҖ” although who cares, really? Moreover, вҖңthe brother whoвҖҷs going to the armyвҖқ wasnвҖҷt Enes anyway, but does that matter? No one felt like deleting a post that had gotten 4.6 million views.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
section: The Detention of Salim GГјran and the Accompanying Reports
text:
The Detention of Salim GГјran and the Accompanying Reports
When Salim GГјran was detained on 31 August and arrested on 2 September, one of the claims accompanying this was that вҖңDNA was found in the vehicle.вҖқ Nearly all newspapers and online outlets naturally shared the related reports; some (like TRT Haber or DW TГјrkГ§e) presented it as their own reporting, some quoted according to their style, some simply went with copy-paste. The вҖңmost editorially carefulвҖқ versions included the following:

DNA found on the driverвҖҷs seat of S.G.вҖҷs vehicle matched the DNA taken from Narin GГјranвҖҷs clothes and blanket. This development made the uncle a suspect.
It was also determined that the uncle, S.G., left the village quickly 15 minutes after Narin GГјran was last seen with her friends.
Gendarmerie teams also examined the uncleвҖҷs phone. As a result of this examination, it was determined that S.G. had deleted WhatsApp messages and call logs from his phone.

Clearly these were gendarmerie-sourced details. The statement вҖңit was determined that he left the village quickly 15 minutes after Narin GГјran was last seen with her friendsвҖқ was rather striking; even more striking was the вҖңinteractionвҖқ between the post from the fake Facebook account and this information.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
section: A Fake News Bomb: Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca
text:
A Fake News Bomb: Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca
One of the incidents whose impact was later forgotten вҖ” the way it steered the process overshadowed вҖ” was the Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca incident. On 31 August 2024, a certain MГҮГҮ left striking allegations in a comment under a Facebook post that Salim GГјran had made on 15 August. That same day, an account with 300 followers took a screenshot of the FB comment and shared it on X without comment:

#FindNarinGГјran #FindNarinNow
#WeAreLookingForNarinGГјran
The message written by a man named Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca on the social media account of NarinвҖҷs uncle..

Despite the limited follower count, it received 80,000 views, but it took another day or two for the matter to become publicly mainstream. The fact that content shared from anonymous or fake accounts could become mainstream within one or two days is of course partly a result of the catastrophic condition of the media, but on the other hand it raises suspicion that there were people acting with full awareness of that condition. This particular case is the most typical example of that.
The public only learned that MГҮГҮ was a fake account on 2 October 2024, from a report by Mustafa ЕһekeroДҹlu at HabertГјrk. Until then, hundreds of articles and thousands of posts treated what was written under that name as вҖңthe absolute truth,вҖқ and the content became, for millions of people, the greatest proof of reality. To this post, the lie вҖңblood and vomit samples were found in Salim GГјranвҖҷs carвҖқ was also added.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
section: A Flood of Reports
text:
A Flood of Reports
An example report[2] using all of these elements together came out in the newspaper Sabah on 04.09.2024; I mention it only as an example, because A Haber, GerГ§ek GГјndem, Halk TV, Takvim, CNN TГјrk, Karar, T24, and TГјrkiye, among others, all produced similar reports, and smaller news portals kept multiplying the same reports with slight variations.





In that report, another false piece of information pointing to the uncle was used:

A Haber correspondent Sinan YДұlmaz gave details on Salim GГјranвҖҷs statement in the case Turkey is talking about. The allegation was raised that Salim GГјran had said to NarinвҖҷs father, вҖңI will make you suffer such a pain that you will never forget it for the rest of your life.вҖқ

It was raised вҖ” but whether it was true or not was apparently not seen as important. MГҮГҮвҖҷs statements were also included in the report, of course not forgetting to add the phrase вҖңit was learned that he gave a statement to the gendarmerie.вҖқ

title: The Narin GГјran Case #3
section: A Highly Assertive Report That Contains Its Own Contradiction
text:
A Highly Assertive Report That Contains Its Own Contradiction
On the same 4 September, the halktv.com.tr site published a signed, вҖңexclusiveвҖқ-tagged report[3] titled вҖңThis is how the first clue in the Narin case was caughtвҖқ. The sub-headline stated that Salim GГјran had been arrested and said, вҖңUncle GГјran was taken into custody because of a post he made on Facebook.вҖқ
I chose the Halk TV report partly because it was signed вҖ” relying both on institutional and personal trust вҖ” and partly because it was one of the earliest and most widely cited. Here too, as with the outlets that either quoted or produced their own versions, editorial work was highly limited.
The article stated, вҖңFirst ГҮatalcaвҖҷs statement was taken, then Uncle GГјran was detainedвҖқ; it did not say вҖңit was claimed,вҖқ вҖңit was alleged,вҖқ вҖңit was said,вҖқ вҖңit was reported,вҖқ вҖңit was asserted,вҖқ etc. The writer and the publisher were sure. The situation was definite. But that certainty flipped in the last sentence of the article: вҖңOn the other hand, it was learned that the gendarmerie investigating the incident did not take the statement of any person named Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca who commented on social media.вҖқ
In short, it seems that neither the two outlets I cited nor any outlet that quoted them or produced their own reports had actually opened and looked at the social media post вҖ” that is, the FB comment. Nothing about the user had been checked, the oddities in the content had not been scrutinized, it had simply been accepted as true. Until 2 October, when it was вҖңdefinitively learnedвҖқ that the account was fake and operating from abroad, there had been a downpour of news, video packages, TV programs, and posts. In another outlet (koza24), the sentence вҖңit was learned that ГҮatalca went to the gendarmerie and gave a statementвҖқ was used confidently. On 4 September, A Haber, in its extensive report[4], featured snippets from the uncleвҖҷs interrogation, repeated ГҮatalcaвҖҷs allegations without criticism, but did label them as вҖңallegations.вҖқ
Yeni Еһafak, for example, repeated the вҖңhe went and gave a statementвҖқ claim on 5 September and turned what he had written into news without any critical filter. Another site (TRHaber) repeated the same things on 9 September; it did not include the detail about вҖңgiving a statement.вҖқ
A вҖңbrandвҖқ like Milliyet, in a 10 September report that also included expert opinions, saw no problem in using the following phrasing: вҖңDNA samples belonging to Narin GГјran were found on the front seat of the car belonging to her uncle, who was arrested, Salim GГјran. For Salim GГјran, a gas station attendant named Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca gave a statement to the gendarmerie saying вҖҳEither Narin was unconscious or Salim had strangled her with his own hands and she was lying on the front seat, and there was a dark brown blanket on top of her,вҖҷ and afterward the uncle was arrested.вҖқ
After Nevzat Bahtiyar was caught, on 11 September there was inevitably a report[5] titled вҖңThe gas station workerвҖҷs statements confirmed what Bahtiyar saidвҖқ: вҖңThe words of a gas station employee who commented on Uncle Salim GГјranвҖҷs social media accountвҖқ had, it said, confirmed BahtiyarвҖҷs statement.
By now not only the brother but also the uncle and the mother had been included almost definitively in the вҖңguiltyвҖқ lineup. Now we can look more closely at the content and form of the MГҮГҮ post; but this is already so long that I will leave that for the next section. In my next section, titled вҖңThe content, language, and tone of the information bomb,вҖқ (perhaps the most critical part), we will meet again.



External References (5)


YENД° ЕһAFAK: Father of Missing Narin GГјran Spoke About the Detention and Statements: Why This Smear? (31 August 2024) вҶ©пёҺ


SABAH: Shocking Allegation About Missing Narin GГјranвҖҷs Uncle Salim GГјran: Blood, Vomit, and Urine Samples Were Taken from His Vehicle! (04 September 2024) вҶ©пёҺ

A HABER: Has Missing Narin Been Found? Why Was Uncle Salim GГјran Arrested? The вҖңMurder Without a BodyвҖқ Debate вҖ” Here Are the Details (04 September 2024) вҶ©пёҺ


TAKVД°M: Has the Narin GГјran Murder Been Solved? Whose Blanket Was the Body Wrapped In? The Mother GГјranвҖҷs Confessions Will Untie the Knot! (11 September 2024) вҶ©пёҺ

title: The Narin GГјran Case #1
text:
AUTHOR


Ali Duran Topuz


LAST UPDATED


October 21, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 20 min


Word Count


3514
Judgment belongs to God; the final, absolute, flawless judgment is His. Human judgment is flawed; no one, not even the most competent judge and the most competent judicial body, is free of error. For this reason it was said: вҖңDo not judge lest you also be judged.вҖқ God commands being just; whoever is not just commits oppression and becomes one of the tyrants. Yet He does not say how to be just. Striving for justice is the duty of the human being. There is no ready-made formula for justice; it is always a search, an effort, a field of struggle. Because there is no ready-made formula and because judgment belongs to God, judges do not rule by вҖңtruthвҖқ or вҖңthe absolute fact,вҖқ but by their вҖңconscientious conviction.вҖқ For the same reasons, every form of adjudication, including judicial adjudication, has been bound to rules and principles; it is subject to developed protocols and specialized methods. Not only the judge, not only the prosecutor, not only the lawyer, but everyone is bound by these rules. Where everyone, relevant or not, acts in violation of the rules, contrary to the principles, indifferent to the protocols, and heedless of the methods, the adjudication performed in that place can produce nothing but oppression. There is no advance justice; even if one complies with all rules a hundred times, when one does not comply once, disaster emerges.
I am speaking of the Narin GГјran case. The case showed that in Turkey the вҖңcapacity to adjudicateвҖқ is filled with problems so serious and so weighty that they make it almost impossible to reach justice вҖ” problems that begin with individuals one by one and extend to the judiciary itself, to politics, to the media, to academia. From the media and politics first and foremost, to the investigative and prosecutorial bodies, various actors emerged as perpetrators of this loss of capacity: Even if the judgment rendered by the court happened to be вҖңcorrectвҖқ by coincidence, everything that led to that вҖңcorrectnessвҖқ was faulty, and therefore that correctness could only be an accident; justice cannot be based on accident. Of course, starting with the dissenting opinion written at the appellate stage and the judge who wrote it, there were those who watched out for вҖңthe right thingsвҖқ from the day the matter became public, there were those who warned of the errors, but neither those warnings nor that dissent вҖ” full of legal knowledge, insight, and insistence вҖ” were able to transform the decision from a mere coincidence into a decision reached through a judicial activity aiming at the ideal of justice. Likewise, the decision to overturn the вҖңharboring the offenderвҖқ case, which forms another bundle of lost judicial competence, displayed a hopeful perspective and a strong grasp of law. But we still do not know whether this вҖңlightвҖқ and вҖңreasonable speechвҖқ will be able to overcome the darkness and noise that have surrounded the case from the very beginning. The howling of the wolves drowned out the silence of the lambs so completely, and there are so many who are pleased with that noise, that oneвҖҷs hope breaks.
In this study I will focus especially on how the media вҖ” and soon after, politics вҖ” handled the matter starting from the very first day, because primarily what led the whole affair to turn into a вҖңdisaster of investigation and prosecutionвҖқ was the high-decibel noise environment created jointly by the media and politics. Of course, if the subject is the judiciary, the government must be seen as the primary responsible actor in the widest frame; I see it so, because the responsibility for, for example in this file, the obvious and difficult-to-understand inadequacy of the judicial police, which is one of the steps of the judiciary, naturally lies with the government. But before sticking the big needle into the government, there are smaller needles owed to the media of which I am a member, to the law, and to politics as a whole. The fact that journalists do not work in line with journalistic norms, that politics steps in without caring about the ideal of justice, that the вҖңdefense,вҖқ that bar associations, throw the вҖңpresumption of innocenceвҖқ into the trash and become blind partners to the joint wrongdoing of media and politics вҖ” none of this can be excused solely by the governmentвҖҷs flawed media and legal policies.
The second aim of this study is to distinguish the shadows that flawed attitudes in the media and in politics have cast upon the investigation and prosecution. Of course, the problems of the judiciary, especially in the investigative phase, will also remain within the frame. Let me note immediately that I will not enter into вҖңevidence discussions,вҖқ because that requires another method and another focus; this work, essentially, is not a legal study but a study on the relationship between law, media, and politics. In short, this work focuses exclusively on the malfunctions in how the media, politics, and legal mechanisms operate, and in how they relate to one another. Let me also state at the outset that I am not looking for a вҖңculpritвҖқ in these areas, and therefore I will continue, as far as possible, without mentioning proper names, for two reasons: First, it benefits no one to lock the discussion onto the sins or virtues of particular individuals; second, the problem is not personal mistakes but entirely structural, systemic in character.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #1
section: The Reason for the Intense Attention: Concience?
text:
The Reason for the Intense Attention: Concience?
First, we must begin with the intense attention the media and politics showed to the matter. Of course, if a child is missing it is good and necessary that the media, politics, and society pay close attention. But from many similar, very painful, very grave cases that occurred before and after, we know that the way interest in the Narin case spread socially is almost unique. Why? If we say вҖңconscience,вҖқ then the scarcity of interest in other matters remains unexplained; conscience is not something that erupts like a volcano now and then.
One explanation is that the month of August, when the incident occurred, was вҖңweak in other agenda items.вҖқ In the absence of agenda, the media has the ability to create one for itself, especially if what is at stake is the disappearance of an innocent child. But this вҖңtruthвҖқ is not very sufficient, because the same media that does not bring to the forefront of the national agenda the case of a two-year-old baby whose body cigarettes were extinguished on and who was raped, that is not very eager to report on the barely adolescent вҖңyes-sir boysвҖқ who lose their arms and legs in machines at workplaces, that writes вҖңthe child laborer killed by his bossвҖқ twice and moves on, must also have had other motives underlying its attention to the Narin case.

Reason #1: A child coming out of a QurвҖҷan course!
The first reason was that the child disappeared after coming out of the QurвҖҷan course. The potential to build a discourse against the government around criminal incidents that occur especially in places where children are present collectively was the first major reason why those who live in the media and social media with the label вҖңopposition,вҖқ and of course opposition parties, took close interest in the matter; naturally, the вҖңoversightвҖқ function of the media requires this, but the intensity of that interest compared to other cases still needs further explanation.
Until the date the childвҖҷs body was found, nearly all the reports in вҖңopposition outletsвҖқ and in a significant part of pro-government outlets began with the phrase вҖңNarin, who could not be reached after leaving the QurвҖҷan courseвҖҰвҖқ That formula continued to be used for a long time even after the childвҖҷs body was found. One of the developments that increased this motivation was the early-stage reports that the village imam was вҖңunder suspicion.вҖқ These were grave reports: it was recklessly written and broadcast that вҖңpornographic images were found on the imamвҖҷs phone,вҖқ even that he was a вҖңswinger,вҖқ and that this had come up in police questioning. Much later, one journalist finally thought to ask the authorities: the investigation was being conducted not by the police but by the gendarmerie, there were no such images on the phone, and such a question had not been asked; questions unrelated to the investigation would not be asked.
Both that lie and the persistent use of the formula вҖңthe child who disappeared after leaving the QurвҖҷan courseвҖҰвҖқ had a clear purpose: it is a formula that is supported by, and in turn strengthens, the claim that the government has no priority such as protecting women and children, and therefore in places where children are collectively present, including QurвҖҷan courses, every sort of threat is possible; that there are ill-intentioned people there; that the government is not interested in eliminating those threats. Many media workers, politicians, and jurists sadly see statements and narratives based on these templates as equivalent to вҖңbeing political,вҖқ and they produce and disseminate these statements without scrutiny, without filtering them through criticism, thinking that they are thereby вҖңdoing opposition.вҖқ
But the interest was not limited only to вҖңsecular groups absolutely opposed to the government.вҖқ From the very beginning other groups also entered in with вҖңspecial motivations.вҖқ


Reason #2: Hizbullah, the village guard system!
While the QurвҖҷan course issue was still hot, two more claims were released into circulation: the village was a вҖңvillage guard village,вҖқ even a вҖңHizbullah village,вҖқ and bullets and weapons had been found during the searches. These had already reached the rank of вҖңtruth,вҖқ and thus the stance in the media and social media spread also to opposition parties and civil society organizations. Statements poured down like rain; marches were organized. Thus, just as much as the вҖңsecularвҖқ segments seeking to oppose the government, those deeply (and absolutely justifiably) disturbed by the terms вҖңvillage guardвҖқ and вҖңHizbullah,вҖқ the regionвҖҷs lawyers, politicians, and institutions also mobilized to publicize and socialize the issue. If the child of a village and a family that are this intertwined with the state, this close to the state, is missing, then the responsible party must be the state.
Finding themselves under a two-pronged barrage of criticism, government figures began to try to produce вҖңcounter-arguments and counter-statements.вҖқ The visit to the village and the family by one of the regionвҖҷs well-known pro-government politicians, Galip EnsarioДҹlu, and the words he said afterward made the voices of the two camps that had already risen to their feet grow even louder, and caused many segments of society who had not noticed the matter until that day to begin following it closely. According to his own speech, the reason for EnsarioДҹluвҖҷs visit was to respond to the atmosphere that had been formed against the government because of the incident and to some of the arguments circulating there. He was going to say that the family was not Hizbullah, but from the Welfare Party tradition, and he was going to emphasize that it was not right for politics to be involved. Indeed, politics should not have become involved, but his own visit and words did not prevent the very thing he criticized; they amplified it. One particular remark would ensure that the outcome was the exact opposite of his intention:

вҖңThere is confidentiality in the investigation. Also we have come to the final stage. There are things that we sometimes do not know, and sometimes know but must not say. Because the family, you know, is our friends. Because the matter is at such a sensitive level, we do not want to say something that will upset them too much. After we reached the lifeless body of our girl Narin, we are about to reach the perpetrators very quickly.вҖқ [1]



  

 
   
     
     YouTube'da izlemek iГ§in tДұklayДұn
   
 

    
  
Galip EnsarioДҹlu


These words drew intense reactions; Mr.В Galip and other government officials stated that his intention had only been to emphasize the confidentiality and sensitivity of the investigation. But these statements had little effect. At the time this explanation was made, 21 members of the GГјran family were in custody. Mr.В EnsarioДҹluвҖҷs political intervention under the theme of вҖңnot involving politics,вҖқ rather than preventing, only cemented the inclinations and convictions that had been forming against the family since 21 August. It is likely that the anti-government tendencies and beliefs formed in the political sphere led to the emergence of the idea of the вҖңfamily being guilty,вҖқ not only among opposition segments but also within government circles. Indeed, the next day EnsarioДҹlu reiterated the argument that Narin might have вҖңseen something she was not supposed to see,вҖқ thereby positioning himself at a common point with that tendency. In fact, in Mr.В GalipвҖҷs statement there is also an implication of вҖңthere may be offenders within the family,вҖқ and his aim seems to be to prevent labeling the entire family as guilty.


Reason #3: Hatred of Kurds!
In the early days of the case, that is, in the period up to 25вҖ“26 August, those who viewed the matter within the framework of an вҖңopportunity to oppose the governmentвҖқ were active, but after an unbelievable distortion and implausible conclusions drawn from words spoken by the mother, YГјksel GГјran, on a television broadcast, вҖңKurd hatredвҖқ also entered into play вҖ” sometimes openly, sometimes dressed up in various colonialist discourses. Shares and analyses began to fly around on themes such as feudalism, the tribe-state relationship, the tribe-crime relationship, and the вҖңculture of the region.вҖқ (As this article proceeds we will also see examples.) The manner in which YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs words were distorted also showed how grave the condition of the media is. Through various arguments about the intertwined relations among a reactionary government, its social allies, and the tribal structures in the Kurdish provinces вҖ” namely the triangle of вҖңstate, religious order, KurdishnessвҖқ вҖ” the hostility against Kurds was then mobilized.
By then, four groups of actors had lined up, using the media and social media, and the mediaвҖ“social media dynamic further fanned their flames.
Those seeking an opportunity to oppose the government through secular desires.
Those wanting to conduct opposition to the government on the HizbullahвҖ“village guard axis.
Racist segments who dress up their hostility to Kurds with right-wing or left-wing arguments.
And, of course, the government and those who seek to protect and defend it.
As the first example of the grave role played by the media, let us now look closely at the words of YГјksel GГјran on television; if you scan internet sites and social media platforms dated 28 August 2024 and after, you will see the following report/information:
вҖңThe Mother of Missing Narin GГјran in DiyarbakДұr Made a Confession Live on Air: I DidnвҖҷt Say It Because They Would Suspect Us.вҖқ
Before moving to what is written under this long headline, let us look at the headline itself. The word вҖңconfessionвҖқ shows that, from the perspective of those making the report, the mother is assumed to be guilty or to know who is guilty. Because the editor (when we read the story with the headline) takes us to a cluster of suspects: the son who is about to leave for military service and/or his two friends.
The sentence chosen from the mother for the headline was specially selected (and is not correct) in order to focus suspicion on the family. The mother has вҖңremained silent until now,вҖқ so we have a strong вҖңevidenceвҖқ that вҖңsilence means consent.вҖқ Yet under criminal law principles, silence is not used as evidence; silence is a right, and it signifies not вҖңadmission,вҖқ but вҖңnon-acceptance.вҖқ Who cares about law? Before the unleashed genius of the journalist-editor, what science вҖ” let alone law вҖ” could stand?
Now let us look closely at the report:
вҖңNarinвҖҷs mother YГјksek GГјran, on the program вҖҳDidem Arslan YДұlmazвҖҷla VazgeГ§me,вҖҷ revealed a detail from the day of the incident, saying, вҖҳI couldnвҖҷt say it because you would suspect us,вҖҷ and confessed as follows:
вҖҳThe dog ate the turkey, and my son who is about to go to the army and two of his friends were smoking in the back of the house at the time Narin disappeared and were torturing a dog. I said, donвҖҷt do it, itвҖҷs a sin. IвҖҷm saying this for the first time, I didnвҖҷt say it because they would suspect us.вҖҷвҖқ
How nice, how convincing, how вҖңevidentiary,вҖқ is it not? Of course, these are sentences re-written by вҖңeditors,вҖқ not what the mother actually said. She does not in fact say on the program, вҖңI didnвҖҷt say it because you would suspect us.вҖқ
On the program she says:

вҖңI went up to the roof. I laid out my bedding. I saw that Enes was over by the stable, I could hear his voice. I couldnвҖҷt see him. (вҖҰ) I said, Enes, the dog ate my turkey last night. I called out to Enes. I saw that his two friends were there too. I hadnвҖҷt mentioned this before, I hadnвҖҷt said it first. His two friends were there. I saw (SILENCE) I said, itвҖҷs a shame, itвҖҷs a sin (SILENCE) The host cuts in saying, вҖҳTheyвҖҷre smoking.вҖҷвҖқ [2]


  

 
   
     
     YouTube'da izlemek iГ§in tДұklayДұn
   
 

    
  
(The segment from 34:45 to 35:25 of the broadcast. In the first вҖңSilenceвҖқ she says вҖңtheyвҖҷre smoking,вҖқ in the second silence she says вҖңyouвҖҷre smoking at this age вҖ” your lungsвҖ”вҖқ.)

There is no such thing as torture of a dog. Where did that come from, then? From the phrase вҖңitвҖҷs a shame, itвҖҷs a sin.вҖқ But she is saying that to the young men smoking. What is the confession? Nothing. She says вҖңI hadnвҖҷt mentioned this before,вҖқ meaning that as she appears on the program and is asked questions, she recounts details and events that come to mind. During the program a journalist sitting next to YГјksel gives information: law enforcement officers have arrived, the program will end, statements will be taken. The same journalist, at the beginning of the segment, recounts that up to that day statements had been taken вҖңfor informationвҖқ from many people including family members вҖңwithout any detention order.вҖқ
But of course it did not end there; the invented phrase вҖңtorture/abuse of a dogвҖқ quickly turned into вҖңrape of a dogвҖқ on social media, and the word вҖңcigaretteвҖқ turned into cannabis. Two days after the first broadcast on 26 August, that is after the spread of the вҖңcigarette into cannabisвҖқ and вҖңtorture of a dogвҖқ claims, the program host took up the matter again, effectively correcting and refuting reports that were not her fault but had been attributed to her program; she stated that there was absolutely no mention of cannabis or any illegal substance, and that RTГңK had fined them for the utterance of the word вҖңcigarette,вҖқ which is why that part had been muted.
When I compare the words I took from the program to the sentence in the published reports, the following is seen: the editors have made the sentence discursive, in order for it to вҖңhave effect,вҖқ whereas Ms.В YГјksel speaks in fragmented phrases. But if they took it as she spoke, it probably would not вҖңhave effect.вҖқ There is nothing to justify the word вҖңconfession,вҖқ which they placed in the headline. There is no such thing as cruelty to a dog. Also, the mother does not say вҖңmy son who is going to the army, EnesвҖқ; because she knows which son is going to the army вҖ” the ones who do not know are the editors! The one going to the army is not Enes, it is Baran.
What is happening here? How can a report like this be made? For people who have no editorial competence, or whose only competence is to value вҖңinfluencing public opinionвҖқ above editorial-journalistic norms, there is nothing abnormal in this. This prototype report spread extraordinarily that day; it was used mostly by copy-paste. No one bothered to watch the program. Those who did this were not only small news portals; the websites of two quite serious outlets, Cumhuriyet[3] and Karar[4], ran the report without using the word вҖңconfession,вҖқ but with an emphasis on вҖңdetail,вҖқ while still preserving вҖңabuse of a dogвҖқ and the phrase вҖңI couldnвҖҷt say it because they would suspect us.вҖқ
If we also add the social media users who further distorted the so-called facts in the report (вҖңIвҖҷm not saying вҖҳtroll,вҖҷ because in this process countless ordinary users behaved like вҖҳtrollsвҖҷвҖқ), it would still be difficult to estimate the impact produced.
After this report, вҖңenemies of KurdsвҖқ became perhaps the loudest contributors to the noise that had formed around the matter.
The effect of this report lasted quite a long time. In fact, she had said neither вҖңtortureвҖқ nor вҖңabuse,вҖқ but what does that matter? This report and the clamors that followed led (again) to Enes GГјran being questioned as the first candidate offender in the killing. Drug tests showed no cannabis or any narcotic trace, but we did not see any headline in any newspaper such as вҖңMajor mistake, Enes did not smoke cannabis.вҖқ Far from seeing that, even as late as around 16 September, professors on television and вҖңsuccessful detectivesвҖқ were still explaining the importance of the dog scenario. Of the scenario that did not exist.
This has gone on too long. I will cut the introduction here. I will publish the second part in a day or two. Thank you for your patience.



External References (4)

title: The Narin GГјran Case and the Search for a Scapegoat: The Decay of the Maximalist Gaze
text:
AUTHOR


Alaattin OДҹuz


LAST UPDATED


July 16, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 7 min


Word Count


1170
When RenГ© GirardвҖҷs scapegoat theory and Гүmile DurkheimвҖҷs concepts of punishment and solidarity are taken together, it becomes clear that, in moments of social crisis, sacrificial rituals serve a fundamental function for the preservation of order and the reinforcement of solidarity. According to Girard, mimetic desires вҖ” desires modelled on the desires of others вҖ” lie at the root of conflict between people; because the goals are limited, this state of affairs makes tension and conflict inevitable.
When mimetic desires intensify to a point that threatens the social order, that violence is discharged by being focused on a victim. In the fragile, crisis-ridden moments of the social order, the scapegoat вҖ” the one onto whom accusations are directed and onto whom collective violence is loaded вҖ” becomes a focal point for releasing anger and for re-establishing a temporary unity. Girard defines this process as a non-coincidental form of violence directed, in fact, at a victim deliberately chosen for the sustainability of society, and notes that these patterns of behaviour continue in the modern world in such forms as bullying and lynching.
Durkheim, in parallel, evaluates punishment as an expression of collective conscience and a re-affirmation of social solidarity. Crime is an act contrary to societyвҖҷs shared conscience, and punishment is a passionate response that shows that this shared conscience has been attacked. Crime means a violation of collective norms and values; punishment, by re-mobilizing this shared conscience, takes on the function of reinforcing social cohesion.

title: The Narin GГјran Case and the Search for a Scapegoat: The Decay of the Maximalist Gaze
section: The Social Lynching in the Narin GГјran Case
text:
The Social Lynching in the Narin GГјran Case
The public reactions developed around the killing of eight-year-old Narin GГјran in the summer of 2024 in DiyarbakДұr are an example of how, in a moment of crisis, rational reasoning can be set aside and a family and a village can be declared scapegoats on the basis of fictitious claims. In this process, agendas woven from political and ideological motives, racist influencers in pursuit of engagement, retired police officers, and media figures, between them turned the practice of justice that was being demanded into something resembling carded cotton.
In addition to the wave of lynching whipped up by broad middle-class crowds, certain figures from the left and from Kurdish politics tried to make sense of the affair through critiques of feudalism and patriarchy, while local actors such as the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖ” caught up in the excitement of cooperating with the state in the legal arena for the first time вҖ” sadly did not object to the lynching atmosphere in public opinion.
The parallels between this stance of the left and Kurdish politics, and the orientalist clichГ©s about Kurdish village life advanced by Turkish nationalist and modernist actors, are striking. Some racist circles, hiding behind such totalizing perspectives, made attributions amounting to insults вҖ” about consanguineous marriage and incest in particular.

title: The Narin GГјran Case and the Search for a Scapegoat: The Decay of the Maximalist Gaze
section: Maximalist Narratives and Legal Processes
text:
Maximalist Narratives and Legal Processes
Through the maximalist narratives put forward by some Kurdish politicians вҖ” JД°TEM, Hizbullah, state-local-element collusion, feudalism вҖ” and by Turkish nationalists вҖ” consanguineous marriage, aДҹa-style domination, the alleged worthlessness of girls in the eyes of the family вҖ” narratives that have no contact whatsoever with the concrete, criminal reality of the case, the lives of a family and a village were thoughtlessly turned into hell.
Legal institutions such as the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association also took a stance one would never have expected from them. Even when, during the investigation, allegations of torture were on the agenda, no action was taken on the grounds that there was no signed written statement. The former bar president behaved as if the details of the murder had been proven from the outset, and spoke of the village as a site of organized evil.
Media outlets on the nationalist-modernist line and racist influencers chasing engagement also fanned prejudice by triggering orientalist stereotypes about rural life in the east. On television news and programmes, sudden inferences about вҖңfatalismвҖқ were made regarding the geography of the region.

title: The Narin GГјran Case and the Search for a Scapegoat: The Decay of the Maximalist Gaze
section: Time Analysis and the Crime Scenario
text:
Time Analysis and the Crime Scenario
Between the moment Narin appeared heading toward the path and the moment her neighbour Nevzat Bahtiyar drove her toward the riverbed, there are 25 minutes. If we also subtract the time Narin spent walking on the path and the time it took the car to leave the village, this drops to 18 minutes. If we are to believe the dreadful stories that have been constructed: Narin would climb the hill, come home, witness in her home an event she should not have seen, someone would kill her, the mother-brother-uncle would coolly accept this, the uncle would then call his neighbour Nevzat Bahtiyar вҖ” a man he hadnвҖҷt called in months вҖ” to dispose of the body, would summon down to the path the neighbour who was watering eggplants at the entrance to it, the neighbour would see the body and would be given a hint that Narin had seen a forbidden sexual relationship, this neighbour would also coolly accept this, and together with the uncle would put the body in his own car and set off toward the river.
All this fantastic story, recounted as if it were an ordinary daily occurrence, took place in exactly 18 minutes. And how striking it is that this story is being believed.

title: The Narin GГјran Case and the Search for a Scapegoat: The Decay of the Maximalist Gaze
section: Social Psychology and the Search for Justice
text:
Social Psychology and the Search for Justice
The social explosion around NarinвҖҷs murder stands before us as a vivid example of the mechanisms shown by Girard and Durkheim. In a moment of crisis, different classes, ideologies and faith groups quickly came together; they united in the conviction that the culprit had to be punished. Even thin evidence proved sufficient for a community of believers, and the family was declared the scapegoat.
In the end, society achieved a short-term relief and cleared its collective conscience. But, in GirardвҖҷs terms, this is like a placebo. As long as the community continues to accept this remedy on faith, it relaxes and abandons the search for the truth. Yet the only path that will lead us to a resolution of crises is the bringing to light of the actual situation through concrete and convincing evidence.
Why were people so ready to believe in the possibility that so many fantastic events occurred in 18 minutes? If a structural analysis is to be made, I think a backstory must be supplied first. What was it that detached democratic, middle-class, educated people from concrete data and made them feel the need to cling with both hands to grand narratives?

title: The Narin GГјran Case and the Search for a Scapegoat: The Decay of the Maximalist Gaze
section: Conclusion
text:
Conclusion
Yes, I repeat: some crimes are simply crimes. Tons can be said вҖ” and is being said and written вҖ” about the social structure of the east of Turkey and its troubled relations with the state. But some crimes are simply crimes. In this context, what was it that prevented people of conscience from thinking this way, and that allowed them, by means of grand narratives, easily to take aim at a family and arrive at a sensation of moral goodness, truth and beauty? Pondering these questions, I think, is more striking and more instructive.
At whose sacrifice is the social group to which we feel we belong, or society as a whole, decorated with the laurels of вҖңgood,вҖқ вҖңtrueвҖқ and вҖңbeautifulвҖқ? This is the question we need to ask ourselves.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #2
text:
AUTHOR


Ali Duran Topuz


LAST UPDATED


October 23, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 17 min


Word Count


2757
Yesterday, we focused on noise; today, I will continue with the issue of silence. As always, I ask for your patience вҖ” I will delve into long, intricate matters that will surely be found tedious by anyone outside the field, but I must do so to make my point clear. In this section, I will address some events prior to August 31, when things completely went off track and the noise began to shake peopleвҖҷs capacity for sound judgment вҖ” so that what happened afterward can be better understood.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #2
section: First Silence: Be Quiet, The Judiciary is at Work!
text:
First Silence: Be Quiet, The Judiciary is at Work!
LetвҖҷs begin then: there are two crucial forms of silence in the judiciary. The first is the silence necessary to conduct investigations and prosecutions properly. The second вҖ” which took on a special dimension in the Narin GГјran case вҖ” is the вҖңsilence of the family members (even the entire village), allegedly concealing the crime,вҖқ an accusation that quickly became a presumption, thereby erasing the presumption of innocence itself. The first rule of silence was violated by almost everyone вҖ” the media, bureaucracy, politicians, and social media users вҖ” especially after August 31, 2024. As for the second kind of silence, a large chorus formed вҖ” led by lawyers and politicians вҖ” in which the presumption of innocence and its natural extension, the right to remain silent, were utterly disregarded. Indeed, all emphasis on the familyвҖҷs silence could only exist by ignoring these principles вҖ” and that is exactly what happened.
Silence is vital for justice. When judges demand silence in court, it is not only to maintain physical order in the courtroom. In the judiciary, silence is a necessity вҖ” it allows the case or investigation to reach a clear and fair conclusion by ensuring that speech occurs only within prescribed procedures and at appropriate times. It is an unwritten principle. The secrecy of investigation exists to secure this silence at the highest level and essentially means: вҖңTo prevent anyone outside the investigation authorities from interfering with the process, documents, or information; and to ensure that those authorities protect these with utmost care.вҖқ

The tension between silence and the right to information
There is a natural tension between this requirement of silence and the right to access or share information. It is true that judicial or political authorities often abuse this principle вҖ” one reason why the public instinctively reacts against secrecy in legal matters. Yet such abuse does not invalidate the principle itself. The balance between respecting confidentiality and ensuring public access to information вҖ” like justice itself вҖ” must be reestablished in every case. Each stage of justice, each procedure, requires constant inquiry, consideration, discovery, and a sense of responsibility. Judicial authorities (including political ones) must strive to rebuild this balance in every instance, creating mechanisms that respect the publicвҖҷs right to know, while also explaining when patience and restraint are required. Ideally, these mechanisms should be managed and supervised by the prosecutorвҖҷs offices conducting the investigation. Every other authority that speaks out effectively opens a breach in this principle of silence.
Journalists, too, are bound by these rules. When they access information related to an ongoing investigation, they must evaluate it carefully rather than automatically publishing it. They must ask themselves: Where does the public interest lie вҖ” in keeping the information or in sharing it? Perhaps it should only be conveyed to the investigators? Verifying the authenticity and clarity of the information is an obvious prerequisite. A crude example: if the information reveals that judicial or political authorities are obstructing justice вҖ” in a country where enforced disappearances, unsolved murders, and paramilitary violence are not relics of the past вҖ” then, of course, it must be published. But if its publication risks helping the perpetrators escape, destroying evidence, or misleading both investigators and the public, then silence must prevail. This requirement applies from the reporterвҖҷs first contact with information through every stage of editorial decision-making.


The crime scene is not a stage
Another implication of this rule is that crime scenes вҖ” places of examination or investigation вҖ” must remain calm and isolated enough for proper work to be done. In the Narin GГјran case, this principle of silence and calm was never respected. From the second day onward, countless journalists, politicians, and lawyers flocked to the village. Broadcasts were made constantly вҖ” by everyone from individual content creators to national TV channels. The GГјran familyвҖҷs homes were flooded with visitors. Of course, the public nature of the case would inevitably draw media attention, and the presence of lawyers and politicians might have seemed understandable at first glance. Yet it is impossible to claim that most of them respected the principle of silence. Quite the opposite вҖ” everyone who went to the village sought to talk, to collect and spread information, and to share details about the investigation itself in real time, without any filter. Those who didnвҖҷt go made up for it by spinning theories in studios or at editorial desks, while others simply copied and republished whatever fit their narrative.


Turning grief into spectacle
Worse still was the distortion of information as it spread. The вҖңDog/Abuse/Cigarette/MarijuanaвҖқ incident discussed in the previous section was the first of many such examples. The issue with that so-called вҖңdog incidentвҖқ was not merely that YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs words were professionally distorted вҖ” the very format of those interviews and talk shows poses serious ethical problems. When the subject is a judicial process вҖ” especially one involving a childвҖҷs disappearance or death вҖ” there are strict journalistic norms: do not casually interview or publicly broadcast statements from grieving relatives; do not publish their words without verification. At this point, the typical counterargument appears: вҖңThen why did the mother go on TV?вҖқ When institutions and individuals are involved, accountability must begin with institutional failures, not personal ones. Grieving people cannot be expected to know when to speak or remain silent, what to say, or how their words will be interpreted. Even when they are entirely innocent, they are vulnerable вҖ” desperate. Reporters, editors, and producers, however, are obliged to know better. Thus, the fault lies not only in the distortion of words but also in the very act of repeatedly putting YГјksel GГјran on television. That alone was a serious act of irresponsibility. Judicial and political authorities, too, should have taken preventive measures вҖ” not by imposing bans, but by warning family members about the potential harm of such exposure and ensuring that public communication followed a disciplined procedure. This is, and always has been, essential.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #2
section: Second Silence: The Right to Remain Silent and the Presumption of Innocence
text:
Second Silence: The Right to Remain Silent and the Presumption of Innocence
The second issue of silence arose from a claim that appeared very early вҖ” and quickly became a major source of noise: вҖңThe family knows something but remains silent. The mother knows but remains silent. The village knows but remains silent.вҖқ After NevzatвҖҷs arrest on September 8, 2024, this silence turned into a presumption. Party leaders, human rights defenders, lawyers of all kinds, experts, analysts, and theorists began making speeches, giving interviews, and writing articles вҖ” all feeding this presumption with passionate conviction. This presumption of silence obscured another silence: the right to remain silent of those accused.
The popular saying вҖңsilence implies consentвҖқ effectively nullified the criminal law principle that silence is not an admission but a right. What is this principle? The right to remain silent rests on the presumption of innocence. It is the duty of investigators and prosecutors to find sufficient evidence to prove guilt. The constitutional guarantee that вҖңno one shall be compelled to testify against themselvesвҖқ is the ultimate affirmation of this right. Otherwise, coercion becomes normalized. One mechanism that protects both the presumption of innocence and the right to silence is the prohibition of pre-judgment (ihsasДұ rey). If justice is a process of investigation and deliberation, and judges must evaluate evidence freely according to conscience before reaching a verdict, they cannot signal that verdict beforehand. Doing so negates the presumption of innocence. Although this rule applies specifically to judges, in truth вҖ” like silence itself вҖ” it concerns everyone: no one has the right to declare another guilty before due process is complete. (The violation of the presumption of innocence intensified after September 8; I will return to this in later sections.)

title: The Narin GГјran Case #2
section: First Leak: Blood on the Rocks and the GovernorвҖҷs Statement
text:
First Leak: Blood on the Rocks and the GovernorвҖҷs Statement
We can now turn to three separate incidents that publicly broke the silence in the Narin case.
The first seems simple: the вҖңblood on the rocksвҖқ incident. The other two foreshadowed the storm after August 31 вҖ” unlike the dog incident, these now involved the judicial authorities themselves: the detention of Enes GГјran (due to bruises on his eye and bite marks on his arm), and the arrest of uncle Salim GГјran following reports that вҖңDNA was found in his car.вҖқ
LetвҖҷs begin with the blood on the rocks. Until August 28, the press had not produced much news. Reporters wandered around the village, interviewing locals, asking why the search was taking so long вҖ” while others reassured, вҖңBe patient, answers are coming.вҖқ The first news-like report claimed that blood traces had been found on rocks near the GГјran familyвҖҷs home. Before the reports spread, the DiyarbakДұr Governorship issued a statement on August 25, the key part of which read:

вҖңIt has been observed that news and posts have circulated claiming that blood stains found on rocks near the home of missing child Narin GГјran belong to her. It was determined that the stains resulted from a neighborhood childвҖҷs nosebleed.вҖқ

Two points are clear before we move on: Who provided the information that blood was found and being examined? The police, of course вҖ” meaning there was already an internal leak, a breach of confidentiality. Second: why did the GovernorвҖҷs Office feel the need to issue a statement? Initially, it gave the impression that official information would henceforth come through the governor, but this soon proved untrue. The statement likely reflected unease about the leak itself.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #2
section: Second Leak: The Bite Mark on EnesвҖҷs Arm
text:
Second Leak: The Bite Mark on EnesвҖҷs Arm
From the very beginning, law enforcement interviewed many individuals, including family members вҖ” not formal interrogations but so-called information interviews resulting in informal notes. In fact, the interview method has no place in Turkish criminal procedure. In its decision of January 14, 2025 (application no. 2020/14678), the Constitutional Court criticized this practice and ruled that statements obtained through it have no evidentiary value:

вҖңConsidering that the applicantвҖҷs statements obtained through the interview method, which has no basis in criminal procedure, were not confirmed during trialвҖҰ it is understood that the digital data mentioned in the conviction for membership in an armed terrorist organization, though not the sole evidence, were of decisive importance.вҖқ

(Full decision available at: Kararlar Bilgi BankasДұ[1])
Leaving aside the legal issues surrounding such unlawful practices, let us stay with the theme of silence.
Law enforcement conducted an interview with Enes GГјran as early as August 23вҖ“24. They noticed a bruise on his eye and later a bite mark on his arm. Forensic photographs dated August 25 confirm this. However, the public learned of his official detention only on August 28, and by the next day, the news had exploded with sensational headlines like вҖңShocking details emergeвҖқ. Enes was released on August 29. Forensic analysis determined that the bite mark did not belong to Narin GГјran. Yet this second leak, combined with the earlier вҖңDog/Abuse/MarijuanaвҖқ narrative, paved the way for a flood of reports implying Enes was the culprit. Soon, grotesque and baseless scenarios began to circulate вҖ” the first being: вҖңNarin saw Enes and his friends harming a dog andвҖҰвҖқ
Although forensics confirmed the bite mark did not belong to Narin, the reports remained chaotic: most said no DNA was found and the bite didnвҖҷt match, yet some insisted the truth could only be confirmed once Narin was located. Almost all mentioned an important detail: Enes had taken three showers, so DNA couldnвҖҷt be recovered. Sometimes this was attributed to вҖңpublicly available information,вҖқ other times falsely cited as вҖңfrom the Forensic Medicine report.вҖқ Meanwhile, experts appeared on TV explaining the significance of bite marks; retired detectives claimed to have solved murders through them. Thus emerged a market for detectives, retired officers, and self-styled criminologists.
The following three reports represent the most professionally written among them вҖ” yet they share the same fundamental flaws:

SABAH вҖ“ Was Narin GГјran Found? Major development in DiyarbakДұr disappearance! Forensics confirm: Bite marks on brotherвҖҷs arm[2]
GAZETE PENCERE вҖ“ Major development in missing Narin case: Bite marks on brotherвҖҷs arm not from Narin[3]
TAKVД°M вҖ“ Missing Narin case bite-mark detail! Detective comments: I once solved a murder from a bite mark.[4]

Despite these reports, social media quickly escalated matters, claiming вҖңBite marks confirmed to belong to Narin.вҖқ

title: The Narin GГјran Case #2
section: The Commander and The MinisterвҖҷs Statements
text:
The Commander and The MinisterвҖҷs Statements
The core issue here was the leaks. At the time, in August, these might not have seemed serious, but by September, unfiltered information from law enforcement to journalists became uncontrollable. As public noise grew, вҖңofficial statementsвҖқ вҖ” no matter the intent вҖ” served as confirmation for emerging theories. On August 28, when we learned that Enes GГјran had been detained, the Provincial Gendarmerie Commander announced:

вҖңWe have evidence and statements. We tracked NarinвҖҷs movements in the village minute by minute. We are following the evidence closely вҖ” we are very close to a conclusion. Inevitably, we view this as a criminal case, considering all possibilities.вҖқ

The commanderвҖҷs statement was essentially a вҖңpublic relationsвҖқ effort: search operations had covered 12,565 vehicles and 32,952 people; 47 houses and 22 abandoned buildings were inspected; 78 surveillance cameras were reviewed; 66 people were interviewed, including 8 suspects and 58 witnesses. It was clearly an attempt to defend his team against criticism вҖ” explaining how extensive the efforts were. That same day, the Minister of Justice also made a statement, saying there was one person in custody and that all details were being examined, expressing hope that Narin would be found safe.
Taken together вҖ” the statements from the commander, the governor, and the minister вҖ” they collectively breached the principle of silence. Yes, public communication is necessary, but when a gendarmerie commander speaks instead of the prosecutor, and both the governor and minister add comments, the risk of distorting the investigation is immense. In a climate where judicial independence carries little weight, such interventions by political and bureaucratic figures have become routine вҖ” which only normalizes the problem further. In truth, their words tend to influence public opinion rather than inform it, serving as subtle admissions that the judiciary is not truly independent.
Among these three statements, the commanderвҖҷs was particularly significant: вҖңWe are very close to a conclusion. We have strong evidence.вҖқ Naturally, this was interpreted as pointing to Enes GГјran, who had just been detained. The ministerвҖҷs statement was seen as confirmation. When uncle Salim GГјran was detained on August 31, the implication expanded further. By early September, the MinisterвҖҷs remarks made the issue even clearer вҖ” the political authority itself was hinting at guilt.

title: The Narin GГјran Case #2
section: A Futile Attempt at Silence: The Broadcast Ban
text:
A Futile Attempt at Silence: The Broadcast Ban
Of course, all these statements were deeply tied to the political climate. On August 24, 2024, DEM Party MP Ceylan AkГ§a questioned the suspicious power outage in the village during critical search hours, saying, вҖңWe cannot accept a power cut that could allow potential perpetrators to move freely.вҖқ Social media reactions were, predictably, filled with hatred. On August 27, the HГңDA PAR deputy leader visited the GГјran family, saying he would maintain dialogue with officials. Within days, that visit was reframed as evidence of the familyвҖҷs nature, and by early September, the issue had evolved into an outright вҖңHizbullah connectionвҖқ narrative вҖ” one of the loudest sources of noise.
In response to growing leaks, the DiyarbakДұr ProsecutorвҖҷs Office requested a broadcast ban on August 29. The DiyarbakДұr 5th Criminal Court of Peace granted it the same day. The head of RTГңK (the media regulatory authority) then stated: вҖңThe sharing of unverified information and speculation in the media undermines the sensitivity of a case involving a child. We strongly urge all broadcasters to comply with the ban to protect the family and ensure an effective investigation.вҖқ
In an environment where institutions responsible for maintaining silence either did not exist or were not functioning, this move could not be more than a late bureaucratic plea for silence. Those same authorities who were supposed to safeguard silence were, in practice, violating it. Moreover, the ban itself вҖ” given the stateвҖҷs poor record in such matters вҖ” immediately became part of new narratives and fueled new theories.
Linked to this overture of three incidents, but far more consequential than the others, was a critical development on August 31, 2024, when Salim GГјran was taken into custody. A Facebook post made under the name Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca, and the way that post was turned into вҖңnews,вҖқ caused events to spiral out of control. This incident вҖ” along with subsequent reporting and statements about Salim GГјran вҖ” is pivotal both for this analysis and for the case itself, and it must be examined in detail.
That will be the subject of the third section.



External References (4)
