title: Narin GГјran Case #4
text:
AUTHOR


Ali Duran Topuz


LAST UPDATED


October 30, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 16 min


Word Count


2936
On 31 August 2024, the day Salim GГјran was taken into custody, a post appeared on Facebook (again, in response to an earlier post by Salim GГјran) which the media/social media immediately and unquestioningly jumped on. It was written by someone named вҖңMurat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca,вҖқ and the вҖңtiming,вҖқ language, contentвҖ”everything about the text was striking.
First, the full text:
It did not stop there, another user named Yusuf Mahir KoГ§yiДҹit stepped in, he wrote the following:
Then another account appeared, named Murat Akkaya, this is what he wrote:

title: Narin GГјran Case #4
section: The Report Published on the Day of Custody
text:
The Report Published on the Day of Custody
Before moving on to the analysis of these texts, a reminder: the same day, a significant report based on information given by law enforcement was also published. This report, which appeared on DW Turkish, was used by many sites in line with their own style (some of them even made it a вҖңgallery,вҖқ that is, they put one or two sentences under each photo with many photos). The report included the following information:

DNA found on the driverвҖҷs seat of the vehicle used by S.G. matched the DNA samples taken from Narin GГјranвҖҷs clothes and blanket. This development made the uncle a suspect.
In addition, it was determined that uncle S.G. left the village quickly 15 minutes after his niece Narin GГјran was last seen with her friends.
The gendarmerie teams also examined the phone of the detained uncle. As a result of the examination, it was determined that S.G. had deleted WhatsApp messages and calls from his phone.




A Facebook message posted by a fake Facebook user named Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca.


The journalist who accessed this information was one known for doing their job well, and it was natural for the report to be shared, but the Facebook post contained parallel content and, because it featured a kind of story, a scenario, it spread in an unbelievable way in a short time; now, with this information, let us look at the Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca post sentence by sentence:

title: Narin GГјran Case #4
section: Reference to the 28 August Post
text:
Reference to the 28 August Post
The following part in the first sentence of the text takes us to the вҖңfamily and uncle relations are a bit complicatedвҖқ expression in the X post shared on 28 August from a very strong account with 9 million followers:

вҖңIn the HTS records there are both messages and call logs with Narin GГјranвҖҷs mother before and after the incident.вҖқ

Its immediate continuation, however, overlaps with the information in the report on DNA published the same day:

вҖңвҖҰ on the day of the incident 15вҖ“20 minutes after Narin disappeared Salim GГјran left the village quickly in his own vehicle, he doesnвҖҷt even get fuel, he enters a gas station, he buys wet wipes from the market.вҖқ

The news sentence in the report may not be particularly striking due to its dryness, but it is obvious that it will be highly striking within a narrative, because it is not only reported that he left, it is also stated that he left quickly. Moreover, the supposedly factual detail that he bought wet wipes from the market has a special importance, as very few expressions could better convey that he had done a dirty job.
The fact that the camera footage was given to the gendarmerie commander and would not be given to the media, etc. is not written merely as ornament; these are used as elements to increase credibility, an early answer to the sort of social media question вҖңthen why is there no footageвҖқ: There isnвҖҷt any because the gendarmerie took it, and in fact it is a crime to give it. Another critical sentence in the post that takes us back to the famous accountвҖҷs statement вҖңfamily relations are complicatedвҖқ is this: вҖңThis mukhtar was also in contact with NarinвҖҷs brother Enes.вҖқ

title: Narin GГјran Case #4
section: A Timeline Embedded in the Post
text:
A Timeline Embedded in the Post
Thus, uncle and mother were seated next to the brother who had already been marked as guilty through the dog/cannabis frenzy since 27 August. But the issues in this short post are not limited to these:

вҖңand in the camera footage unfortunately Narin is either unconscious or lying in the front seat strangled by SalimвҖҷs hands and there was a dark brown blanket on top of her.вҖқ

The most critical point of this part of the post, as will be discussed later, is not so much that the child is вҖңeither unconscious or strangled by SalimвҖҷs hands,вҖқ but that she is in the front seat. According to the report published the same day, NarinвҖҷs DNA sample was found on the front seat.
The last sentences are not insignificant either: for those who might ask вҖңwould a man carrying a strangled child on the front seat go into a gas station,вҖқ the following sentence was written preemptively: вҖңSince the gas station staff also knew the mukhtar there was no doubtвҖҰвҖқ On the other hand, the fact that he entered the gas station вҖңquicklyвҖқ and parked the car not in front of the market or at the pump but by the car wash had skillfully inserted suspicion into the text without explicitly stating it.
The critical point of the remaining expressions is: вҖңвҖҰ his phone was off (вҖҰ) and he returned to the village in the dark 2 hours laterвҖҰвҖқ
The information in the post was not only in parallels with the information in the report published and widely shared that day, it was also in special and careful harmony with another piece of information that was not yet known to the public at that time: upon the statement of some children that they had seen Narin at 17:40, law enforcement had focused the investigation around that time, as we later learned. The report said, вҖңit was determined that he left the village 15 minutes after Narin was last seen,вҖқ but no time was given for the departure. The post, however, was arranged exactly according to that time, namely the 17:40 information: he comes quickly, buys wet wipes, leaves, and returns in the dark two hours later.

title: Narin GГјran Case #4
section: Either a Journalist, or a Lawyer, or Law Enforcement
text:
Either a Journalist, or a Lawyer, or Law Enforcement
A journalist reports the news, their source may naturally be law enforcement or other bureaucrats and lawyers in close cooperation with law enforcement, there is no need to repeat the criticism of information leaks by law enforcement, it is a situation we have already become accustomed to but we should not become accustomed to. So who made the post? Who could know so many things? Who could hope to gain what from such a post?
The post itself does not provide evidence on this, of course, but it gives some clues, some data to think with: the text was written by someone who knows incident reporting, little attention was paid to spelling, but this carelessness may carry the possibility of being deliberate, considering the narrative skill. Incident reporting, and being able to embed subtext into it, is not something that can easily be done by people with no experience in this field. Therefore, the likelihood is very high that this text was written by a journalist, a lawyer, or a law enforcement officer.
The fact that the text was drafted in a way that observed consistency with other information (reports and/or posts) that became public between 27 and 31 August is an indication that this was not some kind of trolling activity, something written by a random person just to stir things upвҖ”in other words, it is an indication that it could not have been a coincidence.
The user named Yusuf Mahir KoГ§yiДҹit, who interacted with this post, was both entering the scene in the role of вҖңthe wine-sellerвҖҷs witness is the pitcher-sellerвҖқ and, with a tone that would prompt and cue aggressive statements against Salim GГјran, was triggering those statements.
The account named Murat Akkaya became the first virtual personality to extend the issue to the whole family, and also formed one of the prototypes of statements against the family: вҖңFor hours I have been threatened with death by Salim GГјranвҖҷs elder brother, his younger brother, his family and his wifeвҖҷs family. Threat and insult messages have been sent to me. The owner of the gas station I work at has been threatened.вҖқ The Akkaya personality, with phrases like вҖңhe was sweaty, tense, panickedвҖқ for Salim GГјran, and with an equally effective narrative deliberately careless about spelling, such as вҖңhe asked for wet wipes, he took three,вҖқ or вҖңhe first said cash, he checked his pockets, there was no cash, then he paid by credit card, those slips were given to the police,вҖқ also managed to increase the credibility of the matter.



A Facebook message posted by a fake Facebook user named Murat Akkaya.


The reports that leaned on these posts spread rapidly especially after 4 September. In the meantime, some social media users posted that the accounts were suspicious, that they could be fake, but both the вҖңdigital publicвҖқ that had now вҖңfound the truth, and that truth along with a good/explanatory story tied to itвҖқ and, unfortunately, the journalists, had no time to bother with such things. The public could only learn that the accounts were fake on 2 October, but again, no one went back and corrected their reports, let alone undertook any research into examining the effect those posts had caused and their source.

title: Narin GГјran Case #4
section: There Is Still an MГҮГҮ on Facebook
text:
There Is Still an MГҮГҮ on Facebook
When searched on FB today, a user named Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca still appears. There is information that he is a lawyer, that he joined in April 2015, he made just one post on 9 May 2017, a visual from the Behzat ГҮ. series, which reads: вҖңThere is a full parliamentary system reigning in me, I mean a different voice comes from every head but whatever I say goes.вҖқ (Accessed: 26.10.2025) It got nine likes. There is a heading that says 700 friends but when clicked it does not open.
There is little info in info: we learn that he is male, born in 1989, and speaks Kurdish and English.
There is a favorite quotes section, and there is an answer to it: вҖңHeger em nebin yek, eme herin bДұ yek u yek..вҖқ (I did not touch the spelling.)
Is there a connection between this user and him? Is this a real user but the post that stirred things up was fake? It is not possible to know. Not for us mortals, at least; but in an ideal judicial process, should all the means not be mobilized to find out who made such a strange, critical, and openly investigation-related post/posts? Is the Turkish judiciaryвҖҷs capacity for examining digital matters limited to вҖңFake, opened from abroadвҖқ findings? Are not all public institutions, including universities and companies, obliged to provide the judiciary with the information, equipment, and research facilities they possess when necessary? Even if the accounts were opened from abroad, the information is highly insider.
I will continue, we are still on 31 August.

title: Narin GГјran Case #4
section: NOTES
text:
NOTES

We learned that the post was fake on 2 October 2024 from a report[1] by Murat ЕһekeroДҹlu at HaberTГјrk. In the report, there was also a perspective indicating that the post had similarities with the statements given by Nevzat Bahtiyar, who had by then been arrested. However, in my opinion, the relation with those statements is a different matter, I will return to that in later sections, the truly important aspect is that it was in harmony with the information and developments between 27 and 31 August, and that it later determined the direction of the reports and was treated almost as a case/information that pre-approved the developments.
In his work The MediaвҖҷs 12 Mistakes Faruk Bildirici also discussed this case in detail. Because Brother Faruk is a polite person he called them mistakes, whereas a very significant part of these are nothing but professional misconduct.
One of the people who had addressed the issue earlier was also Rana Polat SГ¶nmez. The relevant section in Rana Polat SГ¶nmezвҖҷs article[2] deals with the issue of time in a different way but in a way that produces the same result, and also uses an important detail about GГјranвҖҷs vehicle in a meaningful way:

The investigations revealed that the accounts in question were fake; there was no such gas station and no such employees as claimed. Moreover, for Salim GГјran to have left the village, stopped at such a gas station and returned, did not fit into the time interval of the incident.
During the first hearing, Nevzat Bahtiyar was asked whether he had seen these posts; he replied, вҖңI did not see, but I heard.вҖқ
However, there was a common aspect between these posts and BahtiyarвҖҷs statement that pointed to material evidence: it was consistent with the DNA data detected in Salim GГјranвҖҷs vehicle. What was interesting was that these posts were published on the day the DNA detection was made. Moreover, the posts gave the impression that they were aware of the investigationвҖҷs scenarios. Mother YГјksel GГјran and brother Enes GГјran were being pointed to, and the communication traffics, which we frequently heard from the media and which are now understood to be baseless, were mentioned. As for uncle Salim GГјran, a scenario was constructed based on the image of him leaving the village at 18:55.
Carrying a corpse in the front seat was not an ordinary thing either, but someone who knew Salim GГјranвҖҷs vehicle could put forward such testimony only in this way; since the rear windows of the vehicle were tinted.

ABOUT THE TITLE: THE DISINFORMATION REVOLUTION
The Information Bomb is the title of a book by the French thinker Paul Virilo. Based on the atomic bomb, вҖңJust as the radioactivity of the components of matter made the atomic bomb possible, so does the interactivity of the components of informationвҖҰвҖқ makes this bomb possible.
The book, which deals with striking theses such as modern scienceвҖҷs departure from its philosophical foundations and taking different paths, states that today the mental analogical approach is steadily fading, and instrumental and numerical approaches are taking its place. вҖңUnlike in the past, science, which now depends on effectiveness rather than truth, is being dragged towards its own collapse, towards a civilizational degeneration.вҖқ (Paul Virilo, The Information Bomb, translated by Kaya Еһahin, Metis YayДұnlarДұ)
If we put media instead of science here, the sentence becomes more suitable for these writings. The situation of the media is too bad to be described merely by the word bad, even the word degeneration may remain an innocent expression.
While saying, вҖңFollowing the atomic bomb, which shattered matter with radioactive energy, at the end of the millennium there emerged the specter of a second bomb. This bomb is the information bomb that, through the interactivity of information, has the power to shatter peace between nations,вҖқ Virilo, in line with the themes of the book, is mostly thinking of international issues and the course of the world. Yet his highly prescient book also helps us understand today that not only peace between nations but also peace between societies, peace within societies, and increasingly society itself is being shattered.
The book also contains observations that will help us understand what has been happening around the Narin GГјran case, both as media and as society, it is possible to say that we are floundering beside the usual technical problems of communication, in the frightening traps brought about by instantaneous commenting, Virilo says: вҖңThese precursory signs show that the real information revolution is in fact the virtual disinformation revolution.вҖқ




External References (2)

title: Joint Call from 128 Figures on the Narin GГјran Case
text:
AUTHOR


bianet


LAST UPDATED


April 4, 2026


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 10 min


Word Count


1544
A total of 128 intellectuals, writers, journalists, academics, lawyers, and politicians вҖ” including Hasan Cemal, Oya Baydar, Cengiz ГҮandar, Г–mer Madra, Г–mer Faruk GergerlioДҹlu, HГјda Kaya, Nurcan Baysal, Murat Г–zbank, Sevilay ГҮelenk, and Еһebnem Korur FincancДұ вҖ” issued a joint statement regarding the Narin GГјran case, which has generated widespread public attention.
The statement emphasised that serious procedural irregularities occurred during the investigation and trial process, that the principle of fair trial was undermined, and that the material truth could not be established.
The text drew attention to shortcomings in the trial process, particularly regarding the evaluation of evidence and the use of digital data, and called for the case to be reconsidered fairly. The signatoriesвҖҷ statement, addressed to both judicial authorities and the public, also highlighted the impact of lynching culture and media pressure on the mechanism of justice.
The full text of the statement is as follows:

title: Joint Call from 128 Figures on the Narin GГјran Case
section: For Justice and Truth: Stop the Lawlessness in the Narin GГјran Case!
text:
For Justice and Truth: Stop the Lawlessness in the Narin GГјran Case!
The killing of 8-year-old Narin GГјran, who disappeared on 21 August 2024 and whose lifeless body was found 19 days later, has left a deep wound in all our hearts. Yet the investigation and prosecution conducted in the wake of this painful event, rather than uncovering the material truth, have turned into a flawed judicial practice riddled with legal violations and breaches of rights, deeply undermining the right to a fair trial.
From day one, the case gave rise to a tabloidized television journalism and social media practice driven largely by ratings concerns, gossip, and sensationalism. Many baseless scenarios were produced. The lynching culture incited by this public outcry demonized the family in the absence of any concrete evidence and stripped them of their right to a fair trial. Instead of seeking the truth, the courts handed down rulings that satisfy no conscience and rest on no concrete evidence, all but operating under the influence of this lynching culture and noise.
The PSA (Prostate Specific Antigen) finding identified on NarinвҖҷs lifeless body вҖ” a finding medically capable of pointing to the suspicion of sexual contact and abuse вҖ” was a critical piece of data, yet the investigation was not adequately deepened on this point. The presence of PSA raises a strong possibility that the case may have a sexual abuse dimension. Despite this, the failure to carry out the advanced and comprehensive analyses requested to determine the source and nature of this finding represents a serious shortcoming in establishing the material truth. Leaving such a vital suspicion unaddressed has both compromised the integrity of the investigation and prevented the identification of possible perpetrators and the true nature of the crime.
One of the greatest absurdities of this case, now before the Constitutional Court, is the technical data referred to as вҖңnarrowed-baseвҖқ (daraltДұlmДұЕҹ baz) analysis, which was used to justify the convictions of the defendants. TГјrkiyeвҖҷs leading digital forensics experts have repeatedly stated that вҖңnarrowed-baseвҖқ analysis does not appear in the literature, has no scientific basis, and that room-by-room location identification cannot be performed through retrospective signal measurements; they have prepared expert opinions to this effect.
Furthermore, the expert opinions submitted to the file regarding the examination of the defendantsвҖҷ digital devices have made it abundantly clear that the allegations and the constructed scenario are not grounded in reality. The expert examinations have shown that digital evidence such as phone image records, background internet activity, and step-counter data does not match the alleged time of the murder or the narrow timeline that was constructed; consequently, the scenario presented to the court was demonstrated to be physically impossible and contrary to the ordinary course of life.
In addition to all these shortcomings, the courtвҖҷs failure to conduct a live on-site reconstruction throughout the trial and the fact that the digital evidence used as the basis for the convictions, such as HTS records, was not transparent, reliable, or amenable to expert review, have fundamentally shaken the principles of a fair trial and the pursuit of material truth. It is unacceptable for the prosecution and the court to set aside the foundational principle of universal criminal law вҖ” in dubio pro reo (the benefit of the doubt belongs to the defendant) вҖ” and to base their judgment on assumptions and unauditable reports.
In this process, family members, foremost among them NarinвҖҷs mother YГјksel GГјran, have been subjected to unjust punishment. Although there is no concrete evidence beyond reasonable doubt that the mother participated in the murder, the manufactured perception of a вҖңguilty motherвҖқ has been turned into a judicial decision and she has been sentenced to aggravated life imprisonment.
While even the reasoned judgment itself acknowledges that the motive for the murder could not be determined, the lives of three family members have been ruined on the basis of assumptions.

Demands
We, the undersigned, demand:

That the true perpetrators and motive of the Narin GГјran murder be uncovered through concrete and verifiable scientific evidence beyond reasonable doubt;
That the verdicts based on flawed and unauditable digital data such as вҖңnarrowed-baseвҖқ analysis вҖ” which experts have rejected as вҖңunscientificвҖқ вҖ” be overturned;
That the influence of lynching culture and media pressure on the judiciary be eliminated; and that, through a new on-site reconstruction and an independent review of the evidence, those who have been wronged вҖ” foremost among them the mother YГјksel GГјran вҖ” be retried fairly.

In this painful event that has touched the entire country, we will not allow the truth to be buried or the process to be surrendered to lynching culture. In the face of this picture that deeply offends the sense of justice, we remind everyone that it is of vital importance for judicial assessments to rest on the strongest and most objective evidence; and we call upon the relevant individuals and institutions to take responsibility.

title: The disgrace of harvesting ratings from pain
text:
AUTHOR


Faruk Bildirici


LAST UPDATED


September 16, 2024


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 8 min


Word Count


1303
This is what has been happening on the screens for days. Broadcasts are being made about the killing of a sweet, charming little girl by trampling on journalistic codes and principles and without any regard for the traumatic effects on society. People are not allowed to live their pain; the entire life of a villageвҖҷs inhabitants is crudely tossed about. A bleeding wound is probed and torn open with a pornographic hunger.
We already knew that some screen-addicted journalists considered themselves experts on everything that comes to mind: politics, education, sports, foreign policy, the judiciary. In discussing the Narin murder вҖ“ together with the media вҖңparsleyвҖқ types who are experts in every field вҖ“ they became police, detectives, prosecutors and judges all at once. In fact, what most of them did was scriptwriting based on assumptions.
The headlines вҖңFour scenarios in the Narin murderвҖқ in HГјrriyet and вҖңHere are those three possibilitiesвҖқ in Korkusuz were striking examples of journalistsвҖҷ assumptions being mistaken for news. A journalist creates a scenario to untie the knot and find answers to questions. Then they investigate and try to find evidence. A scenario, just like claims or rumors, does not by itself constitute news as long as no evidence is found.
From the day NarinвҖҷs body was found, indeed from the first days of her disappearance, the mother, YГјksel GГјran, was branded as вҖңa woman living in an illegitimate relationship.вҖқ There are still those who speak of her relationship with uncle Salim GГјran, who remains in pre-trial detention. For example, Sabah and AHaber published the statement of the detained Nevzat Bahtiyar as an вҖңadmissionвҖқ confirming the relationship between the mother and the uncle. Yet no data has emerged so far to verify these relationship scenarios. On the contrary, her husband Arif GГјran denied it; NarinвҖҷs DNA tests also did not confirm the scenarios about her father.
Still, on Didem Arslan YДұlmazвҖҷs program, sexual innuendos were made by writing that, on the days when Narin was being searched for, YГјksel GГјran had вҖңblood drawn with a syringe she brought with her from the nurses in the ambulance she called to her house, saying вҖҳThe state wants it, a secret test will be done.вҖҷвҖқ Many outlets, from Cumhuriyet to Ekonomim, reported these under вҖңallegationвҖқ headlines but with definitive expressions such as вҖңit has emerged.вҖқ
Emotional exploitation is also at its peak. On Star and Show TV, news of NarinвҖҷs death and her funeral were presented with music like a film credit sequence. The main news bulletin presented by Ece Гңner on Halk TV opened with a lament. On Now TV and some other channels, footage of Narin dancing at a wedding was aired together with the news of her death. Unfortunately, while trying to generate ratings, circulation and clicks off peopleвҖҷs grief, the traumatic impact is being deepened. Yet in news reports, вҖңeffects and music beyond the natural soundвҖқ should not be used, and archival footage should be selected in line with the content of the news.
TV100 reporter Canan AltДұntaЕҹ crying on live broadcast and saying вҖңI saw something, I cannot tellвҖқ was another example of turning journalism into a visual show. A journalist tells and writes what they know. If they cannot verify it and there is an ethical issue, they simply do not say that they have something they cannot tell. Moreover, she later explained that what she had seen was village women reciting prayers in front of a house before NarinвҖҷs body was found. In fact, it was not something that warranted such exaggeration.
Unfortunately, the zeal shown in relaying scenarios, allegations and possibilities was not shown in investigation. For example, it was written for days that вҖңWhatsApp is not providing messages.вҖқ This continued until a reporter from DW called META officials and received the response, вҖңWe do not store messages, we do not have any messages to provide.вҖқ A journalist first investigates and then writes/tells, but in our media this rule has been reversed.
Of course, there were also journalists who pursued the truth, revealed concrete evidence and did successful work. In fact, the real progress was achieved thanks to them; they were the ones who informed the public.
The presumption of innocence also worked in reverse. First, everyone in the village was declared guilty in our media. Some journalists also preemptively decided and ruled that the murder had a political aspect. The evidence is only now beginning to emerge and the perpetrators are being identified; the scenarios that were produced are being refuted one by one.
The authorities bear a large share of responsibility in the emergence of this confusion of information. Even the Minister of the Interior, Ali Yerlikaya, preemptively declared the entire family guilty days before by saying, вҖңIs it not very clear?вҖқ From the outset, the police, gendarmerie and prosecutorвҖҷs office did not establish a transparent channel to inform journalists. By preferring to provide information under the table, leaking the suspectsвҖҷ statement texts, they encouraged rumor- and scenario-based journalism.
Nevertheless, I must say that the mediaвҖҷs embracing of Narin in this way and making her the main agenda item had a major effect on the progress made toward finding the killers. Had there been more care and a race in examples of investigative journalism, results could have been achieved more quickly.

title: The disgrace of harvesting ratings from pain
section: THE JOURNALIST WHO PLAYED AN INTERVIEW ROLE
text:
THE JOURNALIST WHO PLAYED AN INTERVIEW ROLE
AKP DiyarbakДұr MP Galip EnsarioДҹlu stood out in this process as the person who voiced the most inappropriate statements at the most inappropriate times.
His remark to SГ¶zcГј TV about NarinвҖҷs killing, вҖңThere are things that we sometimes do not know, and sometimes know but must not say, because the family are our friends,вҖқ rightly drew criticism. While trying to correct his words by claiming he had been misunderstood, he again spoke to SГ¶zcГј TV. But this time his words became news as вҖңAKP MP signals resignation.вҖқ
As a result, EnsarioДҹlu had to make yet another statement, saying that his words had been distorted and that he was not thinking of leaving politics. The most striking aspect of these developments in terms of journalism was the broadcast SГ¶zcГј made to defend its story.
SГ¶zcГј TV Ankara Bureau Chief Mehmet Bal appeared on screen and aired the audio recording of the telephone interview with EnsarioДҹlu[1]. However, as if a video interview were being conducted, they placed EnsarioДҹluвҖҷs portrait on one side of the screen; on the other side, Mehmet Bal voiced the questions as though EnsarioДҹlu were actually in front of him at that moment and he were conducting a live interview. Incredibly, he acted on screen as a journalist, playing the role of the reporter conducting the interview! Meanwhile, the caption read вҖңEnsarioДҹlu interview on SГ¶zcГј TV,вҖқ and viewers were not warned that this was a вҖңre-enactment.вҖқ





This is an extremely misleading style of reporting that distorts reality and is incompatible with journalism. First, a journalist does not act, does not add acting to the news, and there is no re-enactment in the news. An interview is what it is; you present it as is: if it is audio, it is audio; if it is video, it is video.
Second, when EnsarioДҹluвҖҷs words are broadcast from a recording and Mehmet BalвҖҷs questions are delivered through a re-enactment, it is impossible to be sure how faithfully the original form of the interview is reflected. For example, in a long question, Mehmet Bal tries to get EnsarioДҹlu to say that he is not blaming SГ¶zcГј TV; he ends one of his questions with the sentence, вҖңYou are saying that there was no distortion in the questions we directed, am I understanding correctly?вҖқ EnsarioДҹluвҖҷs answer, however, does not directly correspond to this sentence.
The fact that technology allows it does not make it ethical to present a telephone interview as if it were a video interview on television. There may be no limits in technology, but there are limits in journalism; journalists, editors and presenters must internalize those boundaries.



External References (1)

title: What Was Written, What Was Said About the Narin GГјran Murder?
text:
AUTHOR


Faruk Bildirici

August 14, 2025


SOURCE


Original Source

1882
I have compiled the headlines and summaries of news reports and commentary published in the media during the roughly 1.5-month period from Narin GГјranвҖҷs disappearance on 21 August 2024 to the opening days of the trial. In doing so, I prepared a roadmap for myself to examine the journalistic errors related to the Narin murder.
Additionally, for those who have read my analysis titled вҖң12 media errors that influenced the judiciary in the Narin murderвҖқ, the following timeline emerged so that they can see the atmosphere that was created in the media during those days:

title: What Was Written, What Was Said About the Narin GГјran Murder?
section: TIMELINE OF NARIN COVERAGE (21 August вҖ“ 7 November 2024)
text:
TIMELINE OF NARIN COVERAGE (21 August вҖ“ 7 November 2024)

21 AUGUST

Narin went to her Quran course after having lunch. However, she did not return home after the course. Footage appeared[1] on the primary schoolвҖҷs camera at 15:15 (TV100).



22 AUGUST

Gendarmerie, AFAD and medical teams launched a search in the village. A single slipper, claimed to belong to Narin, was found 3 kilometres from the village.



24 AUGUST

Mobilisation for Narin / Mother YГјksel GГјran: The slipper found is not NarinвҖҷs (AkЕҹam / Sabah / ATV / Haber Global[2]).



25 AUGUST

She vanished on the hilltop road / NarinвҖҷs last image emerged / A 100-person special team is searching[3] (AkЕҹam / Sabah / SГ¶zcГј / DHA / Star TV).



26 AUGUST

Blood on the rock does not belong to Narin / False tip-offs infuriated searchers (AkЕҹam).
NarinвҖҷs mother YГјksel GГјran appeared on Didem Aslan YДұlmazвҖҷs programme (Show TV).



27 AUGUST

Blood trace belongs to the cousin (AkЕҹam / Sabah).
NarinвҖҷs father protested against false tip-offs[4] (TGRT Haber).



28 AUGUST

Gendarmerie Major General SelГ§uk YДұldДұrДұm issued a statement[5] saying вҖңThe circle is closing, Narin could be found at any momentвҖқ (Medyascope).



29 AUGUST

Elder brother Enes was detained due to a bite mark on his arm. However, the owner of the teeth marks could not be determined[6] (HГјrriyet / Sabah / Enson Haber / Halk TV).
DiyarbakДұr 5th MagistrateвҖҷs Court imposed a media blackout[7] (A Haber).



30 AUGUST

It was assessed that Enes having showered three times that day may have contributed to the inability to identify the owner of the teeth marks on his arm (Sabah).



31 AUGUST

Uncle Salim GГјran, in whose vehicle NarinвҖҷs DNA sample was found, was detained[8] (Halk TV).
NarinвҖҷs elder brother released / Cross-examination of family members (AkЕҹam / Sabah / SГ¶zcГј TV).



1 SEPTEMBER

New details emerged from the uncleвҖҷs phone / Why did the uncle delete[9] his phone records? (Halk TV[10] / Sabah)



2 SEPTEMBER

The hashtag #NarinNerede became one of the most shared hashtags in TГјrkiye on X.
Teams searching for Narin found clothing in a cornfield / The entire village is installing cameras / NarinвҖҷs uncle is being questioned by a special team (AkЕҹam / Sabah).
NarinвҖҷs uncle Salim GГјran was arrested on charges of premeditated murder (Haber Global[11]).
Latest findings in the search for Narin / Retired detective assessed the case (NTV[12]).



3 SEPTEMBER

Uncle arrested for вҖҳpremeditated murderвҖҷ / It had emerged that the uncleвҖҷs phone was off for вҖң1 hourвҖқ (AkЕҹam / Sabah).
Justice Minister TunГ§ spoke about the uncle for the first time (Haber Global[13]).



4 SEPTEMBER

Search operations were conducted in the cemetery with underground imaging equipment under gendarmerie escort (HГјrriyet / Haber Global[14]).
NarinвҖҷs DNA found on the driverвҖҷs seat of the uncleвҖҷs vehicle / Uncle could not recall the 2-hour-40-minute gap (AkЕҹam).
Shocking claim from a petrol station employee! Was she lying unconscious in her uncleвҖҷs vehicle? (A Haber / GerГ§ek GГјndem / Takvim / CNN TГјrk / Karar / T24 / TГјrkiye)
Striking development in the Narin case! Were the uncleвҖҷs phone records accessed[15]? (Halk TV)



5 SEPTEMBER

Vomit, urine and blood traces in the uncleвҖҷs vehicle belong to Narin (AkЕҹam).
Cemetery searched with underground imaging equipment (NTV[16] / Sabah).
The mystery of 18:47 in the Narin GГјran case (ATV[17] Main News 5 September 2024).



6 SEPTEMBER

NarinвҖҷs friends are receiving psychological support (AkЕҹam / Sabah / Tele1[18]).



7 SEPTEMBER

A new tip-off came in; search operations intensified[19] at EДҹertutmaz Creek (NTV / AA / SГ¶zcГј TV).



8 SEPTEMBER

Missing NarinвҖҷs lifeless body was found in the creek after 19 days / 24 people, including her mother, father and sibling, were detained (NTV).
AKP DiyarbakДұr MP Galip EnsarioДҹlu: вҖңThere are things we sometimes do not know, and sometimes know but must not say. Because the family are also our friends.вҖқ (KRT TV[20])
Did she witness things she should not have seen involving her uncle? (TGRT[21])
Expert analysed the footage: She was first strangled, then stabbed (Haber Global[22]).



9 SEPTEMBER

Who killed her, who kept silent? 23 people, including family members, are in custody. Now the task is to identify those who committed the murder and those who knew and stayed silent. Is the uncle the killer? (HГјrriyet)
Was her disabled sibling also killed? / A secret witness gave testimony about those involved in the murder (AkЕҹam / SГ¶zcГј[23] / Halk TV[24]).
The secret of the murder lies in messages: The prosecutorвҖҷs office is pursuing the messages deleted by jailed uncle Salim GГјran. (Sabah)
The Hizbullah riddle in NarinвҖҷs village (SГ¶zcГј TV). Claim that NarinвҖҷs family is connected to Hizbullah (Tele1).
Narin protest in DiyarbakДұr: вҖңMay the hands that touched Narin be brokenвҖқ, вҖңKiller Hizbullah, collaborator AKPвҖқ (Bianet)
Media blackout lifted (Halk TV).
Fatih Portakal: вҖңA dark family. From the governor to the gendarmerie, from ErdoДҹan to the Justice Minister, from the Interior Minister to Galip EnsarioДҹlu вҖ” everyone knew the killer. The sisterвҖҷs departure from life is also full of question marksвҖқ (SГ¶zcГј TV[25]).
Д°smail Saymaz: вҖңThere are inconsistencies in Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs statementвҖҰвҖқ (Halk TV[26]).



10 SEPTEMBER

Take this corpse and dispose of it / The first confession came from plasterer Nevzat B. (HГјrriyet / AkЕҹam / Medyascope / HabertГјrk[27] / SГ¶zcГј TV[28]).
Gendarmerie is pursuing the second person in the vehicle (Sabah).
NazlДұ ГҮelik: вҖңEveryone kept silent, everyone turned a blind eyeвҖҰвҖқ (Star TV)
Unbelievable developments in NarinвҖҷs death: Her uncle may turn out to be her father (SГ¶zcГј TV[29]).
Was NarinвҖҷs death a collective murder? Were immoral relationships the cause? (CNN TГјrk[30])
Blood-chilling detail about NarinвҖҷs death! (SГ¶zcГј TV[31]).
Retired police officer said вҖңThe uncle is experiencedвҖқ (SГ¶zcГј TV[32]).



11 SEPTEMBER

Which one is the killer: the brother or the uncle? / 4 scenarios in the Narin murder (HГјrriyet)
According to senior officials, Narin may have been killed because she witnessed a scene in their own home (Hande FДұrat).
Her sisterвҖҷs file was reopened: TГјlin GГјranвҖҷs suspicious death is also being investigated (AkЕҹam / Halk TV[33] / CNN TГјrk[34]).
Uncle Salim is NarinвҖҷs killer: Informant Nevzat Bahtiyar stated in his new testimony that the young girl was murdered by uncle Salim GГјran (Sabah).
The 25-minute mystery in the Narin murder: Whatever happened, it happened in this red car (Haber Global[35]).
Breaking development: The вҖҳ3 stonesвҖҷ detail in the Narin murder! The informant contradicted himself (SГ¶zcГј[36]).



12 SEPTEMBER

Where does Nevzat fit in? 5 contradictions emerged across Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs three separate statements (HГјrriyet).
Was the jailed uncleвҖҷs 353-second phone call a diversion? (AkЕҹam).
Illicit affair confession (Sabah[37] / Haber Global[38]).
Green headscarf detail in the Narin murder (Haber Global[39]).



13 SEPTEMBER

TavЕҹantepeвҖҷs collaborators: Villagers who wanted to misdirect the investigation filed false tip-offs saying вҖҳThe body is hereвҖҷ for 19 days (HГјrriyet).
The motherвҖҷs contradictory statement (A. Selvi).
Mother YГјksel GГјran and elder brother Enes GГјran were arrested on murder charges (HГјrriyet / Sabah / Halk TV[40]).
Uncle made his niece give a false statement / вҖҳSay you saw her at 17:40вҖҷ (AkЕҹam).
The recording that will solve the murder on Halk TV / Uncle Salim asks his worker: вҖңDid Narin die or is she alive?вҖқ (Ferit Demir / Halk TV)
Villagers hurriedly installed cameras at their homes (HГјrriyet / F. KДұzДұlkoyun).
Most homes in the village had security cameras installed while the search for Narin was underway (Now TV / A Haber[41]).



14 SEPTEMBER

The devilвҖҷs triangle: The entire family is involved (HГјrriyet).
SalimвҖҷs conversation with his worker was deciphered: вҖңShe hasnвҖҷt died yetвҖқ (AkЕҹam / SГ¶zcГј TV[42]).
вҖңвҖҳI told him to tell everything,вҖҷ he did not listenвҖқ: Jailed uncleвҖҷs wife Melek GГјran exposed the lies (Sabah).
Communications expert HГјseyin Kazan analysed body language (A Haber[43] / Takvim).



15 SEPTEMBER

Two chilling words: вҖңShe hasnвҖҷt died yet!вҖқ (HГјrriyet / Haber Global[44]).
Audio recordings will crack the secret (AkЕҹam).
Following uncle Salim GГјran, Nevzat Bahtiyar, mother YГјksel and elder brother Enes were also arrested (Halk TV[45]).
Many scenarios, no confession (Sabah).
What kind of conscience is this! The family filed false tip-offs to prevent Narin from being found (Tele1[46]).
Forensic medicine expert said вҖңOrganisedвҖқ (SГ¶zcГј TV[47] / Tele1[48]).



16 SEPTEMBER

They even started a fire (HГјrriyet).
They threw investigators off track with 50 fake tip-offs! (AkЕҹam / Sabah).
Did elder brother Enes GГјran kill Narin? (Sabah).
Beyond the devilвҖҷs imagination (SГ¶zcГј TV[49]).
They used 6 different methods to keep NarinвҖҷs body hidden (Tele1[50]).
Red slippers were placed at every spot in the village to prevent Narin from being found (SГ¶zcГј TV[51]).



17 SEPTEMBER

Planning at home, murder in the barn (HГјrriyet).
The sister-in-law revealed the barn secret (AkЕҹam / Haber Global / Yeni Akit / Star).
What did uncle Salim and sister-in-law Hediye discuss? (Sabah).
Planning at home, murder in the barn! (Tele1[52] / CNN TГјrk[53]).
Claim that NarinвҖҷs decomposition fluid was found on the uncleвҖҷs hands (NTV[54]).



18 SEPTEMBER

Strangulation evidence in the car (AkЕҹam / SГ¶zcГј TV[55]).
Arrested for murder, played dumb (Sabah).
Twisted relationships in the dark village (Show TV).
Narin case solved! Fatih Portakal (SГ¶zcГј TV[56]).



19 SEPTEMBER

We asked forensic medicine experts: Does the decomposition fluid mean вҖҳThe uncle is the killerвҖҷ? (HГјrriyet).

20 SEPTEMBER

21 SEPTEMBER

22 SEPTEMBER

23 SEPTEMBER

DNA traces were searched for in the informantвҖҷs barn (AkЕҹam / Sabah).

24 SEPTEMBER

26 SEPTEMBER

YГјksel GГјran washed the laundry and carpets and bathed twice on the day her daughter disappeared (SГјperhaber).



27 SEPTEMBER

NarinвҖҷs sister-in-law Hediye GГјran was arrested on charges of incitement to premeditated murder (HГјrriyet).
The secrets of NarinвҖҷs mother YГјksel GГјran and sister-in-law Hediye GГјran were revealed (SГ¶zcГј TV[64]).



28 SEPTEMBER

29 SEPTEMBER

One week of footage missing from the camera at NarinвҖҷs uncle HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs house (AkЕҹam / CNN TГјrk[66] / Halk TV[67] / SГ¶zcГј).



30 SEPTEMBER

1 OCTOBER

2 OCTOBER

7 OCTOBER

Presidential Chief Adviser Saadet OruГ§ challenged the GГјran family (YouTube[71]).

During the search for Narin GГјran, the gendarmerie visited a faith healer (DW TГјrkГ§e[74] / Rudaw / A Haber / Halk TV[75]).

title: To Kill a Mockingbird in DiyarbakДұr: The Forensic Execution of YГјksel GГјran
text:
AUTHOR


Hilal Seven


LAST UPDATED


March 14, 2026


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 7 min


Word Count


1063
In the sweltering heat of Maycomb, Alabama, Tom Robinson was convicted not by evidence, but by the вҖңbanality of prejudiceвҖқ вҖ“ the collective need of a terrified society for a culprit who looked the part. Decades later, a similar tragedy has unfolded in DiyarbakДұr, Turkey.
In August 2024, 8-year-old Narin GГјran vanished from a Quran course. After 19 days, her body was found hidden under stones in a riverbed. While the nation mourned, the focus shifted from the predator to an easier target: her mother, YГјksel GГјran. She became the modern Tom Robinson, sacrificed on the altar of a sensationalist media and a judiciary that decided her guilt before the first autopsy.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird in DiyarbakДұr: The Forensic Execution of YГјksel GГјran
section: Where the Rose Ends and the Razor Begins
text:
Where the Rose Ends and the Razor Begins

Lacking immediate answers, the media handed the public вҖңrazor bladesвҖқ. Within days, YГјksel GГјran was no longer a grieving mother but a вҖңcold-hearted accompliceвҖқ in a televised drama.

Marina AbramovicвҖҷs 1974 performance, Rhythm 0, revealed a terrifying truth: when a person is stripped of humanity, the crowd chooses the razor over the rose. As Abramovic stood still, offering objects for pleasure or pain, the public dove headfirst into violence.
The GГјran family saw the truth in AbramovicвҖҷs work firsthand. Lacking immediate answers, the media handed the public вҖңrazor bladesвҖқ. Within days, YГјksel GГјran was no longer a grieving mother but a вҖңcold-hearted accompliceвҖқ in a televised drama. This was a modern stoning performed with microphones, turning a house of mourning into a crime scene of the imagination.



Narin ve YГјksel GГјran

title: To Kill a Mockingbird in DiyarbakДұr: The Forensic Execution of YГјksel GГјran
section: When Physics Refutes the Prosecution
text:
When Physics Refutes the Prosecution
The prosecutionвҖҷs narrative eventually hit the wall built by the familyвҖҷs attorney, Onur AkdaДҹ, and forensic informatics expert Tuncay BeЕҹikГ§i.
BeЕҹikГ§iвҖҷs reports[1] proclamation and a thorough critique: вҖңPerception is being managed through media and bureaucracy.вҖқ He dismantled the вҖңnarrowed cell tower analysisвҖқ (daraltДұlmДұЕҹ baz) вҖ“ a technique claiming meter-by-meter accuracy, which BeЕҹikГ§i proved has zero standing in international forensic literature.
The findings by AkdaДҹ and BeЕҹikГ§i are irrefutable:
The pedometer truth: During the alleged murder, Salim GГјranвҖҷs phone вҖ“ NarinвҖҷs uncle вҖ“ recorded zero activity. His pedometer showed only 45 steps, and the device was connected to a fast-charging cable.
The digital footprints: At 15:28 вҖ“ the supposed moment of concealment вҖ“ Salim was paying bills via mobile banking and browsing news.

While the GГјrans never deleted content from their devices, the stateвҖҷs вҖңstar witness,вҖқ Nevzat Bahtiyar, performed multiple professional data wipes.

Onur AkdaДҹ has highlighted[2] a critical institutional failure: an audio recording from 15:08 вҖ“ proving Salim was home with his children вҖ“ was mysteriously deleted 24 hours after the phone was handed over to the gendarmerie.
While the GГјrans never deleted content from their devices, the stateвҖҷs вҖңstar witness,вҖқ Nevzat Bahtiyar вҖ“ who confessed to hiding the body вҖ“ performed multiple professional data wipes. BeЕҹikГ§iвҖҷs analysis revealed Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs phone was вҖңdeadвҖқ during the murder, suggesting an ambush confirmed by specialized forensic imaging.
Furthermore, Professor Dr.В Veysi ГҮeri revealed the presence of PSA (Prostate-Specific Antigen) on the victimвҖҷs body вҖ“ biological proof of a single-perpetrator sexual assault. This вҖңsmoking gunвҖқ points to an individual predator, yet it was sidelined to maintain the вҖңfamily conspiracyвҖқ script.
The DiyarbakДұr courtroom echoed Hannah ArendtвҖҷs Banality of Evil. Nevzat Bahtiyar, the neighbor who admitted to hiding the body, was labeled a вҖңpoor soulвҖқ (gariban) by the media. This allowed a cold-blooded accomplice to manipulate the court while the GГјran family was demonized. Forensic data suggests Bahtiyar was the only one whose presence matched the timing of the ambush.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird in DiyarbakДұr: The Forensic Execution of YГјksel GГјran
section: Mourning in a Forbidden Language
text:
Mourning in a Forbidden Language
YГјksel GГјran, a native Kurdish speaker, was forced to defend her life in Turkish under immense psychological coercion, mirroring Alejandro G. IГұГЎrrituвҖҷs Babel: a mother screams her grief in a language that is not the one she used for lullabies, and justice becomes a cruel translation. Her primal feryad (lament) was treated as mere noise by the state.
The GГјran case is a Kurdish tragedy. In a polarized landscape, the family committed the sin of remaining linguistically Kurdish but ideologically aligned with the state. Consequently, they were punished with silence.
Amidst technical jargon stands Remziye, YГјkselвҖҷs 80-year-old mother. Her journey to the distant cells of Erzincan Prison evokes YДұlmaz GГјneyвҖҷs Yol (The Road). Trapped between the stateвҖҷs weight and suffocating traditions, her only weapon is her prayer rug. The Kurdish WomenвҖҷs MovementвҖҷs refusal to support this grandmother is the ultimate indictment of modern morality.





On International WomenвҖҷs Day, YГјkselвҖҷs name was an invisible stain. Human rights organizations abandoned her because she wasnвҖҷt a political activist. When ideology replaces evidence, justice is the first casualty.

title: To Kill a Mockingbird in DiyarbakДұr: The Forensic Execution of YГјksel GГјran
section: The Silence of Conscience
text:
The Silence of Conscience
The most dangerous weapon was the microphone. During the initial search, a вҖңmedia gallowsвҖқ was constructed. Reporters sought ratings through a modern witch hunt, broadcasting baseless allegations as fact. This pre-packaged guilt pressured the judiciary; to acquit the family now would be to admit to a 19-day-long lie. The media didnвҖҷt just report the news; they became the architects of a life sentence.

This is fundamentally a Kurdish problem вҖ“ the tragedy of a people whose mother tongue is suppressed and whose identity is hollowed out.

This is more than a legal failure; it is a deep-seated societal sickness вҖ“ the byproduct of a century of a rigid, fascist education system that manufactures enemies. It is the effort of an unconscious public, deprived of true democracy, to sanitize a killer by sacrificing the innocent.



KadДұkГ¶yвҖҷde protesto


This is fundamentally a Kurdish problem вҖ“ the tragedy of a people whose mother tongue is suppressed and whose identity is hollowed out. It is the story of minds broken by the state, turned against one another in a fratricidal struggle. It is a crisis of the вҖңimpoverished otherвҖқ. It is a human catastrophe where state institutions abandon the defenseless, and self-proclaimed democrats stain their hands with evil for public relations. In this world, a вҖңviral momentвҖқ outweighs the sanctity of a motherвҖҷs life.
In To Kill a Mockingbird, Maycomb returns to вҖңnormalвҖқ, leaving Tom Robinson in the dust. In Turkey, the news cycle has moved on, but YГјksel remains behind bars, and the true killer remains in the shadows. When we ignore the forensic truth provided by experts like BeЕҹikГ§i and advocates like AkdaДҹ, we execute Justice itself.
Forgive us, YГјksel. History will remember Narin, whose song was cut short. It will remember YГјksel, struck through her womanhood. And it will remember the truth, which, though buried under stones, refuses to die.
вҖңMockingbirds donвҖҷt do one thing but make music for us to enjoyвҖҰ ThatвҖҷs why itвҖҷs a sin to kill a mockingbird.вҖқ
вҖ“ Atticus Finch

title: An Open Letter to the Judges of the Narin Case
text:
AUTHOR


E. Miham Akkul


LAST UPDATED


November 7, 2024


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 16 min


Word Count


2992







About 2000 years ago, at the very beginning of our calendar, they tortured Jesus Christ with spiked rods and barbed whips; they placed on the head of this good-hearted man, who spoke of the kingdom of heaven вҖ” or the son of God вҖ” a crown of thorns, and then threw him into a dungeon to bring him before Governor Pontius Pilate the next day. That night, Pontius PilateвҖҷs wife, Claudia Procula вҖ” who, though not considered a saint by Catholics, is regarded as one by the Orthodox вҖ” pleaded with her governor husband: вҖңCan you not pardon him?вҖқ Pontius Pilate said that, should a new uprising of the Israelites occur, Emperor Augustus would have his head; he was trapped between his office and justice. And finally the day came, they stood the Messiah beside Pontius Pilate; he was covered in blood, the vindictive crowd had gathered. вҖңHere is the pitiful state of this insolent one,вҖқ said Pontius Pilate. вҖңIs this not enough?вҖқ
It was not enough! The rabbis cried out in unison, shouting their true demand: Crucify him!вҖҰ
Honorable Judges, Esteemed Justices, I wish to address you in whatever manner most flatters your pride; I do not wish to take risks, for this is my final article on the matter, the last bullet in my rifle. Whether you are truly venerable, whether you possess any respectable side, I do not know. Perhaps you arrived at your positions through various patronages. Perhaps you arrived through hard work, yet all you ever did was pass a few exams, keep your hands clean, become judges, and in this life, take pleasure in the tension your arrival and departure вҖ” вҖңthe judges have come, the judges have goneвҖқ вҖ” created among a few drivers and bailiffs. You strove for this and received your reward. The bailiff, the driver, the police officer, even the young clerk are anxious; rest assured, you have succeeded. But perhaps it is not so; perhaps you realized, even in childhood, how terrible and suffocating tyranny and injustice can be, and you labored since those years, and now you have become such sensitive and serious practitioners of law that you can sense the rise and fall of a prisonerвҖҷs anxiety in the swallow of the throat you button your shirt over every morning in front of the mirror. The weight of the duty to deliver justice presses upon your chest like a gravestone, and you speak only after thinking a thousand times with utmost seriousness. You live with the noble relief of passing through the finest scales the judgments you issue about defendants whose ties to life and relationships hang by a thread. How beautiful it would be if you were such people; in a country with such judges, people would sleep peacefully at night. I did not try to learn who you are; had I tried, I would likely have had no strength left to write this letter вҖ” perhaps it would have scourged me further. You see, prejudice is a terrible thing, is it not, Honorable Judges? At the start of the paragraph, your eyebrows furrowed at my belittling assumptions, then, when I mentioned your noble purpose, a sacred illumination crossed your face, your hearts sweetened. Yes, these pre-judgments are terrible things; according to the man known as the smartest on earth, they are more difficult than atomic physics.
Still, let us incline toward the best possibility and assume you are the kind of human beings who deserve these noble stories, these invaluable quotations from precious novels, and let us come to our main matter.
They placed before you a family whose bodies may not be covered in blood, but whose world has been shattered; this family is called the GГјran Family. They have a ringleader, a cruel village headman: Salim. He is not a Jesus Christ, yet вҖ” just as Jesus advised his apostles eager to stone a prostitute вҖ” he is as guilty as the one among us who must cast the first stone, meaning he is as guilty as all of us, as innocent as all of us. The greatest crime of this headman, who has been stoned by nearly the entire pure and spotless total of 85 million people, is said to be the messages he deleted to cover up the sex workers he slept with and a few illegal electricity dealings вҖ” and, of course, the вҖңDNA taken from a strand of hair fallen from Narin,вҖқ allegedly found on the right rear seat when the entire Narin family got into the headmanвҖҷs car on their way to a wedding. Whether or not you are familiar with what we previously wrote, let us repeat the most crucial point: how this whole affair became a tangled mess and how it ultimately turned into a 14-page document called an indictment. Let us examine once more what happened. As we have written repeatedly, everything began with a fabricated, baseless gas station lie about Salim GГјran in a Facebook post. In that post were NarinвҖҷs mother, the blanket, the front seat, the car, and of course Salim. This entire storm of lies was built upon that, Honorable Judges; the source of the lie вҖңNarin saw something and was killedвҖқ is that Facebook post, which drove an entire country into this mental blackout through the media. In fact, in NevzatвҖҷs first statement вҖ” the very same Nevzat whom the media wrapped delicately in cotton as the real likely murderer вҖ” you can see these exact core details from that post. In his panic, the first thing Nevzat clung to was this Facebook lie. When the crime scene investigation images surfaced recently, our clever public exclaimed, вҖңThis man is too stupid; he couldnвҖҷt have killed this girl!вҖқ As if killing and burying an eight-year-old girl is something an intelligent person would do вҖ” I truly cannot grasp the logic. In any case, while everything was this clear, the Prosecutor did not do what a legal professional should have done and instead tossed the matter to you.
This indictment clearly relies on NevzatвҖҷs latest statement, yet it does not even dare to admit this. The reason for all this timidity is obvious, for the Prosecutor seems to have chosen, as a cunning escape route, not to base everything on the words of a liar full of blatant contradictions. Naturally, the Court will ask the Prosecutor: вҖңYou accepted everything Nevzat said, yet why did you not write вҖҳmurdererвҖҷ when he said вҖҳmurdererвҖҷ?вҖқ Honorable Court Panel, I hope you show a bit of reproach to this Prosecutor who tries to shift responsibility onto you to escape the mediaвҖҷs wrath. I am very curious about what answer he will give: Will he say, вҖңWe cannot trust all of his statements, but I had no choice except to present some of NevzatвҖҷs accusations to you as an indictment in order to save myselfвҖқ? Or will he say, вҖңBe clever like me, hand down the sentence and send it to the Court of Cassation, enough of this dragging onвҖқ?
According to the ProsecutorвҖҷs indictment, Salim GГјran informed the gendarmerie that the Roma had mentioned a red car вҖ” supposedly to secretly expose Nevzat. He supposedly committed such foolishness without caring that he might be implicating himself! His going back and forth to the stream right beside the village was also considered suspicious. If they searched for the girl, it was a crime; if they did not search, it was also a crime вҖ” what else were these вҖңdiabolical yokelsвҖқ supposed to do? Did you, by chance, have any suggestions for them during the search? Forgive me, I forgot: on the third day, villagers were actually prohibited from participating in the search. Yet according to the shabby media, of course they were not eager to search for the child they allegedly killed! In a village surrounded by a stream and an irrigation channel, could there be any place more plausible to look for a missing child? What crime did the Prosecutor perceive in the occasional visits of the village headman and the head of the family to these places? I leave that judgment to you.
It is also sufficient to look merely at the statements of the mother and Enes to see how hollow the idea of an organized murder is; neither of them is speaking in SalimвҖҷs favor вҖ” they have said whatever they know. Enes and Nevzat describe Salim as almost their commander, indeed nearly their servant-master; so what exactly are they trying to protect of SalimвҖҷs, Honorable Judges?
Moreover, in this indictment based solely on NevzatвҖҷs statement, is there any reasonable explanation for the sentence sought for Enes? Is the Prosecutor truly claiming that eight-year-old Narin was more sensitive about honor than a 20-year-old male youth?
They say human memory is afflicted with forgetfulness. Shall we take a look, Honorable Judges, at the chain of lies that has collapsed one by one over the past three months:

The gas station allegation вҖ” debunked.
The claim that the sandal belonged to Narin and the mother was misleading вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that the carpets were washed вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that they used button phones as a precaution вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that the imam was involved вҖ” debunked.
The allegation regarding the вҖңIs the girl dead or alive?вҖқ voice recording вҖ” debunked.
The a burner SIM card allegation вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that the accomplices misleading the gendarmerie were six years old вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that the family member working at the State Hydraulic Works continuously increased the water flow so the body would decompose faster вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that there were cameras in the houses and they hid this вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that Narin was SalimвҖҷs biological daughter вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that NarinвҖҷs DNA was on the front seat and the steering wheel вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that the village was a Hezbollah village вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that ammunition was found in the village вҖ” debunked.
The allegation that Salim misled the gendarmerie and never mentioned the red car вҖ” debunked.

Let us close the topic of this boundless chain of lies with a personal anecdote: one day, a friend well aware of my preoccupation with this matter sent me a message: вҖңSee? You kept desperately defending the family, and now CNN TГңRK is breaking news: вҖҳAccording to the forensic report, the death fluid from NarinвҖҷs throat got onto Salim GГјranвҖҷs hand, and from there onto the steering wheelвҖҰвҖҷвҖқ Yes, Honorable Judges, they said exactly this вҖ” they invented a new term for forensic literature, and to provoke the foolish masses, they called it вҖңdeath fluidвҖқвҖҰ Death fluidвҖҰ
I am inviting you not to conscience or mercy, but to your true duty вҖ” the task to which you owe your livelihood and your entire being вҖ” that is, reasoning, in other words, intellect and discernment, more concretely, the law. And I want to ask you this question: if the body of this delicate child had been found not after 19 days but after 2 days, and if the mediaвҖҷs ratings-driven and social media clownsвҖҷ engagement-hungry fantastical scenarios about the village had not existed, and if Nevzat had given this statement вҖ” would you have believed him?
A thief confesses to burying a bag full of stolen jewelry and tells you, вҖңI didnвҖҷt steal whatвҖҷs inside; someone else stole them and asked me to hide the bag,вҖқ and would you act as though you believed this merely to avoid the wrath of media buffoons?
On November 7, you will surely witness a commotion, an unprecedented uproar in front of the courthouse вҖ” countless vehicles with antennas, around 50 bar associations that have joined the invitation to lynch the family under the pretext of representing rights, law, and justice, perhaps numerous messages from ministers on your phones, various kinds of clownery вҖ” an unparalleled chaos. Do not say, вҖңWell, all these people, all this media вҖ” they canвҖҷt all be fools; of course we must listen to the voice of our noble nation.вҖқ Yes, so many people can be mistaken вҖ” they always have been, and they will continue to be. I hope I am not pressing on your nationalist nerve, but I quote Ali Nesin: вҖңNinety percent of Turkey believes that sixty percent of Turkey is stupid.вҖқ As I said, there is no need to take offense; TurkeyвҖҷs ignorance and foolishness, though delayed, have also appeared in now-just societies a century ago. The best-known example for you is the Dreyfus Affair. They taught you this at the very beginning of your education вҖ” I am writing this letter with that assumption in mind. If, in the future, when this cautionary event is taught in communication and law faculties, you do not want your names to be included, then I kindly ask you to continue reading this letter in good faith.
I implore you, Honorable Judges, free yourselves from the captivity of manipulative questions; there is no murder network masterfully covered up, no organized evil expertly concealed.
On a scorching afternoon, in a central village of DiyarbakДұr, a Savage took a little girl who was joyfully skipping past the front of his house; in that stillest moment when the cicadas lull a person into drowsiness, he strangled her, buried her in that cursed stream, and placed stones over her вҖ” that is all that happened. Saying вҖңI donвҖҷt knowвҖқ is not the same as cunningly hiding what you know, Honorable Judges; sometimes we truly do not see, do not hear, do not know.
The law cannot function based on вҖңWhat will people say,вҖқ Honorable Judges; you do not wear that robe to keep your back warm!
Unfortunately, in Turkey today, there also exists a monstrosity of social media that has slipped into the hierarchy of the judiciary, and you have recently witnessed this uncontrolled madness; this unhinged, hysterical herd has even had graves opened вҖ” that is how powerful they are. Now, so that our esteemed ministers are not subjected for a few days to the lynching of this deranged clan of monkeys, you may be pressured into issuing decisions you do not believe in and do not wish to render. Your situation reminds me not only of Pontius Pilate but also of the character Kaymakam Halil Hilmi Bey in ReЕҹat NuriвҖҷs work, who was subjected to the bullying of the cruel Unionists; in enduring every kind of humiliation, Halil Hilmi Bey would take refuge in the couplet:

If I were on my own, I would never bow to the cruelty of fate;
but in this ruined household, I have a family I must provide for.

Of course, the children and family members waiting at home cannot be dismissed lightly; however, conscience and justice cannot be confined within this narrow couplet either, Honorable Judges. Yours are children and family members, but so are everyone elseвҖҷs. And rest assured, fulfilling the requirements of the law will not bring disastrous consequences upon you; if there is a city more Kurdish and further east, it would be Kermanshah in Iran вҖ” they are certainly not going to send you there. If you say, вҖңYou cannot deceive us with such things; we do what is easiest for us,вҖқ be assured that after doing so, you will be honored with a few days of exaggerated praise from the base and contemptible elements of social and conventional media. вҖңBehold, the great Turkish justice has manifested, long live our judges!вҖқ they will say. But do not be overly proud; when the same people later see a survey concerning trust in the judiciary, they will not hesitate to mark you as 90% untrustworthy. This rotten case, built upon the rumor that вҖңNarin saw something and was killed,вҖқ will thus be closed from your perspective вҖ” you will not have brought trouble upon yourselves. Everything will be that easy, done and over.
But there is also the difficult path вҖ” allow me to describe it. In the passage we quoted above, where TurkeyвҖҷs greatest living writer Orhan Pamuk refers to the vile murderer, there is one more sentence spoken by the victim: вҖңI see now that my life was nothing but a light stretched between two darknesses extending into eternityвҖҰвҖқ Yes, Honorable Judges, that is what we call life. If you are believers, perhaps for you that light continues; in any case, doing something unjust within this eternity вҖ” setting aside whether it is moral or conscientious вҖ” is, in my view, not even a wise act for the sake of our earthly comfort. If you say, вҖңWe have rendered many decisions, this means nothing to us,вҖқ I am not convinced. I do not believe you have ever faced a case as sharp as deciding between aggravated life imprisonment or acquittal for a mother whose child was murdered in a barbaric way; this is not a dilemma to be taken lightly. And do not deceive yourselves by saying, вҖңWe will issue this decision, YargДұtay will fix it anyway.вҖқ Years pass, hearts stop in prison cells, brains bleed, organs turn cancerous вҖ” countless calamities await the convictedвҖҰ This is not a burden one can shoulder. I am not even counting the viran lives of those outside.
And if you still insist on doing as you please, and wish to please the base, ignoble vulgarity of social media and the media, know this: those charlatans will find another spectacle to trample on three days later, but the shadow of the moral burden this matter brings upon you will continue to envelop your life. This injustice will appear to you like a misplaced ghost вҖ” sometimes as a repulsive fly landing on the cheese at a Sunday breakfast table, sometimes bursting forth like a whale from the water at the moment you are on the verge of finding peace while gazing at the sea. For years, whenever it comes to mind, you will lament, saying, вҖңIf only we had fulfilled the requirements of the law on that day,вҖқ and you will grieve. If anything resembling peace and happiness exists on this earth, know that nothing is a more insurmountable, lasting, or unyielding obstacle than pangs of conscience. Ten years pass, twenty years pass вҖ” everyone forgets, but you will remember; you will begin your sorrowful tale with, вҖңOnce, in a village of DiyarbakДұr, on an August afternoon, a little girl was killedвҖҰвҖқ



 Back to top

title: Turkey now has its own Dreyfus affair
text:
AUTHOR


E. Miham Akkul


LAST UPDATED


December 29, 2024


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 3 min


Word Count


609







Nevzat Bahtiyar will be among us by 2026 at the latest; this вҖңfine fellowвҖқ backed by nearly 85 million people obviously can no longer return to вҖңЕһeytantepeвҖқ village, and the pleasure of this manвҖҷs company is something most deserved by the journalists and lawyers who saved him from this murder.

Dialogue

Salim GГјran: Listen up, Nevzat, just now the three of us together wrapped our hands around NarinвҖҷs throat and killed her. There wasnвҖҷt room enough for everyoneвҖҷs hands, but for it to count as a joint murder we still went through all the necessary motions. Why did we do this? Because NarinвҖҷs mother and I were having an affair, and Narin saw us. As for Enes, well, heвҖҷs a very modern Kurdish young man, you know, he doesnвҖҷt care about such things. Narin isnвҖҷt like that вҖ” sheвҖҷs very pious. Goes to Quran courses and so on. Anyway, weвҖҷve decided that if you take this body and dispose of it, we can forget about the three-month estrangement between us. By the way, you can pretend you never even saw Enes if you like вҖ” but in that case, how the judge will hand him aggravated life imprisonment too, that I cannot say.
Nevzat: All right.
Judge: Whereupon it is consideredвҖҰ


The plot of the murder that the supposedly sovereign of Turkish media believed in вҖ” or pretended to believe in вҖ” in December 2024 reads exactly like that uncaricatured dialogue.
(Supposedly sovereign, because I believe almost nothing I read in the Turkish media.)
In this first round of the search for justice we unfortunately could not find that brave and just вҖңhuman beingвҖқ who would turn everything around in the bureaucratic chain.
Unfortunately, we are a handful of people fighting against this unprecedented legal disaster. Facing us is a Turkish-Kurdish alliance of a magnitude and filthiness not seen since the Armenian Massacre.
At the head of this dirty alliance stands one of TurkeyвҖҷs most important fronts of democracy: the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association.
Those who dragged the name of this long-suffering Bar into the cautionary tales of law are now, while everything is in plain sight, smug and proud, thinking they have started climbing the first steps of their political careers by stepping on a bound innocent mother and pushing her behind iron doors.
We have also seen, in this world, a DiyarbakДұr Bar Association that defends a gendarmerie that tries to cover its incompetence with torture.
Nevzat Bahtiyar will be among us by 2026 at the latest; this вҖңfine fellowвҖқ backed by nearly 85 million people obviously can no longer return to вҖңЕһeytantepeвҖқ village, and the pleasure of this manвҖҷs company is something most deserved by the journalists and lawyers who saved him from this murder.
After all, what harm could there be in being neighbors with this gentle man вҖ” together with your wives and children вҖ” who would never lay a finger on a child, who would never kill them?
And besides, God forbid, when an accident happens at home and you call out вҖңNevzaaaaat!вҖқ he comes running, takes the body lying on the floor and walks off with it, and offers the guarantee of not opening his mouth until the body is found вҖ” who wouldnвҖҷt want such a versatile neighbor!
I donвҖҷt think such a legal disaster would even be possible in North Korea; even there, when the offense is not against the system, ordinary cases conclude more justly.
LetвҖҷs shift up a gear: even in 2015 in Raqqa, in a zone under ISIS control where there was no state at all, the outcome would have been more just had this incident occurred there.
These extreme examples cannot capture the gravity of the situation, yet not everything has ended.
TurkeyвҖҷs Dreyfus affair is only just beginning.




 Back to top

title: Has the Narin murder turned into a media spectacle?
text:
AUTHOR


Nazife GГјngГ¶r


LAST UPDATED


September 13, 2024


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 4 min


Word Count


592
The death of 8-year-old Narin GГјran, whose lifeless body was found 19 days after she disappeared in the TavЕҹantepe neighborhood of DiyarbakДұrвҖҷs BaДҹlar district, deeply affected all of Turkey.
With arrests, releases, search efforts, claims and speculations, the case shook the media agenda and sparked broad public reaction, bringing back into discussion the mediaвҖҷs approach to women and child murders. While some newspapers covered NarinвҖҷs death with emotional language such as вҖңShe became an angelвҖқ and вҖңHer eyes were sparkling,вҖқ social media influencers screamed on television, and a reporter commented, вҖңI had a feeling she would be found today.вҖқ In addition, a news anchor delivered the news while crying live on air.
How should such incidents be covered in the media, and what points require attention? ГңskГјdar University Rector Prof.В Dr.В Nazife GГјngГ¶r evaluated how Narin GГјranвҖҷs death was treated in the media and the impact of these approaches on society.

title: Has the Narin murder turned into a media spectacle?
section: Ratings, circulation, personal showmanship
text:
Ratings, circulation, personal showmanship
Stating that such incidents can sometimes turn into spectacles, Nazife GГјngГ¶r said:
вҖңSome groups try to turn such incidents to their own advantage. There is a horrific incident, yet some groups try to derive maximum benefit from it. Unfortunately, our media also pursued maximum benefit. Some turned this grave incident into a ratings concern, some into a circulation concern, and others into their own personal show,вҖқ she said.

title: Has the Narin murder turned into a media spectacle?
section: вҖңA journalist has no such rightвҖқ
text:
вҖңA journalist has no such rightвҖқ
On some broadcasts, news presenters deliver the news while crying. This overshadows the incident itself. A journalist has no such right; using the incident to stage oneвҖҷs own show is unacceptable. We conceal the event with our own tears; instead of conveying information, we perform our own show. This must be taken seriously. Now they will say: вҖңWe are human too, we cannot stand it, we get emotional.вҖқ You must stand it; you must organize your emotions somehow. Deliver your news, and after you finish, go behind the camera and cry, but why cry in front of the screens?вҖқ

title: Has the Narin murder turned into a media spectacle?
section: вҖңTargeting is not the mediaвҖҷs jobвҖқ
text:
вҖңTargeting is not the mediaвҖҷs jobвҖқ
вҖңSome reporters make news in constant tears in the village where the incident took place but say, вҖҳI cannot get information.вҖҷ Then do not stay in that village. This grave incident is also being handled within a legal context. The media must not declare anyone guilty or define criminality before investigation and judicial processes are completed. Targeting or labeling someone as guilty in information delivery is not the mediaвҖҷs job.вҖқ

title: Has the Narin murder turned into a media spectacle?
section: вҖңThey are pursuing their own populism as wellвҖқ
text:
вҖңThey are pursuing their own populism as wellвҖқ
GГјngГ¶r evaluated politiciansвҖҷ approach to the Narin incident as follows:
вҖңIn the Narin incident, some politicians also intervened and made statements. They too are pursuing their own populism. The incident is turned into a fictional scenario, and the main problem is detached from its context. Then it is forgotten. Such incidents have always occurred in Turkey, but most remained within a closed societal structure. With the spread of social media, local incidents quickly reflect in the media.вҖқ

title: Has the Narin murder turned into a media spectacle?
section: вҖңHave our genes deteriorated?вҖқ
text:
вҖңHave our genes deteriorated?вҖқ
Addressing the social dimension of the incident, GГјngГ¶r said:
вҖңA young child, not yet mature within social relations, is killed due to family problems or chaotic environments; their right to life is taken away. This shows a major social blockage and contamination. Are the problems within family structures, economic issues, or cultural crises? Have our genes deteriorated? All of this needs to be questioned and researched with expert perspectives and academically. Not informing society does not solve the problem. Society must be informed and enlightened. When information is provided, problems are contemplated and solutions are produced. A society unaware of its own problems cannot solve them.вҖқ
