title: A Salem Court in DiyarbakДұr
text:
AUTHOR


YДұldДұray OДҹur


LAST UPDATED


November 11, 2024


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 10 min


Word Count


1817







Arthur MillerвҖҷs famous play The Crucible tells the story of 29 people tried and executed for witchcraft in Salem, Massachusetts, between 1692 and 1693.
In fact, what Miller narrated was not the witch trials in Salem, but the McCarthyist communist witch hunt that began in the United States in the late 1940s.
If there were a courageous Arthur Miller in Turkey today, he would write about another trial resembling the witch courts of Salem 400 years ago: the Narin Case that began in DiyarbakДұr.
This requires courage because this case has turned into a mass lynching ritual against a supposedly demonic family, provoked by fake news and allegations that have almost entirely collapsed.
The statements made recently in a broadcast by a widely watched journalist were a summary of the demonic, witch-family image created by this enormous pile of misinformation:

If there is such a thing as the Devil and if you were to give him flesh and bone, he would run away from TavЕҹantepe village, my brother. That bunch of unknown creatures there is mocking 80 million, 85 million Turkish citizens. Clearly, they have politicians behind them. Clearly, they have taken some powerful AKP figures in the region with them. But the social pressure is so high that even those powerful AKP figures are not enough to cover up the issue in the regionвҖ”at least as far as we can see for now. The Prosecutor dug deeper and looked at HTS records. Where was whose phone at the time of the murder, or the time thought to be the moment of the murder? They have sort of solved that everything happened in NarinвҖҷs house. Because the accused uncle keeps entering and exiting the house. He entered and exited three times. All the aunts, uncles, everyone keeps going in and out, and clearly something was happening inside the house at that time. They went even further and checked water consumption. And at that hour, around the time of the incident, a significant increase in water usage was noticed. During that period. And clearly, a cleaning had been done in the house. They also found that cleaning agents capable of removing DNA traces were used. What we are seeing is that this family is actually a highly conscious pack of killers. They know how to destroy evidence, how to anonymize their crime, how to remove DNAвҖҰ They know everything. The family behaves like an organized criminal gang. You know what I really think? We are facing such a demonic village that I wish such a place didnвҖҷt exist on the map. You are a mother for GodвҖҷs sake, a mother. And that mother is an accomplice to this crime, my brother. They say theyвҖҷre protecting them in prison, doing something for their safety. What safety? They have lost all humanity.

He is angry enough to advocate for the village to be wiped off the map and the family members not to be protected in prison, but he hasnвҖҷt even bothered to read the 12-page indictment.
The indictment does not claim that all family members were entering and exiting the house; only three family members are alleged to have been there.
The claims that carpets were washed or water consumption increased are lies.
The allegation that a family whose members have been detained repeatedly, whose three members are on trial for life, and six more are in prison is being protected by the government is entirely baseless. Why would a family accused of murdering a little girl be protected? What special quality could this rural family possibly have? There is not even an answer to such a simple question.
In fact, ministers did not even offer their condolences to the family. The Ministry of Family is represented as a complainant in the trial.
The MinistryвҖҷs lawyer, despite the lack of any evidence, said: вҖңIt is considered that the murder occurred due to an incident within the family. The defendants should be punished at the upper limit. No child has ever been left this alone by their closest relatives. Therefore, all of Turkey has become NarinвҖҷs family,вҖқ and asked defendants about the most absurd allegations.
So what is the reason for this collective lynching?
Of course, the allegations that have been circulating in the media for months and have almost entirely collapsed or been disproven.
LetвҖҷs look at a few:

It was claimed that a gas station attendant saw NarinвҖҷs body wrapped in a blanket on the front seat of Salim GГјranвҖҷs car and handed over the camera footage to the Gendarmerie. The attendant turned out to be a fake account; the station did not exist, and there was no camera footage.
While Narin was missing, a slipper was found. When the mother said вҖңthe slipper does not belong to Narin,вҖқ she was accused of trying to obstruct her daughterвҖҷs discovery. When Narin was found, her slippers came out of the same sack she had been placed in.
It was claimed that the carpets in NarinвҖҷs home were washed to destroy evidence. However, the autopsy established that the little girl was manually strangled. So there could be no evidence on the carpets. The indictment shows that all carpets and blankets in the house were examined and no evidence was found.
For days it was said that the вҖңFamily CouncilвҖқ ordered all villagers to switch from smartphones to keypad phones, and the media reported on the вҖңmafia-like silenceвҖқ of the demonic family. The indictment revealed that the Gendarmerie had collected the smartphones and distributed keypad phones itself.
It was claimed that the QurвҖҷan-course imam was involved and that sexual videos were found on his phone; this was disproven.
It was claimed that family members used вҖңburnerвҖқ phone lines; this was denied.
It was claimed that a family member working at the State Waterworks increased the creekвҖҷs water flow so the body would decompose faster; false.
It was said the house had cameras and recordings were deleted; the cameras were found to have been installed after the incident.
It was claimed that recordings of supposed secret family meetings were examined; nothing was found.
It was said that Narin was actually SalimвҖҷs biological daughter. The indictment includes a DNA report confirming NarinвҖҷs biological parents.
It was claimed that NarinвҖҷs DNA was found on the front seat and steering wheel of SalimвҖҷs car; it was actually found on the back seat.
Claims that the village was a Hezbollah village, a guard village, a tribal village were false. Claims that ammunition was found were also disproven.
A supposed audio message in which Salim asked his 15-year-old worker R.A. вҖңRamazan, is the girl dead or alive?вҖқ was circulated for days as the recording that solved the murder. This was completely false.
In the last court session, the original Kurdish recording was listened to. Not only were the words вҖңgirlвҖқ or вҖңdeadвҖқ not present, but even the phrase tiЕҹtek te was debatedвҖ”whether it meant вҖңsomething of yoursвҖқ or вҖңsprinkler.вҖқ

In this witch hunt, ideological divides have disappeared; the government and DEM Party stand united.
Even the defendantsвҖҷ detailed torture allegations in court are met with applause on social media: вҖңGood, they deserve it, well done Gendarmerie.вҖқ When journalists report that mother YГјksel GГјran cried in court saying, вҖңHang me, but donвҖҷt insult my honor, why does no one listen to us?вҖқ commenters respond with insults, the mildest being вҖңstop the theatrics, you never loved Narin anyway.вҖқ
Bar associations and human rights groups that normally react immediately to вҖңallegations of Gendarmerie torture against Kurdish villagersвҖқ now ask cold, bureaucratic questions like вҖңWhere is the evidence? Have you filed your application?вҖқвҖ”simply because they have taken sides in the case based on prejudice.
There is a murder, and months laterвҖ”after the indictment and after three days of hearingsвҖ”still the only piece of evidence is a so-called conflicting HTS base-station result, a method no one fully understands, placing the mother, uncle, brother, and neighbor Nevzat at home when Narin disappeared.
And the only evidence of what they supposedly did inside the house is the third statement of Nevzat, the man who confessed to burying the body in the creek. His first two statements were based on entirely different scenarios.
The first murder claim is this: the uncle and the mother were having sexual relations; Narin saw them, and the uncle killed her.
According to this scenario, the 20-year-old brother was also at home while his mother and uncle were having sexual relations. But when Narin saw it, the uncle killed her. Not the brotherвҖ”her seeing her mother and uncle supposedly dishonors only her, not him.
The second claim is that the brother committed the murder, because Narin saw him in a homosexual relationship, incest, or with a dog. Or there was an accident. The family members then supposedly took a vow of silence to protect him. At the end of the day the brother, mother, and uncle are going toward life sentences, yet for some reason they do not break their silence.
The third claim is the strangest: the family is hiding something so terrible that three people choose to destroy their lives to keep it.
But the supposedly demonic and calculating family patriarch, Salim, after the murder, calls their neighborвҖ”whom he has been in conflict with over a vehicle saleвҖ”and confesses the relationship that supposedly caused him to murder Narin, and then informs him about the murder. Despite having dozens of family members, he assigns the task of disposing of the body to this neighbor.
And all of this supposedly happens within 15 minutes, according to the camera footage.
These are the murder scenarios that all of Turkey has accepted without question.
And the witness is a man who immediately agreed to dispose of the body of an 8-year-old girl, put her in a sack, tied the sack with the string from her school bag, placed it by the creek, weighted it down with a 20-kilogram stone, and then went to buy cheese for his aunt.
Moreover, he confessed not because his conscience troubled him, but because his car was seen on the creek-side security cameras.
Those who question whether he could be the murderer are accused of protecting the family, being paid by them, or siding with the government. This cold-blooded potential killer is being defended by millions.
Even the torture of all family members and their tearful statements do not change this powerful public conviction.
The crowd is waiting for the hammer to fall. ThatвҖҷs why they cannot tolerate even a defense. GГјlben Ergen even demanded that the names of the lawyers defending the suspects be made public.
Fortunately, the presiding judge is fair and listens to the claims, not joining the lynch.
New camera footage from the Gendarmerie outpost tower was submitted to the court. Information about how and by whom the conflicting HTS result was created will arrive at the next hearing.
But at this point, even if the family turns out not to be witches, the crowd will not be satisfied.
Yes, this is happening in DiyarbakДұr in 2024, not in Salem in 1692.



 Back to top

title: Could TavЕҹantepe village be innocent?
text:
AUTHOR


YДұldДұray OДҹur


LAST UPDATED


September 16, 2024


SOURCE


Original Source


Reading Time


~ 19 min


Word Count


3545


Almost a month has passed since 21 August, when she was killed, yet we still do not know who killed 8-year-old Narin GГјran or why.
So why?
According to television, because the village took a vow of silence and is protecting the killer.
According to some commentators, because the gendarmerie is inadequate in murder investigations.
But what if the biggest obstacle to solving this murder right now is not the silence of the villagers, but the noise of everyone else?
A village was declared sectarian, Hizbullah-affiliated, feudal; allegations of forbidden sexual relations were made about almost everyone in the family; the entire village population was demonized like determined militants who never crack under interrogation, faithful to the Omerta Code; every arrow was directed at the GГјran family.
If, after all this, the killer does not turn out to be a member of the GГјran family, how will the millions of conditioned people be convinced of this?
Apparently, the biggest obstacle to the investigation right now is this rubbish heap of cultural prejudices, ideological propaganda, and political score-settling broadcast for days from television sets.
Could all these prejudices, leaks, and accusations be helping the killer?
What made me ask this question was the striking letter written, far from the entire propaganda machine, by Miham Akkul вҖ” a DiyarbakДұr-born masterвҖҷs student in France with no closeness to the neighbourhood or to the family вҖ” who has followed all the news, evidence, and statements from the very beginning.
The letter begins as follows:

вҖңFor the events that occurred in the place where I was born and raised, I will say вҖҳjвҖҷaccuseвҖҷ (I accuse). For a mother whose 8-year-old daughter has been murdered has been pushed behind iron bars on the accusation that she sacrificed her daughter to the lust of her unchastity.
The reporter of the channel that is the flagship for the countryвҖҷs opposition, and his studios in Istanbul, are not seeking the truth вҖ” they are practically searching for evidence to accuse Salim GГјran. In all these talks and debates, the real culprits are insinuated by squeezing in incest among Kurds, Mustafa KemalвҖҷs relentless struggle against feudalism by making aghas CHP MPs, sects, religious orders.
Even the lawyer of the imprisoned former general chair of the Kurdish movement вҖ” caught up in this wind вҖ” feels the embarrassment of not having been sufficiently civilized, saying вҖҳwe should question our mentality.вҖҷ The DEM Party organizes marches with slogans like вҖҳDown with HizbullahвҖҷ; a video taken by вҖҳ32. GГјnвҖҷ from YolaГ§ village in Silvan вҖ” where the Hizbullah grave-houses were uncovered вҖ” is shared as if it were TavЕҹantepe village and gets thousands of likes.
Some, unable to stop themselves, claim that HizbullahвҖҷs missing weapons were detected by underground search devices and that, by directives from the deep state, the Narin case was therefore closed.
So what really happened, and who were these people of TavЕҹantepe village?
When we look at the election results in TavЕҹantepe village, we see that since 2015 various parties have come first. In 2015 it was HDP, in 2023 Д°YД° Party, in 2024 AK Party. The village was not Hizbullah at all; in fact Baran GГјran, whose family was in custody, had received his sisterвҖҷs body, and the state and pro-government media shared BaranвҖҷs Newroz footage and accused the family of DEM and PKK sympathies. The Hizbullah wing too had posted tweets implying that DEM members had taken little Narin to Syria to be raised for the YPG. HГјda-Par supporters had used вҖҳwhatever befell us came from EuropeвҖҷ rhetoric in front of those waiting for the body at the morgue.
The GГјran family had migrated centuries ago from the Еһehrizor region to DiyarbakДұr. They were not a family with deep political affiliations. They had become quite wealthy thanks to lands that gained value as DiyarbakДұr grew beyond its walls. The village mukhtar Salim GГјran also looked after the fields of those who had migrated to the city, providing various information to everyone. In short, he watched over relativesвҖҷ property and played the role of head of the family.
On 21 August, the day Narin disappeared, he had also called Mehmet Еһerif GГјran in Antalya and told him, with photographs, that his cotton was being attacked by worms. It was even claimed that he made this call to obtain professional help to cover up the murder.вҖқ

The letter then looks at the evidence and the claims.
The first claim that drew suspicion onto uncle Salim GГјran, taken into custody on 2 September, was a note written under his last Facebook post dated 15 August.
The note allegedly written by gas-station worker Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca made the news in all media on 4 September:

вҖңSalim GГјran is the village mukhtar. In HTS records there are both messages and call records with Narin GГјranвҖҷs mother before and after the incident. On the day of the incident, 15-20 minutes after Narin disappeared, this dog Salim GГјran left the village quickly in his own vehicle. He doesnвҖҷt even take fuel. He goes into a petrol station and buys wet wipes from the store. The camera footage was taken. I swear I personally handed the camera footage to the gendarmerie commander. I was told not to share anything from the footage. I was even told that if I shared it I would be charged with leaking evidence to the media. This mukhtar was also in contact with NarinвҖҷs brother Enes. According to phone records after the incident and the camera footage, unfortunately Narin is either unconscious or being strangled by SalimвҖҷs hands lying on the front seat with a dark-brown blanket on top of her. Since the petrol station employees know the mukhtar, there was no doubt. Also, the mukhtarвҖҷs phone was off and he came back to the village in the dark two hours later. This time, when he came back, he acted as if he didnвҖҷt know anything and put on a sad face crying вҖҳWhat happened to Narin?вҖҷ to fake grief.вҖқ

In the news it was claimed that gas-station worker Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca had gone to the gendarmerie after this note and given a statement.
Then, oddly, other Facebook accounts writing the same claims appeared.
But there was actually no statement, no gas-station worker, and no security camera footage handed to the gendarmerie from the gas station.
In fact, there wasnвҖҷt even a Murat ГҮДұnar ГҮatalca who had supposedly shared a suicide note in 2019.
Soon afterwards, both his account and the others supporting him were closed and disappeared.
LetвҖҷs read from the letter:

вҖңIn the first week after Narin GГјran disappeared, a baseless gas-station-worker story was circulated about Salim GГјran. Supposedly Salim GГјran had killed Narin in the 40-degree August heat of DiyarbakДұr, wrapped her in blankets in that heat, sat her in the front seat where everyone could see, and gone out to buy wet wipes. This absurd claim was discussed for days; the pump attendant was even said to have been threatened with death.вҖқ

But the claim of вҖңNarinвҖҷs body wrapped in a blanket in the front seat of GГјranвҖҷs car,вҖқ which had circulated for days in the media in this fake post, came up a few days later in a critical statement.
The most critical turning point in the murder was footage from the security cameras at the gendarmerie observation post near the village.
The footage showed a red car arriving near the riverbed at 15:40 on 21 August вҖ” the day Narin disappeared вҖ” and parking there, remaining for about 50 minutes.
Searches in the river were intensified. And after three days, on the morning of 8 September, NarinвҖҷs body was found inside a sack.
The owner of the red car was also taken into custody: plasterer Nevzat Bahtiyar.
There was now a suspect who had touched the body and could have been directly involved in the murder.
But Bahtiyar, who like other villagers had previously given a statement and said nothing, became a confessing witness as soon as he was caught, and on 9 September, in his first statement to the gendarmerie, he pinned the crime on mukhtar Salim GГјran.
In doing so, he used the claims of the fake gas-station worker that he had read for days in the media:

вҖңIn his first statement, he claimed that he had run into Salim in the village and that Salim had handed Narin to him in the front seat of the car wrapped in a blanket. Nevzat was making use of the fictitious вҖҳblanket in the front seatвҖҷ story of the non-existent gas-station worker. Of course it never occurred to the media members who had been playing detective for 3 weeks to ask why Salim would put Narin in the front seat вҖ” the most visible place вҖ” instead of the trunk.вҖқ

Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs statement at the BaДҹlar District Gendarmerie Command, beginning on the night of 9 September at 00:52, ended at 03:12.
In this statement, Bahtiyar described why he took NarinвҖҷs body and buried it in the river:

вҖң(Salim GГјran), pointing at something wrapped in a blanket on the front passenger seat of the car, said, вҖҳYou will dispose of this.вҖҷ I came closer to what he was pointing at, and when I looked I saw a motionless person wrapped in a blanket. I was shocked and hesitated. Salim GГјran said to me, вҖҳThink of the family, IвҖҷll give you 200 thousand liras.вҖҷвҖқ

But the next day, in his statement to the prosecutorвҖҷs office, Nevzat Bahtiyar changed this story.
In his hearing statement he explained why he had changed it:

вҖңThe statement in my prosecutorial statement, where Salim said to me вҖҳI killed ArifвҖҷs daughter; you take the body and dispose of it. Otherwise IвҖҷll kill you and your family,вҖҷ is correct. I had not mentioned this in my earlier statement because I was afraid of Salim GГјran.вҖқ

There were many contradictions between the two statements given a day apart.
In his gendarmerie statement he had said that they put the body into the sack together with Salim GГјran:

вҖңHe said to me, вҖҳDo you have a sack in your car?вҖҷ I took out a sack вҖ” whose colour I do not remember вҖ” from the trunk of my car and gave it to Salim GГјran. We took the child wrapped in a blanket and put it together into the sack. Going down by the cemetery, I went to the Villas area (вҖҰ) Going down on the gravel road by the river I looked for a suitable place. I stopped my vehicle by the river.вҖқ

In his prosecutorial statement, however, he said he went to his home and put the body into the sack himself:

вҖңThen I placed NarinвҖҷs body alone in the sack at my own residence.вҖқ

Again, in his gendarmerie statement, Nevzat Bahtiyar had said he didnвҖҷt know whose body was in the sack:

вҖңWhen we were putting it together into the sack, I didnвҖҷt realize whether the body was Narin GГјran. When I went down to the riverbed and tied the mouth of the sack, I realized that the body was Narin GГјran.вҖқ

But in his prosecutorial and trial statement he changed this too:

вҖңSalim said to me, вҖҳI killed ArifвҖҷs daughter; you take the body and dispose of it.вҖҷвҖқ

Yet, in his 9 September Gendarmerie statement, he had answered the question, вҖңDo you have any information about why Salim GГјran killed Narin?вҖқ as follows:

вҖңHe didnвҖҷt say to me why he killed her or that he killed her. But why would he say to me вҖҳdispose of this bodyвҖҷ if he himself didnвҖҷt kill her?вҖқ

In his 10 September prosecutorial statement, however, he put forward ambitious theories about why Salim GГјran might have killed Narin:

вҖңIt was being said that Salim GГјran had relationships with YГјksel GГјran (NarinвҖҷs mother) and his uncleвҖҷs wife MaЕҹallah GГјran. But I donвҖҷt think anyone will give a statement on this. My guess is that Narin saw the sexual relationship he had with one of these women. For this reason I think he could have killed Narin GГјran. However, I myself did not see with my own eyes that he had a relationship with one of these women. Furthermore, I would like to state the following. UДҹurcan GГјranвҖҷs wedding was to take place on 25 August. Since most of the men were outside the village to distribute the invitations, I think Salim GГјran may have stayed in TavЕҹantepe village to have such a sexual relationship. The first place Salim GГјran called me to was the place Arif GГјran used as a barn. There may have been a sexual relationship in that barn. Narin may have seen something at that point.вҖқ

So where had this вҖңforbidden relationship,вҖқ вҖңNarin may have been killed because she saw a sexual relationship in the barnвҖқ claim вҖ” which wasnвҖҷt in his statement of the day before вҖ” come from?
Very simply.
Because the day before this prosecutorial statement, on 9 September, Didem Arslan YДұlmaz had told on television about a relationship between uncle Salim GГјran and NarinвҖҷs mother and the claim that Narin was killed because she saw it.
Nevzat Bahtiyar, after the gas-station workerвҖҷs blanket story, had also added this forbidden-relationship claim to his statement.
But these exaggerated and contradictory statements вҖ” sounding like YeЕҹilГ§am dialogue lines such as вҖңI killed ArifвҖҷs daughter, you take this body and dispose of itвҖқ вҖ” had not made anyone suspicious.
LetвҖҷs read again from the letter:

вҖңIt never occurred to the media members who had been playing detective for 3 weeks to ask why Salim would put Narin in the front seat вҖ” the most visible place вҖ” instead of the trunk. Later, Nevzat, changing his absurd statement, was forced to admit that he had taken Narin home and claimed that Salim threatened him not with money but with his family. And he said he guessed that Salim killed her because of the affair with NarinвҖҷs mother in the barn. Nobody asked Nevzat why he felt the need to accuse Salim вҖ” whom he was already accusing of killing Narin вҖ” with a false statement. That is, Nevzat was already saying the worst; nobody could think to ask why, having put forward this worst claim, he could not tell the truth and changed his first statement. It was even claimed that Nevzat, a 30-year construction worker, could not within 40 minutes have left 8-year-old Narin in the river and placed a few stones on her.вҖқ

But because Nevzat Bahtiyar gained immunity as a confessor, no one cared about the contradictions in his statements; instead, the villagers were demonized.
After Narin was found, the villagersвҖҷ having begun to use keypad phones instead of smartphones was attributed to вҖңtheir being terribly criminal, cool-headed, organized, taking instructions from a higher mind.вҖқ
Yet the truth was very simple: the gendarmerie had confiscated the phones and given the villagers keypad phones:

вҖңWhile the family and its members were accused of taking an organized strict vow of silence, and the village was deemed appropriate for the name OMERTA VILLAGE, those more traditional said it would be more fitting to put up a вҖҳDEVILвҖҷS HILL OR THE VILLAGE OF THE MUTE DEVILSвҖҷ sign. The impossibility of so many villagers вҖ” women and young people вҖ” being tight-lipped and resistant to interrogation like the militants of a Marxist organization was not discussed. In a manipulative way, вҖҳWhy arenвҖҷt they speaking?вҖҷ was endlessly debated on screens. They might have had nothing to say, but who cared about that possibility? That there was no organized murder was clearly visible in subsequent custodial proceedings, since when the mother was asked, вҖҳDid your daughter get into SalimвҖҷs car?вҖҷ, she said, вҖҳYes, she went to the engagement in that car; the car was being driven by SalimвҖҷs son Devran but my daughter sat in the back.вҖҷ That is, she did not change a detail that could be against Salim. Likewise, Enes had said there was a financial problem between his father and his uncle 2-3 years ago вҖ” he wasnвҖҷt trying to protect his uncle either; everyone was telling things just as they knew.вҖқ

Meanwhile, there had been a change of location in NarinвҖҷs DNA found in his car вҖ” one of the strongest pieces of evidence against Salim GГјran.
According to the questions asked by the gendarmerie in Salim GГјranвҖҷs first statement transcript, NarinвҖҷs DNA sample in his car had been detected on the front-right seat. That is, this was a finding that confirmed the thesis that the body had been carried in the front seat of the car.
But in a question asked in Salim GГјranвҖҷs new statement, it was stated that вҖңNarinвҖҷs DNA was found in the back-right seat, on the inner side of the door.вҖқ
Д°smail Saymaz explained this critical contradiction in the report on television:

вҖңWhere is this DNA? ThereвҖҷs a difference between being on the front-right seat and being on the back-right seat. Because earlier, when SalimвҖҷs wife was asked about this, she had said вҖҳfront-right seat.вҖҷ When asked whether NarinвҖҷs DNA was on the front-right seat, they had given this response: вҖҳA month ago we went somewhere outside the village for our nephewвҖҷs engagement. Hediye was sitting in the front seat. Devran was driving. The children were in the back seat. Narin was sitting in the back.вҖҷ Now in this questioning he says it was actually in the back seat, on the inner side of the door. Now if the DNA is in the front, it turns out that Salim and his wife were lying. Or if the DNA is in the back, it turns out that NarinвҖҷs mother was telling the truth.вҖқ

And we come to the last and strongest piece of evidence.
On the evening of 12 September on Halk TV, Ferit Demir, calling it вҖңthe audio recording that will solve the murder,вҖқ shared a sentence from a phone conversation between Salim GГјran and 15-year-old R.A. who worked for him:

вҖңSalim GГјran has a phone call with RвҖҰ AвҖҰ shortly after NarinвҖҷs disappearance at 15:15. In that call he asks the following вҖ” in the audio recording obtained from RвҖҰ AвҖҰвҖҳs phone: вҖҷRвҖҰ, is the girl alive or dead?вҖҷ Let me repeat: вҖҳRвҖҰ, is the girl alive or dead?вҖҷ ThatвҖҷs it, a tiny audio recording, recorded on RвҖҰ AвҖҰвҖҷs phone. This was also entered into the record, and we have verified it from a few different sources. We verified it from at least 4 different sources.вҖқ

The next day the transcripts of the phone audio recording came out.
There was no sentence вҖңRвҖҰ, is the girl alive or dead?вҖқ in the recordings.
The conversation was not, as Ferit Demir claimed, shortly after NarinвҖҷs disappearance, but at 18:38 вҖ” about 3.5 hours after NarinвҖҷs disappearance.
The audio messaging, originally in Kurdish, had been translated into Turkish as follows:

Salim GГјran: At that corner at the end something of yours has fallen, ha вҖ” something belonging to you on the slope at the end, the corner of the slope, stone.
R.A.: Yeah.
Salim GГјran: Someone is on the ground.
R.A.: OK, itвҖҷs not in my hand yet, it hasnвҖҷt died yet.

Salim GГјran was asked about this conversation in his new statement:

вҖңI remembered the content of the conversation you mentioned. While irrigating the cornfield I sometimes used a device to draw illegal electricity from the transformer. On the day of the incident I called R.A. so that he would pick up the device that I had left under a stone. The content of the conversation is entirely about that. It has nothing to do with NarinвҖҷs death.вҖқ

Miham Akkul, in her letter, claims there is a problem with the translation of this audio recording:

вҖңIn this dialogue, the disconnection of the word вҖҳhasnвҖҷt diedвҖҷ at the end from the context was treated as if they were speaking in code, and the media advertised it as the biggest evidence in the file. So what was the truth? In fact this dialogue was around 18:35 between Salim and his 15-year-old worker вҖ” it was about an illegal-electricity matter. The original of this 18:35 conversation was in Kurdish; that is, there was an obvious translation error. In Kurdish вҖҳit hasnвҖҷt died yetвҖҷ is said as вҖҳhin nemiri.вҖҷ Probably it wasnвҖҷt вҖҳhin nemiriвҖҷ but вҖҳhin neliviriвҖҷ (it hasnвҖҷt moved yet) or вҖҳnemirayiвҖҷ (itвҖҷs not in my hand). Indeed, the Kurdish negative prefix вҖҳneвҖҷ could easily have been confused with many other words. Another obvious strange contradiction is the disconnection between this incident and NevzatвҖҷs story вҖ” Nevzat had already buried the body around 15:30. This cruel, cunning, mercenary, Kurdish, Islamist mukhtar wanted Narin to be killed and disposed of by both Nevzat and a 15-year-old worker. Narin was being murdered in two places at once, continuously buried by feudal SalimвҖҷs serfs who had not benefited from the values of the republic.вҖқ

The Kurdish-to-Turkish translation of this critical audio recording is being redone by judicial authorities. The new translation may bring out the truth.
In short, what we have is an investigation tangled into something inextricable by ideological prejudices in which the media has played a leading role, by continually leaked information, and by some journalistsвҖҷ manipulations to pin the crime on a family and a village and not be wrong at the end of the day.
And the killer is benefiting from all of this.
Miham Akkul ends her letter, which began with вҖңI accuse,вҖқ as follows:

вҖңNarin GГјranвҖҷs story reminds me of films Billy Wilder or Sidney Lumet made in the 50s and 60s about the destructive and lethal sides of the media. Especially the 1952 film Ace in the Hole. In August 2024, Narin was buried by Nevzat Bahtiyar; her family, with the contrast this pitch-black murder would produce, was stoned and buried in the same way by the spite of a hysterical society wishing to feel white. Now, over this burial, will flow not the yellowish stream but years-long hearings ending with the villageвҖҷs ruin, opening and closing iron doors, hurried bailiffs, money-grubbing lawyersвҖҰвҖқ

title: The Truths of the Narin GГјran Case
section: Description
text:
Description
In this 31 May 2025 broadcast by Rojda AltДұntaЕҹ, the guests are DEM Party DiyarbakДұr MP Sevilay ГҮelenk, lawyer Mustafa Demir, YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs mother Remziye, and Arif GГјran. The text below is the edited full transcript of Sevilay ГҮelenkвҖҷs remarks throughout the broadcast (translated from Turkish).

Sevilay ГҮelenkвҖҷs remarks

вҖңA child homicide case is, of course, important and grave in every respect. But this case, together with a great many irregularities, has effectively been turned into вҖҳthe trial of the century.вҖҷ In this process, we have very clearly seen two fundamental forms of decay in Turkey: first, the decay in the field of the media, and second, the decay in the judiciary. And these two have manifested themselves very concretely around this case.
From this point on, we cannot trivialise the matter by saying, вҖҳA decision has been reached, it is over.вҖҷ Until now this incident has occupied every space in a sensational manner; ministers went to the scene, journalists went, citizens flocked to the location from all corners of Turkey. The event was framed almost with the excitement of a TV series or a film. Everyone made room for it. Refusing now to make room for this issue is a sign of exploitation. This case has, in this sense, been exploited.
Anyone who opens their studios to this case today must, at the very least, approach the contradictions in the file with care. Particularly after reading the dissenting opinion written by the chief judge in the appellate ruling, this case should have been examined in depth.
Because that dissent вҖ” even if I personally do not agree with it, even if I find it mistaken regarding the person it singles out вҖ” laid bare how baseless certain reports are (for example, the National Criminal BureauвҖҷs report on the camera recordings). The basis of the verdict is these reports; there is no other concrete evidence. Yet these contradictions found almost no place in the media.
Under all these circumstances, I think that what the handful of people who insist on pursuing justice are doing is something very valuable. We are looking at whether justice is genuinely being served in a case that is not trivial вҖ” one that has been loaded with great significance and could have very serious consequences. That is how I see this matter.
This is not just a matter of feeling. What I say here, I say not merely with feelings; but with reason, examination and research. I have taken a deep interest in this subject and worked on it seriously. Despite this, there are some lawyers who, while saying вҖҳI have engaged closely with this subject,вҖҷ oppose what we are saying and try to put pressure on us; they try to use against us completely false, fabricated information that circulates in the media but is in fact nowhere to be found in the file.
None of the things they ask вҖҳAnd what do you say to this?вҖҷ, вҖҳAnd to that?вҖҷ are in the file, nor in the indictment, nor in the reasoned ruling, nor in the appellate ruling. These baseless allegations the media has put forward have been blown up like balloons, and we cannot somehow free ourselves from these balloons.
This case is not an individual matter. It is a subject that concerns the life of a village, of a community. What you call a village is a community. What is at stake here is not the right and the justice of one person, but of a community. Moreover, we are dealing with a family that cannot properly express itself in Turkish. If the MP of their province does not stand up for them, who will?
You make a post, and immediately beneath it appear comments so devoid of moral grounding that one really doesnвҖҷt know what to say. They tell us, вҖҳYou drop your work and busy yourself with this?вҖҷ No, we have not dropped our work. I read this file until 4:30 or 5:00 in the morning, in the time left after I finish my other tasks. I still examine every new document that comes out, watch the documentaries, read the appellate decisions, the indictment of the obstruction-of-justice case, and each ruling, one by one.
If you simply look at Twitter, you can see what else I have been engaged with in the past month. In one month I attended three different important meetings on three different continents on behalf of my party. I contributed to the work being done there and helped develop the reports that were prepared. Inside Turkey I also took part in three different cities. All of this is what I did in just one month. Yet we are still met with criticisms of, вҖҳYou have dropped your work.вҖҷ The fact that we are forced to explain such things is, on its own, deeply shameful.
Yes, we have not dropped our work. But this is, in any case, the primary duty of a member of parliament. If there is unlawfulness вҖ” and there is вҖ” examining it and intervening is our duty. Even before becoming an MP, I was already actively working on womenвҖҷs and childrenвҖҷs homicides as a feminist academic and a social scientist. At the same time, having spent 25 years in an institution that provides journalism education and works on the media, I also engaged in journalism over the last seven years. Just like Rojda HanДұm, I made weekly programmes. I wrote weekly columns at Medyascope and Gazete Duvar. So those who know me, who know how I approach these issues, already know.
Now, in my own province, in a very serious child-homicide case rushed to a verdict under enormous public pressure, where a vast community has been victimised вҖ” am I going to look the other way? Of course not. We have to examine this matter. This is our job. I underline this: this is the duty of a politician who is on a quest for justice.
Think of the helplessness experienced by a mother who cannot speak TurkishвҖҰ One of the criticisms that shook me most was this: вҖҳWhy do you not stand up for other Kurdish children?вҖҷ What kind of question is this? She is also a Kurdish child. But we cannot look at this matter through ethnic distinctions like вҖҳKurdish childвҖҷ or вҖҳTurkish child.вҖҷ The struggle for justice is not waged on the basis of parties or ethnic identities.
If we only stand up for those close to our own party or our own political line, then why do we criticise the government? They too protect those next to them and punish those who stand against them. That is precisely why the prisons are overflowing.
When I say вҖҳI cannot sleep,вҖҷ I am not speaking of an individual sentimentalism. I feel this constriction, this helplessness, in my heart. And I believe that any human being who has not lost their conscience should feel the same way. Every politician should see it like this.
Actually, in this case, one realises that the truth stands somewhere else. While listening to KurtuluЕҹ Bey, I thought the same thing. I must confess that I went into the courtroom with a prejudice вҖ” looking at Salim Bey almost as if he were a вҖҳmonster.вҖҷ But then I remembered that our knowledge of life вҖ” though not everything вҖ” is something important.
Salim Bey, just like KurtuluЕҹ Bey, was trying to express his suffering in a consistent way, at length, sincerely and at great personal cost. While listening to him, a shift occurred in my mind. That image of a вҖҳmonsterвҖҷ was erased; in its place came an ordinary person trying to explain his trouble вҖ” a face resembling many people from the region. I lived this transition in my mind very clearly. And I thought: вҖҳA person like this cannot lie.вҖҷ
Then, when I looked at the cues for who was lying in court, I saw this: the person speaking falsely did not go beyond five sentences, did not stray onto side paths, did not utter a single extra word. Because he knew he would expose himself if he did.
Today everyone asks, вҖҳWhy did they not speak?вҖҷ When I hear this, a great rebellion rises within me. Yet for three days in court, YГјksel HanДұm poured herself out. With her broken Turkish, but without any reservation, she spoke and spoke. Enes did the same; Salim Bey did the same. You can see this in the trial records.
As the accusations against them were being read, there was rebellion on every one of their faces; it was as if their hearts would leap out of their chests. These facial expressions cannot be ignored.
Perhaps it is a professional reflex; I am a communications scholar. I work in language, psychology, journalism. So I read a great deal from facial expressions and ways of telling. And when I looked at these people, I thought that behind what they said stood the truth.
At first I had gone to the courtroom for only an hour, but I could not leave for three days. When I returned to Ankara, I asked the lawyers for their defence statements again. There were certain points that especially drew my attention. I examined the forensic medicine reports. I had largely written up my piece during the week I followed the trial, but I waited for the reasoned ruling too, in case I had missed something. I also examined the 950-page appendices, sometimes scanning them for keywords.
Yes, life experience tells you something. But if you also make the effort, with that knowledge you can reach the truth. And I came to believe: these people were telling the truth. They were describing an everyday life.
The internet traffic data, which was submitted to the appeals stage and somehow still not taken seriously, also supports this. Do not listen to those who say, вҖҳThis was already there before.вҖҷ Yes, there was a previous billing record, an SMS notification through which it could be raised, but there was no detailed log. Now a detailed traffic log has been submitted. When Onur AkdaДҹ brought this information to court, it caught my attention very strongly. But the court attached no importance to this data.
Yet we see that this telephone traffic matches one-to-one with YГјksel GГјranвҖҷs, Enes GГјranвҖҷs, and Salim GГјranвҖҷs statements. Everyone is at home. But it is very hard to exhaust claims of this kind. You refute one claim, and immediately another comes up.
But the matter does not end there either. Each exhausted claim is replaced with a new one.
Actually, having to lay these out one by one has turned into a kind of helplessness for us. Because by now I know what many of the claims in the file actually are. The lawyer knows five times more than I do. We essentially know thanks to them. But you explain one, and another comes out of the pocket.
Now, for instance, you know that a вҖҳNarin CommissionвҖҷ has been set up in Parliament. That is what it is called publicly, but of course its name is not exactly that: вҖҳThe Commission for the Investigation of Violence against Women and Children.вҖҷ To this commission, the Deputy General Commander of the Gendarmerie, I believe, came recently вҖ” months after the incident вҖ” and said something like, вҖҳThis case has been resolved fully and properly; from now on it is just curiosity, just gossip.вҖҷ
I was not at the commission, but I read the minutes afterwards. While the commander was speaking, he referred to SalimвҖҷs car as вҖҳthe car in which the child was carried.вҖҷ Now, those who were on that commission have a responsibility to be informed of this concrete reality. If we take the reasoned ruling as our basis, the childвҖҷs body was never placed in his car at all. Yet the commander still says this and not even one person there speaks up to correct it. There are countless errors of this kind. We cannot finish listing them.
One thing must simply be done here: this internet traffic and the elapsed time tell us a great deal. You cannot fit this incident into that elapsed time. There is no possibility of it. To begin with, that is one thing. And throughout the process people cannot have done this on the internet вҖ” both on the internet and elsewhere.
For example, Salim GГјran had plugged his phone into the charger. We even know virtually the brand of the charger, when it was plugged in, when it was unplugged, how long it stayed there. To this they say, вҖҳHe must be using a power bank.вҖҷ Meaning, with a power bank in his pocket, with the phone, he runs from room to barn, from barn to room. And meanwhile there are operations on the internet performed by the man himself. Even to this they say, вҖҳIt might be possible.вҖҷ
Or while you are speaking you say: in 8 minutes, in 11 minutesвҖҰ Even today, when you read the chief judgeвҖҷs most recent dissenting opinion, when you look at the moment the child was last seen on camera and the moment Salim GГјran first appeared at home, the time between is 11 minutes. That means Narin had not yet parted from her friends; she was already there. You say, вҖҳThese cannot fit into 11 minutes.вҖҷ Someone replies to you: вҖҳI think they can fit.вҖҷ
Now this is something that leaves you so helplessвҖҰ I mean, just because you think soвҖҰ Life does not work this way. We have to think about these things properly.
All the concrete evidence and witnesses for these peopleвҖҰ SalimвҖҷs worker is not accepted as a witness, the children are not accepted. The witnesses for YГјksel GГјran and Hediye GГјran are not accepted. The childrenвҖҷs statements are not accepted. What are these people supposed to do?
On the other side stands a man whom no one has said вҖҳlet us not dig too deepвҖҷ about вҖ” and who is the only person to have touched the childвҖҷs lifeless body.
Were there camera recordings before? Did we examine every homicide by watching it on camera and reaching a conclusion? A man took the child, put her in his own sack, loaded her into his own car, drove off and buried her. On top of her вҖ” look, in his first statement he says вҖҳ3 stonesвҖҷ вҖ” that video is available everywhere. Later he reduced it to one, вҖҳso you would say вҖҷothers came along and added stones.вҖҷвҖҷ You cannot explain that there.
He spent 38 minutes. While saying, вҖҳWe do not see the beginning of the incident,вҖҷ even those who know very well today that the family is not the murderer cannot accept this incident being shrunk down so much. And they say: вҖҳI wonder who helped Nevzat? Whom is Nevzat really hiding?вҖҷ
So this is a simple child-homicide case вҖ” by вҖҳsimpleвҖҷ I do not mean it is simple at all. The fact that it is something this complex, this intricate, does not diminish anything from you. You do not need to feel ashamed because you have come to believe that this village is a terrible place. We are living human beings. The opinions of the living change; we are not the dead.
At the very beginning I believed that the family was guilty. But of course there is something called journalistic ethics. Because I know it from my own fieldвҖҰ There are also other things I wrote and drew up earlier. Even at the moment when I thought the family was guilty, you cannot make a single sentence pass that demonises them like this. People had not yet appeared in court.
I had simply written a piece that, instead of jumping to the sociology of this incident, looked at the situation of the geography and the country: вҖҳHow can it be that a family kills its own child?вҖҷ We need to be discussing these things in Turkey today. A formidable culture of lynching is at work. And in fact, many of us cannot escape it.
For example, today you see that there is a suicide case in DiyarbakДұr вҖ” perhaps you covered it as well. A name comes up there. Actually, it is not right to immediately put these things into headlines. They should be followed in some way, raised properly. But the moment everyone jumps onto these allegations, the truth becomes invisible. This is something very dangerous. The invisibility of the truth could one day inflict very heavy harm on all of us. Today this is being inflicted on the village of TavЕҹantepe.
I have thought about this issue a great deal. They went so harshly after this village. From seven to seventy, they suffered cruelty, they suffered torture. And so muchвҖҰ in this country, amid this decay, this villageвҖҰ вҖҳLet one of you take the blame,вҖҷ they were told for example. None of this villageвҖҰ Of course, taking on a crime is one thing вҖ” but they did not falsely say вҖҳSalim did it, I saw it.вҖҷ Nor did they say вҖҳYГјksel did such-and-such.вҖҷ They so very muchвҖҰ stood up for a certain dignity, the dignity of a communityвҖҰ Instead of seeing this as concealing something, instead of seeing it as covering up a crimeвҖҰ in such concrete circumstancesвҖҰ look, this villageвҖҷs people, even amid this decay, do not throw blame on someone in order to save themselves. There can be no such reticence. No reasoning can explain this to us.
They are all relatives. They do not speak. The relative is the wolf of the relative. Anyone with even a little life experience knows this. Meaning a relative can do many things to save themselves. But hereвҖҰ they are not doing something they do not know. YГјksel HanДұm, despite all the suffering she has endured, still says: вҖҳI am a Muslim. I have not seen it with my own eyes.вҖҷ By saying вҖҳI have not seen it with my own eyes,вҖҷ sheвҖҰ even though her suspicionвҖҰ for instance, she names Nevzat with a certain caution. These people are not protecting anything.
If you look at these people with even a little of what I would call the eye of the heart, you will see their helplessness. And reallyвҖҰ in my opinion, TavЕҹantepe is a terribly abandoned village. A terribly abandoned village. And this hurts me deeply inside. We must stand up for this place.
Now they tell us: вҖҳWhat did the family give you?вҖҷ These accusations are so foolish, so disgusting. Under normal circumstances I am someone who would actually like to go to that village, visit Arif Baba, hold his children, drop by NarinвҖҷs grave. I am the MP of that province. But on the day I wrote that piece I knew very well that I would face a barrage of accusations.
Look, I am very saddened as I say this, but something will happen, in such a miraculous wayвҖҰ The way I am thinking about it is this: I went to court on an ordinary day. This subject got tangled around my foot. I mean I could not escape it, because I saw something. And I thought that I needed to examine it. Maybe at the Court of Cassation a lawyer will see something like this. Maybe another, larger, more powerful state figure will see this. And will say: вҖҳWell, this did not happen this way.вҖҷ
Or this will happen: ten years from now, twenty years from now, a novel will be written, a film will be made. And unfortunately on that dayвҖҰ because everyone, since all of this is forgotten so quicklyвҖҰ will have forgotten all this information, will have forgotten everything they had defended, and will turn towards that work with a more virgin mindвҖҰ everyone will leave that film in tremendous sorrow.
And this will be the subject of such a thing. That is, this will become a story of the century. I do not use these expressions easily.
This is the most terrifying event that can befall a person within a household. You must have the capacity to think about it this way. You must have that human capacity. To think вҖ” without saying вҖҳIt could have been meвҖҷвҖҰ The most beautiful thing вҖ” the most beautiful, the most precious вҖ” is that. You will also think that it was not you. But, вҖҳthis could have been someone close to me, whose child has been killed and is now in a cell trying to cope with this thoughtвҖҰ and labelled, on top of everything, as a murderer.вҖҷ
Are we going to give this its due only by seeing it in a film, years later? If today something new really came out and they said to me: вҖҳThere you go, the murderer is in fact Salim or Enes,вҖҷ I would say, again, that I am sorry for everything I have thought, and I would re-examine it.
As I said, we are alive. We have to do this. But at the moment we do not have this in our hands. The gossip about thisвҖҰ nonsense, вҖҳso-and-so said this, so-and-so said thatвҖҷвҖҰ the electric wires this wayвҖҰ the childrenвҖҷs вҖҳI saw her at 7, I saw her at 5вҖҷвҖҰ I have said this many times: let a child go missing, walk into a classroom, and at least three children will tell different stories. This is not really a lie; it is a confusion of mind, a desire to be interesting. This is what I call life experience. From this, how can you produce a вҖҳchild drawn into crimeвҖҷ? It hasnвҖҷt come out. There is no such thing. Later the child too said, вҖҳI didnвҖҷt actually see her,вҖҷ вҖҳnot twice but only once.вҖҷвҖҰ
Now this verdict cannot be founded on these things. And this is an endlessly unjust verdict. Here, with all this вҖҳwell, what do you say to this, and to thatвҖҰвҖҷ I wish we could open a Google Drive and write each one out, one by one. вҖҳTo this we say this. This is so. This is not even in the file. This is not even in the file. What you, today, as a lawyer, are saying вҖ” that this family also killed another child of theirs вҖ” is not even in the file. The childвҖҷs grave was not even reopened. Because there are hospital reports.вҖҷ
One has to say: stop saying this now, it is shameful.
As a final word I will say thisвҖҰ someone wrote, beneath one of the comments I made on the internet, on social media. The most beautiful words I have heard in recent yearsвҖҰ I would like, by tying them somewhat to the peace process we are in, to repeat them again here in their place.
They had written:

вҖңI want to live in the country of those who defend NarinвҖҷs family.вҖқ

Oh, how precious that is. What does it mean вҖ” вҖҳthe country of those who defend NarinвҖҷs familyвҖҷ? You look aroundвҖҰ I am, of course, someone who pays close attention to social media. For me it has always been a field of work, an ethnography. I look at everything there. Those who defend Narin are slowly forming a public. Among them are psychologists, doctors, lawyers, workers, the unemployed, ordinary housewivesвҖҰ the devout and the religious, the atheist, members of the DEM Party, members of the AKPвҖҰ and all of them, together, are demanding justice for this child.
What a peaceful and what a precious thing this is. So this is what is meant by вҖҳthe country of those who defend Narin.вҖҷ
I would never want to get into such personal things вҖ” they truly were not worth it вҖ” but I want to say this: while on one side such a public is growing, what do we see on the other?
On MotherвҖҷs Day, we see a despicable lawyerly humanity that says, вҖҳHappy MotherвҖҷs Day to all mothers, may they not die, may they not be killed, except for YГјksel GГјran.вҖҷ
We see despicable lawyering that says, вҖҳEnes attempted suicide, supposedlyвҖҰ he didnвҖҷt croak.вҖҷ If you say this to a childвҖҰ detained, defenceless, with terrible accusations, held in a province unknown to him, you can drive someone to suicide. Someone harms him, and they can stage it as suicide. Have such things not happened?
Again, in this context, a final word: Nevzat cannot do this. He knows he will be killed; terrible things will happen to him. And the basis for him not being interrogated is something else: think about this so farвҖҰ What human has ever held back from committing a crime? Most of the time it is just like this. Those who commit this crime do not commit it with the confidence that вҖҳnothing will happen to them.вҖҷ Nor would Nevzat. How can the sentence вҖҳno one in the village can shoo their chickensвҖҷ be a piece of evidence for a lawyer?
ReallyвҖҰ wellвҖҰ I am not at all someone who easily loses their temper. I am very patient. But it is very painful.
I hope justice will be served. At the Court of Cassation, this issue will really be resolved as it should be. And this torment will come to an end.
Meanwhile, to everyone living under this oppression I want to say this: may God grant you patience. Be patient. Knowing that you are right may, perhaps, be a great strength.
If onlyвҖҰ today I were beside YГјksel HanДұm, I would want to say certain things to her, perhaps to give her strength. Imagine you are in a war and have been taken prisoner. And this will pass. Try to look at it this way. Otherwise it is very hard to bear.вҖқ

In response to a question by Rojda AltДұntaЕҹ:

вҖңDear teacher, how would you have explained all of this at Ankara UniversityвҖҷs Faculty of Communication? Or back when you were at school, when you saw what was happening, and now that we have come to todayвҖҷs process вҖ” what would you have thought, what would your students have thought?вҖқ


вҖңI would probably have opened a course on thisвҖҰ a new course. A course in which we could evaluate how this disinformation, this вҖҳpost-truthвҖҷ вҖ” this thing called the post-truth вҖ” has become concrete in a single judicial case; how the media has shaped public opinionвҖҰ we could evaluate all of this, but in the new media world. This is, on the one hand, a world of tremendous advantages вҖ” that is, a world that brings together both вҖҳthose who defend NarinвҖҷ and those who defend NarinвҖҷs mother and family вҖ” but at the same time it is full of tremendous dangers. And all of this was already our subject of study.
I was already teaching a course called вҖҳNew Media and Social Activism.вҖҷ Probably, when I open many other courses around this new media, this case would have been a very major case study for all of us. And I am sure many theses will be written. In my early days of speaking I thought of this: if we did not come out and speak, all the theses would have gone in the direction of this familyвҖҷs guilt. And in the field of science a corpus of garbage would have formed. But now a path has been opened. I spoke. You spoke first. And I keep saying: in those early days I was, frankly, looking at these lawyer friends on the day I attended the first hearing вҖ” wondering how they were defending this family. The first two hours. But after listening to their defenceвҖҰ on one side them, on the other those trying to accuse the familyвҖҰ I saw two such different stances thatвҖҰ it is thanks to them that we have come to this point.
This has opened a new field. These things will no longer be written so easily. And this truth will definitely come out. But today; today, only somewhat later. This cannot continue this way. And really, even that internet traffic alone, if just that were sufficiently examined, not a single one of these family members should remain in prison for a single minute.
And in this sense I want to call out once more: please, let everyone bear the responsibility of their words. Let everyone know what they are saying. And let them not take on guilt. Guilt is a very dangerous thing. ReallyвҖҰ how is it that we can use, so easily, words about another personвҖҷs life that are aimed at destroying that life? If we are going to speak, we must first research.
I have seen this: вҖҳI have not read at all, but the lawyers say so.вҖҷ If law had exhausted all subjects, then why have we, until now, in all these womenвҖҷs homicides, been filling courtrooms? Why have we filled the corridors? Why have we kept giving press statements with bar associations? There are things law cannot exhaust. Sometimes investigative journalism, sometimes good social psychology fills that gap.
IвҖҰ I wish I had not been one of the people on whom this work fell. Truly, I wish I had not. I have even rebuked some journalist friends and students. This was your job. I mean, there was much room here that an investigative journalist could have penetrated, where the law could not seal it, could not exhaust it. You did not do this, I told them. I say it again: I wish that everyone speaks with this responsibility, and that they no longer harm this family further.
As an MP for DiyarbakДұr, I will do everything in my power. And I will be able to explain this matter every dayвҖҰ I will explain to anyone who is curious how I came to this conviction, as far as time allows.вҖқ

вҖ” Sevilay ГҮelenk, 31 May 2025
Channel: Rojda AltДұntaЕҹ Broadcast date: 31 May 2025

title: What was Nevzat Bahtiyar asked? Latest hearing (Part 1)
section: Description
text:
Description
IвҖҷm back on YouTube streaming after a long pause. Tomorrow, 16 April, the second hearing in Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs retrial takes place; I wanted to do this stream specifically before it.
Over the past 20 months we tried to tell the Narin GГјran story in X (formerly Twitter) Spaces. Recently a widely-watched documentary channel heard us, and condensed what we have been saying into a summary documentary that resonated widely. I hope that resonance reaches the responsible authorities and the case is reopened.
In this stream I read the first part of the 6 April hearing transcript: what Nevzat Bahtiyar said, the moment Salim GГјran called him to Arif GГјranвҖҷs house, the prosecutorвҖҷs questions, and defence lawyer Adnan AtaЕҹвҖҷs arguments on the Court of CassationвҖҷs reversal and the 15:08 call. My real focus is the murder hours in the file вҖ” once that half-hour window is understood, the dust and smoke will clear.
With this return broadcast, in the coming programmes I will explain everything in the file with documents вҖ” in visual and video form, down to fine detail. Along the way IвҖҷve taken a sharp tone at times toward those who continue to produce content and earn money from NarinвҖҷs body вҖ” whom I called вҖңbridge-side panhandlersвҖқ in the stream; IвҖҷm determined to expose that dirty content piece by piece.
Channel: UДҹur Korkmaz Release date: 15 April 2026

title: The lie that Salim GГјran drove to the creek bed at night
section: Description
text:
Description
Yesterday and the day before I read the Nevzat Bahtiyar hearing transcripts. In this stream I enter the file itself вҖ” in an atmosphere where almost everyone who has narrated this case looked at the GГјran family as the killers, and where the court and the prosecutor pursued the same line, I will try to show clearly that the family is innocent.
Narin GГјran was last seen on the school camera on 21 August 2024 while walking home from her Quran class; no news came from her after that and 18 days later we learned that she had been murdered. During that process the statements in the file were leaked before any indictment had been finalised, and the family was convicted in the public conscience through disinformation before a hearing had even taken place. The court then functioned merely as a confirmation of that public verdict.
The focus of this stream is the uncle, Salim GГјran. According to the prosecutor and the narrowed-base report, between 22:34 and 23:01 on the night of the murder Salim drove a vehicle from the village toward the EДҹertutmaz creek. Yet the original farm-camera footage shows that while that vehicle was in motion, the voice recordings on SalimвҖҷs phone place him on foot in the village вҖ” surrounded by crowds, with ambient voices and conversations captured around him. At 23:01 Salim makes a second phone call, while the vehicle is still on the dirt road and has not yet entered TavЕҹantepe. He cannot have been in that vehicle.
In the streams ahead I will lay out Salim GГјranвҖҷs 24 hours on 21 August as far as possible with concrete material evidence: from leaving for the field in the morning to going shopping in DiyarbakДұr in the evening, from phone records to camera footage. I believe this case, like those of GГјlistan Doku, ГҮaДҹla TuДҹaltay and Rogin KabayДұЕҹ, will not close in its current form. Sweeping it under the rug helps no one вҖ” those responsible need to understand this.
Channel: UДҹur Korkmaz Release date: 17 April 2026
