Presiding Judge : And you never spoke with Nevzat either.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : With NevzatвҖ”on the fifteenth day, he came to the mosque.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you ever talk about this matter with your mother?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : How, Your Honor?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When these allegations arose, when you were detained, werenвҖҷt you and your mother detained together?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : We were detained together.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : During that time, did you have a chance to ask her?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No, there wasnвҖҷt an opportunity, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you never asked, вҖңMother, did you have a relationship with someone? Did you kill my sister?вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I did not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : All right. Is Salim GГјran the head of this household?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Other than being the village headman, does Salim GГјran have any title in your household?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.В No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Is it possible that Salim GГјran committed the killing?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt know, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You donвҖҷt know. Do you have any opinion as to whether Nevzat might have committed the killing?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Only about the car saleвҖ”thatвҖҷs what stuck in my mind.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So would he be so vicious as to kill your sister over a car sale?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : In that gathering of eldersвҖ”my father Arif GГјran said to Nevzat Bahtiyar, because he stuttersвҖ”вҖңDonвҖҷt stutter, son.вҖқ Pardon me, Mr.В Prosecutor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Go on, go onвҖ”you can even curse openly.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : вҖңDonвҖҷt talk back, son; wherever you get that money, you will bring it. It is a right. A case has been filed; he must pay the money.вҖқ And then, Your Honor, as for Nevzat Bahtiyar saying, вҖңI went up the hill; I gave the money there to YГјksel GГјran and Arif GГјranвҖқвҖ”I was there too, Your Honor. Nevzat Bahtiyar came and handed the money to my father. My father handed it to my mother. And Nevzat Bahtiyar sat among us and said, вҖңThereвҖҷs no problem. If you like, you never have to come at all.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : All right. ThereвҖҷs also this, Enes. In your fatherвҖҷs press statement and in your brotherвҖҷs statements, you start by saying what you just said: вҖңWho is Nevzat?вҖқ You say, вҖңWho is Nevzat to come to our house?вҖқ Your brother even had a grave remark: вҖңWho is Nevzat to even look at our chicken?вҖқ So, after you received the money from the man you call вҖңWho is Nevzat,вҖқ you still say вҖңWho is Nevzat.вҖқ This man is timid and meek; we have all concluded he is a stutterer. Do you really believe that such a person would be capable of killing your sisterвҖ”according to the opposing sideвҖҷs claim, that he would announce that everyone had left the village, pay the girl money, lure her over, and execute such a plan?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I do, Your Honor. He had already paid her before.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : WhatвҖҷs wrong with giving money?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I mean, by giving money he got her accustomed to him.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So youвҖҷre saying he had been planning this for a week or two.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : How much money are we talking about? Fifty thousand lira? Would Nevzat really lose his mind over a car matter to the point of killing someone?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : In fact, because my father used that phrase to him, from thereвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When did this happen?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Before I went to Malatya, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Approximately?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Approximately four months ago.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Four months. So you think he bore a grudge from that incident and made a plan. All right. But you still have no definite opinion on whether Nevzat actually did the killing.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Exactly.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : NarinвҖҷs friends, in statements taken with a child psychologist present, said thatвҖ”as her name suggestsвҖ”she was delicate, and that she would not get into strangersвҖҷ cars or meet with strangers. Would Narin consider Nevzat a stranger or not?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : If he had given her money, she wouldnвҖҷt see him as a stranger, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : If he had given her money, she wouldnвҖҷt. Before these events came to light, did you ever see Nevzat giving Narin money, were you present, or did Narin tell you anything like that?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : My mother told me. She said Nevzat was giving her money.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : And did you not demand an explanationвҖ”вҖңWho is Nevzat to give Narin money?вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No, Your Honor. My mother told me this before the court proceedings.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Before the court matterвҖ”before Narin went missingвҖ”did you ever see or hear that Nevzat was giving her money?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Your Honor, I was in Arguvan, Malatya at that time.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did Narin not tell you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : вҖңBrother, Nevzat is giving me money?вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I arrived on the 20th anyway.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Even if not you, she would tell Muhammed, Eren, Osman, YГјksel, Arif. I donвҖҷt mean you personallyвҖ”you were out of town. Did none of them hear anything?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Because hereвҖҷs the thing: if Nevzat were giving money, and given your family structure, if you heard about that money, with your вҖңWho is Nevzat?вҖқ attitude you would easily confront himвҖ”вҖңWhatвҖҷs this about, brother, why are you giving her money?вҖқ Even Eren, as I understand it, could ask this.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Your Honor, it was never told to me.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : It was never told.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Do any of our judges have questions? No.В Mr.В Prosecutor, do you have any questions? Please, speak close to the microphone, Mr.В Prosecutor.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Enes GГјran
section: Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ
text:
Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ

Trial Prosecutor : Enes GГјran. Are you a graduate of the Science and Technology High School?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : As the Presiding Judge emphasized, when you said around 18 you thought it meant 5вҖ”what did you mean? Around 18 is 6, right?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Now, in the gendarmerie interview record of August 23, the information-taking record of August 24, and again on August 25вҖ”three records in a rowвҖ”you say you started searching for Narin around 16:30, correct? IвҖҷm asking because we see you as a sensible young man.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, exactly.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : These are the points we find odd. You say you started searching at 16:30, outside?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, exactly.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Yet you do not accept this now, correct?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You donвҖҷt accept it. Why did you give a false statement? You say your mind was confused, is that it?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : My sister was missing and still not found, and people in the neighborhood mostly kept saying times: вҖңI saw her here at this time; I saw her at that time.вҖқвҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So thatвҖҷs why you gave the same statement for the first three days.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, exactly.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Not one day, not twoвҖ”three days you said, вҖңI was searching for my sister at 16:30,вҖқ purely due to confusion?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, exactly.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : All right. How many times did you leave the house?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Twice, Your Honor. Twice.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : The first time? At 4 oвҖҷclock. You went out at 16:00вҖ”around 15:57. Then the base data matches, right?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Base data?вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Yes.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : What do you mean?вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : The base station data we allegedвҖ”at 15:57 you went to SalimвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So that matches. No? You say you went out at 16:00.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I didnвҖҷt go to his house.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : That matchesвҖ”IвҖҷm talking about leaving the house. You went out once?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What did you do that first time?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I went down toward the fountain, to Tahir Kaya; I went to the grocery and asked for an energy drink.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What time did you get back?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : At 05:30.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You returned at 05:30. When did you go out again?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Around 6.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You went out at 05:30. What time did you say you called Osman?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember calling Osman.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Could it have been between 05:30 and 06:00?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : When I got home, I noticed my phone was off and my slippers and shoes were torn. ThatвҖҷs when I called my brother Osman.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : X-XXX-XXX-26-21вҖ”thatвҖҷs OsmanвҖҷs number, isnвҖҷt it?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Osman uses that number. YesвҖ”the one ending in 26-21.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You have a call at 17:49вҖ”10 seconds. Another at 17:50вҖ”four minutes, 220 seconds. Correct?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : There was talk about slippers, right?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You told him to get size 42 slippers from ГҮarДұklДұ. You couldnвҖҷt have talked about slippers for four minutes. What did you talk about for four minutes?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Well, since my brother Osman was in OngГ¶zlГј, and I have friends in OngГ¶zlГј, I also talked with my friends.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Your brother was working as a waiter in OngГ¶zlГј?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : At that time. What did you do? I donвҖҷt quite understandвҖ”what did you talk about for four minutes?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Since my friends also work at that cafГ©, I asked him to hand the phone to themвҖ”so that no one would bother or talk to my brother Osman.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You brought this up at a time when your battery was low. Your battery was low, right? ThatвҖҷs what you say.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What did you talk about with your father at 17:43 for 24 seconds?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Just three to five minutes before the slipper call.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What time did your battery die then?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Around six oвҖҷclock, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Around six. According to the base data, itвҖҷs said your phone turned off at 18:13. That matches exactly, doesnвҖҷt it?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : If you look at it according to that, Mr.В Prosecutor.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : IвҖҷm not asking about the base data. It says here your phone turned off at 18:13.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Does that match?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : It doesnвҖҷt exactly.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What time did it turn off? You said around six.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, thatвҖҷs correct.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Then when your phone turned off, you just said your mother asked you to call your father to see if he was coming for dinner.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : And you said you couldnвҖҷt call because the phone died, right? Then you spoke from your motherвҖҷs phone, correct?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, my mother spoke with him.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Your mother spoke.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : But your last call was a four-minute one with Osman.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I didnвҖҷt call my father from my own phone anyway.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You did call your fatherвҖ”at 17:43.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I didnвҖҷt call my father to say something like вҖңWill you be late for dinnerвҖқ or anything like that.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You donвҖҷt remember what you talked about with your father during that minute.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : And, Your Honor, Mr.В Prosecutor, my mother came to me and said, вҖңSon, call your father and brother to see if theyвҖҷll make it for dinner.вҖқ So I checked my phone and realized it was off. Then I told my mother to bring her phone so we could call from it. Since my mother canвҖҷt read or write, I dialed and handed her the phone. She spoke with him.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : All right, your phone turned off around six, at 18:13. What were you doing then?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I put my phone on charge.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You were home.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, I was home.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Why didnвҖҷt you plug it in earlier?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I did plug it in.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What time did you turn it back on?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : When we realized that Narin was missing.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You didnвҖҷt turn your phone on until 19:11вҖ”about an hour. The phone was on charge for an hour, according to your statements. Around 19:11 is when you noticed Narin was missingвҖ”around dinner, correct? ThatвҖҷs what the base data indicates. Your mother brought her phone to you, and you started calling people with it, right? You said, вҖңMy mother asked us to call some people since she canвҖҷt read or write,вҖқ didnвҖҷt you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You said that. Then why didnвҖҷt you turn on your own phone to help contact people yourself?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : It didnвҖҷt occur to me.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Why was your mother the only one calling people? Why didnвҖҷt you call your father to let him know at that time?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : It didnвҖҷt occur to me; I didnвҖҷt even realize the seriousness of the situation yet.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Your sister was missing. Your phone was charged and plugged in, but you went out without taking it, is that right?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You went out to search for your sister without taking or turning on your phone.вҖӢ


Defense Attorneys : Your Honor, I apologize. There will be a misunderstanding here. At that time, there was no confirmed report of a disappearance. But Enes has this desire to answer every question, to satisfy everyone. It might turn into one of those public misinterpretations again.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : I didnвҖҷt understand what you meant by вҖңmisinterpretation.вҖқвҖӢ


Defense Attorney Mahir Akbilek : Your Honor, as my colleague mentioned, I forgot to point out that regarding these questionsвҖ”excuse meвҖ”we intentionally didnвҖҷt refer to Article 206/2a. We discussed it among ourselves. For example, the basis of whatвҖҷs being defended hereвҖ”the concept and its legal nature are uncertain, and itвҖҷs unclear who prepared these documents. Did we all agree beforehand to treat this document as material evidence, as a conclusive basis, before using it as the backbone for questioning?вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : First of all, Your Honor, HTS and base data are separate things. My questions to Enes concern HTS only. The BTK responses show a 17:43 call. Do you acknowledge that?вҖӢ


Defense Attorney Mahir Akbilek : IвҖҷm very seriousвҖ”believe me, when itвҖҷs our turn to speak according to proper CMK procedure, youвҖҷll see that the document you think is reliable evidence is, in fact, incredibly misleadingвҖ”you wonвҖҷt believe it.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : But, Counselor, my question logically follows. IвҖҷm referring to HTS data, not base dataвҖ”HTS.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Since I read your written defense thoroughly, I know your position regarding those base recordsвҖ”no, no, let me finish. When I asked Nevzat a question earlier, I made a referenceвҖ”to you, correct? You said you did not accept the base data. So why did you find it inappropriate when I used that same base record, which is also in the indictment as evidence, to question with?вҖӢ


Defense Attorney Mahir Akbilek : Not at all, Your Honor, itвҖҷs a misunderstanding.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I see. All right, no problem. ThatвҖҷs why I made that referenceвҖҰвҖӢ


In this part of our recording, simultaneous conversation among the defense attorneys, complainantвҖҷs counsel, and the court panel caused overlapping audio, and approximately two minutes of the recording could not be transcribed.вҖӢ


Defense Attorney Mahir Akbilek : We all know thereвҖҷs a background here, and the mistakes from that past are before us. Each of usвҖ”lawyers, all jurists, and the publicвҖ”we all share thisвҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : All right, letвҖҷs get to the conclusion. Proceed with the defense.вҖӢ


Defense Attorney Mahir Akbilek : Your Honor, at least in these direct questions drawn from the limited data set, the defendant should be given time to think. At least five or ten seconds to breathe before answering. Just because heвҖҷs a science high school graduate, does that make him Einstein?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I only wish you had shown the same sensitivity when questioning Nevzat BahtiyarвҖ”instead of firing off questions one after another. If you had waited for him to breathe, IвҖҷd be more convinced of your sincerity. But IвҖҷm not.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : In your four-minute call with Osman, you said you also talked with your friends. You explained thatвҖҷs why it took that long. Since you have that level of detail, I naturally feel entitled to ask what you discussed with your father at 17:43, based on the HTS record. The court accepted the indictmentвҖҷs base data, so asking HTS-related questions is entirely legitimate.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Please, go ahead, Mr.В Prosecutor.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Why did you ask for slippers?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Because my shoes were torn, Your Honor. I had come from Arguvan, Malatya.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : ThatвҖҷs all?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes. Because I came from out of town.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Did he bring the slippers?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : He did. But I didnвҖҷt wear them.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You didnвҖҷt wear them. All right. No further questions, Your Honor. Thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : WeвҖҷll begin with questions from the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association. As the court, we do not take sides with any of the defendants. The way I treated Nevzat is the same way I treated Enes. If anyone has a different impression, please say so. If anyone says, вҖңYour Honor, we got such an impression,вҖқ IвҖҷll leave my robe right here. IвҖҷm being very clearвҖ”I would leave my robe and withdraw from this case. I will show the same approach to YГјksel and to Salim. On Monday, I have another trial; IвҖҷll act exactly the same there. I donвҖҷt look at any case based on who the defendant is. For me, this case has no special distinction from any other. If I ever made such a distinction, I would quit this professionвҖ”very clearly. If anyone here, including lawyers from the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association, can say the Presiding Judge shows a different level of attention in this case compared to others, IвҖҷll step down immediately. Can anyone say that? Let someone from the DiyarbakДұr Bar say it. IвҖҷm saying this openly and clearly. IвҖҷll even adjourn this hearing today if needed and withdraw from it. I show the same diligence in every case. Let me clarify thatвҖ”to clear any doubts. I say the same thing to the press: for us, this case is just one of hundreds of cases we handle. It only receives more attention because itвҖҷs more significant in your eyes, and we respond with proportional care. WeвҖҷll continue on Monday as well. Monday we have a murder case with detained defendants; weвҖҷll show the same diligence there. I show equal respect to every defendant. IвҖҷve shown the same respect to drug dealers, to those accused of killing children, and to those accused of killing their spouses. Therefore, regarding our courtвҖҷs stanceвҖ”IвҖҷm explaining, not defendingвҖ”I wonвҖҷt engage with these allegations. Let there be no such doubt in anyoneвҖҷs mind. The same respect I show you, I showed Nevzat. HeвҖҷs not my fatherвҖҷs son, and neither are you. IвҖҷm seeing you for the first time. IвҖҷll show the same respect to Ms.В YГјksel and to Mr.В Salim. IвҖҷm not even from DiyarbakДұr; I may be transferred this year or next. If anyone has any gossip or doubts about bias, please state it openly, so everyone knows the court stands above suspicion. Let me make that very clear. Mr.В Nahit knows this, and so do you. IвҖҷve been transparent from the beginning. Every time you spoke, I said вҖңamennaвҖқ and acted accordingly. IвҖҷm saying all this because of the impression IвҖҷve received.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Enes GГјran
section: DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ
text:
DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ

Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir вҖӢ

Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Enes, you said that around 17:30 on the day of the incident, you returned home. When you came back, was there anyone in the house?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I didnвҖҷt see anyone.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : I mean your own house. When you returned, did you see anyone at all? Your mother?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No, I didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Did you see Hediye GГјran?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No, I didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : You didnвҖҷt see Hediye GГјran?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : How many times did Ms.В Hediye go and come that day?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I only saw her once.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : You only saw her once.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, when I woke up inside the house.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : After that, she didnвҖҷt come back?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : So when you returned home at 17:30, Hediye wasnвҖҷt thereвҖ”youвҖҷre sure of that. All right. How did you bite your arm? Can you show us?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Should I?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No need to show it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Take off your jacket. You can show it clearly. LetвҖҷs record it on camera.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I was sitting on the couch like this. I bit my arm like this and rested it on the couch. My father was sitting by the window. Because I heard the sound of him crying, I bit my arm hard and leaned it toward the couchвҖ”so that he wouldnвҖҷt get upset again, so he could sleep at night.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Where did you bite?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Here, look.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Can you show it?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : LookвҖ”itвҖҷs still here, look.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : All right. When you bit, didnвҖҷt your body reflexively pull back?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : There are three visible bite marks, and according to the forensic report, at least three to six days had passed. Yet the teeth marks are clearly visible in the photos. Only whether itвҖҷs from an adult or not couldnвҖҷt be determined. When pain reaches a certain level, the body reflexively pulls away, whether you want it to or not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Counselor, thatвҖҷs not really a fair question.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : It didnвҖҷt?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Sometimes in extreme situations, people donвҖҷt even realize theyвҖҷve been shot until a minute laterвҖ”or even five minutes. LetвҖҷs not go into medical science. LetвҖҷs move on.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Your Honor, there are medical articles about this. We can request an expert opinion later.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : LetвҖҷs not debate medicine here. Leave that to the doctors.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : I only asked because itвҖҷs medical data.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : The cornfieldвҖ”on the day of the incident. What time did you go there?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : It was nighttime.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Nighttime. Approximately?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I didnвҖҷt have my phone.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : All right. What were you wearing that night?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : A sweatshirt with вҖңColumbiaвҖқ written on this side.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : I canвҖҷt quite hear you.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : A sweatshirt with вҖңColumbiaвҖқ written on this side. It was all black. And these pants.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : A black sweatshirt. Did you have a jacket?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : No jacket?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Then how do you explain the marks on your back if you were wearing a sweatshirt?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : The marks on my back came from when we went into the barns on the second or third day. We entered Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs barn and also the barns near SГјheyla GГјranвҖҷs house. Those places hadnвҖҷt been entered before. I was with Yunus KaracoЕҹan. When we went into the barns near SГјheyla GГјranвҖҷs house, Yunus KaracoЕҹan saw it too. Everything there is nailed and woodenвҖ”itвҖҷs a rundown place.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : So, the cornfieldвҖ”those marks on your back, they didnвҖҷt happen there?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Not in the cornfield. All right, thank you. I have no further questions. Thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Please state your name. Go ahead.вҖӢ



Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk вҖӢ

Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : You had lunch on the day of the incident. Then Narin looked at the clock, correct? She noticed the time.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : вҖңMom, itвҖҷs late. IвҖҷm going to the mosque.вҖқ My mother said, вҖңItвҖҷs hot, dear; donвҖҷt go today.вҖқ And Narin insisted, вҖңHatice and Fatma are going to the mosque, so I want to go too.вҖқ My mother said okay.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Did Narin make a gesture with her fingers and say, вҖңThere is a course until four oвҖҷclock; let me go thereвҖқ?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I did not see anything like that.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : I do.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : Monthly.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : He couldnвҖҷt pay. He was in a difficult situation. I did not insist on asking him for money.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : Yes. He said, вҖңWhen you go to DiyarbakДұr, in three or four daysвҖ”before the weddingвҖ”IвҖҷll send it to you.вҖқ I said okay.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : He gives me cash on hand. My father transferred money to my account. He knows I donвҖҷt have cash on me. I am his son and I work for him for months. So, 200 TL in the morning, too.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : You said you met with Muhammed YaДҹmur when you left the house. How long did your conversation with Muhammed YaДҹmur last?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Do you mean only with him, or with Еһevket as well?вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : With Muhammed YaДҹmur.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : About half an hour.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt quite remember the times.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : There is a significant difference between half an hour and one and a half hours. It makes a serious difference whether you sit with someone for half an hour or for one and a half hours. How accurate is it to explain this by saying you donвҖҷt remember?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Very accurate.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : LetвҖҷs avoid argumentative questions. WeвҖҷll skip this one, yes.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : When did you ask your brother Osman for slippers?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Me? All right. I came home around the eveningвҖ”around half past five. ThatвҖҷs when I asked my brother for slippers.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : After asking Osman for slippers, did you go out again?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Then why did you ask him to send them to your motherвҖҷs phone even though you were going out again?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Because my battery was low. My battery indicator doesnвҖҷt show the exact percentage. When itвҖҷs 20% or 15%, it turns red, and itвҖҷs unclear when it will shut down. ThatвҖҷs what I want to tell you.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Yes. All right, thatвҖҷs natural of course. Since your phone battery would die, you could ask him to send it to your motherвҖҷs phone. ThereвҖҷs no dispute there.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : All right.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : But you were not at homeвҖ”you were going to leave the house. Why did you ask him to send it to your motherвҖҷs phone? You wouldnвҖҷt see it if it arrived. Why ask for it on your motherвҖҷs phone?вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Are you asking why my brother Osman sent the photos to my motherвҖҷs phone? Yes. Why did you ask for them there and not to your own phone? I asked for them that way.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : In your prison conversations with Eren, as the panel asked as well, you give him suggestions like вҖңDonвҖҷt tell anyone. Stay quiet.вҖқвҖӢ


Defense Attorneys : We object.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThereвҖҷs nothing to object to. Objection overruled. Go ahead, Counsel. Ask your question.вҖӢ

Presiding Judge : Explain whether he could hide that he went to prison.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : EveryoneвҖ”the entire 85 millionвҖ”knows you were in prison, the whole GГјran family knows. Do you think you could hide this information from Eren?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : We are not hiding information from Eren.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Then whyвҖ”just now you said in your statementвҖ”right. When explaining the reason for that prison conversation with Eren, you said you didnвҖҷt want Eren to know.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : ThatвҖҷs what IвҖҷm saying. He is still my little brother. He doesnвҖҷt even know his sister was killed. We told him she went on vacation. I didnвҖҷt want him to know I went to prison either.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : ThatвҖҷs what you think. All right. ThatвҖҷs all my questions. Thank you.вҖӢ

Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : ThatвҖҷs correct.вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : Do you remember what you replied?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I said if there is something in the reportвҖ”if anything has come outвҖ”tell the lawyers to inform me. ThatвҖҷs how I recall it.вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : NoвҖ”let me tell you what you replied: вҖңWhen will I get out of here?вҖқ You didnвҖҷt express any surprise about the manner of NarinвҖҷs death. You didnвҖҷt say, вҖңHow could that be?вҖқвҖ”that her mouth and nose were coveredвҖҰвҖӢ

Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : IвҖҷm saying you did not react with surprise to the manner of NarinвҖҷs death. You directly asked when you would be released. You didnвҖҷt show any curiosityвҖ”вҖңWas she killed this way?вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I was in prison; IвҖҷm an 18-year-old child. ThatвҖҷs why I said it. Because when the report came outвҖҰвҖӢ

Enes GГјran : I was in shock, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : There you also asked your father a question about bite marks not appearing after the third day.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : My father?вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : Exactly.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : What did I ask?вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : вҖңAfter the third day, bite marks wonвҖҷt appear.вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : вҖңBite marks wonвҖҷt appear?вҖқ I donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : And the response to you was something like, вҖңTell the lawyer, donвҖҷt tell me.вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : Then why is it so important to you whether the bite mark appears or not?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Because I know I bit myself. I bit myself on the third day. It has nothing to do with Narin, my mother, or anyone else.вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : Then why do you insist on asking that? ThatвҖҷs what IвҖҷm askingвҖ”whether the bite mark appears or notвҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He said he doesnвҖҷt remember, Counsel. He said he doesnвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Law. Derya YДұldДұrДұm : ThatвҖҷs all my questions.вҖӢ



Law. Nahit Eren вҖӢ

Law. Nahit Eren : I will ask you questions as counsel for the DiyarbakДұr Bar.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Of course, go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : NarinвҖ”your sisterвҖ”who was lost at age eight, first made to go missing, and then we learned she was killed. IвҖҷll ask you about Narin.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : All right, go ahead.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : How many times do you think it might have been?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Let me tell youвҖ”you gave statements six times.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Six statementsвҖ”and as the Presiding Judge said, your narratives about the dayвҖҷs timeline contradict each other across all of them. IвҖҷll now ask questions by laying out those contradictions.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : All right, go ahead.вҖӢ

Enes GГјran : Law enforcement?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Law enforcement, yes. When did they first contact you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Was it the third day? Or the fourth day? Up on the hillвҖ”by MaЕҹallah GГјranвҖҷs hillsideвҖ”blood was found among the stones near the house. My first statement was taken there.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What did they ask you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Where I went, what I was doing where, where I was. They asked these. Why I came from Malatya.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did they ask you, вҖңWhen did Narin go missing?вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : They did.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What did you say?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I said I last saw her at 1 oвҖҷclock.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did they ask you, вҖңWhen did you learn that Narin was missing?вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : They did.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What time did you say?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Back then I said 04:30.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You said 04:30.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So first it was 04:30 that Narin went missing. In other words, regarding the day Narin went missingвҖ”when a personвҖҷs mind and memory are soundвҖ”the first contact is at 16:00.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : LookвҖ”вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : No, IвҖҷm asking something. Let me ask my question, then you answer, Enes.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : All right, go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What was the first time you gave in that first contactвҖ”say it again?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : 04:30.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : 04:30.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Look, IвҖҷm saying thisвҖ”вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm not asking anything else; you donвҖҷt need to elaborate. I want an answer to my question only. Did you say you saw her at 4:30?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Along with seeing her at 04:30, did you say what else you did?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : If you believed at 04:30 that your sister had gone missing, wouldnвҖҷt a person take action?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : IвҖҷm telling you I donвҖҷt remember. I donвҖҷt remember what I spoke about.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm asking you so that you recall. You learned at 16:30 that your sister was missing.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did you start searching?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I said I did.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You said you did.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : But you hadnвҖҷt?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I realized at the evening call to prayer that my sister was missing. Around 4 oвҖҷclock.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The Presiding Judge asked earlierвҖ”yes.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The Prosecutor asked as well.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : вҖңI saw Furkan at 17:30, at 16:00вҖҰвҖқ How many days have passed? Two months. Almost two months and ten days, right? You remember all of those hour by hour.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Because these were things I experiencedвҖ”true things.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : These first contacts with law enforcementвҖ”are those things you didnвҖҷt experience, then? From the officers?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : If I speak now, it wonвҖҷt work.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : SpeakвҖ”thatвҖҷs why weвҖҷre here. WeвҖҷre here to find NarinвҖҷs killer, arenвҖҷt we? It was 16:00.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : May I speak with my lawyer?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You may. Allow him one minute to speak with his lawyer.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : All right, what are you saying? Say it again. I couldnвҖҷt enter it in the minutes; we didnвҖҷt record it on SEGBД°S.вҖӢ


Av. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : You can enter it in the minutes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : All right, go ahead.вҖӢ


Av. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Your Honor, I am truly proud to be a member of the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association. Our mission has long been to fight against torture and ill-treatment, and we are proud of that. However, when we first took this case file and first met with the defendantsвҖ”who were then suspectsвҖ”they told us they had been subjected to inhuman treatment, that is, ill-treatment by law enforcement. As a former administrator of the DiyarbakДұr Bar on this matter, we were truly surprised by these allegations. The Bar President at the time had stated they were striving to shed light on the Narin case. We acted with that confidence, and when the hearing began, we stated that even though we are defense counsel, our aim is to serve a conclusion in accordance with justice and equity. With that service mindsetвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Are we debating the institutional identity of the DiyarbakДұr Bar? Its superior performance?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I donвҖҷt understandвҖ”weвҖҷll need to wrap this up. I didnвҖҷt understand what you meant. Please. Now, let our colleague not speakвҖ”give me a moment to understand his point. You speak of respect, and I still donвҖҷt grasp his pointвҖ”how is that respectful? Goodness. Wrap it up.вҖӢ


Av. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Sir, we all know legally that there isnвҖҷt even a right to intervene in this case. But what did we say? Our goal was to uncover the material truth, correct?вҖӢ


At this point, due to simultaneous conversation among the defense attorneys, complainantвҖҷs counsel, observers, and the court panel, the audio overlapped and could not be transcribed.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : That is his own statement. As I said, if you have information on this, filing a criminal complaint with the DiyarbakДұr Chief Public ProsecutorвҖҷs Office is your most natural right. You can submit the relevant information and documents. Then cases can be opened against those responsible for failure to report a crime or for shielding offenders. Mr.В Nahit, continue with your questions.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : May I ask something as well?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : As I said, EnesвҖ”you may even repeat the insults the other side used. There is no problem whatsoever. If you wish to speak nowвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Your Honor. A moment ago, you judges expressed your perspective and approach to this case. Please allow me to respond as well. My colleague reminded us that he served for many years on the barвҖҷs executive board. Let me also remind youвҖ”we served together. He knows well how we fought against torture. The family came to me twice before spring; I believe I even heard someone from the family shouting at me from the gallery just now. The family came to me, and in all my meetings I met together with my fellow board membersвҖ”who are all in the courtroom. When they first came, they told me this: while in custody, our relatives were subjected to ill-treatment and torture. I told them: since all the lawyers assigned from the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association under the CMK are CMK-appointed counsel, if there is any allegation of ill-treatment or torture, please make sure these are recorded in the minutes during the statement. I have three lawyer colleagues, and when the family members who came to the bar raised this allegation, I asked them: could you tell us to whom, and in what manner, it was done? Perhaps they are listening in the courtroom now. Some said a tooth was pulled; some said a nail was pulled; some said punches were thrown to the face, iron rods used on the head; some said they were taken before their mother with high-pressure waterвҖ”many things were described. I said: please file an application with us regarding these matters; they were in the process of retaining counsel. I said: file an application with us about this. In fact, one of the family members called someone after the remand on the 12^th and subsequent releases. вҖңCome to the bar and explain what they did to youвҖ”file an application,вҖқ he said. вҖңI canвҖҷt come and file an application,вҖқ he replied. And why did he say he wouldnвҖҷt apply? In short, they said they wouldnвҖҷt file any application on this matter. There has not been a single application to the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association concerning allegations of torture. This was the conversation. Afterwards, not only did the family refrain from making any application to us on this matterвҖ”regardless of this file or any fileвҖ”if a suspect, perpetrator, defendant, whatever you call them, has been subjected to torture, we stand against that as well. We would continue the legal struggle for that too. But in the courtroom, let us avoid language and discourse that would put the institutional identity of the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association up for debate and place us as the target regarding our participation in this case. Law. Nahit Eren : вҖңWhen Furkan arrived, it was between 17:00 and 17:30, and when he arrived, he called Enes. When he called Enes on the phone, I went to drink water. He told me, вҖҳI called EnesвҖ”he just woke up from sleep and is heading up the hill. You go; IвҖҷll come too.вҖҷвҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : NoвҖ”absolutely not.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm asking whether such a call took place.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : There was no call. In any case, UfukвҖҷs and FurkanвҖҷs statements contradict each other. After I went thereвҖ”вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When your mother cameвҖ”she came and told you not to smokeвҖ”вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : She came down and called me to close the window.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Was Furkan with you at that moment?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did Furkan come later?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : He came later.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Furkan later came over to you. When Your Honor asked earlier and you explained your sequence of events, you said, вҖңI went back up again,вҖқ after putting the turkeys away. Did you go back up to your friends again? You went back to your friends once more.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : When I put the turkeys away and closed the window?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Yes.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, I went.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : They also sayвҖ”Furkan saysвҖ”that his mother never came to us.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Because when my mother came at that time, Furkan wasnвҖҷt with us.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : He wasnвҖҷt with you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Do you think Furkan actually described the incident correctly?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Correct. Furkan didnвҖҷt see my mother at all then.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Furkan never saw your mother.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : A moment ago, my colleague askedвҖ”he said, вҖңShow us your arm.вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You showed it.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You said maybe it was overlooked at the time. вҖңI bit it.вҖқ Then did you say you placed it on something? Did I misunderstand?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I said I was sitting on the couch. I opened my arm like this. By biting it like this, I bit down hard and let it drop toward the couchвҖ”just so my sobbing wouldnвҖҷt reach my father.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Who else was in the room besides you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No one.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You were alone?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I was alone.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : All right. The Prosecutor also asked; IвҖҷll ask as well.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Please, go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : This is the sort of question one wants to ask in the ordinary course of life. ItвҖҷs the same question I asked Nevzat while he was here.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Just as I asked Nevzat whether, if this were a normal disappearance, the fatherвҖ”or rather SalimвҖ”should or shouldnвҖҷt have called him, IвҖҷll ask you something similar. You have the right not to answer. IвҖҷll ask this: if a personвҖҷs sibling goes missing, the thing one needs most for communication is the phone, isnвҖҷt it?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : For example, can you give an explanation that would satisfy everyone here as to why you didnвҖҷt take your phone with you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Of course I can explain. Because, Your Honor, when my mother took the phone and called Hediye and others, when we went up the hill with my mother and my brother Muhammed toward MaЕҹallah GГјranвҖҷs house, MaЕҹallah GГјran said he hadnвҖҷt come to our homeвҖ”said вҖңnoвҖқвҖ”and I panicked. I immediately took Muhammed and went down toward the mosque. I went down the same road as at four oвҖҷclock. Down the same way. And I sent Muhammed to Azize KayaвҖҷs house. I was calling for Narin softly near the mosque. I wasnвҖҷt thinking about the phone or my family or my fatherвҖ”only, вҖңWhere is Narin?вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So at that moment, while calling out, you didnвҖҷt feel any need for a phone.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.В In fact, when I went into the cornfield, I used Yunus KaracoЕҹanвҖҷs phone to turn on the light.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : All right. You said that when you got home and woke up, you saw Hediye.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You said you and Hediye asked about each other.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Do you know when Hediye left your house?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt know. How would I know?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : In other words, you didnвҖҷt see herвҖ”no?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : When I saw my aunt-in-law Hediye, I left and went downstairs. I didnвҖҷt see her again.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Hediye says she stayed in your house for about two hours. She said, вҖңWhen I went out, it looked like Enes was coming in from outside.вҖқ Is that a lie?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I definitely didnвҖҷt see Hediye GГјran in that way.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You didnвҖҷt see Hediye at home?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.В I only saw her around four oвҖҷclock when I woke up and asked about her. I didnвҖҷt see her otherwise.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : After you went out and came back, you didnвҖҷt see Hediye at home again?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.В I didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Hediye says this: вҖңWhile I was at the house, Enes was there. Later, I saw Enes at the door from outside. I donвҖҷt know when he went out and came back.вҖқ The counsel is asking about that. Since you said you didnвҖҷt remember, we didnвҖҷt go into the details of HediyeвҖҷs statements. WeвҖҷll ask again when Hediye testifies.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You didnвҖҷt see Hediye again.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When you first went out with Muhammed, you went to the fountain and came back, right?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Then youвҖҷre talking about your brother Muhammed Emre, correct?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, IвҖҷm talking about Emre.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Have you read Muhammed EmreвҖҷs statement?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Do you think Muhammed Emre is at an age where he can express himself properly?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Well, as long as the child knows his sibling is missing, I donвҖҷt think heвҖҷs at that level yet.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When Hediye came to visit you, do you remember what Muhammed was doing?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt know exactly when Hediye came. You mean when I woke up, right? I just saw that Muhammed was lying down.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Muhammed was lying down. Since you were awake, he later came to you.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : All right.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When Muhammed came to you, did he say who was in the house at that time?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When Muhammed woke up, would he have been in a position to see Hediye in the house?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Well, when I was lying downвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Were you all in the same room?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : We were all in the same room. If he had woken up at that hour and seen Hediye GГјran, he would have said so.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : In all his statements, Muhammed says, вҖңI didnвҖҷt see Hediye that day.вҖқвҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Well, I only woke up around four oвҖҷclock. Let me describe the sofa for you, brother. My mother was on this side, Hediye GГјran was lying on that side. They were sitting. I was lying on the side near the window, where the air conditioner blows. When I woke up, I said, вҖңWelcome, Aunt Hediye.вҖқ She said, вҖңThank you,вҖқ and after that, I went downstairs through the door.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, Mr.В Prosecutor, go ahead.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : IвҖҷll make one statement to support my Presiding Judge. You say you didnвҖҷt see Hediye. Hediye says about you, вҖңWhen Enes was coming in from outside, I was going out the door.вҖқ She says this happened around 17:00вҖ“17:30. She even says EnesвҖҷs mother called out, вҖңEnes, why did you come back early?вҖқ And Enes, you replied, вҖңThere was no one outside.вҖқ Hediye even describes this dialogue. So, Hediye is saying that you came back home at that time.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : ThereвҖҷs no such thing.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Since you said you didnвҖҷt remember, we didnвҖҷt go into the details of HediyeвҖҷs statement. WeвҖҷll ask about it again when Hediye testifies.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : All right, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : LetвҖҷs wrap it up now. IвҖҷm asking you, please.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷll finish after this question. You said you gave a statement to the prosecution under torture and ill-treatment. Were the assessments you made about Salim also given under that pressure and torture, or were the descriptions you made about Salim true?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : They are not true.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : NoвҖ”SalimвҖ”actually, I mean Nevzat, pardon me. You made a description about Nevzat, didnвҖҷt you?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : That descriptionвҖ”the one you made about NevzatвҖ”was it given under that torture?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No torture was inflicted on me about Nevzat.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Only by Salim GГјran, you say, you were tortured. No, all right.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So, he says what he said about Nevzat is true.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The things you said about Nevzat were not statements made under pressure or torture.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : LetвҖҷs have you describe Nevzat for us.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : вҖңHeвҖҷs a filthy man. Huh. IвҖҷll speak frankly. HeвҖҷs a filthy manвҖ”the filthiest man in the village. He comes and goes, but no one ever asks anything of him. Whether he went to work, what he didвҖ”no one knows. ThereвҖҷs a car under him, one under his son, one under him again. In fact, one of my lawyersвҖ”probably hereвҖ”said that. He was his lawyer, I think. He even said, вҖҳNevzat started staying in ГҮarДұklДұ at night. At that time, while we were in custody, my uncle BarДұЕҹ GГјran came himself,вҖҷ he said. вҖҳI personally took the gendarmerie officers to their house, and when Nevzat tried to escape through the window, they caught him there,вҖҷ he said.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So, how would you describe the relationship between Nevzat and Salim?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : As far as I know, Nevzat and SalimвҖ”Salim GГјranвҖ”are friends. I used to see them together quite often. They were always side by side.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : WeвҖҷre reasoning here about what вҖңfriendsвҖқ means between two people, so IвҖҷm asking these questions to clarify the statement.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : All right, go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : About someone whoвҖҷs a friendвҖ”you said this sentence: вҖңHeвҖҷs such a cowardly person that he wouldnвҖҷt question what Salim says.вҖқ A man so cowardly he wouldnвҖҷt question SalimвҖҷs words.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Is that a friendship? In that friendship, would he be someone cowardly enough to carry a bodyвҖ”I mean, pardon meвҖ”NarinвҖҷs body? Or, if their relationship is one of submission, is he someone who would carry it without question? Because when they read that statement to you, they asked you about it, and thatвҖҷs why IвҖҷm asking this question now.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You said this personвҖ”this personвҖ”is a coward.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : And you know the relationship between this cowardly man and Salim.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : They have ties, a friendship.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : They spend time together. There are even statements saying they went to restaurants together. In your statement, you said that Nevzat is such a cowardly man that he would not question what Salim says.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : May I explain why?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Because Nevzat is the poorest man in the village, has nothing, and has no friends. Salim GГјran, on the other hand, is the village headman and can do whatever he wants. For example, he can go to the prosecutorвҖҷs office. If thereвҖҷs an objection to something, Nevzat might call him and say, вҖңCan you handle this issue for me?вҖқ So, I said that to mean he didnвҖҷt want to spoil his good relationship with Salim.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Let me ask you very plainly.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Go ahead, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : The counsel asked politely, but IвҖҷll ask directly, as a friend would. If Salim killedвҖ”pardon meвҖ”if Salim killed and told Nevzat, вҖңHide the body,вҖқ would Nevzat do it?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : He would not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs it. ThatвҖҷs the blunt version of the question, correct? All right. You just phrased it more elegantly; I asked it more directly.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Because these are statements made under pressure.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I understand, but I asked in a straightforward way he could clearly grasp. When you asked it in more polished words, it got complicated. Go ahead, please continue.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷll ask you one last question.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Our colleagues asked you as well. We have all your prison conversations in the fileвҖ”all of them, including the video calls and recordings.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : ThatвҖҷs correct. They were even leaked on social mediaвҖ”the recordings of my conversations with my family.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Yes, thatвҖҷs what IвҖҷm referring to. You spoke with your family, your father, your brotherвҖ”all of them.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Something caught my attention. Please answer as NarinвҖҷs brother.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Please. In all your conversations, especially with your brother BaranвҖ”вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You constantly show interest in the case file.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You constantly ask about the reports.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You ask about the carpets being sent to Van. You ask about your toothbrush.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You say, вҖңI kissed NarinвҖ”something that wasnвҖҷt in any of your statementsвҖ”before going to the mosque. Brother, could that cause any trouble?вҖқ Your brother even asks, вҖңDid you tell the lawyers that?вҖқ and you reply, вҖңNo, I didnвҖҷt.вҖқ Now, I want to ask you this: whenever you bring up such topics, you keep asking about the report.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When we, as criminal lawyers, see someone so curious about a report, we tend to think theyвҖҷre worried about the possibility of incriminating evidence emerging. IвҖҷm asking you openlyвҖ”were you afraid of anything coming out in those reports?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Absolutely not. Definitely not.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When you asked about the toothbrush, what were you thinking? For example, you said Narin brushed her teeth with your toothbrush that morning.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When you said, вҖңBrother, could that cause any trouble?вҖқ what exactly did you mean?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : That morning, before Narin used my toothbrush, I had already used it. Later, when she used my toothbrush, I was already in prison. For the first time in my life, I was in a prison cell.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : ThatвҖҷs what IвҖҷm asking.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, thatвҖҷs it. It was my first time in prison in my life. I had never even seen a police station before. And in thereвҖ”between those four wallsвҖ”some guards behave harshly, say bad things, and itвҖҷs hard to bear. When a person has no father or family behind them, they canвҖҷt speak up. As for why I was so curious about the reportsвҖ”itвҖҷs because I thought, вҖңWhen my sisterвҖҷs report comes out, the killers will be revealed, and it will prove we are innocent.вҖқ As for the carpets, we were treated so badly at the provincial gendarmerieвҖ”insults, cursesвҖ”and when my mother was tortured and later watched the video of it being played aloud in front of me, it broke me psychologically. ThatвҖҷs why I became suspicious when they questioned me. IвҖҷm telling the truth.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What kind of suspicion?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : They said things like, вҖңNevzat did this,вҖқ вҖңHe did that with Salim,вҖқ вҖңHe was involved.вҖқ So many accusations. But if the gendarmerie had treated us properly, humanely, we would have told them everything from the beginning. Because from the very first day, IвҖҷve been telling the truth to the soldiers and everyone. From the beginning, IвҖҷve wanted my sister to be found.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What was your concern, technically speaking, about the toothbrush?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I didnвҖҷt know what the report would sayвҖ”whether it would show traces or not. She had used my toothbrush after all, even though I was innocent.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So, youвҖҷre saying it was a question asked out of fear and panic, in short.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes. For example, right now IвҖҷm staying in solitary confinement, but my family doesnвҖҷt know that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Does the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association have any further questions? No.В Representative of Mr.В Arif GГјran, do you have any questions? No.В Ministry of Family and Social Services, any questions without repetition? Counsel, weвҖҷll address contradictions later. For now, weвҖҷll take only questions. Please, state your name.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Enes GГјran
section: Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ
text:
Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ

Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun вҖӢ

Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : On the day of the incident, your mother, YГјksel GГјran, turned off her phone at 14:28 and turned it back on at 15:38. You turned yours off at 14:30 and back on at 15:41. So, both phones were turned off around the same time and turned back on an hour later. What happened during that hour? Why did you turn it off?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I was lying down until around four oвҖҷclock. My phone was there.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : Until four. But you turned your phone on at 15:41. At 15:41. You turned it on.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : At 15:41, I must have received a callвҖ”most likely from Еһahin. He probably called me at that time.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : The main call records show that at that time, there were four people in the house. Narin arrived.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : ThatвҖҷs not true. There was no such thing. Either Salim GГјran or Nevzat BahtiyarвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, he already said he didnвҖҷt know anyone had come.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I mean, no one could have come into my house. Because I was there. No one could take my sister from my homeвҖ”whether itвҖҷs a dog or even my own uncle.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : DonвҖҷt use insults.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : Did Narin see something she wasnвҖҷt supposed to, Mr.В Enes?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : All right.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ItвҖҷs not an insult, Enes. Calm down. Would you like it if someone insulted you here? You complain about the gendarmerie insulting you. LetвҖҷs not do the same, all right? All right, apology accepted. ItвҖҷs not a contradiction, itвҖҷs a question.вҖӢ



Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz вҖӢ

Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Around 9 p.m. on the 24thвҖ”that is, three days after Narin disappeared.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : There was a fire.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : And there were also frequent power outages. Were such things common in your village before?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt really know about the power cuts. I had come back on the 20th. On the 21st, my sisterвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I mean, two or three months earlier, were there power cuts in the village?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, there were. The circuit breaker would trip, and weвҖҷd go and reset it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Sometimes there are always outages in DiyarbakДұr.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Could you describe the fire in more detail?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Let me explain the fire to you in detail, brother. Because I was there when it happened. The fireвҖ”well, there was Г–mer GГјran and a friend of his. We were sitting near the mosque. Г–mer GГјran was talking either on TikTok or with a sheikh, saying thereвҖҷs water flowing in front of a house door and a broken tree. So we said, thereвҖҷs HacДұ RДұza GГјranвҖҷs placeвҖ”it could be there, because thereвҖҷs a broken tree and water in front of the door. We went to check. We looked around everywhere, found nothing. And at the moment we were about to leave toward HacДұ RДұza GГјranвҖҷs garden and enter near Erhan GГјranвҖҷs house, the wires hit each other. I was standing under the wire outside the garden, not inside. Then suddenly, the fire started. When they tried to extinguish that part, it spread to my side. It happened four or five times in a row, my Lord. Which fire should I try to put out?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : No, no oneвҖҷs accusing you of that. The issue here, Enes, is the claim that the fire was suspiciousвҖ”that it was organized by Salim or others to destroy evidence, since it wasnвҖҷt a windy day and there was no natural reason for a fire. ThatвҖҷs why the attorney is asking.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Look, Enes, IвҖҷll speak frankly, with the Presiding JudgeвҖҷs permission.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Of course, go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Narin disappeared on the 21st.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : On the 23rd, there were conflicting statements about the exact time she went missing.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Okay.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : IвҖҷm listing this chronologically.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : On the 24th, there was the fire.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : On the 26th, you were sent for a forensic report.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Then on the 27th, there was a fight among the women.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Correct.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : There were many women, as the Presiding Judge already asked.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : All these are suspicious events, arenвҖҷt they? DonвҖҷt they seem abnormal compared to ordinary life?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I donвҖҷt really know.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : ThatвҖҷs why these questions are being asked.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Let me tell you about the fire again, my lawyer brother. The wires really did hit each otherвҖ”no one struck them. Because I was standing right beneath the wire.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you didnвҖҷt see anyone hit it?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : No, all the wires hit each other. It happened on its own.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Enes GГјran
section: Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir : A truly incomprehensible investigation has been carried outвҖ”IвҖҷm struggling to find the right words. The prison conversations my client had with his family have been included in the file. These recordings even appear in the indictment, and all of them were deliberately included. They were intentionally leaked to social mediaвҖ”this case is entirely built on perception, and that perception is still being fueled.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Who released those recordings of the prison conversations?вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : We donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThereвҖҷs an allegation that you released them yourselves, to shape public perception.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Yes, yes, as if to create the impression that вҖңLook, the family is laughing.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I donвҖҷt know. When I was asked, as the court, I said all videos are accessible to the parties, including the defense attorneys.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Their inclusion in the file is already debatableвҖ”thatвҖҷs a separate matter. We didnвҖҷt object to it then, because everyone already knew everything. We didnвҖҷt want it to seem like we were hiding something. We have no such concern. We said, вҖңLet it be discussed.вҖқ But we also want to state here that since these recordings constitute illegally obtained evidence, they should be removed from the file. Why? Because the conversations were cut and edited. If the full conversations were listened toвҖ”there are two callsвҖ”one with his brother Eren, and one with his six-year-old brother. In that call, he says, вҖңDonвҖҷt talk to anyone.вҖқ But if you listen to the beginning, itвҖҷs about normal thingsвҖ”вҖңHow are you, brother? IвҖҷm fine,вҖқ he says. вҖңIвҖҷm working here, donвҖҷt worry.вҖқ LetвҖҷs not forgetвҖ”his six-year-old brother lost his sister. The whole family was devastated. His mother was detained, the child lost his sister, one brother is gone, and the father is completely exhausted. So trying to protect the child from othersвҖҷ words is a human reaction. Taking only that sentenceвҖ”вҖңDonвҖҷt talk to anyoneвҖқвҖ”out of context to twist it is unfair. The indictment itself is forced. Soon weвҖҷll show many examples. For instance, the question about the phone and WhatsAppвҖ”youвҖҷll see what it really means. Everyone will understand. The precedent cited here is from the 18^th Criminal Chamber of the Court of Cassation, 2016. In that case, a convicted person insulted a deputy director during a prison call. The trial court convicted him; the Court of Cassation overturned it. The Chief Public Prosecutor appealed under Article 308, and by majority voteвҖ”not unanimousвҖ”a ruling was made. It stated: вҖңIf a person is detained or convicted, they are under the stateвҖҷs supervision. Monitoring is permissible to prevent or detect crimes.вҖқ But thatвҖҷs to prevent a new crime, not to prosecute a person already in custody. Our situation is entirely different.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : WeвҖҷll review all of that.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Article 135 of the Code of Criminal Procedure clearly regulates this. These cannot be used as evidence. My client has already explained his concerns openly. Everyone listened to selective parts of the conversationsвҖ”let them listen to all of them. If theyвҖҷre public everywhere, let everyone hear the full contextвҖ”how the child cried, how the family struggled to talk at first, and what happened later. WeвҖҷre stating clearly that this is illegally obtained evidence. We didnвҖҷt object at first, but as youвҖҷll see throughout the case, there are multiple such examples. This is one of them, and weвҖҷre formally objecting to this evidence.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Salim GГјran
text:
Reading Time
~ 1 h 29 min


Word Count
