YГјksel GГјran : Yes, my phone was at home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : EnesвҖҷs phone was also at home.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I donвҖҷt know if Enes took it or not. Maybe Muhammed took it. I wasnвҖҷt using the phone at all.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : At 15:20, Salim enters the house.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely not!вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Listen now.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : He absolutely did not come to our house!вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : YouвҖҷre saying Nevzat is the one who killed her вҖ” thatвҖҷs what you said, right? According to NevzatвҖҷs statement вҖ” and you said вҖңthatвҖҷs what Nevzat saidвҖқ вҖ” Nevzat claims he came to your house. The base station data also shows that Nevzat entered ArifвҖҷs house. But the same base data shows you were at home, Enes was at home, and Salim also came to the house.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I swear, neither Salim nor Nevzat came to our house. They may have walked around outside the house вҖ” we didnвҖҷt know. We were sleeping at home, I swear.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You werenвҖҷt sleeping at 16:00. You were awake at the same time they were supposedly walking around the house.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : What time was that?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Around 15:30 вҖ” half past three.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Three, fifteen. We were at home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay, you were at home, awake. If they were walking around the house, wouldnвҖҷt you have seen them?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Even if they came around the house, we were inside. It was hot. The air conditioner was on. The windows were closed. How would we see or hear anyone?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But there must be shade outside.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, we couldnвҖҷt see. The windowsвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : In a village, donвҖҷt houses have shaded areas visible from the window?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Whoever comes outside, how would we know? We were inside, sitting or lying down.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : What kind of village is this?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : There are no houses around ours.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Exactly. Since there are no nearby houses, youвҖҷd be even more curious if you heard a sound outside.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We were inside. Until Hediye came, we were lying down. We didnвҖҷt go out at all. I swear, we didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you didnвҖҷt hear any footsteps, voices, nothing outside?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Cars? Any car at all?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : IвҖҷve given statements a thousand times. If IвҖҷd heard or seen a car, IвҖҷd have opened the window to see who it was.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Let me ask another claim вҖ” did you have any kind of relationship with Salim?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.В I swear, I swear, I had no connection with Salim. I swear on the QurвҖҷan.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, Ms.В YГјksel. LetвҖҷs assume Nevzat is lying. IвҖҷm reading from the accusations вҖ” the entire indictment, the defenseвҖҷs claims, and those from the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association. IвҖҷm not accepting or rejecting any yet, okay? WeвҖҷll explain our reasoning in the verdict later. For now, let me ask вҖ” when you went into the barn, did you see your son in an inappropriate situation with someone?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you see him with another girl?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you see any sexual act with an animal?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, no, no. My children are intelligent boys. ThereвҖҷs no such thing.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you didnвҖҷt witness Enes doing anything like that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay. Now, about the relationship between SalimвҖҷs family and ArifвҖҷs family вҖ” I mean family relations, not sexual relations вҖ” was everything normal between your families?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We had no problems at all.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Was Salim GГјran the head of the family?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Salim is the head of his own household. Arif is the head of his. Only Salim owns some farmland. No one is the head over anyone elseвҖҷs home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Understood.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Is it possible that Nevzat is slandering Salim?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : They know. God knows.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : God knows.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : God knows.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : God knows everything. Is it possible that Nevzat is slandering you?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Of course. HeвҖҷs definitely slandering me.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs what I was going to ask. Why? Why would Nevzat slander two people at once? If he just said, вҖңI killed her, and Salim gave me the body,вҖқ heвҖҷd only implicate Salim. Why would he also drag you into it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Let me tell you something вҖ” heвҖҷs an idiot. Salim killed a chicken, and in two minutes, he becomes an accomplice.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Seven minutes.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : How can someone become an accomplice in five minutes?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : WeвҖҷll see.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I want to get out of here. I want Narin. I want the reason for Narin.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Hopefully.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : They didnвҖҷt let me. Your Honor, I wanted to dress Narin in a wedding gown, but they wrapped her in a shroud. They dressed my daughter in a shroud.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs why weвҖҷre here.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : They destroyed our dreams. They destroyed our home. They stained my honor. IвҖҷve been married for 22 years. Go ask anyone in that village. Check every phone. Anyone who saw me betray my husband вҖ” I couldnвҖҷt live with that. IвҖҷve been married 22 years. I never made a mistake. I have five children. May God reform them. Look, all of this is nonsense. How could I stain my own honor? My husband вҖ” for 22 years, not once did he slap me. Not once did he spit in my face. He treated me like a flower. I devoted myself to him completely. ThatвҖҷs the kind of woman I am. I get up at five in the morning for four or five kilos of okra. If I had my eye on anyone else, I wouldnвҖҷt get up at five for okra. I do it for my childrenвҖҷs allowance, for their future. To buy a dish for my house, a blanket for my children. Kill me if you must. But I have nothing to do with Salim. Hang me right here.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Then why is Nevzat lying like this?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : HeвҖҷs lying. Look at his face.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Look at me.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Is he even human?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Look at me, look at me.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Is he human? Kill me, but donвҖҷt stain my honor. DonвҖҷt stain the GГјran family.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He says thereвҖҷs no such thing.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely, there isnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright. Enes didnвҖҷt come home, and Salim didnвҖҷt either.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I swear, I swear on the QurвҖҷan, Salim and Nevzat never came to our house. This is my sin now. I have five children. Eren says, вҖңMom, why are you here? Why arenвҖҷt you coming home?вҖқ Poor kids вҖ” their psychology is completely ruined.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes. ItвҖҷs always the children who suffer. What time did you go to the mosque? You said you knocked on the mosque door around evening prayer and the villagers came.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Around what time was that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We were home, the call to prayer was being recited.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Evening prayer. The evening adhan is between 7:30 and 8:00 p.m. on August 21.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Then when we went to the mosque to look for Narin, and when we came back home, all the gendarmerie had already arrived. ThatвҖҷs when these statements started. Еһeyma Kaya said, вҖңI saw Narin around 4 or 4:30, picking grape leaves.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, you said that.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I said that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I understand. I was just asking about the time because you mentioned a different hour before. Any questions? Judge? Anything to ask? Alright, weвҖҷll continue later. Mr.В Prosecutor, do you have any questions? Please, bring the microphone closer. Go ahead.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of YГјksel GГјran
section: Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ
text:
Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ

Trial Prosecutor : Around 13:30, after Narin left the house, you said you went to sleep, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : When Narin left, we went to sleep.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright. Not important. About that time. When did you wake up?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt know what time it was.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Approximately?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I canвҖҷt remember the time at all. I was the first to get up and drink some water.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Two, three, four oвҖҷclock? You didnвҖҷt sleep until eight?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, no. When Hediye came, I donвҖҷt know what time it was. I swear, I didnвҖҷt know the time.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Okay. Was Muhammed also asleep?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : When Hediye came, Muhammed had already woken up.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Who opened the door for Hediye? She came twice.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : You can ask Hediye about that.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Who told you Hediye had arrived the first time?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Muhammed and Eren.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Muhammed, yes.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I think Muhammed or Eren opened the door for her.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Eren opened the door, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : He opened it the first time. The second time, the door was open, and Hediye came inside.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : In your first statement to the prosecutor, you said Muhammed opened the door.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I didnвҖҷt know. You told me that. I didnвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You said Muhammed opened the door.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I said that. IвҖҷm telling the truth. I said both of them got up and said, вҖңMom, Aunt Hediye brought my dress.вҖқ I said it was Muhammed, but later I realized Eren had opened it.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Because I was lying down.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You said Muhammed also slept at noon. Yes? You both slept, right? Enes said earlier in his statement that he woke up at 16:00. He said, вҖңI woke up at 16:00 and went outside.вҖқвҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : He said Muhammed was still asleep.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Enes was mistaken.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Pardon?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Enes spoke incorrectly. Because I was there.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Enes canвҖҷt be mistaken his whole life. He said the same thing to the prosecutorвҖҷs office.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The children donвҖҷt always remember things.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Wait a moment. Look, you said you went to bed at 1:30. LetвҖҷs go step by step.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : ItвҖҷs now 4:00. Enes is going out. Hediye came вҖ” your sister-in-law Hediye. You woke up. Hediye was in the house.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Muhammed was asleep. Muhammed was still sleeping.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, Muhammed wasnвҖҷt sleeping.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : He wasnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Eren is wrong. Enes is wrong.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So Enes is lying?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : ItвҖҷs not a lie. ItвҖҷs wrong.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You would know whether someone was asleep, YГјksel.вҖӢ


Defense Counsel for Defendant YГјksel : You didnвҖҷt let me ask this question earlier.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes. He said it wrong. Maybe the child remembered it incorrectly.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Good thing I didnвҖҷt. I did well not to.вҖӢ


Defense Counsel for Defendant YГјksel : But you let them ask now.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, and I said it was good that I did вҖ” for both of them. What else can I say?вҖӢ


Defense Counsel for Defendant YГјksel : YouвҖҷre contradicting yourself.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs fine. I said it was good for both sides. What can I say?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Let me also say this. Maybe Enes doesnвҖҷt remember everything. HeвҖҷs a child.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright then. Enes doesnвҖҷt remember everything вҖ” heвҖҷs a child. WeвҖҷll leave that aside. You said you stepped outside the door. Enes went out around 4:00. The exact time isnвҖҷt important. You were seeing Hediye off, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You said you went to the door. Where was Enes at that moment? Was he coming from the other side?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We stepped outside, like from the balcony. Enes came from outside.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Did Enes and Hediye see each other?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Hediye was there. Enes says he didnвҖҷt see her вҖ” I was surprised too.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Okay, Hediye says in her statement that you told her something.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, maybe he didnвҖҷt see her and then remembered later.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You said, вҖңSon, why did you come back so early? There werenвҖҷt many people outside.вҖқвҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Did you say that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I think so, yes, I told him that, son.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Was Hediye with you when that conversation happened?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, she was.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright, so thatвҖҷs the second contradiction for Enes. Enes again. Okay.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, Enes is mistaken. I was there, I saw it, heвҖҷs mistaken.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Okay. Enes called his brother Osman about the slippers. Was he near you when he made that call?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I was in the kitchen, but I could hear their voices.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You heard them. He asked for slippers, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I even told him, вҖңSon, if you go all the way to DГ¶rtyol and back, youвҖҷll be late.вҖқвҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So you knew he asked for slippers?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I was in the kitchen, I heard him say his brother came.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Was there really such a shortage of slippers? Why is that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The child came home, his slippers were torn, so he asked his brother for a pair.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Still, you had a boy who worked as a waiter, coming home from town by minibus вҖ” and youвҖҷd have him go buy slippers from the town?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We just say what we need. Whatever weвҖҷre missing, we tell them.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Now, there was a course that ended around 4:00, right? Your daughterвҖҷs class lasted until 4:00. The time is 19:11 вҖ” according to our calculations, thatвҖҷs evening prayer in that area. About three hours have passed, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So the moment you realized, you called out to MaЕҹallah and started searching. But a girl who left class at 4:00 вҖ” three hours have passed вҖ” did she fall somewhere, get hurt, get hungry? DidnвҖҷt you think about her at all for those three hours?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She told me she was going to Fatma HaticeвҖҷs.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : But thereвҖҷs no Fatma Hatice. You mean MaЕҹallahвҖҷs daughters, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You could have at least called out, вҖңMaЕҹallah, is Narin there?вҖқ Three hours had passed.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She told me, вҖңMom, let me go,вҖқ so I let her.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Okay, so it didnвҖҷt cross your mind?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She was going to stay the night.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright. So you were calm for those three hours.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I had left her at that house. If I had gone to the hospital or somewhere else, Narin would have been there until evening.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Okay. Does your door have a bell?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, we donвҖҷt have a bell.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So how do people enter the house? Is there a wire or something?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, they pull a wire.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So your children and Narin enter by pulling the wire? They donвҖҷt have to knock?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : When Narin was inside, I told the lawyers too вҖ” she could push it open from the inside, but when she was outside, she couldnвҖҷt open it.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright, I understand. So that day, how did the children and Narin usually enter the house вҖ” by pushing?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, they knocked. If someone was inside, they opened it. I told the lawyers, but it was hard to open from the outside. It was stiff, so they struggled.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So itвҖҷs a pull-type latch? You pull it with a string? She wasnвҖҷt strong enough?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She wasnвҖҷt strong enough. When she was inside, she pushed it open.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I see.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She could open it, but it was hard to do from outside.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : I understand. Alright. Thank you, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, Madam Judge. Right вҖ” you said the second time Hediye came, the door was open. Was the door open?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But you said the air conditioners were running in the house.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It wasnвҖҷt wide open. I think it was partly open. But then I saw Hediye arrive.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you didnвҖҷt open the door for Hediye?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Then maybe she pulled the latch and opened it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I think it was already open. You can ask Hediye. I just saw her come inside.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay. So youвҖҷre not sure. WeвҖҷll ask Hediye.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of YГјksel GГјran
section: DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ
text:
DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ

Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir вҖӢ

Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Ms.В YГјksel, do you know Gazal Bahtiyar?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I know her.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : On the evening of the incidentвҖ”вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : After 4:00 p.m., IвҖҷm asking about you.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I can recall that.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : When you realized that Narin was missingвҖ”вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : You told Muhammed. He went, said he couldnвҖҷt find her, and then you went out.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : You went to MaЕҹallahвҖҷs, HediyeвҖҷs, and several other houses to look, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I called them on the phone.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : While you were walking toward Nevzat BahtiyarвҖҷs house, did you run into Gazal?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.В Gazal came to us near the mosque.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Alright.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I was walking around NevzatвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Okay. When you encountered Gazal in the area near the mosque, did you have a conversation with her?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I was searching for Narin вҖ” shouting and crying while looking for her. She was behind me, trying to comfort me. She was saying, вҖңYГјksel, calm down, youвҖҷll find Narin. SheвҖҷs probably resting somewhere. Pull yourself together.вҖқ She was saying things like that to me.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Did you say anything like, вҖңWhich one of you took my daughterвҖқ? Maybe to Gazal, maybe to someone else вҖ” do you remember saying that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, I donвҖҷt remember ever saying that. Gazal didnвҖҷt say Narin was gone either. When Narin disappeared, we were looking for her. We thought she might have been taken or something happened to her. Narin wasnвҖҷt around. We were searching for her. I donвҖҷt remember saying, вҖңWhoever took my daughter, bring her back.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Did you say anything like, вҖңAt least let my daughter have a graveвҖқ?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely not.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Any shouting, any such words, anything at all?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely not, I didnвҖҷt say that.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : You didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I swear.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : On the day of the incident, around 1 p.m., Narin told you she wanted to go to the QurвҖҷan course, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : And at first, you objected, but later you gave her permission. When you gave permission, you knew her class would end around 4:00 and she would return home, correct?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, she said, вҖңIвҖҷm going to Fatma HaticeвҖҷs. Mom, IвҖҷll stay there until evening.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Until what time in the evening?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Normally the kids stay out until 6 or 7. We have to drag them home from the street. The village kids play there, and we bring them back. I knew she wouldnвҖҷt be home until dinner.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : After Narin left the house вҖ” around 1:30 in the afternoon.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Until around 4:00 or even 4:30 in the evening вҖ” were you at home the entire time, or did you go out?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We didnвҖҷt go anywhere.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : You didnвҖҷt go anywhere at all.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, I swear. The children maybe, yes вҖ” Enes went out, Muhammed went out. But I didnвҖҷt go anywhere.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Did you see Salim or anyone else around the house?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : You didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Are you recording? The lady with glasses вҖ” are you an attorney? You? When phones are lifted up like that, it draws attention. YouвҖҷre not recording, right? YouвҖҷre an attorney? Yes? You? YouвҖҷre not recording, right? Friends, please, IвҖҷm asking вҖ” no video recording. Look, video recordings put us in a difficult position. I understand, making videos here, posting them is tempting. I donвҖҷt use Twitter, but it would probably get retweets. ItвҖҷs nice for you, but itвҖҷs disrespectful to the court, and under the Turkish Penal Code, itвҖҷs a crime. Understand? So please, letвҖҷs avoid any accusations of that kind, alright? Because when you hold the phone like that, I perceive it as recording. YouвҖҷre right to want to record, and IвҖҷll be careful too, but IвҖҷm asking you to be careful. Go ahead, counsel.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : During the day or evening вҖ” could be before or after Narin disappeared вҖ” did you ever see Salim?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I swear I didnвҖҷt see him. Not at all. I only said that maybe when I lowered the switch, someone saw him at HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs door. Maybe for a second. I swear I didnвҖҷt see him myself.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : During the day, when you were home, was your phone always with you?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The phone was with the children.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : When you left the house later that evening, did you take the phone with you?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Me?вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Yes.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, never. When the children were home, I didnвҖҷt use the phone. Because when they were home and playing with it, wherever I went I would tell them, вҖңSon, look, IвҖҷm here,вҖқ and they would call me. I absolutely didnвҖҷt take the phone with me.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Can you tell me the names of SalimвҖҷs children?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Of course. Dilek, Gamze, Д°layda, Devran, GГјlsГјm, Miran.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : On the day Narin went missing, did you see any of those children?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, I didnвҖҷt see them at all.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : Not in front of the house, in the barn, the yard, the garden, the street, or in front of SalimвҖҷs house?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, in the late afternoon, around five, his wife, his kids, his sister вҖ” they were all at the door. The children were all outside after five. But I swear, I didnвҖҷt see any of them. I didnвҖҷt even go around the house.вҖӢ


Law. Mehdi Г–zdemir : After the incident, did you wash the carpets or floors inside the house?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely not. In the morning, I sent EnesвҖҷs clothes to HediyeвҖҷs house.вҖӢ



Law. Nahit Eren вҖӢ

Law. Nahit Eren : I have a question for a mother. IвҖҷll preface it with a few things.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, as long as you donвҖҷt make a comment.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : I wonвҖҷt comment. My statement will include some questions, but before that вҖ” ever since the Narin case began вҖ” I want to say this because IвҖҷll be asking a mother a question. Before I question a mother, I must acknowledge the weight of the DiyarbakДұr Bar AssociationвҖҷs institutional identity. I wonвҖҷt interrogate you. IвҖҷll only ask a few questions. Technically, itвҖҷs an interrogation, but I wonвҖҷt treat it like one. And IвҖҷll tell you why. You see this crowd here today, donвҖҷt you? So many people have come. Have you ever seen a trial or a court hearing before?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Never in my life. IвҖҷve never even seen a police station, let alone something like this.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Of course she hasnвҖҷt вҖ” how could she?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : We have, Your Honor, thatвҖҷs why IвҖҷm explaining. Yes, you have lawyers too, and we all respect the right to defense. The right to defense is sacred. Apart from your lawyers, nearly fifty bar presidents from all over Turkey are here, though not all their names were recorded in the minutes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I wrote it down later, during the break.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Around fifty bar presidents are here. Representatives from all organizations in Turkey working on womenвҖҷs and childrenвҖҷs rights are here. Members of Parliament are here.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Thank you.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : WeвҖҷre all here. And let me say something very special вҖ” truly special. You know why weвҖҷre all here? WeвҖҷre all here for Narin.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : WeвҖҷre all here for Narin.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : None of us are here to use this case for personal gain, as some have claimed. Everyone here is a lawyer who has already proven their integrity. Anyone who tries to benefit from NarinвҖҷs case вҖ” in any way, in this courtroom вҖ” in my view, is in the same category as the one who killed Narin.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : God will not let NarinвҖҷs right go unanswered.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : YouвҖҷll see where IвҖҷm going with this.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : He wonвҖҷt let it go.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : We all wanted to take part in this case. Everyone did. To do something for Narin. To stand beside the prosecutor and uncover the truth вҖ” to ensure those who killed her are punished. You вҖ” your daughter died. Your second daughter was also killed. Killed. Killed. Both of your daughters were killed.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : They killed me too.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : May God protect your children for you.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : They killed me too.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm speaking to your conscience. ThatвҖҷs why IвҖҷm saying all this. IвҖҷll appeal to your conscience. Do you see this file?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I see it.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Do you see these papers? The writing on them?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I see them.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Do you know who prepared this file? IвҖҷve been involved since NarinвҖҷs autopsy. I have three daughters. These labels вҖ” my daughters made them at home for Narin, to contribute to her case. All this handwriting вҖ” itвҖҷs theirs. So that NarinвҖҷs killer would be found. So we could do our part. In Turkey, no child has ever been embraced like this before. Maybe that was our mistake вҖ” that we didnвҖҷt show this care earlier, and thatвҖҷs why we lost Narin. If we had, maybe sheвҖҷd still be alive today.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Counsel, letвҖҷs move on to the question now.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The question is tied to what IвҖҷm saying. IвҖҷm speaking to her feelings and her conscience. The solution to this case lies in a motherвҖҷs conscience. IвҖҷll ask her as a mother вҖ” as the defendant.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : We already discussed last session how sensitive the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association is to issues like torture.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Your Honor, everyone knows the DiyarbakДұr Bar well. ThatвҖҷs not a problem. IвҖҷll ask her this. I said I wouldnвҖҷt interrogate her, right? On August 25th, you were called in as a complainant. Do you know what a complainant is? It means you should be standing where I am right now вҖ” you should be here as the mother.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I know.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : ThatвҖҷs what вҖңcomplainantвҖқ means. You were called to the station on the 25th and gave your statement. They asked you what happened to your daughter вҖ” to help find her. I once said something on television, just once, about this case. I said, вҖңIn murder cases, the family helps us. The mother tells us something. The father, the brother, the uncle, the villagers вҖ” they all tell us something. ThatвҖҷs how we solve it.вҖқ Now they came to you and asked, вҖңThereвҖҷs a scarf,вҖқ and you said it was on the stairs.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : WhatвҖҷs that?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Let me ask, Ms.В YГјksel. You said to the imam, вҖңThe day after Narin died, I found a purple scarf on the stairs.вҖқ The imam asked, вҖңDid Narin wear this scarf when she came to the QurвҖҷan course at the mosque?вҖқвҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Let me explain.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm reminding you of the imam so you remember what he told you.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : He said yes, she had a scarf like that on her head.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.В That scarfвҖ”вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : No, the imamвҖҷs statement вҖ” the one based on what you said. I can remind you. Where is that scarf now?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : He came to me on the second day.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Where is that scarf now?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : That wasnвҖҷt the scarf.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What do you mean?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The imam said it looked like it, but he wasnвҖҷt sure.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Let me read it then.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No вҖ” if he had said, вҖңThatвҖҷs the one,вҖқ I would have handed it to the gendarmerie. It was at home.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So youвҖҷre saying he said it looked similar. Let me ask вҖ” where is that scarf you showed the imam?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It was at home.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Where is it now? Still at home, I assume?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Is it still there?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Where did you find it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : You know. But that scarf was there at noon, he said. Because I washed that balcony in the evening.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Of course вҖ” IвҖҷm asking where exactly you found it.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Let me tell it first. The imam came to us. There were many people on the balcony. He called me over and said, вҖңYГјksel, I was teaching at the mosque. I looked up and saw Narin and the children watching us through the window. I told them, вҖҳThis isnвҖҷt a cinema. If you want to learn, come inside and take lessons.вҖҷ Then I saw Narin come in. She was wearing a skirt вҖ” a purple skirt вҖ” and had a purple shawl. I even said, вҖҳMina, is she your sister?вҖҷ and she said, вҖҳNo, sheвҖҷs my cousin, Uncle ArifвҖҷs daughter.вҖҷ I said, вҖҳOh, I didnвҖҷt know she was ArifвҖҷs daughter. Her eyes look like OsmanвҖҷs,вҖҷвҖқ the imam told me. Then he said, вҖңWhen the class ended, she took off her skirt and her shawl. Her hair was uncovered. She removed the purple skirt.вҖқ I said, вҖңYes, teacher, she had a purple skirt, thatвҖҷs correct.вҖқ Muhammed said when she left the house, she had a lunch bag. It was pink. He told that to the gendarmerie. I told the teacher, вҖңI washed that balcony in the evening; there was nothing there.вҖқ That night I was crying. The women were with me there. I didnвҖҷt even own a purple shawl back then. If there was some confusion, I didnвҖҷt know. I told them that when I sat with the women that night, I saw that shawl among the skewers. I said, вҖҳThis is it, teacher.вҖҷвҖқвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : When you say вҖңskewers,вҖқ what do you mean?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The balcony. Outside. Yes, outside.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You saw it there. And it belonged to you, right? To your house?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It was mine.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The one you showed?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : He looked at it and said, вҖңIвҖҷm not sure, but it looks like it.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs what I said in my statement too. If he had said, вҖңYes, thatвҖҷs the shawl,вҖқ I wouldвҖҷve handed it to the gendarmerie immediately. It was on our balcony.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : And that shawl is still at home?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, itвҖҷs still at home. I havenвҖҷt been home for two months, but I believe itвҖҷs there. It should be at home.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : As I said, this isnвҖҷt an interrogation вҖ” IвҖҷm just continuing from the statements. Hediye came to your house.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : She came around three, turned the valve off and on, and went back to her house.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : She dropped off the clothes at your place, went home, saw something had overflowed, and came back at 15:45, she says. When she came back, what was Muhammed doing?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Muhammed вҖ” I think Muhammed was home.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Was he sleeping?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, he couldnвҖҷt have been asleep.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You were all in the same room вҖ” Muhammed, Hediye, everyone. Did Muhammed see Hediye, in your opinion?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I think Muhammed was there when Hediye came, but at that moment, I wasnвҖҷt home. As far as I know, though, he was home.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Muhammed was home. He didnвҖҷt say he wasnвҖҷt home. So did he see her?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : When Hediye came, I told her, вҖңThe kids said, Aunt Hediye came, dropped off the clothes, and left.вҖқ I said, вҖңSon, why didnвҖҷt you wake me up?вҖқ He said, вҖңMom, she didnвҖҷt leave yet.вҖқ I was just saying that when, a minute or two later, I saw Hediye there. Hediye came to us. So when she came, I said the same thing to her. And Muhammed was there.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : YouвҖҷve said all this before. I just want to confirm вҖ” Muhammed was home and saw Hediye with you?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I swear, I think Muhammed was home. He saw her. Yes, IвҖҷm saying he was home. I canвҖҷt remember clearly. Maybe he told me later вҖ” I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You say вҖңI think.вҖқ So youвҖҷre not sure, right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did Muhammed leave the house with Enes?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, Enes was still sleeping.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : But later they left вҖ” Enes got up. Enes woke up.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Enes is mistaken.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Enes says, вҖңI woke up, saw Hediye, asked how she was, then left the house.вҖқ When he left, did Muhammed go out after him?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Muhammed had gone out earlier.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Muhammed went out earlier.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I said here вҖ” Enes was mistaken. When Enes woke up, Muhammed wasnвҖҷt there. He wasnвҖҷt asleep. Enes woke up groggy. IвҖҷm telling you, Enes was wrong. When Enes woke up, Muhammed wasnвҖҷt sleeping.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did Muhammed and Enes return home together?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : In the evening?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Yes.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Enes came once more. When he went up the hill and came back down, he returned home. Up to the hill вҖ” with friends.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : ThatвҖҷs not what IвҖҷm asking.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You heard him too. Enes said, вҖңThe first time I went out, I went to Tahir Market to buy a drink. Then, instead of getting into the car, I walked home to avoid helping SГјheyla.вҖқ So, was Muhammed with him when he came back?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, Muhammed wasnвҖҷt with him.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : He wasnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I canвҖҷt recall clearly. By then he had started working. Muhammed had come but hadnвҖҷt gone out. YouвҖҷre still talking with Hediye вҖ” Hediye was still home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Ms.В YГјksel, Ms.В YГјksel вҖ” microphone, please.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Hediye was still home.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : When Enes came, Muhammed вҖ” when Enes came home, Muhammed or Hediye had just left. The second time.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : But they saw each other.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : When Enes came inside, Hediye was still at the door.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm appealing to your conscience one last time. That day, did Hediye come to your house at all?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I swear she came twice.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : No need to swear.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She came once вҖ” I was asleep. Then the second time.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So Hediye did come to your house that day.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, she stayed with us for about two hours in total.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Before three oвҖҷclock, did Hediye ever tell you where she had been? You know, you chatted вҖ” you sent the clothes around 10 a.m. to be washed.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, she didnвҖҷt mention that.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Not during your conversation either?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, she didnвҖҷt say anything about that.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Let me ask something. You know Abdurrahman вҖ” HediyeвҖҷs son.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : HeвҖҷs an adult, grown up.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : He said when he woke up at 1 p.m., Hediye wasnвҖҷt home. The last time he saw her was around 5 p.m.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt know about that.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So Hediye never talked to you about where she had been until then?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She said, вҖңYГјksel, I brought your clothes. You were sleeping. I went to run the water pump. After I filled the tank, I turned it off. I thought, YГјksel must be awake now, IвҖҷll go to her.вҖқ She didnвҖҷt mention anything else.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Alright, one last thing thatвҖҷs been confusing me вҖ” and the court panel too. Can you explain the matter about the blood? Not the blood from the ambulance вҖ” I mean the turkey blood.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I didnвҖҷt know anything about that.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The turkey, the turkey.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, the turkey. I said before вҖ” I woke up at 5 a.m.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did that happen the day before?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It was that same night.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : But in some places, you said it was the day before. IвҖҷm asking about the blood found on the stairs. Because let me explain вҖ” Your Honor, this is about evidence вҖ” a blood sample was found on the stairs of your annex.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : You mean that one вҖ” that was Tahir KayaвҖҷs blood. I didnвҖҷt know about it at all.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : There was human blood found. But because so much time had passed, the DNA sample was too small to identify who it belonged to. Still, it was confirmed to be human blood, taken from the stairs near your annex. ThatвҖҷs why IвҖҷm asking. Can you explain the issue with the turkey and the dog вҖ” why did you mention them in your statements? Why are we talking about turkey blood? IвҖҷm trying to understand.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It wasnвҖҷt inside. In the morning вҖ” the turkey blood wasnвҖҷt inside. I was picking okra at 5 a.m. I woke up, checked how many rows were left, and saw a dead, torn-up turkey in front of me. I said to myself, вҖңIt must have been the dog,вҖқ because our turkeys stay outside at night. I said, вҖңThe dog must have eaten the turkey during the night.вҖқ When we came back in the evening, we had put the turkey in the barn. As for the blood in the house, I later heard it was Tahir KayaвҖҷs вҖ” he had a nosebleed outside and got blood on himself. When he saw the blood, he went inside and asked for a tissue. ThatвҖҷs what I heard from people. It was that day, maybe the second or third day вҖ” I didnвҖҷt remember at the time. Then I heard about it later. There were many kids outside that day вҖ” they said his nose was bleeding.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : IвҖҷm not talking about the blood on the rocks. YouвҖҷre talking about that. Was the blood on the stones next to the barn TahirвҖҷs blood?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, it was his blood.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Who said that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The children were saying it. I heard it later.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The children said that. There was also a statement by the governor about that blood. Who said it belonged to Tahir?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : There were many kids there. I heard it later.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Let me tell you something вҖ” that blood, the human blood, couldnвҖҷt be matched to anyone because of how much time had passed. But it was human. IвҖҷm asking because the blood seen on the fourth day wasnвҖҷt fresh. So the blood that was said to be TahirвҖҷs вҖ” that wasnвҖҷt the same one. A blood sample was found on the stairs.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I heard about that later too. Yes, I heard later they said it was TahirвҖҷs blood.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Do you think the one on the stairs was also TahirвҖҷs?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Look, IвҖҷll tell you вҖ” later, some gendarmerie came. They said they were taking blood samples. I asked, вҖңWhat happened? What blood are they talking about?вҖқ The children said, вҖңTahir KayaвҖҷs nose bled, and he got blood on the stones. Then he touched it with his hand, went inside, and asked for a tissue.вҖқ I swear I had no idea about that blood. I didnвҖҷt even know there was blood there. I only heard about it later.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Alright.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Any other questions, Mr.В Nahit?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Thank you, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you. Any further questions from the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association? If not, weвҖҷll close after that.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Do you have internet on your phone?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : If thereвҖҷs a line, yes, but the kids usually donвҖҷt let me use it.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So your phone does have an internet line?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I think so вҖ” yes, it does.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So you have WhatsApp.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I have WhatsApp.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Right, the photo of the phone and the slippers came. There was some inconsistency there, but yes, yes, I saw it. ItвҖҷs confirmed. I saw it myself. But the slipper photo вҖ” we didnвҖҷt dwell on it much. We didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : One last question. Among your sisters, which one loved Narin the most?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes. Everyone loved her.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Who loved her the most, for example? Among your sisters?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : My sisters вҖ” truly, everyone loved Narin. Because she was my only daughter, my whole family loved her.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Can we say Yasemin loved her the most?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yasemin loved her too. Everyone was very protective of Narin. Because I had only one daughter вҖ” I had lost my first one, who was disabled вҖ” so everyone loved this one even more.вҖӢ



Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk вҖӢ

Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : This was asked earlier, but IвҖҷd like to clarify. Was the purple headscarf yours?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : That purple headscarf wasnвҖҷt mine.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Not yours? The imam said Narin came wearing a purple scarf. Where did you see that scarf? On the stairs? At the door?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : You know the balcony railing? I didnвҖҷt see it at first. I said, вҖңTeacher, I saw this purple shawl here that night. This is it, or maybe it isnвҖҷt.вҖқ He looked and said, вҖңIвҖҷm not entirely sure.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Alright.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : ThatвҖҷs what he told me. If he had said, вҖңYes, YГјksel, this is the purple shawl,вҖқ I wouldвҖҷve immediately given it to the gendarmerie. The imam said, вҖңIt looks like that shawl.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : The imam told you it looked like the scarf Narin wore on her head?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It looked similar, yes.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : And that scarf was yours.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, it was mine.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : You have no other daughter who would wear a scarf, and you didnвҖҷt place it outside yourself. So didnвҖҷt you wonder how that scarf ended up outside?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Look, I had washed the balcony вҖ” there was nothing there. When I washed it, it was clean. That night, when everything happened, someone must have laid something out there вҖ” maybe a cushion or pillow for sitting. People were coming and sitting around. We were out searching for Narin. Later that night, when I sat down with the women, I noticed that purple scarf. At the time, I didnвҖҷt think вҖ” whether Narin had taken it or someone else had. The imam said she had a purple scarf on her head. I immediately went and brought the purple scarf and said, вҖңTeacher, this is it.вҖқ He looked and said, вҖңIвҖҷm not completely sure.вҖқ If he had said, вҖңYes, YГјksel, this is the one,вҖқ I wouldвҖҷve given it to the gendarmerie immediately.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Alright, Ms.В YГјksel. ItвҖҷs normal that the imam couldnвҖҷt identify it for sure. But this scarf belonged to you, it was inside your home, and yet somehow it ended up outside without you touching it. DidnвҖҷt it occur to you that Narin might have taken it off, and why didnвҖҷt you inform the gendarmerie? After all, only two women in the house could have worn it вҖ” you or Narin.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I did say it. IвҖҷve given thousands of statements. I said if the imam had confirmed it was purple, I wouldвҖҷve told them someone must have brought this shawl there that night.вҖӢ


Law. Asya Cemre IЕҹДұk : Let me ask differently. The imam said it resembled NarinвҖҷs, and you didnвҖҷt put it outside. DidnвҖҷt you think Narin might have taken it off? Why didnвҖҷt you report it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The imam said she had a purple skirt and a purple shawl. I said, вҖңTeacher, if there was a purple shawl, it wasnвҖҷt this one. I saw this one here at night.вҖқ He said, вҖңIвҖҷm not sure.вҖқ If he had said, вҖңYes, this was it,вҖқ I wouldвҖҷve handed it to the gendarmerie immediately. Later we searched the house over and over вҖ” IвҖҷve given thousands of statements. We never saw that lunch bag or the purple skirt again. IвҖҷve said this many times. Yes, no further questions.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Counsel for Arif GГјran, do you have any questions? No.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of YГјksel GГјran
section: Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ
text:
Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ

Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun вҖӢ

Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : You have a statement dated August 25. In it, you said that around the time of the evening call to prayer, you asked Muhammed, вҖңWhereвҖҷs Narin?вҖқ and he said, вҖңMom, NarinвҖҷs not here.вҖқ Then you called MaЕҹallah GГјran over, and she said, вҖңAround 5 oвҖҷclock, Narin was at our house.вҖқвҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The statements laterвҖ”вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : Let me finish. Then you went to the mosque, and Д°sa Kaya came to you and said, вҖңSister, I saw Narin around 6 oвҖҷclock in front of the mosque.вҖқ But by that time, Narin had already been killed and left by the riverbank. As a mother, didnвҖҷt you question these people? Why did they say such things to you?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : That evening Д°brahim GГјran and the gendarmerie didnвҖҷt let me go anywhere. Regarding those statements вҖ” he said, вҖңAunt YГјksel, Еһeyma said she saw her around 4:00вҖ“4:30.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : 4:30, but MaЕҹallah said she was at your house at 5.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : That 5 was MaЕҹallahвҖҷs time. Еһeyma вҖ” Еһeyma Kaya. And Д°sa Kaya told me himself later.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : He said he saw her at 6.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Then MaЕҹallah GГјran said, вҖңI was ironing between 5:00 and 5:30. I was on the phone when someone knocked. I asked, вҖҳWho is it?вҖҷ She said Narin asked the girls something. I told her she must have gone to her grandmotherвҖҷs.вҖқ Then Commander HГјlya told me at the station, вҖңYГјksel, Maide Kaya appears on camera looking up toward the hill.вҖқ I asked Maide, вҖңWho were you talking to?вҖқ She said, вҖңI was talking to Narin. I told her, вҖҳNarin, come down, letвҖҷs play.вҖҷ Narin said, вҖҳMaide, IвҖҷm tired, IвҖҷm sleepy, IвҖҷm hungry. IвҖҷm going home.вҖҷвҖқ Where is Narin? Narin came to you at 7. Enes was here. Where is Narin? They were pressing me. Then Commander HГјlya came to our house, called me out, went west toward the slope вҖ” my sister-in-lawвҖҷs son was there. She told him, вҖңGo up to the house.вҖқ She said, вҖңLook, YГјksel вҖ” it doesnвҖҷt add up. Narin was here at 7, Enes was here. Where is Narin?вҖқ They kept accusing me. I said, вҖңCommander, at 7 there were so many people by HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs house вҖ” SГјheyla was filling hay by the school, Enes GГјran and his friends were there, many women, men, children behind him, the headmanвҖҷs family was all outside. Where could Narin have gone at 7?вҖқ They kept interrogating me. The children werenвҖҷt lying.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : So in summary, MaЕҹallah GГјran said she saw her at 5, and Д°sa Kaya said 6?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : MaЕҹallah told the gendarmerie. Д°brahim GГјran told me. The gendarmerie told me too.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : You said, вҖңI called her over. She came to our house around 5.вҖқвҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : MaЕҹallah later came and told us. She had already given her statement first, then came to us.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : But by that time Narin was already dead. DidnвҖҷt you question them about that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I did. At first the gendarmerie blamed us. вҖңIt happened at 7, it happened at 7. WhereвҖҷs Narin?вҖқ Then they said it happened in the barn.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : So you didnвҖҷt ask these people why they lied?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I didnвҖҷt go anywhere. I wasnвҖҷt in any condition. I told MaЕҹallah, вҖңI have proof. I was on the phone вҖ” the record is there.вҖқ I didnвҖҷt see the others at all. Then they said, вҖңIt happened in the barn, in the barn.вҖқ They kept blaming us. When new things came out, they said, вҖңIt happened inside the house, inside the house.вҖқ Wherever something happens, they throw it on us вҖ” me, my child. I swear we never saw Narin. I swear I wouldnвҖҷt trade even her fingernail for the world.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : Thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you. Any questions?вҖӢ



Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz вҖӢ

Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : After Narin disappeared, when did you inform your husband? When did you tell Arif GГјran?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I didnвҖҷt. Because I knew he was on the road, coming home. If I had said, вҖңNarinвҖҷs missing,вҖқ he would have lost his mind. I didnвҖҷt call him. Everyone was comforting me вҖ” вҖңMaybe she fell somewhere, maybe sheвҖҷs nearby.вҖқ I didnвҖҷt call him. Later he called me. I donвҖҷt know who told him, but I heard it was Cevat Kaya who told Arif, вҖңYouвҖҷve found Narin.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs how I heard it.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : And both Arif GГјran and Salim GГјran said they had no conflict or enmity with Nevzat. Did you ever notice any hostility or tension with Nevzat?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Never. Not at all вҖ” none.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Then why would he kill your daughter? YouвҖҷre also saying he did it. Enes, your son, says the same thing.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : IвҖҷm not saying that. IвҖҷm not accusing anyone, Counselor.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : But why would he kill her, Ms.В YГјksel? Why would Nevzat do such a thing? The uncle says their relations were good, they were family friends. The father says there was no hostility вҖ” thatвҖҷs their own statement, not mine. Why would something like this happen?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : A few days before the incident, my daughter came home holding twenty million lira. She said, вҖңMom, look, I have money.вҖқ I said, вҖңWhere did you get that money, my girl?вҖқ She said, вҖңBrother Nevzat gave it to me.вҖқ Later, I mentioned this to the judge, but not to the prosecutor. When Nevzat was arrested and put into custody, thatвҖҷs when it came to my mind. I said, вҖңMy daughter, shame on him вҖ” why would he give you money?вҖқ He had also given money to SГјheyla. Then I didnвҖҷt think about it again. Now he comes out and says, вҖңShe was here, she was there.вҖқ I said before too вҖ” they claimed there was DNA in SalimвҖҷs car. They said he gave her something by the roadside. As a mother, my heart and mind were overwhelmed вҖ” I thought, вҖңOh God, this man has lost his mind and killed my daughter.вҖқ Later his statements kept changing вҖ” then he said it happened inside the house. I said, вҖңHeвҖҷs lying.вҖқ If he had said it happened on the road, the DNA wouldвҖҷve shown up in his car. He wouldвҖҷve said, вҖңI threatened her, gave her something on the road.вҖқ Maybe, for a moment, my motherвҖҷs heart believed it вҖ” maybe he did it. But then I swore, that dishonorable man never set foot in our home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, alright.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : My last question, Your Honor. NarinвҖҷs aunt, Yasemin GГјl вҖ” when NarinвҖҷs body was recovered, there was an argument among the women in the family. She even turned to you during it.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I was in shock. The ambulance had arrived. Commander HГјlya said, вҖңMy condolences.вҖқ I already knew Narin was gone. I wasnвҖҷt myself вҖ” I fainted. I donвҖҷt remember anything. When I came to, I was inside the ambulance. I said, вҖңCommander, let me go вҖ” I want to see my children.вҖқ I could hear voices outside. I couldnвҖҷt see the women. Through the back doors of the ambulance, I saw the men and the gendarmerie. I said, вҖңLet me out.вҖқ The commander didnвҖҷt let me.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : She said, вҖңIf you had told the truth, this wouldnвҖҷt have happened,вҖқ right? The others said something like that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt know. I donвҖҷt know who was arguing.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : But you never talked to your sister about it afterward?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I really donвҖҷt know. Even the judge asked me. He said, вҖңYour sister MaЕҹallah knew about the fight.вҖқ I said, вҖңI swear I donвҖҷt know.вҖқ I was in detention for five days. I heard Melike and MaЕҹallah mentioning my sisterвҖҷs name, but I donвҖҷt know what happened in the argument.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : So you never talked about that fight afterward?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.В I didnвҖҷt ask, and they didnвҖҷt ask me.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Ms.В YГјksel, you said you saw the money.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you tell your husband or children вҖ” Enes said he didnвҖҷt know about it. Did you tell anyone that Nevzat gave her money?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, I didnвҖҷt. I didnвҖҷt know myself. It seemed normal.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you werenвҖҷt suspicious?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It seemed normal вҖ” maybe he gave it to Narin too, just casually.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Had he ever done that before?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I didnвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She told me about it that day.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright. Any questions from the defense? Please, go ahead. WeвҖҷll take you next.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of YГјksel GГјran
section: Defense Counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Ms.В YГјksel, had Narin ever been in SalimвҖҷs car before?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, on July 14.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : For what reason?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : It was for BeratвҖҷs engagement. Arif wasnвҖҷt home, and we didnвҖҷt have a car or seats available. Narin and Eren rode in that car. Salim had a car then, and Devran was driving. Hediye and the children also went in that car. Muhammed and I went in Memduh GГјranвҖҷs car. We went and returned like that.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : So Narin was in SalimвҖҷs car, correct?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, she rode in that car.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Before the incident, did you ever ride with Narin in your own car?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : In our car?вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : In ArifвҖҷs car?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did Arif take you anywhere a day or two before the incident? Did Narin ride in that car?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, the day before we went to DiyarbakДұr as guests for dinner. Wherever we went, we took our car.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did Nevzat ever come to your house? I mean, had he ever entered your home before the incident day? I donвҖҷt mean that day specifically вҖ” I mean in general. Did he ever visit as a guest?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I never saw Nevzat at our house. A few months before, he had the wells dug, so he was always around the area. When the well work ended, heвҖҷd still come around вҖ” weвҖҷd sit on the hill. But before that, I hadnвҖҷt seen him.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : So Nevzat never came into your home?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, never. I donвҖҷt remember that.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did Gazal Bahtiyar ever come over as a guest?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Gazal did come. God curse her вҖ” they didnвҖҷt have water, so she came every day to fetch water from us. She came to us вҖ” that was our kindness, Counselor. We were helping her because she had no water. But that kindness, Nevzat turned against us.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But look, Ms.В YГјksel вҖ” thereвҖҷs no serious hostility here. IвҖҷve reviewed hundreds, maybe thousands of murder cases. When thereвҖҷs hostility, itвҖҷs usually clear. YouвҖҷre saying there was none вҖ” in fact, you say you were kind, gave them water. So if there was no hostility, and you were on good terms вҖ” could it be that Nevzat was doing something wrong? We asked before whether you or Enes had any sexual relations with him. Forgive me for asking вҖ” itвҖҷs been reported in the news and caused controversy вҖ” but could it be that Nevzat was involved sexually with one of the three of you, and Narin saw it, and thatвҖҷs why he killed her?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : IвҖҷll just say this вҖ” as my lawyers said too вҖ” NevzatвҖҷs wife, even the commander told me, вҖңNevzat came to your house,вҖқ meaning Gazal. I said, вҖңNo, she doesnвҖҷt come to us.вҖқ He said, вҖңShe came to you first.вҖқ I said, вҖңYes, thatвҖҷs true. Gazal came to us.вҖқ Maybe a month before, I donвҖҷt know exactly. I saw Gazal at the door вҖ” she was going toward BelkДұsвҖҷs place. They were cousins. It was around the KarakoГ§ incident. I said, вҖңGazal, come inside.вҖқ She came to us.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Could we quiet down the background noise a bit?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Gazal came to us. I went to BelkДұsвҖҷs house вҖ” I was coming from Yeni Salihagil. I asked how things were. We talked for a bit. Then I went back to Gazal. I said, вҖңGazal, Hatiye Zeyno doesnвҖҷt talk to us anymore. I greet her вҖ” she doesnвҖҷt reply. Why?вҖқ Gazal laughed and said, вҖңDonвҖҷt mind her.вҖқ I said, вҖңI really donвҖҷt know.вҖқ I thought it was because of the car. Hatiye Zeyno wasnвҖҷt speaking to us because of that car issue. Gazal said, вҖңForget her.вҖқ I said, вҖңHonestly, Gazal, I didnвҖҷt know anything about this.вҖқ One day Arif said he was going to the headmanвҖҷs house. I told Gazal. I said, вҖңNo, Nevzat bought a car вҖ” it turned out stolen. WeвҖҷre going there to talk about it.вҖқ Arif came back and said, вҖңWeвҖҷre having a meeting. Neither of us nor anyone else will handle it. Cevat Kaya will come. Rukedin Kaya brought him. WeвҖҷll do what they suggest.вҖқ Then Gazal said, вҖңYГјksel, neither Cevat Kaya nor Rukedin Kaya вҖ” they all took SalimвҖҷs side. They put the blame for the money on Nevzat.вҖқ I said, вҖңI donвҖҷt know about any of that.вҖқ She said, вҖңFerhat refused to pay that money. He said, вҖҳEven if I kill myself or someone else, I wonвҖҷt pay it.вҖҷвҖқ Д°brahim, his son, said, вҖңThatвҖҷs shameful. If Salim doesnвҖҷt pay and Arif doesnвҖҷt pay, where will it come from? ItвҖҷs not a small amount.вҖқ Then I saw Arif and Nevzat talking up on the hill. I went over and said, вҖңWelcome, Nevzat.вҖқ They were talking about the money. Arif said, вҖңIвҖҷll give it to the lawyer. Once the lawyer settles it, weвҖҷll pay you back.вҖқ Nevzat said, вҖңFine.вҖқ I said, вҖңThatвҖҷs good. WeвҖҷve been neighbors for years вҖ” itвҖҷs not a huge amount. Just settle it.вҖқ Our children were all friends. Then I said, вҖңCongrats on your new house.вҖқ He talked with my husband and left. I never thought about that car again. I never told my husband, my kids, or anyone about what Ferhat said. They made up things, but we had nothing to do with it. If we had, we wouldвҖҷve said so. None of this ever crossed our minds.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright. On the day of the incident, did you hear any rumors that Nevzat had sexual relations with someone? Any gossip in the village?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : From whom?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Well, since there were claims about you and Salim вҖ” perhaps Nevzat had an affair with someone else, and Narin saw it, and he killed her because of that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Who said that? May God curse them. I swear itвҖҷs a lie.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you ever hear that Nevzat had a sexual relationship with someone?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.В I never heard that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : IвҖҷm just asking. DonвҖҷt curse me. Hey, why are you cursing me?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt know anything about that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So, thereвҖҷs nothing like that? You never heard such a thing?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You started cursing right away.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : IвҖҷm so angry right now, thatвҖҷs why. Alright, alright вҖ” let me say it piece by piece. Look, IвҖҷm saying it here too: he has no fear of God. I leave his children to God as well.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, look вҖ” you said, вҖңIвҖҷll tear him apart piece by piece,вҖқ didnвҖҷt you? Nevzat also said, вҖңIf I had done it, they would have torn me apart.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs what reminded me of it. Okay, fine. IвҖҷm not saying you meant that. Arif would tear him apart too. Maybe Enes, maybe even Eren вҖ” IвҖҷve said it before. But with such a risk of being torn apart, Nevzat supposedly took the body, hid it, and stayed in the village for 19, 20, maybe 21 days вҖ” the number doesnвҖҷt matter вҖ” right there in front of everyone. Is that correct?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We didnвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But surely you would have heard at some point. When Nevzat learned about it вҖ” the whole country was searching вҖ” if he was the man who killed her, the man who planned it, wouldnвҖҷt he have thought, вҖңIf this comes out, this family will kill me,вҖқ and fled with his whole family?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : If he had that sense, he wouldnвҖҷt have done it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : WouldnвҖҷt have done what?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : His part in the crime вҖ” he wouldnвҖҷt have done it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But heвҖҷs not saying вҖңI did it.вҖқ He says, вҖңThey killed her and gave her to me. I took the body, buried it, and lay down there.вҖқ HeвҖҷs not saying he killed her.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : ThatвҖҷs what weвҖҷre saying. We wouldnвҖҷt do what he did.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, but listen вҖ” Nevzat isnвҖҷt saying вҖңI did it.вҖқ He says, вҖңThey gave me the body. I took it, buried it.вҖқ He says thatвҖҷs why he didnвҖҷt flee вҖ” because he didnвҖҷt do it. How did you know he was lying?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Everyone knows heвҖҷs lying.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Why? How does everyone know heвҖҷs lying?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Because he never came to our house. IвҖҷm saying it right here вҖ” if it had been somewhere elseвҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He didnвҖҷt come to your house?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I swear, he didnвҖҷt. Neither of them did.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : The vehicle Salim was using recently вҖ” whose car was it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : He had one. After his accident, for a few days I saw Metin KayaвҖҷs car parked at his door. I even said, вҖңMaybe he bought a new car.вҖқ The children said, вҖңNo, itвҖҷs Metin KayaвҖҷs car, his brother-in-lawвҖҷs.вҖқ Later, when Metin KayaвҖҷs car disappeared, he started using his brotherвҖҷs car.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : WhatвҖҷs his brotherвҖҷs name?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Fuat.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Fuat. Did you see that car around SalimвҖҷs house that day вҖ” in front of his door, nearby, or anywhere else?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, absolutely not that day.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : So you didnвҖҷt see it parked or moving near his house?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, I swear that day I only saw it once, near HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs door. I didnвҖҷt see him or his children at all. I went by their door until evening and saw only the children.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Which of SalimвҖҷs children did Narin play with the most?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She played with Д°layda and Yusuf. Mostly with Д°layda. My children didnвҖҷt play much with others. SheвҖҷd go to Fatma HaticeвҖҷs house. I often sent the little ones there. Or she went to Hediye GГјranвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did children ever play in front of SalimвҖҷs house?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : If they were there, they might have, but Narin didnвҖҷt play with them much. Whenever I went somewhere, sheвҖҷd either go to HediyeвҖҷs house or to HГјseyin GГјranвҖҷs. She played with their children.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Now youвҖҷre saying whoever is guilty should be revealed.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, thatвҖҷs what I want. I want the killer.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Nevzat is slandering you?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : He says he saw you crying. CouldnвҖҷt he have made the same false claim about Salim?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : So, the man who said he saw you crying вҖ” couldnвҖҷt he also say Salim did it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt know. I leave it to God.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : But you say he slandered you.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Of course.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Yet heвҖҷs telling the same story.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : HeвҖҷs confessing falsely about me.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : He names both Salim and you.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, he accuses both of us вҖ” but itвҖҷs not true. I swear itвҖҷs not true.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : So heвҖҷs lying about you?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : HeвҖҷs falsely confessing about me.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Any other questions, Counselor?вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : No, Your Honor. Thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Ms.В YГјksel, you may sit down. Thank you.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : But I want the killer found.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : We all do.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I want NarinвҖҷs killer. Bring me NarinвҖҷs killer.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : One moment, please. We have another question. Let me ask again. We know you were at home. We also know Narin was last seen on the schoolвҖҷs camera at 15:11. ThereвҖҷs a 4-minute margin of error, but around 15:11. The distance between the school and your house is roughly 5 minutes. Could Narin have seen something she shouldnвҖҷt have? Like I said beforeвҖ”perhaps she saw Nevzat with someone else. IвҖҷm just wondering. Look, you said you talked with Gazal about the car, that youвҖҷd seen another car parked near their door. In a village, this kind of thing is always noticed. ThereвҖҷs plenty of free timeвҖ”people pay attention to who visits whom. We grew up in villages too. Right?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When a car with a foreign plate enters the village, everyone immediately asks who it is and why they came. Right? Is that true?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : If I saw it, I would have told you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So, people gossip among themselves. Did you hear any gossip in the village during that 4- or 5-minute period?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You didnвҖҷt. So could Narin have seen something before she was killedвҖ”something involving Nevzat or Salim?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I didnвҖҷt see anything. If I had, I swear I wouldвҖҷve shouted it from the start.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : YouвҖҷre saying you didnвҖҷt see, but IвҖҷm asking if you heard any gossip.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No gossip, nothing seen or heard.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, thank you. Please take your seat. Wait, one more question.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : The sound isnвҖҷt clearвҖ”please speak up. Counselor, can you hear me?вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : There were many questions about the headscarf. When you asked the imam about it, was it to confirm whether Narin came home that day? To verify if she made it back home? Or was it for another reason? The authorities also mentioned this.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : LetвҖҷs ask thenвҖ”why did you ask the imam about the headscarf?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : The imam said she had a purple skirt and a purple headscarf. When he said that, I said, вҖңLet me check, teacher, this scarf is mine. I saw it here that night when the women were sitting.вҖқ I asked, вҖңCould this be it or not?вҖқ He said, вҖңHonestly, IвҖҷm not sureвҖ”it might not be.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs why I asked.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : WhereвҖҷs the purple skirt?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did she wear the purple skirt that day?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : ThatвҖҷs what the imam said.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did she leave the house wearing it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : We searched, but didnвҖҷt see the purple skirt.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Because it wasnвҖҷt on the body, right?вҖӢ

title: Testimony of YГјksel GГјran
section: Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for Enes GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir вҖӢ

Law. Mustafa Demir : It was, it wasвҖ”on the body.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Was there a purple skirt on the body? Alright, I must have missed that.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : IвҖҷm asking becauseвҖ”did you think someone brought it there, or that Narin came home, left it, and went somewhere else?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No.В Because I had washed that balcony. Later the women came and spread things outвҖ”brought cushions and pillows. They sat me down among the railings while I was crying. ThatвҖҷs when I noticed it. The next morning the imam came and said the skirt was purple, the scarf was purple. I immediately brought the scarf and said, вҖңTeacher, is this the one?вҖқ He said, вҖңHonestly, IвҖҷm not entirely sure.вҖқ He said, вҖңYou brought it.вҖқ That same night I had seen that purple scarf among the railings. He said, вҖңIвҖҷm not sure.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : You donвҖҷt know who placed it there or brought it?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : What would you have concluded if the imam had said, вҖңYes, thatвҖҷs the same purple scarfвҖқ?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I would have understood that someone had brought it there that night, and I would have immediately handed it to the gendarmerie.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : You would have said someone left it there at night?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I would have said, вҖңSomeone brought this purple scarf here at night. ItвҖҷs mine.вҖқ But I had already washed the balconyвҖ”there was nothing there before. When the women came later, I saw it. I would have given it to the gendarmerie right away.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright, thank you. Go ahead.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of YГјksel GГјran
section: Defense Counsel for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Your Honor, the indictment claims that some phone call or message records were deleted. Did you ever delete any calls or messages?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Absolutely not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : No? Absolutely no?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : I donвҖҷt even know how to delete. I donвҖҷt normally use WhatsApp much.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : The indictment also says your phone was turned off during the critical hours, around 15:30. Did that happen?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : No, nobody turned it off. The children had it in their hands.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : You were asleep at the time, I see. Now, Maide Kaya, MaЕҹallah, and Birsen gave timesвҖ”MaideвҖҷs was a little later.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, 07:30вҖ”twenty minutes to seven, thatвҖҷs what they told us.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Twenty to seven, right. The indictment claims the GГјran family influenced these witnesses.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, thatвҖҷs what they said to us.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : ThatвҖҷs the allegation. After the gendarmerie interviewed these witnesses, you gave an interview on Didem Aslan YДұlmazвҖҷs TV program VazgeГ§me.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : In that interview, you said loudly, вҖңNarin didnвҖҷt go there; she either went up the hill or went around from there.вҖқ Can you explain why you said that? Let me remind you: you also said, вҖңMy daughter would never get into a strangerвҖҷs car.вҖқвҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I said that.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : And you said, вҖңIf someone familiar called my daughter, then sheвҖҷd go.вҖқ Correct?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : Yes, I said that too.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : When you heard MaЕҹallahвҖҷs and BirsenвҖҷs statements about those times, what did you think? Did Narin appear around that time?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : They told us MaЕҹallah said 5, Mahide said twenty to 7, Д°sa said 6, Еһeyma said 4:30. We said, вҖңThen where is Narin?вҖқ The gendarmerie blamed us completely. They said Narin disappeared at 7, Narin came home at 7, you and Enes were home. They put all the blame on us. These children werenвҖҷt lying. Then a gendarmerie team came every day with their equipment, checking around the barn. Every day. They said the incident happened at 3. They accused us of forcing witnesses to lie, of influencing them. Then when Nevzat appeared, they said Narin left the houseвҖ”again putting the blame on us. вҖңShe left the house, where did she go?вҖқ they said, вҖңIt was you.вҖқ First they said 7, then changed it to 3. They said, вҖңYou forced these witnesses.вҖқ Later, when Nevzat said he took her from inside the house, again they blamed usвҖ”вҖңYou were there, Enes was there, or maybe Salim.вҖқ They threw everything back on us. Wherever it happened, they pinned it on our necks. Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Alright, thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
text:
Reading Time
~ 54 min


Word Count
10327

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
section: Presiding Judge вҖӢ
text:
Presiding Judge вҖӢ

Presiding Judge : Ms.В Gazal, please. Inform the lawyers. Notify them. Gazal is here. Salim is here. Enes is here. WhereвҖҷs Enes? WeвҖҷre starting, Ms.В Gazal. First, Nevzat is your husband, correct?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : According to the Code of Criminal Procedure, you have the right not to testify. He is your husband. Therefore, I first ask you this: do you wish to testify? No, you canвҖҷt just say it casually. Would you like to be a witness? But by law, since Nevzat is your husband, we may ask questions that could reveal his guilt. Because of this, you have the right not to testify. If you say, вҖңI donвҖҷt want to testify,вҖқ we will respect that. Would you like to testify? As a witness? Yes? Then we will proceed accordingly. WeвҖҷll need a Kurdish interpreterвҖҰ Yes, Counsel. You do not have standing as an intervening party. You are a lawyer from the DiyarbakДұr Bar Association, but you are not participating in this case.вҖӢ


Female Lawyer : The request for intervention was denied. There is wisdom in everything.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : That must be why we denied it. Why did you request intervention anyway? No, no, IвҖҷve already rejected it. Someone from the police who can interpret Kurdish, please. Go ahead. Now, youвҖ”speak some Kurdish with her. Can you communicate in the same Kurdish dialect? Try that. If it works, weвҖҷll continue. Say somethingвҖ”ask how she is, how she feels. Alright, sheвҖҷll testify. Yes, since Ms.В Gazal can express herself better in Kurdish, a police officer present has been appointed by the court as interpreter. Your full name?вҖӢ


Interpreter : HacДұ Ahmet SardoДҹan.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Do you know Ms.В Gazal? You donвҖҷt. You donвҖҷt. Do you swear upon your honor and conscience to tell the truth?вҖӢ


Interpreter : I swear.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, Ms.В Gazal, you said you would testify. But since you are the defendantвҖҷs spouse, we will hear you without oath. Now, on the day Narin disappeared, did Ms.В YГјksel come to your house?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.В She didnвҖҷt come.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Not at all?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Not at all.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You didnвҖҷt see Ms.В YГјksel at all that day?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I saw her at 7 p.m. that day.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright. She came at 7 p.m. What did she say?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, she didnвҖҷt come to our place. Oh, I saw her.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You saw her. Where did you see her?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : The day Narin disappearedвҖ”my husbandвҖ”well, I know a little Turkish.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ThatвҖҷs fine. Half Turkish, half Kurdish, as you wish. What matters is that you can express yourself. Speak in whichever language you prefer. Just tell us what happened.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Okay. On the day Narin disappeared, my husband was in ГҮarДұklДұ doing electrical work at my sonвҖҷs house with another worker. Around two, maybe two-thirty, I donвҖҷt know. It was hot that day. He came home and said, вҖңIвҖҷm hungry, prepare some food.вҖқ I prepared his meal. Then he went to pray. He came back, and we ate together. Then he said, вҖңIs our tap water running?вҖқ I said, вҖңNo, itвҖҷs not.вҖқ He said, вҖңLet me call Salim.вҖқ Salim is the mukhtar.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He said, вҖңIвҖҷll call him so he can fix the water.вҖқ He called SalimвҖ”right next to me. We were together. My daughter and daughter-in-law were at the neighborвҖҷs house, at Sabri KaГ§anвҖҷs. Then he talked.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Who was home besides you?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Just me and him.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So two of you.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Where was your mother-in-law?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : At her own house.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So she was home.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : And where were you?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : At home too.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I see.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My mother-in-law lives separately. Her house is separate.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay, so she wasnвҖҷt at your house?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, my mother-in-law wasnвҖҷt at our house.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes, yes. So we were eating together. He said, вҖңOur water isnвҖҷt coming?вҖқ I said, вҖңNo, itвҖҷs not.вҖқ He said, вҖңLet me call my brother Salim so he can fix it.вҖқ He called. Salim said, вҖңOur water isnвҖҷt running.вҖқ He said, вҖңCall DД°SKД° so they fix it.вҖқ Then Salim said, вҖңAlright, IвҖҷll call.вҖқ My husband hung up. The call lasted barely a minute. Then he said, вҖңIвҖҷll draw water from my motherвҖҷs house to water the trees.вҖқ We had trees in the yard. He said, вҖңIвҖҷll water them.вҖқ I was inside.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He went outside?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Huh? Yes, he went outside to water the trees.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Which trees did he water? WeвҖҷve discussed this a lot hereвҖ”eggplants? No, noвҖ”fruit trees.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He went to water the fruit trees.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you see him outside while he was watering?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I was inside. Okay.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You were inside. Did you see him from the window or anything?вҖӢ

YГјksel GГјran : She ran after me from the mosque.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : That wasnвҖҷt her house though. She ran after you вҖ” did she say anything like that?вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : She was consoling me. She and Hediye came to my house looking for Narin, asking вҖңWhere is Narin?вҖқ Someone kidnapped her, someone killed her. ThatвҖҷs what I said.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay, you said that.вҖӢ
