YГјksel GГјran : I didnвҖҷt know then that my daughter was dead. How could I?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes, you said that already. What do you say about this, Ms.В Gazal?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : YГјksel GГјran is saying that, but look вҖ” at seven oвҖҷclock I was on the roof. YГјksel GГјran, Enes GГјran, and Hediye GГјran came down. You say IвҖҷm lying, but IвҖҷm not. You came down. You were shouting, вҖңMy daughter is gone, sheвҖҷs been kidnapped.вҖқ IвҖҷm telling the truth, YГјksel. Ferhat Bahtiyar was also a witness. Ferhat was with us at the mosque вҖ” the door was locked. Ferhat climbed in through the window and checked the whole mosque. He said, вҖңMaybe the girl fell asleep inside.вҖқ IвҖҷm not lying.вҖӢ


YГјksel GГјran : There are many people here, everyone is here. IвҖҷm not saying I went at the same minute.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay, okay.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : You first came down. You, Enes, and Hediye GГјran. You came down. You said it. Then we went with you to the mosque. We searched around and inside the mosque. My son climbed in through the window and checked the whole mosque, saying, вҖңMaybe she fell asleep.вҖқ He looked вҖ” she wasnвҖҷt there. We searched around the village with them until about 10:30.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay. Anything else you want to ask? No? Alright then. That microphone isnвҖҷt working вҖ” switch it.вҖӢ


Salim GГјran : Excuse me, excuse me, IвҖҷd never call this person вҖңmy dear brother.вҖқ SheвҖҷs lying.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Question. Do you want to ask Gazal anything?вҖӢ


Salim GГјran : She said my husband shot him in the back вҖ” how? She should explain.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : IвҖҷll explain.вҖӢ


Salim GГјran : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I am. How did you do it?вҖӢ


Salim GГјran : How did I shoot him in the back?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : You all keep blaming my husband. My husband couldnвҖҷt even kill your chicken.вҖӢ


Salim GГјran : The camera footage will come out, God willing. That girl never went up the path.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : She did. Are the tests lies? Are the samples lies? Is your car a lie? Is everything a lie?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Enes, come here. Do you have anything to ask?вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Ms.В Gazal, you say we came toward your house. Which path did we take?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : You came down the footpath.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Which footpath?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes, you came down.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : IвҖҷm not denying I came down, but which way did I come?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : That footpath.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Which one?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Behind my house, you came down that way. It was you.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : I came to your house?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : You didnвҖҷt come to my house.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Then where?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : You came down, and we went with you to the mosque.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Who was with us?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Your mother and Hediye GГјran.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Hediye GГјran. Your Honor, when we went up the hill, Hediye GГјran definitely didnвҖҷt come with us.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : The three of you were together.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : IвҖҷm speaking now вҖ” youвҖҷll have your turn. Your Honor, I, my mother, and Muhammed GГјran went up the hill. We called out to MaЕҹallah GГјran. When he said Narin wasnвҖҷt there, I took my brother Muhammed and went toward the mosque вҖ” to Azize GГјran first.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay, youвҖҷve already explained that.вҖӢ


Enes GГјran : Yes, Your Honor.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
section: DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ
text:
DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ

Law. Nahit Eren вҖӢ

Law. Nahit Eren : At 15:08, Nevzat calls Salim about the water, right? When that call happened, was he inside the house?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : When Nevzat was talking on the phone?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Yes.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You heard that conversation?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I did.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : They talked about the water?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : About fixing the water problem?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : After that, you said he left in the car. You also said, вҖңI looked from above and saw him driving toward the cemetery road.вҖқ You have such a statement, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You saw him leaving by car.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Let me ask you this: after that phone call, how long did it take before he got into the car and left? IвҖҷm asking how long passed between that 15:08 call and the time he drove away вҖ” ten minutes, five minutes, half an hour?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He talked on the phone inside. Then he went outside. Maybe twenty minutes, fifteen minutes, I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So he didnвҖҷt leave right after the call?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, he said he was going to water the trees.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Alright.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Then he left. I donвҖҷt know exactly how many minutes passed. Later, he said, вҖңIвҖҷm going to ГҮarДұklДұ.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Okay. You found out from television that Nevzat had taken Narin and hidden her there, correct?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You didnвҖҷt know before that?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Afterwards, you know that he also brought you cheese from Zeynep?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So when he came in the evening, he brought the cheese?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He did.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : After all this, did you ever talk to Zeynep about it? I mean, after Nevzat was caught and sent to prison. She came to you after this incident, right? When Nevzat went to her, he sat there, drank tea, chatted, then came home. Did you ever talk to Zeynep afterward?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Zeynep comes to my house anyway. SheвҖҷs my sister.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did you ever talk to her about this? Look вҖ” it was on TV that he took the body to the stream and hid it. He admitted it himself. When he came back, did he say anything to you? Did you ever suspect him?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I asked him. He said he never suspected anything.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : So nothing at all?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Nothing.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Earlier, the Presiding Judge asked about the offer вҖ” you said there was one. Actually, it was made to NevzatвҖҷs brother, and then he told him in prison, right? You were there too.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : The offer came to us.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Yes, the offer came to your family. So it was made directly to you?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : To us.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The offer was about Nevzat taking the blame, correct? Who brought this to you?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Hasan Kaya. Hasan Kaya said that Ali RДұza GГјran made the offer. He said, вҖңIf Gazal accepts, she can come live in the village, stay in her house, weвҖҷll cover everything. Nevzat should take the blame.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You went to the prison вҖ” with Vecdin and Vedat вҖ” and his brother told him during the visit?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What was NevzatвҖҷs reaction? You were there during the open visit, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : What did Nevzat say?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : We said we donвҖҷt accept it.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Not you вҖ” what did Nevzat himself say about the offer?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He said he wouldnвҖҷt accept it.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did he actually say it, or not?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : You donвҖҷt remember. What was his reaction then? And before going, did you tell your brothers or Vecdi that you wouldnвҖҷt accept this offer?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : That I should accept it, you mean?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Whether you should or shouldnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I was against it.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did you tell that to your brothers? To Vecdin?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I said something like that.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : To Vecdin?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I said, вҖңSuch an offer came, but IвҖҷll never accept it.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : If you hadnвҖҷt been there with Nevzat, do you think he might have accepted it?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : He wouldnвҖҷt have accepted it anyway?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, he wouldnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The Presiding Judge asked earlier вҖ” their stepmother, as I recall, is SГјreyya?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : SГјveyla.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : SГјveyla, right. You know, on the day Narin disappeared, she was seen on the school camera, working in the hayloft, packing hay. Do you think SГјveyla told you вҖңNevzat will be arrested after SalimвҖқ because she had witnessed something? Or did you think nothing of it afterward? At that point, it wasnвҖҷt yet known that Nevzat had hidden the body, but this woman from your village came and said that to you.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : ThatвҖҷs right. Exactly.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did you share that with Nevzat? Tell him, вҖңSГјveyla came and said theyвҖҷll arrest you after SalimвҖқ? Did you ever talk to him about that? Why would she say that to you?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt talk to him about it.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Not at all? You didnвҖҷt worry?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, she just said that. I asked, вҖңWhy will Nevzat be arrested?вҖқ She said, вҖңNo, no, IвҖҷll just go give my statement.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs what she told me later.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Did she say, вҖңIf something happens, IвҖҷll go give my statement,вҖқ or вҖңIвҖҷm already going to give my statementвҖқ?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, no. SГјveyla GГјran told me, вҖңSalim was arrested. Nevzat will be arrested too.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : A few days later?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes. I asked, вҖңWhy was Salim arrested? Why will Nevzat be arrested?вҖқ ThatвҖҷs what I said.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : In one of your statements, you said, вҖңThis family keeps secrets.вҖқвҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : What?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : вҖңKeeps secrets.вҖқ Maybe you said it in Kurdish, I donвҖҷt know. But in your signed statement, it says that phrase.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You can say it in Kurdish, Counsel.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : The phrase вҖңkeeps secretsвҖқ вҖ” was that something you said? Why?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : What do you mean?вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Why did you say they never talk about anything with anyone?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt say вҖңkeeps secrets.вҖқ I said they were always meeting, always holding family gatherings.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Earlier you commented about the family вҖ” that theyвҖҷre very closed off, they donвҖҷt share what happens among themselves with outsiders. ThatвҖҷs what вҖңkeeps secretsвҖқ means. You also said that after the second day, they shut themselves off, didnвҖҷt let anyone else in. By that, did you mean they stopped letting you visit?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : We used to go for two or three days.вҖӢ


Law. Nahit Eren : Why did you go?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : To see YГјksel. We went, but later they said, вҖңWeвҖҷre having a family meeting, no one come.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Let me clarify вҖ” when she said вҖңkeeps secrets,вҖқ she meant that later, during family meetings, they stopped including her. Is that right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Okay. Anyone object? No.В There was a break in the dialogue earlier; let it be recorded correctly. She said she didnвҖҷt use the phrase вҖңkeeps secrets.вҖқ There was some confusion in the transcript. WeвҖҷll correct it accordingly.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
section: Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ
text:
Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ

Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz вҖӢ

Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Your Honor, weвҖҷve discussed everything; IвҖҷll just ask one question.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Regarding the sack in the barn вҖ” according to the account, he goes to the barn, climbs in through the window, takes a sack, puts the body inside. Did you ever witness anything like that?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : So you werenвҖҷt home then?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I was home, inside.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : And you didnвҖҷt hear anything?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : ItвҖҷs a village house, after all.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt see anything. If I had, I wouldвҖҷve turned him in myself. I swear.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : You went to the prison. DidnвҖҷt you ask him about these things? Were you threatened? Was your husband threatened? You mentioned this before вҖ” but who exactly threatened him?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : What do you mean?вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : I mean, could your husband commit such a crime?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Such a crimeвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Could he normally commit such a thing?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Who do you think threatened him?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I donвҖҷt believe it. My husband could never commit such a crime.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Then why do you think all three of them accuse your husband? Was he being threatened?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Look, I never told you this before.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : You never told us?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Look, in my statement I said вҖ” he was threatened. They threatened him and forced the sack and the girl on him. He said, вҖңI took her and hid her.вҖқ It was all shown on TV. I didnвҖҷt see it with my own eyes.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : You didnвҖҷt see it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When you say they didnвҖҷt include you in their family meetings, what do you mean? Do you think this was part of some plan to hide something?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I mean, how can I say, Your Honor? Afterwards they would say, вҖңDonвҖҷt come.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : вҖңDonвҖҷt come.вҖқ So they met among themselves. But is that suspicious behavior? WouldnвҖҷt a family prefer to grieve among themselves? Rather than with outsiders? Did that seem suspicious to you? Does their not including you mean they killed Narin?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Your Honor, what I mean is, we went there to console them, but they said, вҖңWeвҖҷre having a family meeting,вҖқ and didnвҖҷt let us join. ThatвҖҷs all I know.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Who attended the meeting?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : The family.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
section: Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ
text:
Trial Prosecutor вҖӢ

Trial Prosecutor : As far as I recall from your statements and the prosecutorвҖҷs questioning, you and your daughter both said something like вҖңThis area is off-limits, you canвҖҷt enter here.вҖқ Do you remember that?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did they say something like that?вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : вҖңThis is off-limits, you canвҖҷt enter this area.вҖқ From the very first day вҖ” near Arif GГјrangilвҖҷs house. Both you and your daughter said that. You were aware of it.вҖӢ


Interpreter : When we went there, they said, вҖңThereвҖҷs a family meeting, no one should come.вҖқвҖӢ

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
section: Defense Counsels for Enes GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsels for Enes GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Mahir Akbilek вҖӢ

Law. Mahir Akbilek : You may refuse to answer some of my questions, but if so, please say so clearly. On September 9, 2024, you gave a statement titled вҖңinformation record.вҖқ You said that on the morning of the incident, after breakfast, Nevzat went with your son Ferhat Bahtiyar to the construction site in the ГҮarДұklДұ neighborhood to do electrical work. Do you remember that?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My husband went there. Not Ferhat вҖ” Ferhat was working in PirinГ§lik village.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : IвҖҷm reading directly from your 09/09/2024 statement. Do you remember this exact sentence?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I donвҖҷt remember, butвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : You donвҖҷt remember. Okay. Then on September 18, 2024, you gave another statement correcting it, saying Ferhat went separately. Do you remember that one?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I donвҖҷt remember that statement. But as I know it, Ferhat was working in PirinГ§lik village. Nevzat went to ГҮarДұklДұ at 06:30 to do electrical work. ThatвҖҷs what I remember. Nevzat had one worker named Vedat. He was an electrician.вҖӢ


Interpreter : Her son worked in PirinГ§lik, and Nevzat went alone with one worker.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Yes. Earlier, in response to another question, you mentioned your son Ferhat as a witness вҖ” you said, even though Mrs.В YГјksel couldnвҖҷt ask properly, that your son Ferhat was there that day searching for the girl, right? You said he helped search in the mosque.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes, it was around 7. Ferhat came back from work, saw the crowd. We were heading to the mosque with YГјksel, Enes, and Hediye. He asked, вҖңWhat happened?вҖқ We said, вҖңNarin is missing.вҖқ My son searched inside the mosque with us. He climbed through the window and said, вҖңMaybe she fell asleep inside.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Okay, you remember. You said your son is a trustworthy person like your husband, right?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : IsnвҖҷt that a strange question to ask a mother, Counselor?вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : ItвҖҷs fine, Your Honor. Do you consider your son trustworthy?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Trustworthy.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : You trust him?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Then why did your son вҖ” along with your brothers-in-law, like Mehmet Bahtiyar вҖ” initially support the claim that your husband went to work in ГҮarДұklДұ that day, only for you later to correct and contradict it? Why did you feel the need to make that statement?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My husband wasnвҖҷt working there that day вҖ” the day he was arrested.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Please just answer the question directly. Or say if you donвҖҷt want to answer.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : She didnвҖҷt understand the question.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Why did you feel the need?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I think she didnвҖҷt understand.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : In your first statement, you said, вҖңMy son Ferhat and my husband Nevzat went together to ГҮarДұklДұ after breakfast.вҖқвҖӢ


Interpreter : They left early together вҖ” the son went to PirinГ§likвҖҰвҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : So they left together.вҖӢ


Interpreter : I donвҖҷt remember exactly.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Which one? Just now you said your son and husband went together to ГҮarДұklДұ. Later, you said your son went to PirinГ§lik instead.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I donвҖҷt remember. I really donвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : So your answer is вҖңI donвҖҷt remember,вҖқ correct? Okay.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : YouвҖҷre aware, right, that in the ongoing main investigation вҖ” file number 202446201 вҖ” your husband is currently in custody because it was determined that he lied about the time and location, claiming to be with someone who later said he wasnвҖҷt?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I know.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : You know. Okay. Earlier you praised your husband. So can we understand this вҖ” if you had believed your husband was involved in this horrible crimeвҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I wouldвҖҷve turned him in myself. I wouldвҖҷve.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : LetвҖҷs confirm that, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I asked her three times. She said it three times.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : I wonвҖҷt repeat it, Your Honor. Would your trust in him have been broken?вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : She said she doesnвҖҷt believe it, right?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Translate that. I didnвҖҷt understand.вҖӢ


Interpreter : She said, вҖңYour Honor, I donвҖҷt believe he killed her.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : From August 21, 2024, until his arrest, did your husband ever say anything about the murder or act in a way that made you suspect he was involved?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Never. Then let me ask again вҖ” why did you say in your statement that he left with your son Ferhat? Why did you feel the need to say that?вҖӢ


Interpreter and witness converse in Kurdish.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : ThatвҖҷs not what IвҖҷm asking, but you understand my question. From TavЕҹantepe to ГҮarДұklДұ вҖ” thatвҖҷs what I mean. IвҖҷm not asking about after ГҮarДұklДұ. Why did you include your son as if he left with your husband? You trust him; you donвҖҷt suspect him of anything.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I meanвҖҰ yes.вҖӢ


Interpreter and witness converse in Kurdish.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : YouвҖҷre still not answering.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : What did she say, YГјksel? What did she say?вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : She said вҖңI donвҖҷt remember,вҖқ right?вҖӢ


Interpreter : She said, вҖңHe left for work, I just said they went together. Sometimes he left with his sister, sometimes with his uncle, I didnвҖҷt see it myself.вҖқвҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes, they would leave together sometimes. I said it like that. Sometimes with his sister, sometimes by car. When they left the house, I was inside. They left together, but I didnвҖҷt actually see them.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you know he left the house, but not what happened afterward. Is that correct, Counselor?вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Yes. Then why did you later retract that statement in your September 18 testimony? Why did you then say, вҖңMy husband went alone, my son went separately with Mehmet BahtiyarвҖқ?вҖӢ


Interpreter : (Unclear, spoke softly.)вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Please translate clearly. I know Kurdish too вҖ” we couldnвҖҷt fully understand.вҖӢ


Interpreter : She said, вҖңI didnвҖҷt know at the time, so I answered that way.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : The inconsistency couldnвҖҷt be resolved. LetвҖҷs end this line of questioning here.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : With your permission, Your Honor, just a final point. Why did your brother-in-law Mehmet Bahtiyar act in sync with you вҖ” first making the same statement, then retracting it just as you did? Do you have any idea why?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : She said she doesnвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Alright. If I called that coordinated deceit, youвҖҷd be offended, wouldnвҖҷt you? Your Honor, with your permission, IвҖҷll ask just a few short questions about the day of the incident. Thank you for your patience.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Of course, go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : On the day of the incident, you, your husband Nevzat, and your mother-in-law were in separate houses. When you say вҖңseparate,вҖқ do you mean you couldnвҖҷt hear each other at all? No sound from one house to the other?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, you couldnвҖҷt hear.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : In your statement, you said your husband got in the car and left. Did you see which direction he drove вҖ” toward the lower or upper part of the village? Did you look? Toward the school, the cemetery? So to the left, from your doorвҖҷs perspective?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : To the left, toward the cemetery.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Toward the cemetery. Between the phone call and the moment he left by car, did he leave the house on foot for anything? Did you see?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I was inside.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Did you hear any sound вҖ” like him saying вҖңIвҖҷm leaving,вҖқ or footsteps?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He said, вҖңIвҖҷm going to water the trees.вҖқ He was watering them in the yard. I was still inside. I donвҖҷt know how many minutes passed between. Then he said, вҖңGazal, IвҖҷm going to ГҮarДұklДұ, I have some work there вҖ” electrical work at my sonвҖҷs house.вҖқ Then he said, вҖңIвҖҷm leaving.вҖқ I said okay. I saw him get in the car and drive downhill.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Thank you.вҖӢ



Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir вҖӢ

Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : How often did you see Narin in the village?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Pardon?вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : How often did you see Narin in the village? Did you see her every day?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Narin. No, I didnвҖҷt see her.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : You didnвҖҷt see Narin?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : She wasnвҖҷt my childrenвҖҷs age for me to see her.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : DidnвҖҷt Narin go outside?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : She didnвҖҷt go outside, but she would go with children her own age. She went to HacДұ HГјseyinвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : When she went out, didnвҖҷt she pass near your house?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : What do you mean?вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : When Narin went out from her own house.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : She doesnвҖҷt pass near our house.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : DidnвҖҷt she go to the mosque?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : There was a road behind as well. And in front of the house too. There was also a road behind our house.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : So thatвҖҷs why you couldnвҖҷt see her, correct? When was the last time you saw her before the incident?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt see her for a month.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : How far is it between your house and Mr.В ArifвҖҷs house?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : TheyвҖҷre up on the hill. Ours is down below.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Approximately how far? How many minutesвҖҷ walk?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : 3вҖ“4 minutes.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : ItвҖҷs 3вҖ“4 minutes away, yet you never saw Narin. If you had seen her in the village, would you have spoken to her? Would you have greeted her, for example?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : We would have talked.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : You liked Narin, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I mean, if I had seen her, I would have spoken. SheвҖҷs a child.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : You liked her. You would have spoken, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : If your husband had seen her, would he have spoken to her as well?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Where would my husband see her? How would he see her?вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : DonвҖҷt they live in the same village? ItвҖҷs a very small village вҖ” say he saw her once every three months, based on your statement. If he saw her once every three months, would Mr.В Nevzat greet Narin? вҖңHello, Narin. How are you, Narin?вҖқвҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt see it.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : So you have no guess about this. You canвҖҷt speculate, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt see it; I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Alright. On the 21st, the day of the incident, one of your children stayed overnight at Mr.В ArifвҖҷs house. What was that childвҖҷs name?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Д°brahim Bahtiyar.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Earlier you said about Д°brahim Bahtiyar staying there, вҖңWe love each other very much. There is no hostility between us,вҖқ correct? If there is no hostility, if you are two families who like each other, why wouldnвҖҷt Mr.В Nevzat greet her when he saw her?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : YouвҖҷre the one saying he greeted her; IвҖҷm saying I didnвҖҷt see it.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : There are things you saw and things you guessed.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Maybe he would have spoken.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : For example, if Narin passed in front of him and Mr.В Nevzat said вҖңcomeвҖқ to her, called her over, or met at a certain place, would that be a possible scenario?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Narin would not go to anyone.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : She wouldnвҖҷt go to anyone. Your houses are side by side. You are two families who like each other. There is no hostility, yet still Narin wouldnвҖҷt go to anyone, is that so?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : She was a smart girl.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Then who are you there?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I mean, she wouldnвҖҷt go to anyone.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : So you werenвҖҷt close, the two families?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I mean, how? WeвҖҷre not relatives. WeвҖҷre close, but not relatives.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Alright. IвҖҷll ask you something and finish, Your Honor. Many questions have been asked. Your husband normally goes out for plastering work. On the day of the incident, he went to his sonвҖҷs house for electrical work, correct?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : You stated that as well. If he goes for plastering work every day, why did he go for electrical work that day?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My son, his house was finished вҖ” everything. The belongings had been moved in. Only the electrical work remained. There was an electrician working that day. He was doing the wiring.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Did I understand you correctly? There was an electrician at your son FerhatвҖҷs house, right? Vedat.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Vedat. He went there together with my husband.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Your husband was an electrician, right? Then why did your husband go there with Vedat?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He is an electrician.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Okay, I understood that. I mean, why would your husband, an electrician, go there? The house was empty. Newly finished. No personal belongings.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : That man wouldnвҖҷt go to do the electrical work in that house alone. The owner had to be there.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Alright. One last point. They always do character analysis, they ask. I want to hear it from Ms.В Gazal as well. IвҖҷd like to hear Mr.В NevzatвҖҷs character вҖ” I mean, in this sense: what was he like in the village, in social life, with relatives?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He was moral, had a good temperament.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Was he a talkative person or more quiet?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Quiet.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : He was quiet? Understood.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
section: Defense Counsel for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for YГјksel GГјran вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu вҖӢ

Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Ms.В Gazal, you mentioned earlier that you have relatives who are village guards.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : In which village do they live?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : In BatДұkarakoГ§.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : In BatДұkarakoГ§. Their surname is Kaya, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Kaya.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Are there any Kayas in your village, TavЕҹantepe?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : How many Kaya families are there? Are there more GГјrans or more Kayas?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : There are more GГјrans.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Alright. Your son was working in PirinГ§lik on the day of the incident, correct?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : In the evening, when your husband came home, when he came to ГҮarДұklДұ, who was driving the car?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My husband was already in ГҮarДұklДұ. My son came back from work in PirinГ§lik. My husband picked him up. They came home together.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Okay. Was your husband driving?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes. Nevzat was driving.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Nevzat was driving. Earlier you said Ferhat went to the mosque when YГјksel was shouting.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : At 7 oвҖҷclock my husband and son arrived home that evening. I also went up to the roof. I heard shouting. YГјksel GГјran. Enes GГјran and also Hediye GГјran.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Yes.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : They were shouting out loud. That Narin was missing, that Narin had been abducted. Narin. ThatвҖҷs what she said. So we went with her and searched around and inside the mosque вҖ” Ferhat came with us as well. We went together and searched the mosque. My son вҖ” the door was locked. He climbed in through the window. He searched the entire mosque, saying maybe the girl had fallen asleep. My son searched the mosque with us. And we walked around the village with YГјksel searching until ten-thirty.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : You searched. Then why, while your son was so helpful, didnвҖҷt your husband help you? Why didnвҖҷt he join the search?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My husband came straight home.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : DidnвҖҷt your husband do anything then? A child is missing. DidnвҖҷt you suggest going to search?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt say anything.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Do you have a grandchild? Is your grandchild around NarinвҖҷs age?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My grandchild. Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : For example, did your grandchild ever play with Narin?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : They played at school. They played together.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : They played together. And youвҖҷre also neighbors with ArifвҖҷs family, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My daughter is not my neighbor. My daughter is far from me.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : You are neighbors, right? No, thatвҖҷs not what I mean. You are neighbors with Arif, correct?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes. WeвҖҷre nearby.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : YouвҖҷre nearby. When your grandchild came to you, would Narin never come?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.В She never came.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : WouldnвҖҷt they talk there?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I never saw Narin. She never came to my house or my yard. I didnвҖҷt see her.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : When Salim called on the day of the incident вҖ” when he called your husband вҖ” you heard it. You said it was about water, right? How long did that conversation about water last?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : It didnвҖҷt even last a minute.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Not even a minute. Good, you remember that well. You say it didnвҖҷt last a minute. How long after that conversation did Salim leave the house? SorryвҖ”Arif. Nevzat.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : We were at the table, eating and talking.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : How long did the meal take? Did the meal end right after the call?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : The phone rang while we were eating, but I donвҖҷt know how many minutes it took.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Our presiding judge tried to understand this with his questions, and so are we. When someone gets a call during a meal, do they continue eating afterward, or stop?вҖӢ


Interpreter : They ate a bit. Sat for a while.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : So he ate a bit after the call, sat a bit, and then said heвҖҷd go water the trees?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He didnвҖҷt sit long.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : When he went to water the trees, how many trees do you have? Where are they?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : In the yard.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Do you have any eggplants?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Would the eggplants be watered?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : So they wouldnвҖҷt be watered that day, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Hm?вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Since you didnвҖҷt have eggplants, they wouldnвҖҷt be watered, right?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He watered the trees.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Ah. Your husbandвҖ”about the eggplantsвҖҰвҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : There were eggplants, yes. Separate ones.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Where were those eggplants?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He just said he was going to water the trees.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Alright. Were the eggplants in the yard or behind the house on the path?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : At that time the eggplants had dried out. There werenвҖҷt any.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Yet your husband Nevzat said here that he watered the eggplants behind the house. Your husband says he watered eggplants. Which is true?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : There were eggplants, but waitвҖ”there were eggplants, but he told me he was going to water the trees.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : ItвҖҷs in Kurdish, to ensure itвҖҷs recorded correctly. IвҖҷm saying this because the police officer doesnвҖҷt understand it.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Your mother-in-law Zeynep вҖ” because of this money issue with Arif, did she ever act cold or distant toward YГјkselвҖҷs family or others?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.В I didnвҖҷt see anything.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : Did you ever talk about it? Did YГјksel ever ask you, вҖңWhy is Zeynep acting like this?вҖқвҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt see, didnвҖҷt hear anything. We had no hostility between us.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : One last question then. You saw the car in the yard, in front of the gate, right? The red one.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : You were on the balcony at that time.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He told me, вҖңIвҖҷm leaving. IвҖҷm going to ГҮarДұklДұ.вҖқвҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : He called out, вҖңIвҖҷm going to ГҮarДұklДұ.вҖқ He started the car and went down the cemetery road.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He went toward the cemetery road. I mean, I didnвҖҷt see exactly. He went downhill. I saw clearly that he went downhill, but not which direction he turned.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : And you said there was no blanket exchange?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : We didnвҖҷt see any.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : You didnвҖҷt see any other vehicle either?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : From where you saw the car move, can you see Arif GГјranвҖҷs house from there?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : From our house.вҖӢ


Law. YДұlmaz DemiroДҹlu : From your house?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No, you canвҖҷt see it. Ours is lower.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of Gazal Bahtiyar
section: Defense Counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsel for Salim GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ вҖӢ

Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : By nature, is your husband a fearful person? Does he fear anyone?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : YouвҖҷve known your husband for 30 years. Does he fear anyone? Is he intimidated by anyone? Is he afraid of Salim?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Can we quiet the murmuring, please? ThereвҖҷs some noise over there. Thank you.вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He never fought with anyone, so he doesnвҖҷt fight with anyone.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Does your husband lie often?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : Never. He never lies. Never. Even if they cut off his head, he wouldnвҖҷt lie.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Then how do you explain that he has six statements, all different from each other?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did you hear your mother-in-law Zeynep Bahtiyar say, вҖңArif ruined our house, Arif cheated us out of 50,000 liraвҖқ?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I didnвҖҷt hear that.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : YouвҖҷve known your husband for 30 years, you say. Your children know him less than you. For example, Ferhat Bahtiyar said that when my wife YГјksel cried, Nevzat showed no reaction, went home quietly, and watched us from the balcony. Then Fadile said he didnвҖҷt join the search efforts. How is it that your children, at their age, sensed his unease, but you did not?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You have the right not to answer this question. ItвҖҷs incriminating. IвҖҷm skipping it. This question accuses the witness.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Alright. Another question вҖ” on the day Narin was found, when you heard the news, did you faint? Lose consciousness?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : No.В We were all crying for her, truly.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I intervened in the previous question as well, because the defendant would have been self-incriminated otherwise.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : No problem. DonвҖҷt you think carrying NarinвҖҷs body is just as cruel as killing her?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : She said she doesnвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Then why do you defend your husband so much? If I saw my spouse like that, IвҖҷd never defend her. Your husband confessed. In this case, there are only two truths вҖ” NarinвҖҷs death and your husbandвҖҷs confession. Everything else is justвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : IвҖҷm also intervening here. That question would be considered aiding the accused. WeвҖҷll skip it.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Fine. What are your husbandвҖҷs main interests? For example, what does he like to do, what are his hobbies?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : My husband? He always recites prayers, performs his namaz, thatвҖҷs how he is. He was a clean, decent man.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did he often go fishing?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He went fishing.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Often?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : When he didnвҖҷt have work, he went.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : Did he go so often that he knew the EДҹertutmaz stream like the back of his hand?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : He didnвҖҷt go there, but he went elsewhere. You know, where? The commanders came there that day вҖ” he was fishing there.вҖӢ


Law. Onur AkdaДҹ : People in the village of GГ¶nenli often say this about your husband: вҖңWe release carp here so they can multiply and we can collect them later. Nevzat comes, takes the fish, and leaves.вҖқ They complain about him. You never saw him go to EДҹertutmaz stream?вҖӢ


Gazal Bahtiyar : I never saw him go to EДҹertutmaz stream. He went elsewhere, with others. TheyвҖҷd get in the car and go together.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran
text:
Reading Time
~ 35 min


Word Count
6699

title: Testimony of MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran
section: Presiding Judge вҖӢ
text:
Presiding Judge вҖӢ

Presiding Judge : Then under oath. Yes, can everyone please stand? Yes. Ms.В MaЕҹallah. Do you swear upon your honor and conscience to tell the truth?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I do.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Look, perjury is a crime. If, at the end of this trial, we reach such a conclusion, we will file a criminal complaint against you. You are currently in custody, correct? For another offense. Therefore, you also have the right not to answer our questions. If you are asked self-incriminating questions, you have the right not to answer them. I will in any case intervene on this matter. Our intention is to take your statements in a comfortable manner, alright? To remain calm.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : First let me talk about this. The fight among the women. What was this quarrel about? Why did you have such a fight? One moment, a murmur has started. I canвҖҷt hear myself. There is also a serious murmur by the door. We can bring those by the door over to this side.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : On the day Narin was found, Commander HГјlya came. We were all crying. We had all gathered. Commander HГјlya came. She informed YГјksel. YГјksel said, вҖңCommander, you had promised meвҖ”where is my Narin? You were going to bring my Narin.вҖқ At that moment she screamed and fainted. She fainted into my arms. When she fell into my arms, we, together with Commander HГјlya and the team, put her in the ambulance. After we did, I returned to my place. Forgive me, IвҖҷm a bitвҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : However you feel comfortable.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I sat down. I apologize to all of you. I said, вҖңNarin, which bastards did this to you? Which bastards did this to you? May God curse them.вҖқ I apologize. Then someone cameвҖ”YГјkselвҖҷs sister, I forgot her name. She came. вҖңIf you had told, this wouldnвҖҷt have happened,вҖқ she said. вҖңWe donвҖҷt know anything.вҖқ I said, вҖңIf you know, then you tell.вҖқ Then she spoke about it again. I didnвҖҷt speak. My daughter stood up. вҖңIf you know, tell. We donвҖҷt know anything.вҖқ At that moment my daughter fainted. I was tending to my daughter. I took my daughter. I took both my daughters and went home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Which daughter?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Birsen and Melike.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : It is said you shouted, вҖңEveryone knows everything. DonвҖҷt make me talk.вҖқвҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Absolutely not. I didnвҖҷt use such words. I do not accept that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you say, вҖңI donвҖҷt know anything. If I knew, I would tellвҖқ?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : вҖҰвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : No, no. You just said, вҖңI donвҖҷt know anything. If I knewвҖ”if you know, you tell.вҖқвҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I said it to Yaser, to him. I said, вҖңIf you know, you tell. We donвҖҷt know anything.вҖқвҖӢ


Presiding Judge : It is said the men tried to cover your mouths. Did anyone try to cover your mouth?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Absolutely not. Nothing like that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did anyone try to cover your daughterвҖҷs mouth?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.В They were on the other side anyway. Maybe on their side. I took my daughter. I took my other daughter, my younger daughter. I took her as well and went home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You went home. Now then, there is also this statement by Birsen: вҖңDid we do it, so why are you coming at us?вҖқ Why did she use such wording, do you know?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I didnвҖҷt see that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You didnвҖҷt hear anything like that?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I didnвҖҷt hear it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : And Ms.В MaЕҹallah. Let me ask the question like this. I will ask the question everyone wants to ask. After these eventsвҖ”that is, after Narin disappearedвҖ”it could be about Narin, or Salim, or YГјksel, or Enes, or Nevzat. Did anyone direct you? Did they tell you to give false statements in this manner?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, absolutely not.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Because you say Narin came to our house. You say you heard it from your daughter.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Narin comes around two oвҖҷclock. She asks for my daughters. At that time my daughters go to the mosque. Before noon I take my daughters and leave them at my motherвҖҷs. Then I come home. My daughters stay there. After that Narin comes. Around two oвҖҷclock. My daughter opens the door. She didnвҖҷt stay two minutes. She said, вҖңWhere are Hatice and Fatma?вҖқ I said, вҖңAt their grandmotherвҖҷs.вҖқ My daughter didnвҖҷt know they went to the mosque. Then she told me. I said, вҖңWe left them at the mosque.вҖқ She said, вҖңAlright, Narin came.вҖқ She said, вҖңAlright.вҖқ Then the reason we said 05:40 is that Mina came. When Mina cameвҖ”when Narin disappearedвҖ”I completely forgot Mina. I kept saying Narin. There was no ulterior motive, sir.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So when you said Narin came at 05:40, actually it was Mina who came. Mina came.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : The confusion came from that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : And this is your statement? The date you gave your statement is 29.08. So about 7вҖ“8 days after the incident.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : As IвҖҷm sayingвҖ”Narin was on my mind the whole time. Narin was constantly with us.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I understand.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : She was like my child too.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So Narin came in the morning. Around two oвҖҷclock. Yes. Now, there is that well-known video. The one that caused serious debate on social media. Narin comes and then runs off. At that timeвҖ”in betweenвҖ”did Narin come to your house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : At what time, sir?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Two, twoвҖҰвҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes, yes, she goes to the mosque.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So at that time Narin comes to your house. She asks for your daughters. Because your daughters arenвҖҷt at home, she runs to the mosque. On social media there were reports like вҖңWhat did Narin see that made her run?вҖқ Before that video, she came to your house, asked for your daughters, and when she didnвҖҷt see them, we presume she went to the mosque to catch up with them.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Because the three of them had agreed the night before.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Around seventeen, seventeen-thirty Narin did not come. On the day of the incident, you only saw Narin at two oвҖҷclock.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I didnвҖҷt see her. My daughter opened the door.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You said she opened the door at seventeen-forty. Mina came.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Mina came.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When you said you heard from Birsen that you saw Narin, in fact you meant the morning time, but it got recorded as around seventeen-thirty.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : When she disappeared, the confusion came from that. There was no ulterior motive. We wanted her found quickly.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I understand. So giving a false statement in this way to steer someoneвҖ”by SalimвҖ”вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Because there is an allegation in the indictment. The prosecution alleges this. It says Salim arranged this. Because Birsen has a statementвҖ”was it Birsen?вҖ”rather, Melike has a statement: Salim came to us and said, вҖңSay she came at 17:40.вҖқ Given that, is it possible Birsen made such a statement?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : There is no such thing, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : That dayвҖ”вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Birsen and SalimвҖ”after Narin disappearedвҖ”talked about the timing. He told me about itвҖ”he explained it to me at home. вҖңWhichever time she came, tell our state that time so she can be found quickly.вҖқ ThatвҖҷs what he said.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : But your daughter has a statement like вҖңSay 17:40.вҖқ Do you have any knowledge about that?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I have no knowledge of that.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : During these events, did Salim ever come to your house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Salim came. He came at six, six-thirty. He kissed his uncleвҖҷs hand. He kissed his auntвҖҷs hand. His aunt had just arrived. Since we had come from Mersin, he asked about us, too. At that moment, I went upstairs. When I came back, he had left. He didnвҖҷt stay at our door for five minutes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When you say his uncleвҖ”do you mean your father-in-law?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, my husband, HГјseyin GГјran.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : HГјseyin GГјran, pardon. HГјseyin GГјran is SalimвҖҷs uncle.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes, he is his uncle.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Where had you come from on the day of the incident?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : We had come from Mersin.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You had come from Mersin. He came at six to kiss his uncleвҖҷs hand.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes, he came. His uncle was at the door, as was his aunt.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Do you remember the exact time?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember exactly. It was around six, six-thirty.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did you speak with Salim at that time? Did you see him?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I saw him. He was with his son.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : As of that date, Narin had disappeared. According to one claim, Salim killed Narin. Did you sense anything suspicious about Salim?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, he was as he is every day.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Normal, as he is every day?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : NormalвҖ”as he is when he comes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : No panic? No excitement?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I didnвҖҷt see any.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So, as far as you saw, you didnвҖҷt suspect anything?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.В In any case, at that moment I went upstairs, and when I came back, he was gone.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Do you know Nevzat?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Of course, he is our neighbor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : He is your neighbor. Yes. How were relations, generally, between Nevzat and the GГјran family? Or between him and Salim GГјran?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Generally it was good. Up to this day it was good for us. But from now on, for us, it isnвҖҷt good.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Why?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Suspicion keeps going to him. It goes to Salim, too.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : It goes to Salim. It goes to YГјksel. It goes to Enes. Suspicion is now split into four. It goes to all four.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Then if itвҖҷs split into four, bring out that station camera. Bring it out.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : God willing. God willing.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : God willing, if it comes out, everything will be revealed.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : God willing.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Even if itвҖҷs one of us, may God curse them, whoever it is.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : It will come out. We will request the camera, too. You did not witness any problem between Nevzat and Salim up to now. But after this incident, because you suspect Nevzat, youвҖҷve cut him off.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I learned these things at the station anyway.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : After that, you cut off Nevzat. But you have no prior enmity with Nevzat?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Setting aside your suspicionsвҖ”clearing yourself of themвҖ”I ask you this: Is it possible Nevzat could kill someone?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Up to now, no.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Aha. Up to now, no. Is it possible Salim could kill someone?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Not for him either. To this day, with so many children, not a single slap did we take from him.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Then is it possible YГјksel killed her daughter, or Enes killed his sister?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt believe it.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I donвҖҷt believe it. Who is Delal Kaya?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : She is my niece.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : And Mahide Kaya. Did they come to the house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : What time did they come?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt know the exact time. They had come to the village. They called me. I had asked for it. I said, bring me socks.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : They sell socks, right?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : They donвҖҷt sell. A driver had brought them. I said, bring them to me. They brought me the socks; I bought them and they left. I bought socks. They left for home.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Did they come before Salim arrived or after?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt exactly remember.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Because Mahide says in her statement that she saw you around 18:00, thatвҖҷs why we ask. Was the time Mahide came to you before or after Salim came?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt remember. I donвҖҷt remember.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Alright. Now thisвҖ”where is your house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : In the village.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Where in the village?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : A bit outside the village. Behind the school.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : On a hill?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Not on the hill. Just below the hill.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So itвҖҷs not in a place where you can easily have a full view of your surroundings when you look around, correct?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : My house is the only one there.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : The only one.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Behind the school.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Your house, that is. Where is ArifвҖҷs house? How far is it?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Since itвҖҷs on a hill, a drop-off, I canвҖҷt say how far.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Let me put it this way. From your house, can you see ArifвҖҷs house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : From below?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : From your house, ArifвҖҷs house.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I can see the tops of the structures.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Can you see the garden or the barn? Because YГјksel first comes to you, right? Asking where Narin is.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes, she calls from the hill.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : She calls out, вҖңWhere is Narin,вҖқ right? Is that because she went to QurвҖҷan class together with your daughter? In other words, at the time Narin was killed, between 15:15 and 15:30, did you observe any activity at YГјkselвҖҷs house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I did not. I didnвҖҷt leave the house at all.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You didnвҖҷt go out at all. Can you see NevzatвҖҷs house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I cannot see his house.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : So you didnвҖҷt see or hear any vehicle, a red car, a white car, any such activity?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I didnвҖҷt hear. If it passed at my door, I would surely hear it. I didnвҖҷt see it pass at my door either.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You said Narin came at 2, correct, Ms.В MaЕҹЕҹallah? What time did Mine come?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : It was 17:40.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You say Mine came around 17:40. Because you mixed it up.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I understand.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : We mixed that up.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What time did Yusuf Kaya come? He is your nephew as well, right, Yusuf Kaya?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : He is my nephew. It was around one-thirty. He and my son left the house at around two, two-thirty.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Why did Yusuf come to the house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : He brought us bread. We were out of bread. My husband wasnвҖҷt home either. We called; he brought us bread.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Did you have breakfast late?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : So did you have breakfast around one-thirty?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : We had it together when my daughters came.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Around one-thirty.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Later than that, late.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : In your statement you say Yusuf Kaya came around 15:00. Around three.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, no, not around three.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : What time then?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, I kept sayingвҖ”вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Skip that Yusuf business. What time did you have breakfast? ItвҖҷs actually a very simple question.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : We had breakfast around three, three-thirty.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Right? Yes. You gave your statement that way.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You say Yusuf Kaya brought bread around three. And Maide KayaвҖ”she is your husbandвҖҷs niece, right? You are MaideвҖҷs aunt?вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Yes, her aunt.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : And Zelal?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I am her maternal aunt as well.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : I see. Because of this sock matter, they say they came to you around 17:00. They say they left the house at 17:00вҖ”pardon, 17:30вҖ“17:40. The children say they were at your house around that time.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Fine, they brought socks. Exactly. But I cannot say what time it was.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Maide says that while we were at the house, only Yusuf knocked on the door. YusufвҖ”uh. IвҖҷm telling you the statements. Please answer. Around 17вҖ“18 only Yusuf came, she says. So she says Yusuf came at 17:00.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Prosecutor, my nephew Yusuf is one person; my husbandвҖҷs grandson is another. Yusufcan GГјran is another. There are two Yusufs.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Two Yusufs. Yes. They say the Yusuf who came around 18:00 was Yusuf Kaya. Maide is referring to Yusuf Kaya.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Maide did not see Yusuf Kaya.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Understood. She didnвҖҷt see him.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : They took my son; they went to the coffeehouse together.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright. One more thing, Your HonorвҖ”there are also two phone records I wanted to show, if you agree.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Of course.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : This is Birsen, in her first statement. Yes. In her first statement about two oвҖҷclock, she says, вҖңMy mother was speaking with Sister Zerrin.вҖқ Around fourteen.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I spoke with her too.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Could you hand the microphone to the Prosecutor?вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Around two oвҖҷclockвҖ”with Sister ZerrinвҖ” I have the call detail records. Around 13:32, registered to the name Taner MehmetoДҹlu.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : She is also my sister-in-law.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Which Zerrin is this? Your Sister Zerrin?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : She was my husbandвҖҷs daughter.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : There is no number saved as Zerrin?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt know numbers.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : I see. Around 14:00вҖ”this sister-in-law of yours? Is Taner MehmetoДҹlu your sister-in-law?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Because itвҖҷs registered in his name.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Ah, so thatвҖҷs the one. Yes. Alright. So there is no Zerrin listed here, correct? Not among these numbers right now?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt know numbers.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You know this is your sister-in-law though. Look, let me say something. Listen to me. In your call records there is only 13:32. You have no call at 14:00. Do you understand me? So there is no Zerrin. I am asking you only about the 13:30 I have in hand.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Not Zerrin. Look, I also spoke with Zerrin. I donвҖҷt know at what time. Fatma is my sister-in-law.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : IвҖҷm telling you about her daughter. Alright. You say you donвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Fine. She may have mixed it up too.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright. She gives very exact times like 17:40. ThatвҖҷs why I ask. Birsen says, вҖңShe was speaking with Aunt Hatice around 17:40; thatвҖҷs why I mixed up the two oвҖҷclock.вҖқвҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : After Narin disappeared, we all mixed things up.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Aunt Hatice. Yes. Look at these numbers too. You donвҖҷt know them?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt know numbers. I cannot read or write.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : How did you immediately recognize your sister-in-law then?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Because you said it?вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : Alright.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I say it because I speak with my sisters-in-law.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : But you didnвҖҷt identify it by seeing the number?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : But I donвҖҷt know numbers.вҖӢ


Trial Prosecutor : You donвҖҷt know the numbers?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I donвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Yes. I am illiterate. I cannot recognize the numbers you showed me. However, on the day of the incident whom did you speak with?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : My sisters-in-law. Two sisters-in-law. And we spoke with our girl Zerrin.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : We spoke with our girl Zerrin.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : Now, you say Narin came to the house twice.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Is that microphone not working? Is there sound? Could you check, Mr.В Mehmet? Is the battery dead? Try turning it off and on. If that doesnвҖҷt fix it, itвҖҷs broken. Is it okay now?вҖӢ

title: Testimony of MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran
section: DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ
text:
DiyarbakДұr Bar Association вҖӢ

Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ вҖӢ

Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : Now, your statements that Narin came to the house twice that day later turn into she came once. You also say you were on the phone with your sister-in-law; when your daughters commented about the time, then you realized the correct version and say Narin actually came to the house at two oвҖҷclock. However, these are independent of the time of your phone call. According to your first statement, and even your other statements, first around two oвҖҷclock the doorbell rang. After it rang, my daughter opened the door; I was busy. I asked Birsen or my other daughter who had come, and they said Narin came. Toward evening around five the doorbell rang again and I again did not look at the left door. My daughters looked. When I asked my daughters who had come, they again said Narin came.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : One moment, let our counsel finish.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : This is something included in your signed statement. So rather than you mixing up the time, there is a situation where you asked immediately after the door closed on two occasions and received the answer Narin both times. Why did you give a statement to this effect?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : That was confusion. After Narin disappeared, Mina came at that time. I repeat, Mina came. Since after Narin disappeared she was constantly on my mind, I kept saying Narin.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : DidnвҖҷt your daughter actually say вҖңMina cameвҖқ the moment the door closed?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : They told me Mina came. She said that. Mina came slipped my mind. After Narin disappeared, Mina completely left my mind. Completely. Narin is all thatвҖҷs on my mind.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : When Salim came to your house that day, did he make a phone call at the house? Did you see?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I wasnвҖҷt outside at that time. I went upstairs. He came and kissed his uncleвҖҷs hand, kissed his auntвҖҷs hand. He asked after us. We had come from Mersin. He was with his son at that time. I went upstairs; when I came back he was gone. He didnвҖҷt even stand at the door for five minutes.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : After Narin disappeared, when the search began, did Enes come to your house to ask about Narin?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.В YГјksel called me.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : Enes did not come?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, I did not see Enes. I did not see him.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : When YГјksel called out to you from the hill, you responded along the lines of вҖңAre you only now searching for your daughter at this hour?вҖқвҖ”is that correct?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : My husband scolded her.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : Normally, around what time do Narin and the other children go home? Your daughter is also little. When she plays, around what time does she go home?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : If she gets permission from her mother, she goes home even late. Since sheвҖҷs at my house, her mother is at ease. If I send my daughters to her house, IвҖҷm at ease as well. We send our children to each other.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : Did your children come often?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes. She would also frequently leave them with me and go to Batman. Even if there was a mourning, she would go. They would leave them with me.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : So when her mother sent Narin to you, would she ever confirm whether she arrived? Would she call you? вҖңDid she come?вҖқ Or when you sent your children?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, if she was with me, she was at ease. She knew. She knew I wouldnвҖҷt let her go. But that day Narin came to us. When she came, it was to ask about my daughters. She went to the mosque. I did not see Narin after that.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : After Narin disappeared, did you observe any carpet washing, blanket washing, a general cleaning at YГјkselвҖҷs house?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Metin ArkaЕҹ : No.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you.вҖӢ



Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir вҖӢ

Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Are you saying that the argument among the women that day never happened, or are you saying you didnвҖҷt hear it? Did you not witness such an argument, or did it not happen at all?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : She said it did happen.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I said it happened.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : She said it happened. There was an argument.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Did Nesrin GГјran utter any insult in your presence?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Did she turn somewhere and say something, insult someone?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Nesrin GГјran would not put such words in her mouth.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : She would not. Alright. Did your daughter Birsen ever say to you, вҖңSalim told us to say we saw the girl at 17:40, to say we saw Narin, otherwise there will be trouble,вҖқ anything like that?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : You were held in custody. How many days were you together?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : The custody periodвҖ”вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Within the family.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : ThatвҖҷs separateвҖ”this is my 56th day. I am in prison.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : What IвҖҷm asking about your daughter is this. During that period in custody and the prior periodвҖ”your own biological daughter.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : She has a clear statement: вҖңSalim told us to say we saw her at 17:40, otherwise there will be trouble.вҖқ You have been arrested over the time issue as well. That is the reason for your arrest. Did you never have such a conversation?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : With Salim?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I asked this question as well. I asked this question as well.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I asked it very clearly. Very clearly indeed.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : My last question: Did you ever hear in the village about a problem between Arif and Nevzat?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I did not hear. I was not at home.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : How many years have you been living there in the village?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Since I was born.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Since birth. If there had been a feud or a problem, wouldnвҖҷt a closed community hear of it? You would.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I would.вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : Thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you. Now, Ms.В MaЕҹЕҹallah. When Narin came to your house to play with your daughters, would Arif or YГјksel call you to ask if she had come?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Not by phone. Is that what you mean?вҖӢ


Law. AydДұn Г–zdemir : No, noвҖ”in general. A week before, a month beforeвҖ”routinely. Since she would routinely come, I also send my child to the neighbor across the way. You send yours too. I was curious how Arif confirmed that Narin had come to your house.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : She would come with her motherвҖҷs permission.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When she came, would YГјksel keep track of her daughter?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Of course, YГјksel would keep track of her daughter.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : How would she keep track?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : WouldnвҖҷt a mother keep track of her child?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : When it got dark in the evening, how would Narin return home?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : When it was dark, either my son would take her or my two daughters would take her.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I see. So they would take her and she would go home. They would take her like that.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran
section: Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ
text:
Ministry of Family and Social Services вҖӢ

Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun вҖӢ

Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : In your statement dated 12.09вҖ”that is, September 12вҖ”more than 20 days after the incident, you say Narin came home at 17:40. How did the вҖң40вҖқ remain in your mind so precisely up to the minute? What is the significance of вҖң40вҖқ for you?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Counsel, I asked that as well; we are moving on. I asked it very clearly three times; we are moving on. We asked; we are moving on.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : She may ask.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I asked, I asked. WeвҖҷre moving on; we asked.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : On the day of the incident, at 7 oвҖҷclock was YГјksel speaking there? Were Enes or Muhammed present? Or was anyone else there?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : When YГјksel called me, my husband was there. My sister-in-law was there.вҖӢ


Law. Elif AslДұ Еһahin Torun : Was anyone with YГјksel?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No, she was alone. There was no one else.вҖӢ



Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz вҖӢ

Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Now, Your Honor, first I would like to correct something, and we have a request from you. You and the defense keep saying these questions have already been asked, but I think thatвҖҷs the logic of cross-examinationвҖ”questions may be the same, phrased differently. We can ask the same question to each witness, frankly. Because one witness is being heard while the others have not yet been heard. So saying this has been askedвҖ”вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : I reject that as well. I believe asking a question once to a witness is sufficient. I reject that too, Counsel. Go ahead.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Ms.В MaЕҹЕҹallah, Mr.В NevzatвҖ”Nevzat BahtiyarвҖ”also made an allegation against you. What do you think about this with regard to Mr.В Salim?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I do not accept that. I do not accept itвҖ”let him first look to his own circle. Let him look in the mirror. A person does not hide behind slander. He does not slander.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Why do you think he slandered you? Did you have bad relations?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Why did he make such a slander then?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Ask him.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : We asked him as well.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Then ask him again. Why is he making this slander? A person does not slander. He speaks properly, with honor and dignity. He does not hide behind slander.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : LookвҖ”Narin disappears, is murdered, and is found 19 days later. During those 19 days a lot happens in the file. There are fires, the electricity goes out, there are contradictory statements. Just your two daughters Melike and Birsen have three different statements. You have different statements. All of them contradict one another. Therefore, did you never discuss anything that would arouse suspicion as to who did it? For example, did you not speak with your daughter Birsen?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I mean, talkingвҖ”вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Like, who killed this girl? Did you ever think about it?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : MeвҖ”Narin?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : We didnвҖҷt think about it. They asked whom we suspected. No one in the village came to mind. In the end we thought she had been abducted. But she was right under our noses; we didnвҖҷt know.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : Who do you think killed her? Nevzat?вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : You have the right not to answer this question as well. No, do not answer. There is no crime and evidence in these things. I will not take an answer.вҖӢ


Law. Abdullah YДұlmaz : ThatвҖҷs all for now, Your Honor.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Now, I am meeting you with understanding. I think I allowed this quite a bit yesterday as well. When you ask the same question ten timesвҖ” I asked it, that side asked it, that side asked it, that side five times eachвҖҰ No, no, it has been asked. Whether sensitivity was shown or notвҖ” not as criticism, nor as defense. I did that yesterday. But after the situation following the initial beginning today, I changed my mind. I am no longer allowing it. After I receive the answer to the question I asked, there is no such thing as you getting a different answer.вҖӢ


Defendant Enes GГјran : Aunt, you say he went up the hill. Then I was with my mother too. Muhammed was with her, too. Did you see us or not?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : On the hill? When your mother called me. I did not see at that moment, my dear. I did not see.вҖӢ


Defendant Enes GГјran : Okay.вҖӢ


Defendant Salim GГјran : The defendant MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran is my uncleвҖҷs wife. My uncle is not like a father figure; he is my father. He raised us, fed us. May God damn the one who made this slander; may he burn in the fires of hell.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Question, question, question. No question. Thank you. We will take questions. Counsel for the defendant, please proceed.вҖӢ

title: Testimony of MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran
section: Defense Counsels for Enes GГјran вҖӢ
text:
Defense Counsels for Enes GГјran вҖӢ

Law. Mahir Akbilek вҖӢ

Law. Mahir Akbilek : Ms.В MaЕҹallah, do you remember on which day and at what times you first saw Enes during the search and sweep activities?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : On the day Narin disappeared?вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Yes, take that as the basis. After that, did you see him on the 21st or the 22nd? If on the 21st, within what time frame; if on the 22nd, when did you first see him?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : On the 20th, we had just come from Mersin. We had gone for my daughterвҖҷs enrollment. With that travel fatigue and then this incident on the 21st, I did not see Enes. I did not see him during the day. I saw Enes when he called me; I saw him then. I saw only YГјksel. He may have been up above, but I saw only YГјksel. Because there were trees between us. I did not see him.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : When?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : When YГјksel called meвҖ” it was seven, seven-thirty.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : No, try to recall when you saw Enes.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : You mean at the time she disappeared. At the time Narin disappeared.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Go back from today to the day Narin was killed; try to recall the first moment you saw EnesвҖ” face to face, as we are seeing now.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I saw him next to the school. I saw him while we were searching for Narin.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Which day was that?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : The first day she disappeared.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Alright. Did you see EnesвҖҷs face and body?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Of course.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Did you notice any injury, bruise, bleeding on his face?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : I did not.вҖӢ


Law. Mahir Akbilek : Thank you.вҖӢ


Presiding Judge : Thank you. Yes, Ms.В MaЕҹЕҹallah. Thank you. There is a question, please. I thought we were done.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : You say you spoke on the phone that day. Was it only with your own phone, orвҖҰ?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : No.В I also spoke with my daughterвҖҷs phone. I will speak.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Then could this be the reason the call record cannot be understood there? You spoke using your daughterвҖҷs phone that day, correct?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes. I spoke with my daughterвҖҷs phone. I spoke with my own phone. I spoke with my sonвҖҷs phone.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : You said you had come from Mersin the day before.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Do you remember how many children came and went to your home that day? How frequently did they come?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Normally, children always come to my home. I love children. I love children.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : I am asking specifically about that day, for this reason: given your houseвҖҷs location, the road apparently doesnвҖҷt go beyond your house. That is, when you go along the road passing in front of the school, you arrive at your house.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : The camera shows quite a bit of activityвҖ” Yes.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Children come to my home.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Was there very frequent coming and going of children to your house that day? ThatвҖҷs what IвҖҷm asking.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : There was.вҖӢ


Law. Mustafa Demir : Yes, thank you.вҖӢ



Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir вҖӢ

Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Do you know Gazal Bahtiyar?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Yes. She is my neighbor.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : How are relations between you? How is your communication with her?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : WellвҖ”вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Before the incident.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Before the incident it was good. She would come and go. I gave her my garden. She would plant my garden. Our relations were good.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Where did Narin and your children generally play in the village? In front of the school? Near the mosque?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : They would be in front of the school as well. They would go to the front of the mosque, too. They would also go to my motherвҖҷs. Our children could go anywhere comfortably in our village.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : In the last three months before the incident, is it possible that Gazal Bahtiyar or Nevzat Bahtiyar might not have seen Narin?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : On that day?вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : In the month or two or three priorвҖ” it doesnвҖҷt matter.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : How would they not see her? If they went down, they would see her; if they went up, they would see her. How would they not see her?вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Alright. Was there any problem between the Nevzat Bahtiyar family and the Arif GГјran family? How were relations prior to the incident?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : There was no problem at all.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : There was no problem.вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : There wasnвҖҷt. As far as I know, there wasnвҖҷt.вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Now, IвҖҷm asking for NarinвҖ” for the deceased. Was Nevzat Bahtiyar a stranger to her?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Pardon?вҖӢ


Law. Muhammed Fatih Demir : Was he a stranger to Narin? Or would she see him like an uncle? I mean, would she like him?вҖӢ


MaЕҹЕҹallah GГјran : Well, when his grandchildren were around, he would love his grandchildren. He would go.вҖӢ
